# Invention

From Imperator Wiki

[Jump to navigation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invention#mw-head)[Jump to search](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invention#searchInput)

This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 19: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")**Inventions** represent specific technologies or practices that can be adopted by the state in order to get a specific military, economic, or political effects and bonuses. Every invention is associated with a specific field of advances and is structured as part of a tree, with most inventions requiring prerequisites that must be adopted first before the next invention can be taken.

Some inventions also have additional requirements, such as requiring a [![Image 20: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") primary or integrated culture of a specific culture group, a certain [![Image 21: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government type, a certain [country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank"), particular [DLCs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/DLC "DLC"), or simply only being available to certain countries. These culture-, government-, tag-, rank-, and DLC-specific inventions generally will not appear unless their requirements are met.

Most inventions have modifiers that are applied **when active**, which are permanent modifiers that the country gains once it adopts the invention. Some inventions also have **when activated** effects, with some merely descriptions of [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building "Building"), [![Image 22: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonder effects, [![Image 23: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject types, or other mechanics that are permanently unlocked once the invention is adopted, while others are one-time effects and bonuses that triggered immediately when the invention is purchased. Some inventions are **keystone** inventions that are specially marked with a more elaborate border; these mark inventions that are considered important, whether because they have a strong effect or modifier or unlock particularly important mechanics, but are not mechanically different in any way from normal inventions.

Unlike advances, no country has any inventions adopted at the start. However, most countries that start with a higher level of advances do start with a corresponding number of [![Image 24: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovations to purchase inventions with at the beginning of the game.

- [x]

## Innovations[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Innovations") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Innovations")]

Each invention requires spending one [![Image 25: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")**innovation** to adopt. Innovations are gained primarily from technological [advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Advance "Advance"), which grant one innovation for each level, and can also be gained by adopting certain [![Image 26: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, as a reward for certain specific [![Image 27: Menu mission.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Menu_mission.png/28px-Menu_mission.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") missions, as a rare outcome of successfully stealing technology, or occasionally from [![Image 28: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") researchers that have certain traits. All innovations are equal, and there is no requirement to use [![Image 29: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovations gained from a particular field of advances on an invention inside that field. It is entirely possible to invest all of a country's innovations on a single field of inventions, if the country is willing to neglect the others. Note that there are many more inventions than it is realistically possible to adopt over the course of the game, and even technologically advanced countries will be forced to pick and choose which areas they want to prioritize and which inventions they will leave for later - or not at all.

The following [![Image 30: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions grant [![Image 31: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")innovations:

*   **+4** from [![Image 32: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") The Bureaucracy of War ([Indian traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Indian_traditions "Indian traditions"); under _Indian kingdom traditions_)
*   **+4** from [![Image 33: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Cradle of Civilization ([Levantine traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levantine_traditions "Levantine traditions"); under _Levantine kingdom traditions_)
*   **+4** from [![Image 34: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Cavalry Skirmish ([Persian traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Persian_traditions "Persian traditions"); under _Persian rural traditions_)
*   **+3** from [![Image 35: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") The Fabri ([Italic traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions"); under _Roman traditions_)
*   **+3** from [![Image 36: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Roman Roads ([Italic traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions"); under _Roman traditions_)

### Researcher innovations[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Researcher innovations") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Researcher innovations")]

Every researcher that has at least one of the [![Image 37: Obsessive](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2d/Obsessive.png/28px-Obsessive.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Obsessive") obsessive, [![Image 38: Intelligent](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Intelligent.png/28px-Intelligent.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Intelligent") intelligent, [![Image 39: Polymath](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Polymath.png/28px-Polymath.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Polymath") polymath, or [![Image 40: Scholar](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c5/Scholar.png/28px-Scholar.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Scholar") scholar traits has a chance generating an extra innovation each month (as well as gaining [![Image 41: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")**5** statesmanship). The base chance for each researcher is around **0.2%**, multiplied by around **1.25** for each trait that the character has. Since a researcher needs at least one of the traits to be eligible for generating extra innovations at all, the effective base chance is around **0.25%** for a researcher that has one trait, going up to a maximum chance of around **0.49%** per month for a researcher that has all 4 traits. This chance is rolled for all researchers that are eligible, so having multiple researchers that have at least one of these traits significantly increases the chances of getting an extra innovation.

Once a researcher discovers an extra innovation, there is a cooldown of **2** years before the country can get another free innovation, and a cooldown of **5** years before that particular researcher can generate another free innovation (however, during the intervening 3 years other researchers can still generate free innovations).

## Cookies and targeting

To give you a better and more personal experience we use cookies and third-party tracking tools. We process personal data such as browser information and IP adress for analysing our website and e-commerce performance. To provide you with relevant ads and for measuring our marketing efforts we also share data with 3rd party advertising providers such as Google and Meta. By clicking OK you agree to cookies and targeting.[Privacy policy](https://legal.paradoxplaza.com/)

Decline OK

Cookie settings

![Image 54: Company Logo](https://cdn.cookielaw.org/logos/static/ot_company_logo.png)

## Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. 

[Privacy policy](http://legal.paradoxplaza.com/privacy?locale=en)

Allow All
### Manage Consent Preferences

#### Targeting Cookies

- [x] Targeting Cookies 

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Cookies Details‎

#### Performance Cookies

- [x] Performance Cookies 

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

Cookies Details‎

#### Functional Cookies

- [x] Functional Cookies 

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

Cookies Details‎

#### Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

Cookies Details‎

# Civic_inventions
============================================================
Title: Civic inventions - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

## Basic inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Basic inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Basic inventions")]

### Left tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Left tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Left tree")]

[![Image 1: Civic Tech Base Left.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/7/7b/Civic_Tech_Base_Left.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Civic_Tech_Base_Left.png)

| Name | Modifier | On activate | Requirements | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Standardized Measures | * [![Image 2: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value")**+5%** Import Value |  |  | _The amphora capitolina was an amphora kept in the temple of Jupiter, by which all other amphora were intended to be measured._ |
| Logistics Bureau | * [![Image 3: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Capital Import Routes |  | * Standardized Measures | _Our burgeoning state is beginning to struggle under its own weight. A dedicated logistics administration must be instituted to handle the flow of traders and merchants into our capital._ |
| Property Tax | * [![Image 4: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**+2.5%** National Tax |  | * Standardized Measures | _Landowners are every state's main source of revenue; by establishing a code of practice, we can avoid difficult situations when it comes to collection._ |
| Pythagorean Mathematics | * [![Image 5: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**−2.5%** Build Cost |  | * Logistics Bureau | _The ancient Greek, Pythagoras, was a mathematician and philosopher of legendary repute. Embracing the practical aspects of his theories will have numerous scientific applications._ |
| Opus Caementicium | * [![Image 6: Build time.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Build_time.png/28px-Build_time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**−20%** Build Time |  | * Property Tax | _Roman concrete was a seminal invention, developed in the 3rd or 2nd Century BC. It quickly became widely used, and was found in villas, military buildings, roads, and many other constructions._ |
| Movement of Water | * [![Image 7: Civic province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6b/Civic_investment.png/28px-Civic_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Civic province investment")**−10%** Civic Provincial Investment Cost | Get [![Image 8: Province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/State_free_investments.png/28px-State_free_investments.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment")**1** free province investment | * Logistics Bureau | _Mills, aqueducts, and primitive engines, all developed due to the need for flowing water within the confines of a city. This technology drove further innovation, eventually resulting in sawmills and complex irrigation._ |
| The Father of Geometry | * [![Image 9: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output")**+3%** National Citizen Output |  | * Pythagorean Mathematics | _Euclid of Alexandria, a prolific mathematician and scholar, developed the first coherent study of geometry. The basic premises he describes should be taught across our nation._ |
| State Harbor Fees | * [![Image 10: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**+5%** National Commerce Income |  | * Pythagorean Mathematics | _Implementing a small fee on the use of our ports is unlikely to affect footfall, and will earn us a little more from trade._ |
| River Barges | * [![Image 11: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Capital Import Routes * [![Image 12: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value")**+10%** Import Value * [![Image 13: Navy movement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Navy_movement_speed.png/28px-Navy_movement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy")**+10%** Naval Movement Speed |  | One of: * Opus Caementicium * Movement of Water | _The needs of our capital must be served - operating regular grain barges from the coast to the point nearest our capital will alleviate some of the burden on private traders._ |
| Eisphora | * [![Image 14: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**+2.5%** National Tax |  | * Opus Caementicium | _The Greeks had an ephemeral taxation system, but the practice of levying a wealth tax during times of crisis was a valuable addition to the treasury._ |
| Expanded Financial Remit | * [![Image 15: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**−5%** Monthly Wages for Characters |  | * State Harbor Fees | _Offices such as that of the Quaestors of Rome were imbued with judicial power, and supervised the financial affairs of state. Expanding the remit of these persons will give them greater ability to root out financial wrongdoing, and corrupt behavior._ |
| Ius Honorarium | * [![Image 16: Enact law cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Changing_Laws "Enact law cost")**−15%** Enact Law Cost |  | One of: * River Barges * Eisphora | _Yielding to the magistrates, the right to amend, supplement and correct existing laws, lessens the need for our government to pass judgment on iterations of the same law, numerous times._ |
| Lighthouses | * [![Image 17: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value")**+10%** Export Value |  | * Expanded Financial Remit | _Construction of lighthouses is an absolute necessity to keep the flow of trade safe in our nation. The concept of lighthouses developed from simple hill-fires, to grand edifices such as the lighthouse at Alexandria._ |
| Maritime Patrols | * [![Image 18: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Capital Import Routes * [![Image 19: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**+1%** Population Happiness |  | One of: * Expanded Financial Remit * The Father of Geometry | _Piracy was a huge issue in the Mediterranean - until the eradication of the Cilician pirates by Pompey in 66 BC, a strong maritime presence was required to safeguard civilian and military vessels._ |
| Liturgies | * [![Image 20: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**+10%** National Tax | Add permanent **Sponsored Public Spaces** modifier to the capital province | * Ius Honorarium | _The Greeks created a system whereby wealthy individuals were required to sponsor one of a number of different public works. Ranging from dramatic tragedies to regiment formation, we are sure to find this system helpful._ |
| Pedagoguery | * [![Image 21: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity")**+10%** Population Capacity |  | * Liturgies | _Encouraging those of means to employ renowned pedagogues, should ensure that the elite possess a surfeit of intelligence when coming of age._ |
| Gromatici | * [![Image 22: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**−25%** Build Roads Cost |  | * Liturgies | '_Rod Men', as they were otherwise known, assisted the primary surveyor in assessing the straightness of roads. These civil engineers were experts in their field, and greatly improved the efficiency of road building._ |
| Regulated Money Lenders | * [![Image 23: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**+2.5%** National Tax |  | * Pedagoguery | _The plight of the poor is rarely relieved by money lenders, but we must accept that they are a necessary evil. Instituting financial regulations, however, may at least let us profit from them._ |
| River Outposts | * [![Image 24: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Capital Import Routes |  | One of: * Maritime Patrols * Lighthouses | _Trade flows with the tide, they say, but the rivers are the veins and arteries of our commercial network. Establishing regular outposts along them will give traders a sense of security, and attract greater investment._ |
| Use of State Land | * [![Image 25: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**+2.5%** National Tax |  | * Gromatici | _The Mauryan empire levied a land usage tax on territories it considered as crown land. Aggressive zoning of this land can be a significant money-making opportunity._ |
| Limited Governship | * [![Image 26: Governor wages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Change_governor_policy_cost.png/28px-Change_governor_policy_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor wages")**−5%** Governor Wage |  | * Regulated Money Lenders | _Displacing the authority of our governors is essential for reducing the likelihood of them becoming minor independent warlords trying to wrest control from our central leadership._ |
| Temporary Office | * [![Image 27: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**−10%** Monthly Wages for Characters * [![Image 28: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")**−2.5%** Cohort Loyalty Gain Chance * [![Image 29: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold")**+5%** Threshold for Civil War |  | * River Outposts | _By reducing the time any single soldier holds a title in our military, they are less likely to have the time and means to take advantage of the system._ |
| Encourage Exploration | * [![Image 30: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Capital Import Routes |  | * Use of State Land | _There are still unknown lands to the east - by encouraging our brave citizens to venture further afield, we can flood our market with exotic items of all kinds._ |
| Mandated Record Keeping | * [![Image 31: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**+5%** National Commerce Income |  | * Encourage Exploration | _In Rome, the keeping of records by the mercatores and argentii was a legal requirement. Being able to track the flow of revenue is a necessity for a functioning bureaucracy._ |
| Prestigious Trade Goods | * [![Image 32: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value")**+5%** Export Value |  | * Encourage Exploration | _Our merchants and traders have spent years building up a certain flare and some prestige around the goods being exported from our nation, and so more people would like the chance to grow closer and take part in our trade deals._ |
| Import Tax | * [![Image 33: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value")**+5%** Import Value |  | One of: * Temporary Office * Limited Governship | _Many empires in antiquity imposed a tax on the import, and sometimes export, of goods. Whilst Rome felt they had little use for it, the Mauryan empire sometimes imposed taxes of up to 20% in imported goods._ |
| Embracing Philosophy | * [![Image 34: Neutral stance](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5d/Stance_neutral.png/28px-Stance_neutral.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_stance "Neutral stance")**−20%** Diplomatic Stance Cost * [![Image 35: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**+25%** Maximum Research Efficiency | A philosopher arrives in the country. Add permanent **School of Philosophy** modifier to the capital province | One of: * Mandated Record Keeping * Prestigious Trade Goods | _The various schools of philosophy arising out of Greece, and further afield, combine mathematics, theology, cosmology, and much more, to create a distinct world view. The study, debate, and intellectual sparring between these schools of thought is likely to fuel advancement in any number of fields._ |
| Trade Ports | * [![Image 36: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+2** Capital Import Routes | Add permanent **World Port** modifier to a random province with a port | One of: * Prestigious Trade Goods * Import Tax | _With large piers, ports and administration set aside for the merchants visiting our cities, we are sure to get a better handle on all trade being done inside the nation._ |

### Right tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Right tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Right tree")]

[![Image 37: Civic Tech Base Right.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/88/Civic_Tech_Base_Right.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Civic_Tech_Base_Right.png)

| Name | Modifier | On activate | Requirements | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Right to be Heard | * [![Image 38: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output")**+3%** National Citizen Output |  |  | _Acknowledging the right for our nobles to make their argument before the authorities will vastly increase their tendency to work within the system, and not without._ |
| Rights of Man | * [![Image 39: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output")**+3%** National Slave Output |  | * Right to be Heard | _Slave treatment varies wildly from state to state, but we believe that a basic code of human rights will encourage our slaves to work harder._ |
| Tribal Reserve | * [![Image 40: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output")**+3%** National Tribesman Output |  | * Right to be Heard | _Officially recognizing certain areas as belonging to the control of specific tribal people, is bound to increase their willingness to contribute._ |
| Code of Rights | * [![Image 41: Freeman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Pop_freemen_output.png/28px-Pop_freemen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Freeman output")**+3%** National Freeman Output |  | * Right to be Heard | _An established code, detailing the rights of every non-landowning member of our society, is likely to please the majority._ |
| Slave Latifundia | * [![Image 42: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus")**−1** Slaves needed for Surplus |  | * Rights of Man | _The establishment of vast plantations, mines, or other specialized land dedicated to production, populated largely by slaves or working class persons, vastly improves the efficiency of our resource gathering. Such latifundia were often distributed after conquest, to entrepreneurial private citizens, commanders, and those willing to pay for them._ |
| Granted Manumission | * [![Image 43: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion")**+10%** Pop Promotion Speed |  | * Code of Rights | _Enabling slave owners to release their slaves with genuine legal status, is vital to the continued co-existence of owner and slave._ |

## Unique inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Unique inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Unique inventions")]

### Greek tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Greek tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Greek tree")]

These inventions are available to all countries with a primary or an integrated culture belonging to the **Hellenic** culture group.

[![Image 96: Civic Tech Greek1.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/b/b4/Civic_Tech_Greek1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Civic_Tech_Greek1.png)

[![Image 97: Civic Tech Greek2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/1/14/Civic_Tech_Greek2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Civic_Tech_Greek2.png)

| Name | Modifier | On activate | Requirements | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Advanced Mechanics | * [![Image 98: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**−10%** Build Cost * [![Image 99: Build time.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Build_time.png/28px-Build_time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**−10%** Build Time |  | * Standardized Measures (Left tree) | _Greek civil engineers used chain drives, lever systems, and piston mechanics in construction and practical applications, greatly simplifying the requirements of advanced engineering._ |
| Fire Hose | * [![Image 100: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness")**+3%** National Freeman Happiness |  | * Water Pumps (Right tree) | _By applying the mechanics of the double piston pump, Heron of Alexandria invented the fire-hose, which became an efficient tool when fighting conflagrations._ |
| Odometer | * [![Image 101: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**+10%** National Commerce Income |  | * Fire Hose | _Before the odometer, distances were commonly measured by counting steps, a somewhat imprecise method. With an odometer mounted on a chariot, a more exact measurement could be achieved by counting the rotations of the chariot's wheels._ |

| Cartography | * [![Image 103: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food")**0.1** Global Monthly Food Modifier * [![Image 104: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**+10%** National Commerce Income |  | * Odometer | _The ancient Greeks made great advances in making accurate maps, which became important assets for both the military and civic sectors when planning for campaigns and trading._ |

| Stoicism | * [![Image 106: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**+5%** Research Points * [![Image 107: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+5%** Integrated Culture Happiness * [![Image 108: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**+25%** Maximum Research Efficiency |  | * Cartography | _Stoicism is a philosophical system centered around logic and the natural order of things. Those who practice it strive to accept the present as it is, not allowing themselves to be controlled by emotion._ |
| Advanced Astronomy | * [![Image 109: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range")**+1%** Diplomatic Range Modifier * [![Image 110: Naval morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Naval_morale.png/28px-Naval_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_morale "Navy morale")**+10%** Naval Morale Modifier |  | * Astrolabe * Pyoulkos | _By deploying a differential gear to determine the angle between the ecliptic positions of the sun and the moon, advanced mechanisms that were able to predict lunar and solar eclipses were constructed, further expanding on the astronomer's understanding of the universe._ |
| Epicureanism | * [![Image 111: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**−0.01%** Monthly Corruption * [![Image 112: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+5%** Integrated Culture Happiness |  | * Stoicism | _The Epicureanism philosophy teaches that all things should be taken in moderation, limiting one's desires and enjoying simple pleasures to achieve tranquillity, freedom from fear, and absence of pain._ |

### Italic tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Italic tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civic_inventions&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Italic tree")]

These inventions are available to all countries with a primary or an integrated culture belonging to the **Italic** culture group.

| Arches | * [![Image 120: Build time.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Build_time.png/28px-Build_time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**−5%** Build Time |  | * Etruscan Pottery | _While early usage of arches have been recorded in cultures around the Mediterranean, it was the Romans who mastered this form of architecture, allowing them to build massive aqueducts and bridges that tied their empire together._ |

| Cursus Publicus | * [![Image 122: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**+0.01%** National Population Growth |  | * Arches | _A state regulated courier system used for conveying messages, packages and even people between forts and important stations throughout the Roman empire. Couriers, usually traveling by horse-drawn carts, could travel up to 80 kilometers per day, enabling efficient administration and communication to all corners of the nation._ |
| Julian Calendar | * [![Image 123: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**−0.01%** Monthly Corruption |  | * Lituus | _A calendar system proposed by Julius Caesar in 46 BC, designed around the solar year with a leap-year every fourth year to adjust for discrepancies. The length of a year could previously be altered through political means, but the Julian calendar circumvented this corrupt phenomenon by ensuring it remained aligned to the tropical year without human intervention._ |
| Insulae | * [![Image 124: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food")**+1%** Global Monthly Food Modifier |  | * Cursus Publicus | _Primarily populated by the labouring class, the insulae offered decent living conditions in densely populated districts, grouping together several houses into a larger block in a similar way to modern apartment buildings._ |
| Bacon | * [![Image 125: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food")**+1%** Global Monthly Food Modifier |  | * Insulae | _By combining a process of salting, curing and smoking, pork meat could efficiently be preserved, allowing it to be transported over long distances without spoiling the product. This made bacon affordable for the common man, turning it into a staple diet across the Roman empire, as fresh meat otherwise was reserved to the upper classes who could afford it._ |
| Lex Frumentaria | * [![Image 126: Slave happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Pop_slaves_happiness.png/28px-Pop_slaves_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Slave happiness")**+3%** National Slave Happiness * [![Image 127: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness")**+3%** National Freeman Happiness |  | One of: * Julian Calendar * Bacon | _Distribution of wheat to the lower classes at subsidized prices, or even free of charge, gave much needed relief to the less fortunate, in a similar way to modern welfare policies._ |

## Unique inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Martial_inventions&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Unique inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Martial_inventions&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Unique inventions")]

### Gallic tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Martial_inventions&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Gallic tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Martial_inventions&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Gallic tree")]

These inventions are available to all countries with a primary or an integrated culture belonging to the **Belgae**, **Gallic** or **Pannonian** culture group.

[![Image 122: Martial Tech Galls.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/7/7b/Martial_Tech_Galls.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Martial_Tech_Galls.png)

| Oppidum | * [![Image 123: Fort defense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fd/Fort_defense.png/28px-Fort_defense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_defense "Fort defense")**+10%** Fort Defense * [![Image 124: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort")**+1** National Fort Infrastructure Capacity * [![Image 125: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade")**+1%** Commerce Value |  | * Fortified Camps | _The oppida were large, fortified settlements, often built on hilltops which made them easy to defend. Oppida became important economic sites and trade hubs, and also political centres from which the authorities of the Gallic society made their decisions._ |
| Noric Steel | * [![Image 126: Offense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Offense.png/28px-Offense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Offense "Offense")**+5%** Light Infantry Offense * [![Image 127: Offense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Offense.png/28px-Offense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Offense "Offense")**+5%** Heavy Infantry Offense |  | * Oppidum | _Noric steel was mined from regions located around modern days Austria and Slovenia, and offered a natural chemical composition which made it harder than iron._ |

The construction of great halls devoted to such an education must be forever in the mind's eye of our people._ |

## Basic inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Basic inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Basic inventions")]

### Left tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Left tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Left tree")]

[![Image 1: Oratory Tech Base Left.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/c/c6/Oratory_Tech_Base_Left.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Oratory_Tech_Base_Left.png)

## Unique inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Unique inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Unique inventions")]

### India tree[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: India tree") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: India tree")]

These inventions are available to all countries with a primary or an integrated culture belonging to the **Aryan**, **Dravidan** or **Pracyan** culture group.

[![Image 116: Oratory Tech India.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/d/d1/Oratory_Tech_India.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Oratory_Tech_India.png)

| Name | Modifier | On activate | Requirements | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Brahmi Script | * [![Image 117: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+2.5%** Integrated Culture Happiness |  | * Local Embassies (Left tree) | _Spreading from the south, this ancient writing system was used ubiquitously in the Indian subcontinent. Found on the rock edicts of Ashoka, to numerous religious texts, the language provided common ground for the peoples of India._ |
| Wootz Steel | * [![Image 118: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output")**+1%** National Tribesman Output * [![Image 119: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**+1%** National Population Growth |  | * Brahmi Script | _Thanks to the availability of high-carbon steel, found around South India, the locals began the production of steely iron known as Wootz, characterized by its banded patterns and capability to create sharp and durable edges for swords._ |
| Toe Stirrup | * [![Image 120: Unit heavy cavalry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Unit_heavy_cavalry.png/28px-Unit_heavy_cavalry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heavy_Cavalry "Heavy Cavalry")[![Image 121: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline")**+5%** Heavy Cavalry Discipline * [![Image 122: Unit light cavalry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/30/Unit_light_cavalry.png/28px-Unit_light_cavalry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Cavalry "Light Cavalry")[![Image 123: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline")**+5%** Light Cavalry Discipline * [![Image 124: Unit horse archers.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/be/Unit_horse_archers.png/28px-Unit_horse_archers.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Horse_Archers "Horse Archers")[![Image 125: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline")**+5%** Horse Archer Discipline |  | * Wootz Steel | _By attaching a rope - with a loop at the end for the big toe - to the saddle, an early version of a stirrup was created, suitable for the warm India climate where people used to ride horses barefoot._ |

### Government type inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Government type inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Oratory_inventions&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Government type inventions")]

Name Modifier On activate Requirements Description
Due Process*   [![Image 1: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**+0.02** Monthly Ruler Popularity Gain _Ensuring that everyone has recourse to appeal against actions or indictments, protects both the people, and gives our government an air of fairness._

*   War Dedication
*   Formulaic Worship _Instituting a formal hierarchy of careers within our army, gives every common soldier a goal to aspire to, rendering a life in the army a more attractive prospect._
Marine Auxilia*   [![Image 60: Naval morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Naval_morale.png/28px-Naval_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_morale "Navy morale")**+2%** Navy Morale Recovery*   Water Mill _Choosing to serve as crewman or marine was the only choice that potential crew-members would receive. There primarily to defend against boarding, or to capture enemy ships in boarding actions, the presence of a marine detachment was a widespread tradition amongst state navies._
Fleet Hierarchy*   [![Image 61: Naval morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Naval_morale.png/28px-Naval_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_morale "Navy morale")**+2%** Navy Morale Recovery*   Bow Ornamentation _In addition to the complex ship-based hierarchy of officers, the establishment of a command structure for our navies will ensure that responsibility always lies in the correct places._
Expanded Naval Hierarchy*   [![Image 62: Naval morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Naval_morale.png/28px-Naval_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_morale "Navy morale")**+2%** Navy Morale Recovery*   Double Pay _Instituting a formalized hierarchy on board our vessels is vital in keeping order amidst the chaos of combat._
Severe Strictures*   [![Image 63: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery")**+2%** Army Morale Recovery*   Double Pay _Decimation, and other, similar practices, were used as last-resort punishments against units that had engaged in mutiny or cowardice. Decimation would involve the random selection of one tenth of a unit, who would then be executed by their former comrades. The ever-present threat of such a terrible punishment ensured that acts of cowardice were rare indeed._
Religious Board*   [![Image 64: Local manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Local_manpower.png/28px-Local_manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**+2.5%** National Manpower One of:

*   Marine Auxilia
*   Fleet Hierarchy _Insititute a formal board of religious persons, dedicated to deciding which deities, foreign or otherwise, were acceptable to be worshiped by soldiers. Not only does this give us control over our troops' religious observance, but encourages those of minority faiths, to serve us._
Service for Life*   [![Image 65: Local manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Local_manpower.png/28px-Local_manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**+2.5%** National Manpower One of:

*   Expanded Naval Hierarchy
*   Severe Strictures _The wounded, maimed, and otherwise unfit, may still be useful as reserves and border guards. Thus can we ensure that the best soldiers are always serving on the front line._
Zero Gains 4 Free Province Investments in Capital Province.One of:

*   Service for Life
*   Religious Board _The concept of 'zero', did not exist in the standard Roman numeral system. The existence of such a concept was vital to the Greek mathematicians in performing advanced calculations._

# Invention

From Imperator Wiki

(Redirected from [Innovation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Innovation&redirect=no "Innovation"))

[Jump to navigation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation#mw-head)[Jump to search](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation#searchInput)

This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 19: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")**Inventions** represent specific technologies or practices that can be adopted by the state in order to get a specific military, economic, or political effects and bonuses. Every invention is associated with a specific field of advances and is structured as part of a tree, with most inventions requiring prerequisites that must be adopted first before the next invention can be taken.

Some inventions also have additional requirements, such as requiring a [![Image 20: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") primary or integrated culture of a specific culture group, a certain [![Image 21: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government type, a certain [country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank"), particular [DLCs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/DLC "DLC"), or simply only being available to certain countries. These culture-, government-, tag-, rank-, and DLC-specific inventions generally will not appear unless their requirements are met.

Most inventions have modifiers that are applied **when active**, which are permanent modifiers that the country gains once it adopts the invention. Some inventions also have **when activated** effects, with some merely descriptions of [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building "Building"), [![Image 22: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonder effects, [![Image 23: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject types, or other mechanics that are permanently unlocked once the invention is adopted, while others are one-time effects and bonuses that triggered immediately when the invention is purchased. Some inventions are **keystone** inventions that are specially marked with a more elaborate border; these mark inventions that are considered important, whether because they have a strong effect or modifier or unlock particularly important mechanics, but are not mechanically different in any way from normal inventions.

Unlike advances, no country has any inventions adopted at the start. However, most countries that start with a higher level of advances do start with a corresponding number of [![Image 24: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovations to purchase inventions with at the beginning of the game.

- [x] 

## Contents

*   [1 Innovations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation#Innovations)
    *   [1.1 Researcher innovations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation#Researcher_innovations)

*   [2 Lists of inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation#Lists_of_inventions)
*   [3 References](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation#References)

## Innovations[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Innovations") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Innovations")]

Each invention requires spending one [![Image 25: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")**innovation** to adopt. Innovations are gained primarily from technological [advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Advance "Advance"), which grant one innovation for each level, and can also be gained by adopting certain [![Image 26: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, as a reward for certain specific [![Image 27: Menu mission.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Menu_mission.png/28px-Menu_mission.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") missions, as a rare outcome of successfully stealing technology, or occasionally from [![Image 28: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") researchers that have certain traits. All innovations are equal, and there is no requirement to use [![Image 29: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovations gained from a particular field of advances on an invention inside that field. It is entirely possible to invest all of a country's innovations on a single field of inventions, if the country is willing to neglect the others. Note that there are many more inventions than it is realistically possible to adopt over the course of the game, and even technologically advanced countries will be forced to pick and choose which areas they want to prioritize and which inventions they will leave for later - or not at all.

The following [![Image 30: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions grant [![Image 31: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")innovations:

*   **+4** from [![Image 32: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") The Bureaucracy of War ([Indian traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Indian_traditions "Indian traditions"); under _Indian kingdom traditions_)
*   **+4** from [![Image 33: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Cradle of Civilization ([Levantine traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levantine_traditions "Levantine traditions"); under _Levantine kingdom traditions_)
*   **+4** from [![Image 34: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Cavalry Skirmish ([Persian traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Persian_traditions "Persian traditions"); under _Persian rural traditions_)
*   **+3** from [![Image 35: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") The Fabri ([Italic traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions"); under _Roman traditions_)
*   **+3** from [![Image 36: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Roman Roads ([Italic traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions"); under _Roman traditions_)

### Researcher innovations[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Researcher innovations") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Invention&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Researcher innovations")]

Every researcher that has at least one of the [![Image 37: Obsessive](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2d/Obsessive.png/28px-Obsessive.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Obsessive") obsessive, [![Image 38: Intelligent](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Intelligent.png/28px-Intelligent.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Intelligent") intelligent, [![Image 39: Polymath](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Polymath.png/28px-Polymath.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Polymath") polymath, or [![Image 40: Scholar](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c5/Scholar.png/28px-Scholar.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Scholar") scholar traits has a chance generating an extra innovation each month (as well as gaining [![Image 41: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")**5** statesmanship). The base chance for each researcher is around **0.2%**, multiplied by around **1.25** for each trait that the character has. Since a researcher needs at least one of the traits to be eligible for generating extra innovations at all, the effective base chance is around **0.25%** for a researcher that has one trait, going up to a maximum chance of around **0.49%** per month for a researcher that has all 4 traits. This chance is rolled for all researchers that are eligible, so having multiple researchers that have at least one of these traits significantly increases the chances of getting an extra innovation.

Once a researcher discovers an extra innovation, there is a cooldown of **2** years before the country can get another free innovation, and a cooldown of **5** years before that particular researcher can generate another free innovation (however, during the intervening 3 years other researchers can still generate free innovations).

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.

You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

============================================================
# Economy
============================================================
Title: Economy - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/y6n_daFe3Sg/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator: Rome - Economy and Trade Rebuilt

Load video

_**Imperator: Rome - Economy and Trade Rebuilt**_. An overview of how economy and trade work in Imperator: Rome! Although most of these changes happened in previous free updates, anyone who wants to dive back into Imperator will appreciate the changes.

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Economy_screen.png/500px-Economy_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Economy_screen.png)

The main economy interface

In Imperator, a country's **economy** revolves around the generation of [![Image 3: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold **income** from pops and trade that are used for various **expenses** and investments. Used for everything from recruiting [armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") and maintaining [![Image 4: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") forts to constructing [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") and adopting [![Image 5: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, gold is one of the most important resources in the game. Thus, managing and optimizing a country's economy is a critical part of building a strong, prosperous nation. An overview of the country's economy and balance, as well as the adjustment of economic policies, can be viewed in the [![Image 6: Menu economy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Menu_economy.png/28px-Menu_economy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_economy.png)**Economy** screen.

## Treasury[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Treasury") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Treasury")]

A country's [![Image 7: Treasury.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7e/Treasury.png/28px-Treasury.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy")**treasury** stores all of its currently accumulated wealth and is the main source of accessible gold; most events and actions deposits gold into or withdraws gold from the treasury. At the end of each month, the current balance between the country's income and expenses are automatically added or subtracted from the treasury. Most actions that cost gold, such as constructing [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") and completing [mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") tasks, have their costs deducted directly from the treasury and require the treasury to have enough gold to pay the cost outright. Thus, for more expensive items, it will often require saving up for months (if not years) to make the purchase or investment.

If the treasury becomes negative, the country will be considered to be in [![Image 8: Bankruptcy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Bankruptcy.png/28px-Bankruptcy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deficit "Deficit") deficit, destabilizing the country and making any employed [![Image 9: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries disloyal. If the treasure falls below **-50**, negative events can begin to trigger, typically giving additional maluses. It may therefore be useful to keep a reserve of gold if the country is already unstable, as there are many random [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") that require spending gold and can somewhat unexpectedly push a country into deficit.

While the ruler is paid a portion of the country's income each month, a country's treasury should be distinguished from the [![Image 10: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler's personal finances, even in [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"); these two stores of wealth have distinct uses and are not generally interchangeable. If the ruler is [![Image 11: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corrupt, this can become a particularly serious problem as the ruler siphons a large proportion of the country's income into their personal coffers where the state cannot use it - it is entirely possible to have a wealthy ruler but a poor state, and vice versa.

A country's treasury can hold a maximum of 100,000 gold; any excess will be lost.

## Income[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Income") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Income")]

The are three main sources of state income in _Imperator_:

Generally speaking, the effects of [![Image 12: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") governor wages and the stacking effects of [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") means that the [![Image 13: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region"), and especially the capital [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") and [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory"), will produce a disproportionate portion of the country's income.

### Taxes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Taxes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Taxes")]

Tax income is paid by all pops of certain classes in the country, each producing a different amount of base monthly tax income:

As with all pop output, the base tax production of each pop is then multiplied by its [![Image 14: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness as a fraction out of **100%**, which generally means that the tax income of pops is less than the base value. The exception is [![Image 15: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves, who are fixed at **100%** output efficiency regardless of happiness. After this, [![Image 16: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**National tax** and [![Image 17: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**Local tax** modifiers are applied at the [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory"), [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region"), and [country](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country "Country") level. As tax income is produced almost exclusively by pops, it is also affected by the various [![Image 18: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") pop output modifiers, with [![Image 19: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") slave output modifiers being particularly important given that tax income usually comes predominantly from [![Image 20: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves; [![Image 21: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output") tribesman output can also have a significant effect for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") countries or states that otherwise have a large number of [![Image 22: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen.

Some of the more important sources of [![Image 23: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") tax modifiers are listed below:

*   [Territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory") modifiers: 
*   [Province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") modifiers: 
*   [Region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region") modifiers: 
*   [Country](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country "Country") modifiers: 

#### Base tax[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Base tax") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Base tax")]

Every nation, regardless of its [![Image 24: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population or [![Image 25: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") number of territories, has a **base tax** of [![Image 26: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**+2** gold per month, which can then be modified by [![Image 27: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifiers. This case tax is crucial for small nations' economies in the early game, but becomes largely irrelevant as the country expands and grows.

### Commerce[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Commerce") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Commerce")]

_Main article: [Trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade")_

[![Image 28: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**Commerce income** is produced by the import and export of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") from the provinces where they are produced and consumed, and unlike tax happens at the [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") level. Each [![Image 29: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")[trade route](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") generates a certain amount of commerce income for both the importing and exporting province, with certain more valuable and exotic trade goods being worth more and foreign trade routes generating significantly more income for both sides (but especially the exporter). Each unused import route also produces a baseline amount of commerce income, though usually much less than what using the slot for a trade route would produce.
