Commerce income is heavily tied to [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") production and unlike tax income is highly dependent on the ability and willingness of other countries to trade, and typically increases in importance throughout the game as the efficiency of trade good production increases and import ability of cities and provinces is expanded.

Unlike [![Image 30: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") tax income, commerce income is not generated directly by pops, but indirectly through the [![Image 31: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade route production of [![Image 32: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 33: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") as well as the [![Image 34: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") trade good production of [![Image 35: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves, and so is mostly accumulated from large cities with many import routes as well as areas with high [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") production and export potential.

### Tributes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Tributes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Tributes")]

_Main article: [Subject nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject\_nations "Subject nations")_

All [![Image 36: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject states pay a certain amount of [![Image 37: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income")**tribute income** to their overlord, which for most subject types comes as a certain percentage of their [![Image 38: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income. The tribute paid can be changed by various modifiers, and in particular can be adjusted to be lighter or harsher using the associated economic policy, with a corresponding bonus or malus to [![Image 39: Tributary opinion of us.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/35/Tributary_opinion_of_us.png/28px-Tributary_opinion_of_us.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_opinion "Subject opinion") subject opinion (and indirectly [![Image 40: Subject loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Subject_loyalty.png/28px-Subject_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty") subject loyalty). Tributaries are particularly useful for accumulating tribute income as they are available to all countries and do not take up a [![Image 41: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_relations "Diplomatic relations") diplomatic relations slot; because of the effects of base tax, it is generally better to have many smaller tributaries than a few large ones.

## Expenses[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Expenses") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Expenses")]

A state has many monthly expenses that must be paid, most relating to military or administrative manners. These expenses are automatically deducted from the income each month and only the balance (which may be negative) appears in the treasury. Monthly expenses must always be paid, even if the treasury is already in debt.

### Army Maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Army Maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Army Maintenance")]

_See also: [Legion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion")_

In addition to the cost of recruitment, every raised [![Image 42: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion cohort also has a monthly [![Image 43: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**legion maintenance** cost that must be paid, with the base maintenance equal to **1/48** of the recruitment cost. Cohorts that are [![Image 44: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to a specific commander cost only **66%** of their normal maintenance; legions that are led by a disloyal commander do not cost any maintenance at all.

[![Image 45: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") Mercenary upkeep also appears here, which is generally significantly more expensive than normal armies but do not cost [![Image 46: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower and are not restricted by population.

[![Image 47: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levies and [![Image 48: Start migration cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration") migratory armies do not cost any maintenance, though they have other costs associated with them.

### Navy Maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Navy Maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Navy Maintenance")]

_See also: [Navy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy")_

Like permanent armies, owned ships also have a monthly [![Image 49: Navy maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Navy_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Navy_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_maintenance "Navy maintenance")**navy maintenance** cost, with the base maintenance cost equal to **1/60** of the recruitment cost. As with armies, disloyal fleets do not cost any maintenance, while mercenary fleets have a premium that make their upkeep more expensive.

### Paying Tributes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Paying Tributes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Paying Tributes")]

_Main article: [Subject nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject\_nations "Subject nations")_

All [![Image 50: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject nations have a [![Image 51: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income")**tribute** that must be paid to their overlord, which for most subject types is a certain proportion of their income depending on the exact subject type and can be modified by the [![Image 52: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income")**tribute income** modifier of the overlord. The tribute is always paid as long as the country is a subject, even at low loyalty. Note that the tribute paid is a proportion of the total income, not the overall balance.

### Fort Maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Fort Maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Fort Maintenance")]

_See also: [Fort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort")_

Every [![Image 53: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort level costs a base of [![Image 54: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**0.40** gold in [![Image 55: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**fort maintenance** each month, except for the first fort in the capital. An important consideration for fort maintenance is a province's [![Image 56: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort infrastructure capacity - every new fort uses up 3 points of infrastructure, while subsequent fort levels only use 1, and every point over the limit increases fort maintenance in that province by **25%**. For instance, with the default infrastructure of 5 points, this means two level 1 forts in one province cost [![Image 57: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") 0.5 gold each (1 in total), while a level 2 fort costs only [![Image 58: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") 0.8 gold.

It is recommended to destroy unneeded forts in the interior of the country, particularly if they are not protecting any important vulnerable cities or suppressing [![Image 59: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest in a discontent province.

### Wages[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Wages") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Wages")]

_See also: [Position#Mechanics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Position")_

Every character holding a [![Image 60: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") government position in the country is paid a monthly [![Image 61: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**wage**, usually a percentage of the country's total income which is determined by the exact position the character holds. Character wages therefore scale with a country's size and always form a significant part of a country's expenses, with managing the number of [legates](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legate "Legate") and [admirals](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Admiral "Admiral") being particularly important for larger empires who may have many legions and navies that they want to be led. Wages can be modified country-wide with the [![Image 62: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**Monthly Wages for Characters** modifier and individually per character by the [![Image 63: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**Monthly Wage** modifier. Character [![Image 64: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption can significantly increase the wages that a character is paid out of the government's coffers - up to a [![Image 65: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+250%** wage modifier at [![Image 66: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") 100 corruption - making it important to ensure that corruption does not become too endemic in the government (e.g. allowing "free hands"), lest the entirety of the country's income be drained by corrupt officials, leaving nothing for infrastructure or the military. On the other hand, there are many other factors that decrease character wages, such as adopting certain [![Image 67: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, activating certain [![Image 68: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omens, making a family [![Image 69: Grateful family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/65/Grateful_family.png/28px-Grateful_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Grateful family")**grateful**, and increasing the position holder's [![Image 70: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity.

Wages can be increased or decreased across the entire country through the wage economic policy, in addition to the default setting. Low wages can significantly increase the amount of [![Image 71: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold available for other uses but will rapidly increase [![Image 72: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption across all of the country's office holders, which makes it infeasible for any significant length of time unless the country has very strong monthly corruption reduction modifiers as the costs of increased corruption will quickly surpass any savings from the policy. High wages, while potentially quite expensive, can be useful for wealthy countries that seek to reduce corruption without using a [national idea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_idea "National idea") or [law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") slot or already have endemic corruption, so that the expenses from the policy will quickly be made up for by the decline in corruption. Notably, it is also the easiest way to decrease [![Image 73: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")[ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") corruption (which reduces [![Image 74: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence gain), particularly in [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") which do not have access to a corruption reduction law and where a corrupt ruler may stay in power for many decades.

## Economic Policies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Economic Policies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Economic Policies")]

**Economic Policies** can be accessed in the [![Image 75: Menu economy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Menu_economy.png/28px-Menu_economy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_economy.png) economy screen and allow a country to control its income and spending on a country-wide level. Each policy is associated with a specific income or expenses category.

### Taxes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Taxes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Taxes")]

Modifies income from [![Image 76: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") taxes. Taxes can be lowered to increase [![Image 77: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") overall happiness, or raised to increase [![Image 78: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") slave output in return for [![Image 79: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") decreased research.

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lax Taxation | [![Image 80: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**+10%** Population Happiness | [![Image 81: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**-50%** National Tax |
| Default Taxation | _No Effect_ | _No Effect_ |
| Harsh Taxation | [![Image 82: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output")**+12%** National Slave Output | [![Image 83: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**-20%** Research Points |

### Commerce[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Commerce") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Commerce")]

Modifies trade income from [![Image 84: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce. The default policy gives bonus [![Image 85: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes to the capital, while the other two policies may be useful if the country makes a lot of gold from either [![Image 86: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value") importing or [![Image 87: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value") exporting.

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Free Trade | [![Image 88: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value")**+20%** Export Value | [![Image 89: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value")**-15%** Import Value |
| Trading Permits | [![Image 90: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+2** Capital Trade Routes | _None_ |
| Transaction Taxation | [![Image 91: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value")**+20%** Import Value | [![Image 92: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value")**-15%** Export Value |

### Tributes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Tributes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Tributes")]

Modifies the tribute received from [![Image 93: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject states. More [![Image 94: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income") tribute can be demanded in exchange for lower [![Image 95: Tributary opinion of us.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/35/Tributary_opinion_of_us.png/28px-Tributary_opinion_of_us.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_opinion "Subject opinion") subject opinion if the overlord is strong enough to demand it, and vice versa if a country's subject states need to be appeased. Crucially, selecting "Lax Tribute" helps greatly in reaching the **+190** opinion required to integrate eligible subjects.

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lax Tribute | [![Image 96: Tributary opinion of us.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/35/Tributary_opinion_of_us.png/28px-Tributary_opinion_of_us.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_opinion "Subject opinion")**+50** Subject Opinion | [![Image 97: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income")**-33%** Tribute Income from Subjects |
| Default | _No Effect_ | _No Effect_ |
| Extortive Tributes | [![Image 98: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income")**+25%** Tribute Income from Subjects | [![Image 99: Tributary opinion of us.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/35/Tributary_opinion_of_us.png/28px-Tributary_opinion_of_us.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_opinion "Subject opinion")**-50** Subject Opinion |

### Army Maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Army Maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Army Maintenance")]

Modifies [![Image 100: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness and [![Image 101: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") army maintenance cost in return for [![Image 102: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") army morale. Gold can be saved and pops made happier while at peace by decreasing pay when armies are not needed, while wealthier countries can improve the quality of their armies in wartime by increasing pay - even if they do not have any [![Image 103: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions to pay army maintenance on. Note that the Decreased Pay option disables [![Image 104: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army") drilling for [![Image 105: Starting experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Starting_experience.png/28px-Starting_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Starting experience") army experience and [![Image 106: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience.

The default [![Image 107: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") {**+7.5%** levy size modifier is also given through this policy, though note that all three options have the same modifier (and so the choice of economic policy here makes no difference to the levy size).

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Decreased Pay | [![Image 108: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+5%** Integrated Culture Happiness [![Image 109: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**-25%** Legion Maintenance Cost [![Image 110: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy")**+7.5%** Levy Size Modifier | [![Image 111: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**-25%** Morale of Armies |
| Normal Pay | [![Image 112: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy")**+7.5%** Levy Size Modifier | _No Effect_ |
| Increased Pay | [![Image 113: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**+10%** Morale of Armies [![Image 114: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy")**+7.5%** Levy Size Modifier | [![Image 115: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-5%** Integrated Culture Happiness [![Image 116: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**+25%** Legion Maintenance Cost |

### Fleet Maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Fleet Maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Fleet Maintenance")]

Like army maintenance, modifies [![Image 117: Navy maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Navy_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Navy_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_maintenance "Navy maintenance") navy maintenance cost in return for [![Image 118: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") navy morale, and can be used to save gold while at peace or to improve its fighting ability when at war, if higher pay can be afforded.

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Decreased Pay | [![Image 119: Navy maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Navy_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Navy_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_maintenance "Navy maintenance")**-50%** Naval Maintenance Cost | [![Image 120: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**-80%** Morale of Navies |
| Normal Pay | _No Effect_ | _No Effect_ |
| Increased Pay | [![Image 121: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**+5%** Morale of Navies | [![Image 122: Navy maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Navy_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Navy_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_maintenance "Navy maintenance")**+50%** Naval Maintenance Cost |

### Fort Maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Fort Maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Fort Maintenance")]

Modifies [![Image 123: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance") fort maintenance in return for [![Image 124: Garrison](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/52/Garrison.png/28px-Garrison.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Garrison") garrison size and [![Image 125: Fort defense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fd/Fort_defense.png/28px-Fort_defense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_defense "Fort defense") fort defense. Gold can be saved while at peace by decreasing fort maintenance when not needed - though this can leave border forts vulnerable to a sudden attack of declaration of war - while wealthier countries can improve the quality of their existing forts by improving their garrisons and fortifications.

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ignored Garrisons | [![Image 126: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**-15%** Fort Maintenance | [![Image 127: Garrison](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/52/Garrison.png/28px-Garrison.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Garrison")**-50%** Garrison Size [![Image 128: Fort defense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fd/Fort_defense.png/28px-Fort_defense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_defense "Fort defense")**-50%** Fort Defense |
| Paid Garrisons | _No Effect_ | _No Effect_ |
| Extortive Tributes | [![Image 129: Garrison](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/52/Garrison.png/28px-Garrison.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Garrison")**+10%** Garrison Size [![Image 130: Fort defense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fd/Fort_defense.png/28px-Fort_defense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_defense "Fort defense")**+10%** Fort Defense | [![Image 131: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**+25%** Fort Maintenance |

### Wages[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Wages") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Wages")]

Modifies [![Image 132: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") character wages in return for [![Image 133: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption. Reducing wages can be used if gold is urgently needed, but is not viable in the long term as the increased wages from [![Image 134: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption will eventually far outstrip any savings from the wage reductions of the policies. Increasing wages is the most reliable way to reduce countrywide corruption and can be quite useful if the country can afford it.

| Policy | Bonus | Malus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Reduced Wages | [![Image 135: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**-25%** Monthly Wages for Characters [![Image 136: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**-25%** Governor Wage | [![Image 137: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**+0.25** Monthly Corruption |
| Normal Wages | _No Effect_ | _No Effect_ |
| Increased Wages | [![Image 138: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**-0.10** Monthly Corruption | [![Image 139: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+50%** Monthly Wages for all Characters [![Image 140: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+50%** Governor Wage |

## Deficit[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Deficit") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Economy&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Deficit")]

If a country's [![Image 141: Treasury.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7e/Treasury.png/28px-Treasury.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy") treasury is below 0 at the end of a month (after applying that month's income and expenses), whether from a negative balance or from losing [![Image 142: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold in an event, the country will be considered to be in [![Image 143: Bankruptcy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Bankruptcy.png/28px-Bankruptcy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deficit "Deficit")**deficit**. It will be unable to pay its officials and soldiers properly, destabilizing the regime and giving the following modifiers:

Any employed [![Image 144: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries will also become disloyal.

If the treasury falls below **-50**, a number of special events can trigger. Many of these events will give [![Image 145: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold and may well help the country turn around its financial situation, but many of them come at a significant price, such as reduced [![Image 146: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research, lower [![Image 147: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth") population growth, or an [![Image 148: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") army morale malus. Going into deficit while a [![Image 149: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenary army is employed is especially dangerous, as they may offer their services to the opposing side or even seize control over the local province as their 'payment'.

Out of the valid options, one of the following will occur each month as long as a country is in deficit:

*   [**Bad Planning**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **10** ([![Image 150: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**-10%** Morale of Armies and [![Image 151: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery")**-5%** Army Morale Recovery for [![Image 152: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**3 times** the monthly income)
*   [**Deficit!**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **10** ([![Image 153: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**-0.02%** National Population Growth for [![Image 154: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**3 times** the monthly income)
*   [**Desperate Measures**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **10** ([![Image 155: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**-5** stability for [![Image 156: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**3 times** the monthly income)
*   [**A Friend in Need...**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **10** ([![Image 157: Character loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Monthly_character_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_character_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Secondary "Character loyalty")**-3** Loyalty of Characters and [![Image 158: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**-5** stability for [![Image 159: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**6 times** the monthly income)
*   [**Tightening the Strings**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **10** ([![Image 160: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**-25%** Research Points for [![Image 161: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**6 times** the monthly income)
*   [**A Gift from the Gods**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **10** (Gain [![Image 162: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**6 times** the monthly income)
*   [**Defection!**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **100** ([![Image 163: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") Mercenaries attempt to defect)
*   [**Mercenary Secession!**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **25** ([![Image 164: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") Mercenaries seize control of the local [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"))
*   [**Rebels Without a Cause**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Deficit_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deficit events (page does not exist)"), with a weight of **50** ([![Image 165: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") Mercenaries leave)
*   **Nothing**, with a weight of **100**

Note that the deficit alert at the top bar is somewhat misleading - it only appears once the deficit is larger than the monthly balance, but also implies negative events will happen even if the balance is greater than [![Image 166: Treasury.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7e/Treasury.png/28px-Treasury.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy")**-50**. Hovering over the current stability and checking for the **-0.20** monthly stability penalty can be used to properly confirm if the country is in deficit.


============================================================
# Trade
============================================================
Title: Trade - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/y6n_daFe3Sg/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator: Rome - Economy and Trade Rebuilt

Load video

_**Imperator: Rome - Economy and Trade Rebuilt**_. An overview of how economy and trade work in Imperator: Rome! Although most of these changes happened in previous free updates, anyone who wants to dive back into Imperator will appreciate the changes.

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6c/Trade_overview_screen.png/500px-Trade_overview_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Trade_overview_screen.png)

The trade overview interface

**Trade** involves the movement of surplus [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") from one [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") to another, producing [![Image 3: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") commerce income for both the importer and the exporter. It is one of the two main ways that a country produces income and allows a country to profit from surplus production, potentially allowing countries with valuable and exotic [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") to become much wealthier than their population base would suggest. An overview of all trade routes in the country as well as various automatic controls can be viewed in the [![Image 4: Menu trade.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Menu_trade.png/28px-Menu_trade.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_trade.png)**Trade Overview** screen.

## Trade Routes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Trade Routes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Trade Routes")]

_See also: [Trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade\_goods "Trade goods")_

A [![Image 5: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**trade route** is the movement of a [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") from one [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), the **exporter** to another, the **importer**. One of the main effects of a trade route is to effectively move [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") from one province to another, meaning that the importing province will get the bonuses from the imported trade good as though it was produced there, and the exporting province will not get the bonus from the exported trade good even though it was produced there. This is particularly useful for accumulating surpluses of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") in the [![Image 6: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), as well as obtaining food trade goods if the population of a province outstrips its food supply.

As a passive modifier, every active import and export route also gives [![Image 7: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion")**+2.50%** pop promotion speed to every [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory") in the province.

### Trade income[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Trade income") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Trade income")]

Trade routes are possibly most important for the [![Image 8: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") commerce income that they give to both the importing and exporting provinces. The base [![Image 9: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") commerce value of a trade route is determined by the **base trade value** of the [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods"), varying between [![Image 10: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**0.20** gold and [![Image 11: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**0.50** gold with valuable and exotic goods like [![Image 12: Gemstones](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/70/Goods_gems.png/28px-Goods_gems.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Gemstones "Gemstones") gemstones or [![Image 13: Incense](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/18/Goods_incense.png/28px-Goods_incense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Incense "Incense") incense worth much more than common trade goods such as [![Image 14: Grain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/05/Goods_grain.png/28px-Goods_grain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Grain "Grain") grain and [![Image 15: Wood](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Goods_wood.png/28px-Goods_wood.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Wood "Wood") wood. The base trade value is then multiplied by a certain factor based on the the type of the trade route, and whether the province is the importer or the exporter:

*   **×1** for the exporter in a foreign trade route
*   **×0.35** for the importer in a foreign trade route
*   **×0.20** for both the importer and the exporter in a domestic trade route

The commerce income for the importing province is then modified by the province and country [![Image 16: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value")**import value** modifiers, and similarly by the province and country [![Image 17: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value")**export value** modifiers for the exporting province. As it is commerce income that is generated, trade route income is also modified by the local and national [![Image 18: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") commerce income modifiers.

Therefore, foreign trade routes are always more valuable than domestic trade routes and should always be preferred if possible, compensating for how they are more difficult to create (and keep). Exporters will also in general earn more gold from trade than importers, in exchange for losing the effect of the trade good. Generally speaking exporting is almost always better than keeping the trade good for its bonus, unless a capital surplus would be lost or the province needs the trade good for its [![Image 19: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food supply.

### Creating trade routes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Creating trade routes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Creating trade routes")]

In order to create a trade route, the potential exporter must have a [surplus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") of that good (i.e. produces at least 2 of that [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods")), the potential importer must have at least 1 unused [![Image 20: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") import route, and there must be trade access between the two provinces. Provinces belonging to the same country (i.e. domestic trade routes) always have trade access, but creating a trade route between provinces belonging to different countries (foreign trade routes) requires that the exporter be within [![Image 21: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range of the importer (note that the inverse does not necessarily have to apply) and that the two countries are not at war. For foreign trade routes, the exporting country must also approve the trade, which they will generally do as long as [![Image 22: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion") relations are not poor and doing the trade will not result in the loss of a capital surplus bonus.

Once a trade route is established, it will remain in place as long as the importing province has enough [![Image 23: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes, the exporting province has enough of the [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") to fulfill all trades, both provinces remain under the same owner as when the trade route was established, and the two countries (if relevant) are not [![Image 24: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") at war. If any of the conditions are not met, trade routes will be automatically cancelled until they are fulfilled again (particularly, this means that all trade routes will be cancelled if at war, which means that going to war with a major trading partner can result in considerable economic disruption).

### Import routes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Import routes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Import routes")]

Every province has a maximum number of **import routes** that they are allowed to have. Once a province reaches its maximum, it will no longer be able to create more import routes, and if the number of import routes is higher than the maximum then the most recent trade routes created will be cancelled until the number of import routes is at the cap again. Note that there is no limit to how many exports a province can have, other than the amount of [surplus trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") it produces.

The base number of import routes in for each territory is determined by the sum of the [![Image 25: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**Local base trade routes** modifiers; this is then summed up across all the territories in the province and then rounded down to get the baseline maximum number of import routes for the province. [![Image 26: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") Local import routes are produced only by [![Image 27: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble and [![Image 28: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen pops, who produce [![Image 29: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+0.15** Local import routes and [![Image 30: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+0.03** Local import routes per pop, respectively. There are also multiplicative modifiers mostly determined by infrastructure - bonuses of [![Image 31: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+5%** local base trade routes for every [![Image 32: Ability road building.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road "Road") road connecting the territory to another territory and [![Image 33: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+2.5%** local base trade routes for every [![Image 34: Market](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/af/Building_market.png/28px-Building_market.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Market "Market") marketplace in the territory, available only to [![Image 35: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities or [![Image 36: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, while territories whose [![Image 37: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") dominant culture is not an integrated culture get a [![Image 38: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**-25%** malus to their number of trade routes.

This baseline maximum can then be modified at a province level by the [![Image 39: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**Local import routes** province modifier, for all provinces in the country by the [![Image 40: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**Province import routes** country modifier, and for the [![Image 41: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital province specifically by the [![Image 42: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**Capital import routes** modifier. Apart from as a bonus from various [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), [decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions"), and [missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions"), the [![Image 43: Oratory province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b1/Oratory_investment.png/28px-Oratory_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Oratory province investment") Entice Business Investments [province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment") also gives [![Image 44: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Local import routes, making it in general the fastest and easiest way to increase the number of trade routes a province has as long as the country has enough [![Image 45: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold and [![Image 46: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence; the [![Image 47: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis territory rank also gives **+1** Local import routes, as does each tier of the [![Image 48: Gw effect tax and commerce.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/35/Gw_effect_tax_and_commerce.png/28px-Gw_effect_tax_and_commerce.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Gw_effect_tax_and_commerce.png) Tax and Commerce effect for [![Image 49: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") great wonders. The [![Image 50: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") Province import routes modifier is given only by the [![Image 51: Idea tariff exceptions.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/26/Idea_tariff_exceptions.png/28px-Idea_tariff_exceptions.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Idea_tariff_exceptions.png) Institute Tariff Exceptions [national idea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_idea "National idea"), which gives [![Image 52: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**+1** Province import routes, and the [![Image 53: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") Merchant Coast tradition of the [Levantine Maritime Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levantine_traditions "Levantine traditions") tree. [![Image 54: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") Capital import routes, though has many possible static sources, listed below:
