| [Economic policies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy") | [![Image 76: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")_Trading Permits_ (Commerce policy) | **+2** |
| [Deities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Pantheons "Religion") | [![Image 77: State religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/State_religion.png/28px-State_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:State_religion.png)_Al-Quam_ ([Arabic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Arabic "Arabic") deity) | * **+2** without owned [![Image 78: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site")[holy site](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") * **+2.5** with owned [![Image 79: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site")[holy site](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") |
| [![Image 80: State religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/State_religion.png/28px-State_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:State_religion.png)_Caleb_ ([Jewish](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Jewish "Jewish") deity) |
| [National ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas") | [![Image 81: Idea trading posts.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a2/Idea_trading_posts.png/28px-Idea_trading_posts.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Idea_trading_posts.png)_Patronized Trading Posts_ (Civic Tech 4) | **+3** |
| [Heritages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritages "Heritages") | [![Image 82: Flag of Praesos](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Praesos.png/20px-Praesos.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Praesos "Praesos")_Minoan Heritage_ ([Praesos](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Praesos "Praesos")) | **+1** |
| [![Image 83: Flag of Epikrateia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f7/Epikrateia.png/20px-Epikrateia.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Epikrateia "Epikrateia")_Epikrateia Heritage_ ([Epikrateia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Epikrateia&action=edit&redlink=1 "Epikrateia (page does not exist)")) | **+1** |
| _Bosporan Heritage_ (cultural) | **+1** |

Unused import routes in a province give [![Image 84: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**+0.07** commerce income, which is not modified by [![Image 85: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value") import value but is still affected by [![Image 86: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce income modifiers. This means that increasing the amount of trade routes a province has is always beneficial even if no imports can actually be made, although importing actual [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") will almost always produce more gold even before considering the additional benefits of having more trade goods in a province.

The number of unused import routes for every province in the country can be viewed in the **Trade Route** mapmode.

### Automating trade[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Automating trade") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Automating trade")]

There are a number of tools that a player can use to automatically create and accept trade deals, rather than having to manually approve every export request and set up import routes every time a province gains (or regains) an empty import route slot.

*   **Accept All Trades** - A toggle in the [![Image 87: Menu trade.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Menu_trade.png/28px-Menu_trade.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_trade.png) trade menu. When enabled, all export requests are automatically accepted, except for those that would involve losing a capital surplus bonus. 
*   **Automatic Trade** - A toggle in the province interface, below the list of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") currently in the province. When enabled, the province will automatically import goods from other countries of its own accord, generally prioritizing food, though it can sometimes take up to a few months for unused import route slots to be filled. Disabled by default.

## Commerce income[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Commerce income") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Commerce income")]

[![Image 88: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") Trade routes create **commerce income** on both sides of the trade, with the bulk of the income generally going to the exporter. Commerce income is calculated on a per-[province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") basis as the sum of all trade route income (including import routes, export routes, and income from unused trade routes), modified by the [![Image 89: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**Province commerce** province modifier and the [![Image 90: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce")**National commerce income** country modifier, the sum of which across all provinces a country determines the country's total **commerce income**. The value of each individual trade route is determined by the trade value of the [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") involved, whether the trade route is foreign or domestic and if the province is the importer or the exporter, and the effects of either [![Image 91: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value") import value and [![Image 92: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value") export value modifiers. Note that commerce income modifiers stack additively with each other, but multiplicatively with [![Image 93: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value") import value and [![Image 94: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value") export value modifiers as they modify the income from trade routes directly.

Important [![Image 95: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") Commerce Income modifiers include:


============================================================
# Trade_goods
============================================================
Title: Trade goods - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

**Trade goods** come in a wide variety of types, with 34 different trade goods divided into 6 categories, each with different trade values and modifiers. They allow a nation to tailor its provinces, and even more so the country, to its needs and desires by controlling what a province produces and where it is traded to and consumed, and are particularly important as the basis of [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") and will often form the bulk of the income of an exporting nation.

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

### Production[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Production") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Production")]

Every [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory") produces one type of trade good, and all territories in a [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") contribute their trade goods into a common pool. A [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") produces 1 trade good by default (with 2 for cities and 3 for a metropolis), but can produce more with enough [![Image 1: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave labor. For each additional **15**[![Image 2: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves in a settlement and **20**[![Image 3: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves in a city or metropolis (rounded down), the territory will produce one more additional trade good.

The threshold for a surplus is reduced for each territory by the [![Image 4: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus")**Slaves needed for Local Surplus** modifier, and across all territories in the country by the [![Image 5: Slavesurplus2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Slavesurplus2.png/28px-Slavesurplus2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Slavesurplus2.png)**Slaves needed for Surplus** modifier. Sources of these modifiers include:

Some territories also have unique permanent modifiers giving surplus threshold reductions, such as the Rhodian Glass Workshops in Rhodes or the Cedars of the Gods in Phoenicia.

Additionally, the [![Image 6: Base resources](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f7/Base_resources.png/28px-Base_resources.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Production "Base resources")**Base Resource Production** modifier increases the base number of trade goods produced regardless of the number of slaves in the territory. The most important sources of base resource production are [![Image 7: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 8: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, which give [![Image 9: Base resources](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f7/Base_resources.png/28px-Base_resources.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Production "Base resources")**+1** and [![Image 10: Base resources](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f7/Base_resources.png/28px-Base_resources.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Production "Base resources")**+2** base resource production, respectively. This means that cities are still a significant source of trade good surpluses even without the high [![Image 11: Slave ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Pop_slaves_ratio.png/28px-Pop_slaves_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Slave ratio") slave ratios and [![Image 12: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") slaves needed for local surplus modifiers that settlements and their buildings give.

### Surpluses and trade[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Surpluses and trade") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Surpluses and trade")]

_Main article: [Trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade")_

A province will have a trade good **surplus** if it produces more than one of a certain trade good, either because it has more than one territory producing the same trade good and/or if its territories have enough [![Image 13: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves to produce multiple resources in the territory. A surplus typically gives no inherent bonus except for the stacking base trade good bonus; the main use of surplus trade goods is to [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") them away to other provinces for [![Image 14: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce income, as well as to move the location (and bonuses) of the resource to another province. Imported trade goods contribute to the trade good surplus just like locally produced trade goods, while resources that are exported no longer give their effects to their province of origin.

Each trade good has a certain **Base Trade Value** that determines the amount of income countries receive from trading the resource. Trading common resources like [![Image 15: Wood](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Goods_wood.png/28px-Goods_wood.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Wood "Wood") wood or [![Image 16: Furs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Goods_fur.png/28px-Goods_fur.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Furs "Furs") furs will give much less gold than valuable, exotic goods like [![Image 17: Dyes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Goods_dye.png/28px-Goods_dye.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Dyes "Dyes") dyes or [![Image 18: Gemstones](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/70/Goods_gems.png/28px-Goods_gems.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Gemstones "Gemstones") gemstones. For foreign trades (i.e. between provinces belonging to different countries), the exporting province will receive the **full** base trade value, modified by the country's [![Image 19: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value") export value, in [![Image 20: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce income, while the importing province will receive **0.35** times the base trade value in [![Image 21: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce income, modified by the country's [![Image 22: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value") import value. Domestic trades will instead give only **0.20** times the base trade value for both the importing and exporting provinces. Areas that can produce and export valuable trade goods are therefore generally richer and more lucrative to conquer, but making sure that there are available trade partners - for instance, buy having a large network of nearby tributaries - is also important.

Trade route requests can only be initiated by the potential importer. Countries hoping to export resources should instead focus on making sure as many surplus trade goods are available as possible and if necessary make sure there are enough potential trading partners in [![Image 23: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range, and then wait for trade requests to come in (which generally does not take too long).

### Bonuses and effects[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Bonuses and effects") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Bonuses and effects")]

Each individual trade good has a particular **bonus** that is applied to their [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), which stacks based on how many instances of the resource is present. Each category of trade goods has a different set of bonuses - military trade goods allow recruitment of the more specialized [unit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort "Cohort") types as well as giving a small output or resource bonus, food trade goods increase the [![Image 24: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food available in the province, and trade goods in each of the pop type categories increase the local [![Image 25: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of the corresponding class. These bonuses can grow to be quite significant for large and prosperous provinces that produce and import many different trade goods, and are particularly important for providing the [![Image 26: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food necessary for more urbanized provinces that have long since outstripped their limited local food supply, as well as increasing the happiness of the normally restive [![Image 27: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 28: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens who produce most of the [![Image 29: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points and [![Image 30: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes in the country.

Each trade good that has a surplus in the [![Image 31: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital province specifically also gives a special nationwide **capital bonus**. Unlike the normal province modifier, this bonus is not stackable and is only applied once per trade good, which means that it is generally better to have a large variety of surplus resources in the capital to accumulate as many bonuses as possible. As the capital province is likely to be the most populous province in any case with the most higher-class pops, it is almost always better to focus on accumulating trade good bonuses in the capital above every other province.

### Changing trade goods[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Changing trade goods") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Changing trade goods")]

In general, trade goods cannot be changed directly by the player. Trade goods can, however, be changed indirectly with the foundation of a city. Agricultural trade goods ([![Image 32: Grain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/05/Goods_grain.png/28px-Goods_grain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Grain "Grain") grain, [![Image 33: Fish](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Goods_fish.png/28px-Goods_fish.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Fish "Fish") fish, [![Image 34: Livestock](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Goods_cattle.png/28px-Goods_cattle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Livestock "Livestock") livestock, and [![Image 35: Vegetables](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Goods_vegetables.png/28px-Goods_vegetables.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Vegetables "Vegetables") vegetables) can only be produced in settlements; founding a city on a food-producing territory will replace the trade good once the city is finished, typically with a higher value Nobles or Citizens trade good. If the city status is later revoked, the territory will revert to producing its old agricultural trade good. Note that this applies only for cities founded during the game - revoking city status of a city that already exists at start will not change the trade good.

The trade good of a settlement can also be changed by a few special events and missions, most notably the prospecting task of the [generic infrastructure mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic_infrastructure_mission "Generic infrastructure mission").

## Goods list[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Goods list") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Goods list")]

### Strategic resources[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Strategic resources") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Strategic resources")]

| Trade good | Province modifier | Capital surplus | Base trade value | Description | [Collapse](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| [![Image 210: Furs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Goods_fur.png/20px-Goods_fur.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Furs "Furs") Furs |  | [![Image 211: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/20px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay")**-0.50%** Experience decay |

Resources of this category grant mainly military bonuses, advantages or features. The production of cavalry in a province is linked to the availability of horses within that province, while the production of ships in a province is linked to the availability of wood within that province. Any lack of a strategic resource leads to a lack of a certain unit or advantage that may force the player to adapt his military _strategy_.

There are also military resources affecting the quality of units and bonuses of military structures like forts. This type of resource does not change the variety of units the player's nation can build, but it does affect stats and advantages of produced units. Strategic resources should be preferred to military resources: With a hemp surplus in the capital, the ship damage increases about +10%, but missing wood leads to the incapability of building ships and of using the hemp-given advantage of additional damage.

### Production Benefits[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Production Benefits") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Production Benefits")]

#### Happiness[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Happiness") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Happiness")]

Use provincial trade routes to import goods to increase a pop type's happiness within the province. This works best in provinces with large numbers of the pop type.

### Food[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Food") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Trade_goods&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Food")]

Food resources in a province help feed the pops of the province. If there is a surplus of food (food within the province, not as a resource for national or international trade), the pop growth rate increases. A lack of food leads to a decrease in pops and their happiness. Any lack of food can be prevented, by importing a food surplus from one province to a starving one. Any 1 surplus of a food resource within a province can be transferred within the player's nation by using 1 trade route.

On the foundation of a new City, if the territory was producing [![Image 286: Grain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/05/Goods_grain.png/20px-Goods_grain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Grain "Grain") Grain, [![Image 287: Fish](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Goods_fish.png/20px-Goods_fish.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Fish "Fish") Fish, [![Image 288: Livestock](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Goods_cattle.png/20px-Goods_cattle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Livestock "Livestock") Livestock or [![Image 289: Vegetables](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Goods_vegetables.png/20px-Goods_vegetables.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Vegetables "Vegetables") Vegetables, a new Trade Good will replace it. After revoking this City's status, however, the old Trade Good will return. Extra concerns have to be placed on whether the province would end up with enough food or not.

Also, [![Image 290: Salt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Goods_salt.png/20px-Goods_salt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Salt "Salt") Salt, [![Image 291: Honey](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Goods_honey.png/20px-Goods_honey.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Honey "Honey") Honey and [![Image 292: Vegetables](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Goods_vegetables.png/20px-Goods_vegetables.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Vegetables "Vegetables") Vegetables only provides a modifier of **+3%**[![Image 293: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/20px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") Food. Therefore it would only give 3 extra food when there is already 100 [![Image 294: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/20px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") Food. The total production of provincial [![Image 295: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/20px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") Food can be viewed when hovering the Food storage gauge on the province. It tells how much [![Image 296: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/20px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") Food is consumed and how much of it goes to the storage. The sum of these 2 numbers should indicate the total Food production of the province and if it is above 100, then importing [![Image 297: Salt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Goods_salt.png/20px-Goods_salt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Salt "Salt") Salt, [![Image 298: Honey](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Goods_honey.png/20px-Goods_honey.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Honey "Honey") Honey or [![Image 299: Vegetables](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Goods_vegetables.png/20px-Goods_vegetables.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Vegetables "Vegetables") Vegetables will provide better benefits than [![Image 300: Fish](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Goods_fish.png/20px-Goods_fish.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Fish "Fish") Fish or [![Image 301: Livestock](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Goods_cattle.png/20px-Goods_cattle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Livestock "Livestock") Livestock.


============================================================
# Population
============================================================
Title: Population - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/tvoIEuO0_UQ/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator Tutorial - Pops & Cities - pt 3

Load video

Imperator: Rome tutorial #3 - pops and cities.

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Population_screen.png/600px-Population_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Population_screen.png)

The main territory population interface

The population of the world in _Imperator Rome_ is represented by individual **population units**, or **pops**, each of whom live in a particular [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory") and owe their allegiance to the country that owns their home. Pops drive the economy and military of a nation and are the most important source of [![Image 3: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Cost") gold (directly through [![Image 4: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") taxes and indirectly through [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade"), which produces [![Image 5: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce), [![Image 6: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, and [![Image 7: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points, but must also be kept [![Image 8: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happy and content lest they drive [![Image 9: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest in their province and [revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion"). In general, pops cannot be interacted with directly and will [![Image 10: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth") grow, [![Image 11: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilate, [![Image 12: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") convert, [![Image 13: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") promote, and [![Image 14: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") migrate of their own accord, but the use of [governor policies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") and the construction of [infrastructure](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Infrastructure "Infrastructure") can be used to adjust the environment they live in and manipulate and control a country's demographics on a larger, more macro scale.

## [[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Social classes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Social classes")]

A pop belongs to one of the five available **social classes**, or **pop types**. The social class of a pop is one of its most important properties and determines what a pop produces, its [political weight](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_weight "Political weight"), what happiness modifiers it is affected by, and more.

1.   [![Image 15: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") Nobles - generate [![Image 16: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points and [![Image 17: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes
2.   [![Image 18: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") Citizens – generate [![Image 19: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points, [![Image 20: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes and [![Image 21: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower
3.   [![Image 22: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") Freemen – generate [![Image 23: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower and [![Image 24: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income
4.   [![Image 25: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") Tribesmen – generate [![Image 26: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower and [![Image 27: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income
5.   [![Image 28: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") Slaves – generate [![Image 29: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income and [![Image 30: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") additional trade goods

The class structure of a territory's population will approach its **optimal ratio** over time through pop [![Image 31: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") promotion and [![Image 32: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") demotion, the value of which is specific to each territory and varies by the territory's [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") and [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory_rank "Territory rank"), the country's [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") form, local and national modifiers, and many other factors. This ratio and the rate of change can be affected by player choices, and promotion is additionally restricted by the pop's level of [civic rights](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_rights "Civic rights") based on its culture.

There are also a few other effects that can directly change a pop's social class:

### Nobles[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Nobles") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Nobles")]

![Image 33: Nobles icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/40px-Pop_noble.png)

Nobles represent the elites in Rome, and nobility in monarchies. Typically, they would be owners of a [Latifundium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Latifundium "wikipedia:Latifundium").

Each Noble Pop at [![Image 34: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") 100% Happiness produces per month:

Nobles also have the following other properties:

**Nobles** only have a relevant desired Ratio in cities, metropolises, and the capital (even if it does not have city status). Their happiness is heavily affected by [![Image 35: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilisation level and is in general difficult but important to manage, with both the lowest base happiness and the highest political weight of all pop classes. Both their [![Image 36: Noble ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9e/Pop_noble_ratio.png/28px-Pop_noble_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Noble ratio") ratio and [![Image 37: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness") happiness can be increased via the [![Image 38: Library](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Building_library.png/28px-Building_library.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Library "Library") library, while the [![Image 39: Academy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Building_academy.png/28px-Building_academy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Academy "Academy") academy increases their [![Image 40: Noble ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9e/Pop_noble_ratio.png/28px-Pop_noble_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Noble ratio") ratio by half as much along with their (significant) [![Image 41: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research output.

Nobles are generally the most powerful and productive pop type and it can be advantageous to maximize the number of nobles in the country, as long as their happiness can be kept under control - their high [![Image 42: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food consumption is significant, particularly in the [![Image 43: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital where they are concentrated in large numbers and will often outstrip the local food supply, and their low happiness and high political weight makes them dangerous if not properly appeased. [Trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") are an important source of happiness modifiers, and as they are applied-province wide it is usually best to focus most on noble ratio in cities in the capital province (which has the additional benefit of usually being primarily the primary culture).

Noble civic rights are rather exclusive, giving a combined [![Image 44: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-4%** Integrated Culture Happiness malus and [![Image 45: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-5%** Primary Culture Happiness for every non-primary culture, which means that granting these rights give a heavy penalty and generally only primary culture pops will have the civic right and be able to promote to nobles. However, conquered noble pops will in general remain in significant numbers and non-integrated culture noble pops can be a significant source of [![Image 46: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest when conquering the cities and especially capital provinces of other countries, and will additionally be slow to assimilate, convert, and demote, ensuring that they can be a persistent problem.

### Citizens[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Citizens") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Citizens")]

![Image 47: Citizens icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/40px-Pop_citizen.png)

Each Citizen Pop at [![Image 48: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") 100% Happiness produces per month:

Citizens also have the following other properties:

**Citizens** generally only have a significant desired ratio in cities, metropolises, and the capital (even if it does not have city status), though most larger settlements will usually have room for one or two citizen pops as well. Both their [![Image 49: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") ratio and [![Image 50: Citizen happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") happiness can be increased by the [![Image 51: Court of Law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Building_court.png/28px-Building_court.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Court_of_Law "Court of Law") court of law. Citizen civic rights are associated with [![Image 52: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated cultures, and only integrated culture pops will be able to promote to citizens, which can prove to be an obstacle to gaining more citizens in cities with a high non-integrated culture population.

In general, citizens can be considered a weaker form of nobles - less production per pop, but easier to feed, please, and promote to, with a higher desired ratio and a less expensive [civic right](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_rights "Civic rights"). In most cities they will still make up a significant form of the population, and can be useful to focus on in cities dominated by [![Image 53: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated cultures that have not been given the full noble civic right.

### Freemen[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Freemen") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Freemen")]

![Image 54: Freemen icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/40px-Pop_freemen.png)

Freemen represent the plebeians of Rome. Artisans and small holders.

Each Freeman Pop at [![Image 55: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") 100% Happiness produces per month:

Freemen also have the following other properties:

**Freemen** are found primarily in cities, but also have a significant base optimal ratio (20%) in the [![Image 56: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements of [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") and [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"). They are somewhat easier to maintain than citizens, but about equally affected by [![Image 57: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilisation level. While freemen produce some [![Image 58: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax their primary contribution is as main source of [![Image 59: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, so their usefulness is highly situational depending on how much additional manpower is needed (mostly medium-size countries that have enough pops to make a significant difference, while not being so large that additional manpower is not really needed). Both their [![Image 60: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") ratio and [![Image 61: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") happiness can be increased via the [![Image 62: Forum](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2a/Building_forum.png/28px-Building_forum.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Forum "Forum") forum in [![Image 63: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 64: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, and [![Image 65: Barracks](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/28/Building_barracks.png/28px-Building_barracks.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Barracks "Barracks") barracks in [![Image 66: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements, and as the default [civic right](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_rights "Civic rights") in non-integrated cultures they will tend to be disproportionate represented in those areas.

### Tribesmen[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Tribesmen") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Tribesmen")]

![Image 67: Tribesmen icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/40px-Pop_tribesmen.png)

These represent uncivilized folks, barbarians.

Each Tribesmen Pop at [![Image 68: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") 100% Happiness produces per month:

Tribesmen also have the following other properties:

**Tribesmen** have a relatively high base happiness, but unlike all other pop classes receive a penalty for [![Image 69: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Civilisation level. Their production is comparable to [![Image 70: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen, with a focus on [![Image 71: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower and some amount of [![Image 72: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income, though actually getting those outputs is generally easier with their higher base happiness. In [![Image 73: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements, tribesmen [![Image 74: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output") output and [![Image 75: Tribesman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Tribesman happiness") happiness can be increased by building [![Image 76: Tribal Settlement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Building_tribal_settlement.png/28px-Building_tribal_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Tribal_Settlement "Tribal Settlement") Tribal Settlements.

For [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations, they make up 50% of the Settlement Population, limiting the space available for [![Image 77: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves and thus trade goods and tax production. In any cities, they are less common, but still have a noticeable ratio. Tribesmen will therefore usually make up a large proportion of a tribal country's population, increasing their [![Image 78: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower relative to what would be expected from their population but usually with less income. Tribesmen also have a high base [![Image 79: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") migration speed which means that they will move around significantly more quickly than other pop types.

For non-tribal nations, while they are technically superior to [![Image 80: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen the significant happiness penalties from [![Image 81: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value means that tribesmen are usually much better off be promoted or demoted to one of the other classes. For these nations, they have a desired ratio of 0% at any location unless a [![Image 82: Tribal Settlement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Building_tribal_settlement.png/28px-Building_tribal_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Tribal_Settlement "Tribal Settlement") Tribal Settlement is built, though their slow promotion/demotion speed means that tribesman-heavy territories will usually take some time to become properly useful.

### Slaves[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Slaves") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Slaves")]

![Image 83: Slaves icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/40px-Pop_slaves.png)

Slaves are mostly prisoners of war (POWs).

Each Slave Pop produces per month:

*   [![Image 84: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**+0.015** Base Tax

Slaves also have the following other properties:

**Slaves** are the backbone of almost every economy during the time frame of the game and the primary source of income for many countries. There is no nation or territory that does not want, doesn't need or doesn't have slaves. They are mostly found in [![Image 85: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements, where they have a base optimal ratio of 80% ([monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy")/[republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")) or 50% ([tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal")) of the population, but are also found in significant proportions in cities. Their [![Image 86: Slave happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Pop_slaves_happiness.png/28px-Pop_slaves_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Slave happiness") happiness, [![Image 87: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") output and [![Image 88: Slave ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Pop_slaves_ratio.png/28px-Pop_slaves_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Slave ratio") desired ratio can all be increased by the [![Image 89: Mill](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Building_mill.png/28px-Building_mill.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Mill "Mill") mill in territories with city status, and [![Image 90: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") output by the [![Image 91: Slave Estate](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/Building_slave_estate.png/28px-Building_slave_estate.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Slave_Estate "Slave Estate") slave estate in [![Image 92: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements. While slaves can appear through demotion and population growth like all other pop types, slaves can also uniquely be acquired through [![Image 93: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") the enslavement of pops when occupying enemy territories, as well as through [![Image 94: Slave raid](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Ability_naval_raiding.png/28px-Ability_naval_raiding.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy#Actions "Slave raid") slave raiding for countries with the relevant [military traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions"). For many war-focused countries, enslavement can be the primary source of population growth in a nation's core territories, with slaves eventually assimilating/converting and promoting up to swell the ranks of the upper classes as well.

In addition to producing more [![Image 95: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income than any other pop type, slaves can also uniquely produce additional [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") in a territory, based on the [![Image 96: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") slaves needed for local surplus value of the territory. This value is a base of 15 in [![Image 97: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements and 20 in [![Image 98: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 99: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, which can be lowered by the [![Image 100: Mine](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a5/Building_mine.png/28px-Building_mine.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Mine "Mine") mine and [![Image 101: Farming Settlement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Building_farm_estate.png/28px-Building_farm_estate.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Farming_Settlement "Farming Settlement") farming settlement buildings in [![Image 102: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") (as well as a large variety of other modifiers), and gives 1 extra trade good for every multiple (rounded down) of the [![Image 103: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") slaves needed for local surplus in slaves there are in that territory.

Their happiness can be difficult to keep high, as those captured in war tend to be of the wrong [culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") and [religion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion"). Importantly, however, the output of slaves does not depend on their happiness, which means that the primary concern of slave happiness is controlling the [![Image 104: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest they produce, which given their low political weight is typically not too high.

## Output[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Output") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Output")]

Depending on their class, pops produce various different types of resources, including [![Image 105: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, [![Image 106: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, and [![Image 107: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points, and are the often main (or only) source of these resources in the game. In addition to the output modifiers for each specific resource, the [![Image 108: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Local output")**population output** modifier will also increase the production of all resources produced by pops, and is in general one of the most powerful economic modifiers in the game. Pop output modifiers combine additively with resource-specific modifiers, but are combined multiplicatively with the output modifier from [![Image 109: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness (for classes where happiness is relevant to production). This gives the following formula for the output of each individual resource produced by a pop:

![Image 110: {\displaystyle {\text{Pop Output for Resource}}={\text{Base Pop Output for Resource}}\cdot {\frac {100\%+{\text{Pop Output Modifier}}+{\text{Resource Output Modifier}}}{100\%}}\cdot {\frac {\text{Pop Happiness}}{100\%}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/be55fd634a550b0be6dc616edbdf162239908662)
Note that unlike with all other resources, output modifiers do **not** affect the number of [![Image 111: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes produced by a pop, which is always constant for each pop (depending on its class).

Some of the more important sources of [![Image 112: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Local output") population output modifiers include:

Pop output can also be affected on a class-specific basis with the [![Image 113: Noble output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Pop_noble_output.png/28px-Pop_noble_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Noble output")**noble output**, [![Image 114: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output")**citizen output**, [![Image 115: Freeman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Pop_freemen_output.png/28px-Pop_freemen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Freeman output")**freeman output**, [![Image 116: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output")**tribesman output**, and [![Image 117: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output")**slave output** modifiers. Like with happiness modifiers, each of these modifiers has three different variants that determine wits scope - **local**, which only affects pops of that class in a particular territory; **culture**, which affects all pops of that class with a specific [![Image 118: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") culture, or **national**, which affects all pops of a certain class in an entire country. These modifiers are in general much more common than the general pop output modifier and can come from a large variety of sources, such as [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings"), [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods"), [deities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deity "Deity") and [omens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Omen "Omen"), [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") and [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") forms, [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"), governor [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), and more.

## Happiness[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Happiness") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Happiness")]

[![Image 119](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c1/Pop_happiness_-_Hellenic_Roman_Citizen_.jpg/600px-Pop_happiness_-_Hellenic_Roman_Citizen_.jpg)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Pop_happiness_-_Hellenic_Roman_Citizen_.jpg)

You can find the happiness of each pop by going to the Province tab, and then hovering on the icon of one of the pops available

[![Image 120: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**Happiness** represents how content and satisfied a particular pop is with the current government, affecting both how much of its production actually gets collected by the state as well as how willing the pop is to support resistance - or even rebellion - against the current government. Each of the five [pop types](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Social_classes) has a certain **base happiness value**, varying from -30% for [![Image 121: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves to 16% for [![Image 122: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen. Then, there are a large number of local and national happiness modifiers that are applied, including but not limited to:

In general, there are 4 different sets of modifiers that will each affect a different set of pops:

*   General happiness modifiers ([![Image 123: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**(Local) Population Happiness**)
*   Pop class modifiers ([![Image 124: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness")**Noble Happiness**, [![Image 125: Citizen happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness")**Citizen Happiness**, [![Image 126: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness")**Freeman Happiness**, [![Image 127: Tribesman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Tribesman happiness")**Tribesman Happiness**, and [![Image 128: Slave happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Pop_slaves_happiness.png/28px-Pop_slaves_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Slave happiness")**Slave Happiness** modifiers) 
    *   Each of these modifiers has three variants: **Local Happiness**, applied to a single territory or province; **Culture Happiness**, applied to pops of a particular culture; and **National Happiness**, applied to all pops of the specific class across the whole country

*   Culture modifiers ([![Image 129: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**Culture Happiness**, [![Image 130: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**Integrated Culture Happiness**, [![Image 131: Unintegrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6c/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture "Unintegrated culture happiness")**Unintegrated Culture Happiness**, and [![Image 132: Unintegrated culture group happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture_group "Unintegrated culture group happiness")**Unintegrated Culture Group Happiness** modifiers)
*   Religion modifiers ([![Image 133: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness")**State Religion Happiness** and [![Image 134: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") state religion pantheon deity modifiers)

A pop's happiness is the sum of all happiness modifiers it is affected by over its base happiness value, and has a range of **0%** to **100%**, with 50% the median value at which they are neither content nor discontent. The average happiness of all pops of a particular class is displayed in the territory view.

Happiness has two main effects on a pop:

The unrest produced by each unhappy pop is scaled to the total population of the territory, the pop's political weight, and how much its happiness is below 50%, giving a formula of approximately:

![Image 135: {\displaystyle {\text{Unrest}}=10\cdot {\frac {1}{\text{Territory Population}}}\cdot {\text{Pop Political Weight}}\cdot {\frac {50-{\text{Pop Happiness}}}{50}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/82c41cae94ab81ff771b4335ab518bd80702539c)
The total unrest for each territory is simply the sum of the unrest produced over all its pops.

### Strategy[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Strategy") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Strategy")]

As with all modifiers, national modifiers are almost always better than local modifiers, even if the value is somewhat weaker. Even relatively small changes in happiness can have a noticeable impact on pop output or unrest generated if applied across a large enough number of pops.

Generally, the largest differences in overall population happiness are between integrated cultures and unintegrated, especially unintegrated culture group, pops, with different strategies relevant in each area. While there can be significant differences in pop class happiness, these are usually not too relevant as, for instance, [![Image 136: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves do not have an output difference from happiness and have a political weight too low to be particularly relevant, while for many countries [![Image 137: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen will only exist in appreciable quantities in recently conquered areas where low happiness is to be expected in the first place.

In core, primary/integrated culture and state religion areas, happiness will rarely be low enough to produce any significant unrest and the primary concern of happiness is instead to maximize pop output, as these are often the most populated and productive parts of a country. Since [![Image 138: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves do not change their output based on happiness, the primary focus in core areas is to maximize the happiness of the highly productive [![Image 139: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and then [![Image 140: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, typically by building their associated buildings in [![Image 141: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities, importing their specific trade goods (generally only available in significant quantities a little bit into the game), and increasing [![Image 142: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization level (though only as a general, long term goal). Focusing on [![Image 143: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen happiness may also be situationally useful, but by the time a country is wealthy enough to invest in [![Image 144: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen it usually no longer has a pressing need for more manpower, at least as opposed to [![Image 145: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research and [![Image 146: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") commerce income (from trade routes).
