Note that happiness is capped at 100%, which can often be exceeded by the accumulation of various bonuses by the midgame, and while it can be useful to have some buffer in case [![Image 147: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability drops or [![Image 148: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion increases at some point increasing happiness ceases to be useful and it can be better to invest building or trade route slots into other areas.

In contrast, recently conquered provinces of an unintegrated culture (especially of a different culture group) and different religion will be particularly difficult to please, and in general unless the country has a large number of [![Image 149: Unintegrated culture group happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture_group "Unintegrated culture group happiness") unintegrated culture group happiness bonuses they will be virtually impossible to satisfy completely. For these areas, trying to fully manage happiness is very costly and it is easiest to just appoint a loyal, uncorrupt governor and build enough [![Image 150: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") forts in the province to keep [![Image 151: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest and [![Image 152: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty under control in the short to medium term; if not even this is enough, the easiest way to quickly reduce the penalties from unhappiness is to try to appease any nobles and/or citizens in the province (who are normally the most unhappy as well as the largest generators of unrest), typically by building their happiness-improving buildings in the cities and/or importing the specific [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") that improve their happiness. If [![Image 153: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability is low, focusing on raising that can also help significantly with happiness problems. Over time pops will convert/assimilate and become happier, decreasing the proportion of discontent pops to the point where the forts may no longer be needed - building a [![Image 154: Theater](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4d/Building_theater.png/28px-Building_theater.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Theater "Theater") grand theater and [![Image 155: Temple](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/87/Building_temple.png/28px-Building_temple.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Temple "Temple") great temple in the main cities of the province can speed this up process significantly.

## Assimilation[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Assimilation") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Assimilation")]

_Main article: [Assimilation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation")_

A pop's culture can change to the nation's primary culture via [![Image 156: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation")**assimilation**. For every [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories"), a single randomly chosen pop will always be assimilating as long as that territory holds pops that are not of an integrated culture and are not otherwise busy (e.g. converting or promoting). Assimilation progress builds each month, with the exact progress determined by the sum of all [![Image 157: Assimilation speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Assimilation_speed.png/28px-Assimilation_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Assimilation_speed.png)**Pop Assimilation Speed** modifiers. Once progress reaches 100%, the pop will be assimilated to the primary culture and another pop will begin to assimilate, if there are any other pops that can be assimilated. [![Image 158: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") Integrated cultures will never assimilate.

Given that pops of unintegrated pops cannot be raised in a country's army and usually have significant happiness penalties, it is generally beneficial to assimilate any non-integrated culture pops in the country. Using the [![Image 159: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Policy_cultural_assimilation.png) Cultural Assimilation [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy"), [founding colonies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_decisions#found_colonia_cultural_decision "Cultural decisions"), building [![Image 160: Theater](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4d/Building_theater.png/28px-Building_theater.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Theater "Theater") grand theaters, and passing the laws with assimilation bonuses for [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribe "Tribe") and [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") will speed up the assimilation, but in general provinces - especially more populated settlements, as [![Image 161: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities generally have disproportionately faster assimilation - may take decades before they are significantly assimilated. However, as integrated pops count against [![Image 162: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research efficiency while non-integrated pops do not, integrating a large amount of pops - particularly those that are primarily of the lower classes - can have a significant impact on research progression if additional measures in that area are not taken.

## Conversion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Conversion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Conversion")]

_Main article: [Conversion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion")_

[![Image 163: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") In every [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories"), a single pop can undergo **conversion** to the state religion at a time, as long as there are any pops in that territory that can convert and are not otherwise busy (e.g. assimilating or promoting). Outside of events, pops will always convert to the state religion. Conversion progress builds each month, with the exact progress determined by the sum of all [![Image 164: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion")**Pop Conversion Speed** modifiers. Once progress reaches 100%, the pop will be converted to the state religion and another pop will begin to convert, if there are any other pops that can be converted. The speed of conversion is influenced by many factors, particularly the [![Image 165: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Policy_religious_conversion.png) Religious Conversion [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") and the dominant province [![Image 166: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture")[culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") and [![Image 167: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religion being different from the state culture and religion. Note that it is possible for conversion to be completely stalled.

All else being equal, it is generally preferable to convert pops to the state religion, though the differences in happiness are much less than with unintegrated [culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") or culture group pops and can be ameliorated by adopting deities of the pop's religion in the pantheon (though this will necessarily come at the expense of the happiness of pops of other religions), which generally makes conversion less important than [assimilation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation"). Under some circumstances it may also be beneficial to retain pops of other religions in order to have continued access to their deities, though the strong effects of religious unity on [![Image 168: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omen power means that it is still generally undesirable to have too many other pops of a non-state religion.

## Population Change[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Population Change") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Population Change")]

### Population Growth[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Population Growth") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Population Growth")]

[![Image 169: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth") The main way that new pops appear in the game is through natural **population growth**. Every colonizable territory in the game will have one randomly chosen pop growing at a time, gaining progress each month according to the territory's **Population Growth Speed**, determined by the sum of all [![Image 170: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**local** and **national population growth** modifiers. Once the population growth progress reaches **100%**, a new pop will be created in the territory with the same pop class, culture, and religion of the pop it originally grew from. Note that a pop will continue to grow even if the pop it is growing from its killed, moved away, changed in any way, which might result in a newly grown pop no longer sharing the same characteristics of the original pop once it finishes if the original pop changed in the meantime.

Important sources of population growth are listed below:

If aiming to boost population growth, [![Image 171: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability (while it gives the highest potential growth rate, it is the most costly to keep high) and [![Image 172: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") stored food are generally the most effective. The other modifiers are smaller and generally have a higher opportunity cost. There are also a large number of other modifiers from [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), [decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions"), and [missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") that can increase or decrease population growth in a territory, most notably the [Roman Warm Period](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Roman_Warm_Period_events "Roman Warm Period events").

If the population growth speed is negative - usually as a result of starvation, or sometimes significant overpopulation - pops in the territory will instead begin to **die**. Any pop that is growing will instead begin to lose progress, and if it reaches 0 a randomly pop will be chosen to begin dying. The progress bar will be reset to 100% and gradually lose progress according to the current population growth speed, and once the growth progress reaches 0 the pop will be killed and another pop will begin to die.

Note that population growth in general does not scale according to the actual population of the territory, but instead remains constant as the population grows (within the [![Image 173: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity). This means that population growth tends to be linear over the course of the game, not exponential, and that population growth generally scales best to the [![Image 174: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") number of owned territories rather than the [![Image 175: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population of a country.

### Occupation[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Occupation") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Occupation")]

_Main article: [Enslavement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")_

When a territory is occupied an by army led by a [![Image 176: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander, a certain proportion of the population (approximately the population of the territory multiplied by the occupying army's [![Image 177: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") enslavement efficiency) will be captured, with around **1/3** killed directly (and the rest enslaved and sent back to the occupying army's homeland). In a large scale war, this can result in significant demographic losses if a country's population centres are successfully sieged down. When a [![Image 178: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city, [![Image 179: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis, or [![Image 180: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") is occupied, the commander of the army will also get an event to decide how much to sack the city, which can result in further loss of population if the commander decides to let the army loose.

## Pop Movement[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Pop Movement") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Pop Movement")]

While every pop lives in a certain territory, they are generally not directly bound to their current home and under various circumstances may decide to move, or be forcibly moved, to another territory. In addition to [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), [decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions"), [missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions"), and certain interactions, there are a number of mechanics by which pops can move from territory to territory, some happening passively with others depending more strongly on the country's actions.

### Migration[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Migration") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Migration")]

[![Image 181: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") Free pops will **migrate** of their own volition to nearby territories in pursuit of economic opportunities, or to escape deteriorating circumstances in their current home. A territory's general attractiveness is represented by its [![Image 182: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction")**migration attraction**, which is determined largely by available [![Image 183: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity and [![Image 184: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value, but can also be decreased - sometimes significantly so - by disruptive actions and events such as [![Image 185: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest, [![Image 186: Siege.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Siege.png/28px-Siege.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege") sieges and occupation, [![Image 187: Food deficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e6/Food_deficiency.png/28px-Food_deficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Food_deficiency.png)[starvation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food#Province_food "Food"), and overpopulation. Certain [governor policies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") can also be used to some extent to adjust migration attraction.

Important modifiers to [![Image 188: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") migration attraction include:

All pops other than [![Image 189: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves are eligible to migrate as long as they are not currently busy ([![Image 190: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilating, [![Image 191: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") converting, [![Image 192: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") promoting, or [![Image 193: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") demoting). Every territory can then have **1** pop that is migrating away (chosen at random) to another territory with higher [![Image 194: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") migration attraction, with pops generally preferring targets with higher attraction. There is no limit, however, to the number of pops that can be immigrating to a territory at a given time, which generally results in territories with high [![Image 195: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") migration attraction receiving a disproportionate share of migrants. Pops can migrate between neighbouring territories (including those with a different owner, though not to or from uncolonized territories), territories in the same [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), or between any two territories in the same country that both have a [![Image 196: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port.

As with all passively occurring pop activities, when a pop is emigrating it will gain a certain amount of progress each month, determined by the combined [![Image 197: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed")**migration speed** modifiers of the pop, territory (that the pop is emigrating from), and the country, with the pop moving to its new home once the progress bar reaches 100%. Each pop class has a certain base migration speed, with [![Image 198: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen the fastest and [![Image 199: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 200: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens the slowest, while migrating from [![Image 201: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements is also much slower than from [![Image 202: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 203: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises. Geographical considerations such as being adjacent to a [![Image 204: River](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Terrain_river.png/28px-Terrain_river.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "River") river, being on the [![Image 205: Coastal Sea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Terrain_coastal.png/28px-Terrain_coastal.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Coastal Sea") coast, and especially having a [![Image 206: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port will increase migration speed, as do factors disrupting life in the territory of origin such as [![Image 207: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest, overpopulation, and starvation. Migration speed can be further increased by certain [![Image 208: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, [![Image 209: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, and [governor policies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy"), as well.

Important modifiers to [![Image 210: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") migration speed include:

While seemingly quite slow, migration can be a powerful force over the course of the game, and generally gives a substantial contribution to the population growth of cities. Migration between [![Image 211: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port provinces is particularly powerful given the much larger pool of territories that immigrants can be drawn from, which makes it particularly useful to place the [![Image 212: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital and/or other major economic centres at ports beyond the base bonuses that they give. On a smaller scale, immigration from settlements in the countryside to the local provincial capital or other nearby cities also contributes to urbanization over the course of the game, at least in settled nations; while the predominant [![Image 213: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave population in territories cannot migrate, the local [![Image 214: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen can, which reduces the ratio of freemen in the settlement and, unless disabled, allows the remaining [![Image 215: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves to promote into their place, allowing for further migration.

### Manual Movement[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Manual Movement") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Manual Movement")]

While [![Image 216: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves cannot migrate on their own, they can still be **moved manually** by their owner country through the territory population interface to any owned territory that either neighbours or is in the same [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") as the territory of origin. [Tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations can additionally manually move owned [![Image 217: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen pops through the same mechanic as well. Unlike migration, manual movement of pops happens instantaneously with no cooldowns or other restrictions - if desired, a single pop can be moved multiple times between many different territories on the same day - though there is also a base cost of [![Image 218: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**5** gold every time a pop is moved, which for [![Image 219: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves can be modified by the [![Image 220: Slave move cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Pop_slaves_move_cost.png/28px-Pop_slaves_move_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_movement "Slave move cost")**move slaves cost** modifier, available only as a **-25%** reduction with the [![Image 221: Vegetables](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Goods_vegetables.png/28px-Goods_vegetables.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Vegetables "Vegetables") vegetables capital bonus.

Since the number of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") a territory produces is (partly) tied to the number of slaves it has, moving [![Image 222: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves can to some extent can be used to control and manage the country's production of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods"), such as by moving slaves to territories that produce more expensive/desirable trade goods or more careful optimization to push a territory's [![Image 223: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave population just over the next threshold for producing another instance of the trade good sooner than population growth would have occurred naturally. Using rural [![Image 224: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves to increase the population of nearby [![Image 225: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities can also be useful to speed up urbanization, as most of the slaves will eventually promote and begin producing other resources such as [![Image 226: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, [![Image 227: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points, and [![Image 228: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes that may be more useful. Note that moving the last pop away from a territory will decolonize the territory and make it unowned.

In addition, manually moving pops is particularly useful for quickly shifting the demographics of a territory to have a dominant [![Image 229: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") integrated culture and [![Image 230: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") state religion to meet the requirements for [colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization") more quickly than [![Image 231: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilation and [![Image 232: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") conversion would normally allow. Note that with sufficient gold, a [![Image 233: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave pop (or a [![Image 234: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen pop, for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") countries) can be moved an arbitrarily large distance between connected territories by moving the pop to the edge of the [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), using the adjacency condition to move the pop into an adjacent territory in the next province, and continuing until the pop has been moved to its desired location. This means that even territories far away from a significant source of [![Image 235: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") integrated culture and [![Image 236: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") state religion pops can have pops moved there to begin colonization, if the country is willing to spend enough [![Image 237: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold.

### Enslavement[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Enslavement") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Enslavement")]

_Main article: [Enslavement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")_

When an army with a [![Image 238: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander occupies territories of an enemy nation, some of the pops in the territory may be [![Image 239: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")**enslaved**, forcibly demoted to [![Image 240: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave status (but retaining their culture and religion) and sent back to the homeland as a spoil of war. The number of pops that are captured is a percentage of the territory's population depending on the occupying army's [![Image 241: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")**enslavement efficiency**, and of those, around **2/3** will actually be enslaved and sent back to the homeland (with the other 1/3 of captured pops killed instead). The enslaved pops will generally be distributed according to a territory's [![Image 242: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") migration attraction, preferring the [![Image 243: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital territory (and to a lesser extent [![Image 244: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capitals) while strongly avoiding any territories that are at or above their [![Image 245: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity.

Enslavement is one of the most powerful ways to increase a country's population, besides direct conquest, if there are accessible areas that the country can easily occupy, and it is often a good idea to occupy even unimportant territories in enemy countries in order to maximize the number of pops enslaved. The general concentration of enslaved pops in cities also means that they will usually eventually promote, swelling the ranks of the higher classes as well.

While sacking large cities and populous areas can be a great economic and demographic boon to the homeland, the reverse is equally true for areas that have seen their pops enslaved, making enemy occupation dangerous and potentially ruinous even if no demands are actually enforced against the country. Even the most highly populated areas will eventually be heavily or even completely depopulated and ruined as their pops are killed or carted off if they are constantly fought over, occupied, and sacked.

### Colonization[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Colonization") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Colonization")]

_Main article: [Colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization")_

When a nearby unowned territory is colonized, one integrated culture state religion pop will be moved from the origin of colonization to the territory being colonized for free.

## Population Capacity[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Population Capacity") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Population Capacity")]

[![Image 246: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") Each territory has a certain **population capacity**, which determines how large of a [![Image 247: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population a territory's terrain and infrastructure can support. Territories have a base [![Image 248: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity of **10**, which is modified by a number of modifiers from terrain, technology, buildings, events, and more. Knowing which territories are good for population growth is essential in city placement and economic planning; it is almost always better to construct new [![Image 249: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities in territories with a higher base population capacity, as larger cities are almost always richer and more productive than smaller ones, and [![Image 250: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity is usually the main factor that restricts the growth of cities. Note that [![Image 251: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity is a different value and modifier, though related, from [![Image 252: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food, which also services as a limitation to the population of a territory.

Some of the most important modifiers to [![Image 253: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity include:

In addition, there is also the distinct [![Image 254: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity")**Total Population Capacity** modifier, which is applied multiplicatively after all the normal population capacity modifiers have been applied to get the territory's final population capacity. The only source of this is a **-75%** modifier if the territory's province has run out of [![Image 255: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food and has a Critical Food Supply.

It is theoretically possible to raise the population capacity of any territory as high as desired by stacking bonuses from the [![Image 256: Civic province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6b/Civic_investment.png/28px-Civic_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Civic province investment") State Infrastructure [province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment") and [![Image 257: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions")[Civic Advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Advance "Advance"), but the small percentage increases means that this is prohibitively expensive. However, as in [![Image 258: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and metropolises every 10 pops unlocks a new [![Image 259: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot") building slot, it is possible to stack city population capacity arbitrarily high (given enough time) by building [![Image 260: Aqueduct](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Building_aqueduct.png/28px-Building_aqueduct.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Aqueduct "Aqueduct") aqueducts if the total of the percentage modifiers is at least **150%** - possible if focusing on stacking modifiers and improving the the territory/province by the midgame - which will make each [![Image 261: Aqueduct](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Building_aqueduct.png/28px-Building_aqueduct.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Aqueduct "Aqueduct") aqueduct give **+10** population capacity, enough to create another building slot to build another [![Image 262: Aqueduct](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Building_aqueduct.png/28px-Building_aqueduct.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Aqueduct "Aqueduct") aqueduct once the population capacity has been filled. It is usually strongest to do this in the [![Image 263: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital, which has a number of bonuses that makes each individual pop even more productive.

If a territory exceeds its [![Image 264: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, it will get one instance of the **Overpopulation** modifier for every pop over the [![Image 265: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, giving:

The [![Image 266: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population of a territory with any significant amount of overpopulation will therefore usually quickly fall back down through [![Image 267: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") migration and population death, while population growth from natural growth, immigration, and [![Image 268: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") enslavement will be quickly stalled, providing an effective soft cap on the population that a territory can sustainably have. It is generally rarely worth having an overpopulated territory, unless the territory only needs to go slightly over the [![Image 269: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity to meet the threshold to produce another [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods").

## Pop Ratio[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Pop Ratio") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Pop Ratio")]

Every territory has a desired **optimal ratio** for each pop class that the population of the territory will slowly drift towards, determined by the sum of all pop ratio modifiers ([![Image 270: Noble ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9e/Pop_noble_ratio.png/28px-Pop_noble_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Noble ratio")**Noble Desired Ratio**, [![Image 271: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio")**Citizen Desired Ratio**, [![Image 272: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio")**Freeman Desired Ratio**, [![Image 273: Tribesman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9f/Pop_tribesmen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Tribesman ratio")**Tribesman Desired Ratio**, and [![Image 274: Slave ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Pop_slaves_ratio.png/28px-Pop_slaves_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Slave ratio")**Slave Desired Ratio**) that apply to the territory and its owner. Some modifiers are local and apply only to a single territory (**Local Desired Ratio**), some apply to all territories in a country (**National Desired Ratio**), and some apply to all territories with [![Image 275: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city status in a country (**Desired Ratio in Cities**).

Despite what it might suggest, a modifier that increases the ratio of a pop class by a certain percentage does not necessarily actually increase the optimal ratio of that pop class by that amount. Instead, the optimal ratio of each pop class is normalized by summing up all the desired ratio modifiers for that class (with a minimum desired ratio of 0) and dividing it by the total desired ratios over all 5 pop classes:

![Image 276: {\displaystyle {\text{Optimal Ratio for Class}}={\frac {\text{Desired Ratio for Class}}{{\text{Desired Noble Ratio}}+{\text{Desired Citizen Ratio}}+{\text{Desired Freeman Ratio}}+{\text{Desired Tribesman Ratio}}+{\text{Desired Slave Ratio}}}}\cdot 100\%}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/ea9f8299687976a9de64ccc3a5c3f671fe3ef82c)
For instance, in a [![Image 277: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement owned by a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") with no other modifiers, there is a [![Image 278: Slave ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Pop_slaves_ratio.png/28px-Pop_slaves_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Slave ratio")**+10%** Local Slave Desired Ratio modifier along with a [![Image 279: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio")**+2.5%** Local Citizen Desired Ratio from the territory rank and a [![Image 280: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio")**+2.5%** National Freeman Desired Ratio modifier from the government form that is applied. This gives a final desired ratio of ![Image 281: {\displaystyle 2.5/(2.5+2.5+10)\cdot 100\%=16\%}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/138fb10b7f3a13bd37aa61ba841b888f7f787455) for [![Image 282: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens and [![Image 283: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen and a final desired ratio of ![Image 284: {\displaystyle 10/(2.5+2.5+10)\cdot 100\%=66\%}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/cb32223e978c80bdf44892947d94a870c74f020d) for [![Image 285: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves. One major effect of the way that the optimal ratio is calculated is that the effect of further modifiers is high when there are few desired ratio modifiers to begin with, but decreases rapidly as more and more are applied.

The [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory_rank "Territory rank") of a territory is one of the main factors that affect the desired class ratios of a territory:

Each overall [![Image 286: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government form also applies further modifiers to pop ratios in each territory:

Several buildings, particularly those in [![Image 287: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 288: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, also modify the desired class ratios locally:

Certain [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") and [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") types can also affect the desired ratio of pop classes across all territories with [![Image 289: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city status in a country, including:

A number of other local modifiers can also change the pop ratios of a territory.

The overall effect of these modifiers is that [![Image 290: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens and especially [![Image 291: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles are found almost exclusively in [![Image 292: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 293: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, as well as to a lesser extent in [![Image 294: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capitals (even those that are [![Image 295: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements), while [![Image 296: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements will be dominated by [![Image 297: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves and either a smattering of [![Image 298: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens and [![Image 299: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen for [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") and [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") or [![Image 300: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") countries. Increasing the movement and migration of pops from settlements to cities will therefore result in an overall higher proportion of [![Image 301: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 302: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens in the country, which is generally recommended as [![Image 303: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 304: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens are the only source of [![Image 305: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points which is needed to keep up in technology.

### Promotion and Demotion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&veaction=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Promotion and Demotion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Population&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Promotion and Demotion")]

The pop class structure of territories will change over time to approach the optimal ratio through [![Image 306: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion")**promotion** and [![Image 307: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion")**demotion**. Every territory will have one pop promoting and one pop demoting at a time until the actual pop class ratio converges with the optimal ratios for each class in that territory, as long as there are pops that are eligible to be promoted or demoted. Pops cannot promote or demote if they are currently [![Image 308: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilating or [![Image 309: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") converting; as well, pops cannot promote past the assigned [civic rights](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_rights "Civic rights") of its culture, which is particularly important when trying to promote pops into [![Image 310: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens and [![Image 311: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles. [![Image 312: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") Slave promotion (to [![Image 313: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen) can be blocked on a territory-specific basis using the **Slave Promotion Allowed** toggle in the territory population interface at the cost of [![Image 314: Slave happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Pop_slaves_happiness.png/28px-Pop_slaves_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Slave happiness")**-24%** slave happiness, which can be useful to prop up [![Image 315: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income and increase the amount of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") produced, though fewer pops of other classes will mean less [![Image 316: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower and particularly [![Image 317: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points and [![Image 318: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes.

As with other pop activities, promotion and demotion of pops gain progress each month, with the exact amount of progress determined by the [![Image 319: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion")**Pop Promotion Speed** and [![Image 320: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion")**Pop Demotion Speed** modifiers, respectively. Once progress reaches 100%, the pop will promote by one level or demote by one level and another pop will begin to promote or demote, if there are any other pops that can do so. Note that pops will only ever promote or demote by one level regardless of how far the territory's current pop classes are from the optimal ratio - for instance, a [![Image 321: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave will need to promote three times before it can become a [![Image 322: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble, even if there are currently no nobles in the territory. As an exception, while [![Image 323: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen while promote to [![Image 324: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen and demote to [![Image 325: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves as expected, [![Image 326: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen will demote directly down to [![Image 327: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave status rather than passing through [![Image 328: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen first. Similarly, [![Image 329: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves can promote to both [![Image 330: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen or [![Image 331: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen, depending on the current situation.

The base [![Image 332: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") promotion and [![Image 333: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") demotion speed depends on the original class of the pop (not the class it is promoting/demoting to), with [![Image 334: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 335: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen particularly slow to promote or demote:

**Promotion speed**

**Demotion speed**

[![Image 336: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") Promotion speed is then affected by many other modifiers, including:

Similarly, modifiers to [![Image 337: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") demotion speed include:


============================================================
# Buildings
============================================================
Title: Infrastructure - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[Territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") can be upgraded with a variety of **infrastructure** to improve the [![Image 1: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") output and [![Image 2: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of their pops, as well as its accessibility and defensibility, at the cost of [![Image 3: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold. Investing in infrastructure can significantly improve a country's [![Image 4: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income, [![Image 5: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, [![Image 6: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research, and more, and is generally most effective in more highly populated territories.

## Buildings[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Buildings") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Buildings")]

A wide variety of different **buildings** can be built in [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") to give a wide variety of local modifiers, such as improving pop [![Image 7: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") output and [![Image 8: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness, adjusting the ratio of pop classes, driving [![Image 9: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilation and [![Image 10: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") conversion, increasing the local [![Image 11: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, adding [![Image 12: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_level "Fort level") fortifications to the area, building [![Image 13: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port facilities for ships, and more. The buildings available depend on the territory's [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory_rank "Territory rank"), with different sets available to [![Image 14: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements and [![Image 15: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities/[![Image 16: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises; some of the [![Image 17: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement buildings are also restricted based on the [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") that the territory produces.

Every building has an associated [![Image 18: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold cost and [![Image 19: Time](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Time.png/28px-Time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Time.png "Time") build time, which are modified by the [![Image 20: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**build cost** and [![Image 21: Build time.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Build_time.png/28px-Build_time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**build time** modifiers, respectively. Each territory can only build one building at a time, though it is possible to make a queue of buildings to construct. Once it is built, buildings do not cost any maintenance or upkeep. Any building in any owned territory can be torn down instantly at any time, which returns **1/4th** of the its current [![Image 22: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") build cost to the treasury (also affected by the [![Image 23: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") build cost modifier), regardless of when or by whom the building was built. Some [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mission "Mission") results may automatically construct a building in a territory.
