### Building slots[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Building slots") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Building slots")]

[![Image 143: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot") The number of buildings that can be built in any particular territory is limited by that territory's number of **building slots**. Each building takes up 1 slot, including those under construction, and no buildings can be built in a territory that has filled up all its slots. Territories that have more buildings than their number of permitted slots - for instance, after losing population during a sack - will lose any buildings over the limit. [![Image 144: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") Wonders also take up a building slot, and require at least one empty building slot to begin construction.

[![Image 145: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") Settlements normally only have **1** building slot (though settlement buildings are in general stronger than other buildings), which can be increased by the [![Image 146: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**global settlement building slots** modifier to a maximum of **2** by researching the [![Image 147: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Rural Planning [![Image 148: Civic faction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Party_civic.png/28px-Party_civic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senatorial_factions "Civic faction") Civic invention (though each settlement building will still have its territory cap of one). Building slots mostly become relevant when settlements are upgraded to [![Image 149: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities, which gives a base of **2** extra building slots and an additional **1** for every [![Image 150: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population")**10** pops in the city. If a city is further upgraded to a [![Image 151: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis the base number of building slots is increased to **4**. Other important sources of building slots in [![Image 152: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 153: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, given by the [![Image 154: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**local city building slots** and [![Image 155: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**global city building slots** modifier, include:

## Great wonders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Great wonders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Great wonders")]

_Main article: [Great wonders](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Great\_wonders "Great wonders")_

[![Image 156: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder")**Great wonders** are special buildings that can be constructed in territories and give powerful bonuses to their local [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") or owner. Unlike standard buildings, wonders can be customized with various designs, materials, and effects, and are a significant investment that only the large and wealthy empires will be able to afford. As the centerpiece and defining feature of the local area, each territory can normally only support one great wonder, though certain wonders that are built by event can coexist with each other in the same territory. Uniquely, wonders usually give strong nation-wide effects that grow over time as the wonder's fame and prestige increases.

At the start of the game, there are a number of ancient wonders scattered all over the map, from the Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus in Rome to the Great Pyramids of Giza and the University of Taxila. Building additional wonders requires the **[Heirs of Alexander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heirs_of_Alexander "Heirs of Alexander")** DLC.

## Roads[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Roads") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Roads")]

[![Image 157](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Roads.png/300px-Roads.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Roads.png)

Roads built by a Roman army.

[![Image 158: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road "Road")**Roads** can be built by [![Image 159: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") armies to connect two adjacent [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") at the cost of [![Image 160: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, reducing the [![Image 161: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") movement cost by **50%** and effectively doubling the army movement speed between them, making them highly desirable for larger empires that would benefit from being able to move armies between their provinces more quickly. In addition to the army movement bonuses, connecting a territory to the road network also gives a minor boost to development, [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade"), and pop mobility, making them useful to build in highly populated developed territories as well as connecting frontier areas. Roads also appear graphically on the map as the network is built and extended, generally being most prominent on the terrain mapmode; if a road is built over a river, a bridge will appear. Unlike with buildings, roads are not associated with individual territories, but instead with pairs of adjacent territories (including possibly those connected over a strait); it is therefore possible (and common) for a territory to be a part of multiple different road segments. For every road connecting a territory to one of its adjacent territories, a territory will get the following stackable modifiers:

There are two ways to build roads - a special, cheaper method for countries with access to [![Image 162: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[Italic tradition groups](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions"), and more generic one that is available to all countries. Once a road is built, it cannot be removed or destroyed, even if the territories it connects are completely depopulated.

### Regular roads[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regular roads") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regular roads")]

Most countries need to reach at least [![Image 163: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Level **5** of [civic advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Civic_advances "Technology") to begin building roads.

Roads can be built using the [![Image 164: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road")**Build Road** unit ability toggle, which can be activated if all of the following conditions are met:

Any type of army, even [![Image 165: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies and [![Image 166: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries, can build roads, if they meet all of the requirements.

When the [![Image 167: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") build road toggle is on, road construction will begin when the unit moves from one territory to another, as long as both territories either owned and controlled by the army's owner or one of their [![Image 168: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subjects, or are uncolonized (including [uninhabitable](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Uninhabitable "Uninhabitable") territories, though not [impassable terrain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Impassable_terrain "Impassable terrain")). When the army arrives in that other territory, if the two territories do not already have a road between them, the road will be instantly built at a base cost of [![Image 169: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**50** gold. This cost is affected by the [![Image 170: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**build roads cost** modifier, given only by the [![Image 171: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic inventions [![Image 172: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Gromatici ([![Image 173: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**-25%**) and [![Image 174: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") De Architectura ([![Image 175: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**-15%**). If the army has at least [![Image 176: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") 1 engineer cohort, the cost of building a road is instead lowered to [![Image 177: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold (not affected by any modifiers).

While[![Image 178: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") building roads, the following modifiers are applied to the army:

The toggle will be turned off if the army becomes ineligible to build roads, or if the army finishes moving to a new territory without a road on the traversed connection and the country does not have enough money to build a road.

### Military roads[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Military roads") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Military roads")]

If a country has access to [![Image 179: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[Roman traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions") (in the Italic traditions group), adopting the **Roman Roads** tradition will give access to the special [![Image 180: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road")**Build Military Road** toggle, even if it does not currently meet the technology requirements for building roads normally. [![Image 181: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") Building military roads is largely the same as building regular roads, with the main differences that the road-building army can be smaller, requiring only [![Image 182: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army")**5**[cohorts](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"), but must be a [![Image 183: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion, and that each road costs only [![Image 184: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Cost")**25** gold per territory connection. However, note that the cost is **not** affected by the [![Image 185: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road") build roads cost modifier. As with normal road building, the cost is lowered to to [![Image 186: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold (also not affected by any modifiers) if the army has at least [![Image 187: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") 1 engineer cohort.

In addition, a reduced set of modifiers is applied to the road-building army, without the increased maintenance cost or morale penalty:

*   [![Image 188: Army movement without roads](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Movement_speed_if_no_road.png/28px-Movement_speed_if_no_road.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Army movement without roads")**−70%** Army movement without roads

## Forts[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Forts") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Forts")]

_See also: [Siege](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege")_

[![Image 189: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort")**Forts** can be built in territories to block the advance of enemy armies and protect important cities and strategic points from being easily captured. The size of a fort is determined by its **fort level**, where **1** is the minimum and 0 indicates the lack of a fort, and every [![Image 190: Fortress](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Building_fortress.png/28px-Building_fortress.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Fortress "Fortress") fortress building giving **1** fort level. This means that settlements are typically restricted to a fort level of 1, while cities and metropolises are able to support larger forts. The higher the fort level, the harder it is for an enemy army to successfully take the fort.

To fully occupy a province, the attacker must besiege its [![Image 191: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") capital and any forts in the province, after which the rest will be automatically occupied after a few days. An unprotected territory takes only 15 days to occupy, while one with a fort will take multiple months to siege down unless the attacking army launches an [![Image 192: Assault ability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Assault_ability.png/28px-Assault_ability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assault "Assault") assault (usually taking heavy casualties). Each fort level gives **-1** to siege progress and provides the base [![Image 193: Garrison](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/52/Garrison.png/28px-Garrison.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Garrison")**garrison** of **500** per fort level, adjusted by the **garrison size** modifier. A besieging army needs to have at least **2000** men per each fort level to begin a siege, no matter how big the garrison is. Assaulting a fort will damage the garrison, and as soon as it reaches 0, the fort will fall. When it falls (or a new fort level is built), its garrison will start replenishing at a rate of **10%** of the max garrison per month.

In addition, forts exert a **zone of control** (ZoC) over adjacent territories. An army that enters a hostile zone of control will not be able to progress further, and must either proceed to the fort and besiege it or return to the territory they came from. [![Image 194: Flag of Barbarians](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/16/Barbarians.png/20px-Barbarians.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians")[Barbarians](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") ignore all zones of control and can move freely, but territories in the zone of control of a friendly fort will not lose [![Image 195: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value if occupied. Friendly forts also negate [![Image 196: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition from deserts or winter, and will automatically occupy any enemy-controlled territories in its zone of control unless there is a hostile army there.

Every [![Image 197: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort level costs a base of [![Image 198: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**0.40** gold in maintenance each month, adjusted by the [![Image 199: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**fort maintenance** modifier, except for the first capital fort.

Every province has a certain [![Image 200: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort")**fort infrastructure capacity** value, with default capacity of **5**. The first fort in a territory uses up **3** points of capacity, while subsequent fort levels only use **1**, and exceeding the limit gives a [![Image 201: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**+25%** maintenance modifier for all forts in the province by for every point over the limit. This means, with the default limit of 5, two level 1 forts in one province would use 6 points and cost ![Image 202: {\displaystyle 0.4*1.25=0.5}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/ea3fac2d9eb930355174274c4ad79b1916c6f7b3) each per month in maintenance ([![Image 203: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") 1 in total), while a single level 2 fort costs [![Image 204: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") 0.8 gold per month to maintain. Capacity can be increased through the [![Image 205: Military province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Military_investment.png/28px-Military_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Military province investment") Fortify Province province investment, several [![Image 206: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, some [![Image 207: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, and a [![Image 208: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonder effect.

Every territory in the province gets [![Image 209: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion")**-0.25** unrest for every used fort infrastructure point, which means spreading forts out over a number of territories is much more efficient at suppressing unrest than concentrating them in a single territory, even if more expensive. As maintaining forts can be costly, it is often a good idea to remove extraneous forts that no longer serve any meaningful defensive and unrest-suppressing purpose.

### Border forts[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Border forts") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Border forts")]

_See also: [Colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization")_

A country that has adopted the [![Image 210: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Castra** military tradition from the [Italic tribe traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions") tree can have an army build a [![Image 211: Construct border fort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a2/Ability_border_forts.png/28px-Ability_border_forts.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Construct border fort")**border fort** in an owned frontier or neighbouring uncolonized territory. An army can do this action if it meets all the following conditions:

When the ability is activated, the country loses [![Image 212: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**3000** manpower and builds a [![Image 213: Fortress](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Building_fortress.png/28px-Building_fortress.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Fortress "Fortress") fortress in the territory. The country then gains ownership of the province if it was previously uncolonized, in which case the territory will also gain a [![Image 214: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman pop of the primary culture and religion. Finally, one random cohort becomes [![Image 215: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to the commander, unless the general is the ruler of a non-republic, in which case the country gains [![Image 216: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**1** tyranny instead. This ability can notably be used to [colonize](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization") territories even if there is no neighbouring territory with a dominant integrated culture and state religion.

## Ports[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Ports") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Ports")]

[![Image 217: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port")**Ports** are buildings that can be built in any [![Image 218: Coastal Sea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Terrain_coastal.png/28px-Terrain_coastal.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Coastal Sea") coastal territories, as well as certain inland territories on [![Image 219: River](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Terrain_river.png/28px-Terrain_river.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "River") major rivers (those that begin the game with a [![Image 220: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port). They allow [![Image 221: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ship "Ship") ships to dock (including quickly embarking and disembarking troops), be built, repair, and supply, and are important for any nation that seeks to maintain a significant navy. The number of ports in a territory is the territory's **port level**, which in addition to the effects from the port buildings themselves also determines what types of ships it can build and repair: the light [![Image 222: Unit liburnian.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Unit_liburnian.png/28px-Unit_liburnian.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Liburnian "Liburnian") liburnians and [![Image 223: Unit trireme.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d5/Unit_trireme.png/28px-Unit_trireme.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trireme "Trireme") triremes only require a level 1 port, the medium [![Image 224: Unit tetrere.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Unit_tetrere.png/28px-Unit_tetrere.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tetrere "Tetrere") tetreres and [![Image 225: Unit hexere.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/87/Unit_hexere.png/28px-Unit_hexere.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Hexere "Hexere") hexeres require at least level 3 ports, while the very heavy [![Image 226: Unit octere.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a1/Unit_octere.png/28px-Unit_octere.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Octere "Octere") octeres and [![Image 227: Unit mega-polyreme.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/27/Unit_mega-polyreme.png/28px-Unit_mega-polyreme.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mega-polyreme "Mega-polyreme") mega-polyremes can only be built and repaired at ports that are at least level 5.

In addition to effects from the building itself, every [![Image 228: Coastal Sea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Terrain_coastal.png/28px-Terrain_coastal.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Coastal Sea") coastal territory with at least one port gets the following modifiers:

For each territory in a province that has at least one port, the province also gets the following modifier:

*   [![Image 229: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed")**+2.5%** Pop Migration Speed

Note that neither of these effects stack if territory has more than one port.

### Pirate havens[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Pirate havens") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Pirate havens")]

Certain ports are home to [![Image 230: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate")**pirate havens** where groups of pirates are based from. These pirate havens are present in certain ports at the beginning of the game, and in addition to giving an economic boost of [![Image 231: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**+20%** Local Tax and [![Image 232: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**+0.01%** Local Population Growth to their territory they host **pirate [fleets](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy")**, always composed of all light ships, which can rented out as naval mercenaries at times of war. When not being hired pirate fleets will eventually grow restless and go on raids, at which point the fleet while become hostile to all other navies and travel to nearby unfortified ports to **plunder** them, giving [![Image 233: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**-25%** Local Tax and [![Image 234: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion")**+10%** Demotion Speed to the plundered territories.

If pirates grow to be too much of a problem, their havens can be destroyed using the [![Image 235: Root out pirates](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4b/Ability_root_out_pirates.png/28px-Ability_root_out_pirates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Root out pirates")**Root Out Pirates** army ability on any controlled territory once the anti-pirate [law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") has been passed, including those belonging to other countries that have been occupied in a [![Image 236: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war. This will remove the modifier (including its beneficial modifiers) and destroy the associated pirate fleet, even if it is currently out on a raid. Alternatively, pirates can be actively encouraged by passing the pro-pirate law, which will create a new [![Image 237: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate") pirate haven in one of the country's ports to increase its prosperity and provide more available naval mercenaries at the expense of the nearby coasts.

Destroying all ports in a pirate haven will also force any local pirate/mercenary fleets to disband, though this will not remove the pirate haven itself. If the port is ever rebuilt, the pirate fleet will eventually reappear.

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[Territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") can be upgraded with a variety of **infrastructure** to improve the [![Image 1: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") output and [![Image 2: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of their pops, as well as its accessibility and defensibility, at the cost of [![Image 3: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold. Investing in infrastructure can significantly improve a country's [![Image 4: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income, [![Image 5: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, [![Image 6: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research, and more, and is generally most effective in more highly populated territories.

## Buildings[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Buildings") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Buildings")]

A wide variety of different **buildings** can be built in [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") to give a wide variety of local modifiers, such as improving pop [![Image 7: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") output and [![Image 8: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness, adjusting the ratio of pop classes, driving [![Image 9: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilation and [![Image 10: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") conversion, increasing the local [![Image 11: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, adding [![Image 12: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_level "Fort level") fortifications to the area, building [![Image 13: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port facilities for ships, and more. The buildings available depend on the territory's [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory_rank "Territory rank"), with different sets available to [![Image 14: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements and [![Image 15: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities/[![Image 16: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises; some of the [![Image 17: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement buildings are also restricted based on the [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") that the territory produces.

Every building has an associated [![Image 18: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold cost and [![Image 19: Time](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Time.png/28px-Time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Time.png "Time") build time, which are modified by the [![Image 20: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**build cost** and [![Image 21: Build time.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Build_time.png/28px-Build_time.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**build time** modifiers, respectively. Each territory can only build one building at a time, though it is possible to make a queue of buildings to construct. Once it is built, buildings do not cost any maintenance or upkeep. Any building in any owned territory can be torn down instantly at any time, which returns **1/4th** of the its current [![Image 22: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") build cost to the treasury (also affected by the [![Image 23: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") build cost modifier), regardless of when or by whom the building was built. Some [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mission "Mission") results may automatically construct a building in a territory.

### Building slots[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Building slots") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Building slots")]

[![Image 143: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot") The number of buildings that can be built in any particular territory is limited by that territory's number of **building slots**. Each building takes up 1 slot, including those under construction, and no buildings can be built in a territory that has filled up all its slots. Territories that have more buildings than their number of permitted slots - for instance, after losing population during a sack - will lose any buildings over the limit. [![Image 144: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") Wonders also take up a building slot, and require at least one empty building slot to begin construction.

[![Image 145: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") Settlements normally only have **1** building slot (though settlement buildings are in general stronger than other buildings), which can be increased by the [![Image 146: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**global settlement building slots** modifier to a maximum of **2** by researching the [![Image 147: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Rural Planning [![Image 148: Civic faction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Party_civic.png/28px-Party_civic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senatorial_factions "Civic faction") Civic invention (though each settlement building will still have its territory cap of one). Building slots mostly become relevant when settlements are upgraded to [![Image 149: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities, which gives a base of **2** extra building slots and an additional **1** for every [![Image 150: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population")**10** pops in the city. If a city is further upgraded to a [![Image 151: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis the base number of building slots is increased to **4**. Other important sources of building slots in [![Image 152: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 153: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises, given by the [![Image 154: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**local city building slots** and [![Image 155: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**global city building slots** modifier, include:

## Great wonders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Great wonders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Great wonders")]

_Main article: [Great wonders](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Great\_wonders "Great wonders")_

[![Image 156: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder")**Great wonders** are special buildings that can be constructed in territories and give powerful bonuses to their local [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") or owner. Unlike standard buildings, wonders can be customized with various designs, materials, and effects, and are a significant investment that only the large and wealthy empires will be able to afford. As the centerpiece and defining feature of the local area, each territory can normally only support one great wonder, though certain wonders that are built by event can coexist with each other in the same territory. Uniquely, wonders usually give strong nation-wide effects that grow over time as the wonder's fame and prestige increases.

At the start of the game, there are a number of ancient wonders scattered all over the map, from the Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus in Rome to the Great Pyramids of Giza and the University of Taxila. Building additional wonders requires the **[Heirs of Alexander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heirs_of_Alexander "Heirs of Alexander")** DLC.

## Roads[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Roads") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Roads")]

[![Image 157](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Roads.png/300px-Roads.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Roads.png)

Roads built by a Roman army.

[![Image 158: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road "Road")**Roads** can be built by [![Image 159: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") armies to connect two adjacent [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") at the cost of [![Image 160: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, reducing the [![Image 161: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") movement cost by **50%** and effectively doubling the army movement speed between them, making them highly desirable for larger empires that would benefit from being able to move armies between their provinces more quickly. In addition to the army movement bonuses, connecting a territory to the road network also gives a minor boost to development, [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade"), and pop mobility, making them useful to build in highly populated developed territories as well as connecting frontier areas. Roads also appear graphically on the map as the network is built and extended, generally being most prominent on the terrain mapmode; if a road is built over a river, a bridge will appear. Unlike with buildings, roads are not associated with individual territories, but instead with pairs of adjacent territories (including possibly those connected over a strait); it is therefore possible (and common) for a territory to be a part of multiple different road segments. For every road connecting a territory to one of its adjacent territories, a territory will get the following stackable modifiers:

There are two ways to build roads - a special, cheaper method for countries with access to [![Image 162: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[Italic tradition groups](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions"), and more generic one that is available to all countries. Once a road is built, it cannot be removed or destroyed, even if the territories it connects are completely depopulated.

### Regular roads[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regular roads") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regular roads")]

Most countries need to reach at least [![Image 163: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Level **5** of [civic advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Civic_advances "Technology") to begin building roads.

Roads can be built using the [![Image 164: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road")**Build Road** unit ability toggle, which can be activated if all of the following conditions are met:

Any type of army, even [![Image 165: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies and [![Image 166: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries, can build roads, if they meet all of the requirements.

When the [![Image 167: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") build road toggle is on, road construction will begin when the unit moves from one territory to another, as long as both territories either owned and controlled by the army's owner or one of their [![Image 168: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subjects, or are uncolonized (including [uninhabitable](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Uninhabitable "Uninhabitable") territories, though not [impassable terrain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Impassable_terrain "Impassable terrain")). When the army arrives in that other territory, if the two territories do not already have a road between them, the road will be instantly built at a base cost of [![Image 169: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**50** gold. This cost is affected by the [![Image 170: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**build roads cost** modifier, given only by the [![Image 171: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic inventions [![Image 172: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Gromatici ([![Image 173: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**-25%**) and [![Image 174: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") De Architectura ([![Image 175: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road")**-15%**). If the army has at least [![Image 176: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") 1 engineer cohort, the cost of building a road is instead lowered to [![Image 177: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold (not affected by any modifiers).

While[![Image 178: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") building roads, the following modifiers are applied to the army:

The toggle will be turned off if the army becomes ineligible to build roads, or if the army finishes moving to a new territory without a road on the traversed connection and the country does not have enough money to build a road.

### Military roads[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Military roads") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Military roads")]

If a country has access to [![Image 179: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[Roman traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions") (in the Italic traditions group), adopting the **Roman Roads** tradition will give access to the special [![Image 180: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road")**Build Military Road** toggle, even if it does not currently meet the technology requirements for building roads normally. [![Image 181: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") Building military roads is largely the same as building regular roads, with the main differences that the road-building army can be smaller, requiring only [![Image 182: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army")**5**[cohorts](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"), but must be a [![Image 183: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion, and that each road costs only [![Image 184: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Cost")**25** gold per territory connection. However, note that the cost is **not** affected by the [![Image 185: Build roads cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Build_roads_cost_modifier.png/28px-Build_roads_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road#Regular_roads "Road") build roads cost modifier. As with normal road building, the cost is lowered to to [![Image 186: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold (also not affected by any modifiers) if the army has at least [![Image 187: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") 1 engineer cohort.

In addition, a reduced set of modifiers is applied to the road-building army, without the increased maintenance cost or morale penalty:

*   [![Image 188: Army movement without roads](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Movement_speed_if_no_road.png/28px-Movement_speed_if_no_road.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Army movement without roads")**−70%** Army movement without roads

## Forts[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Forts") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Forts")]

_See also: [Siege](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege")_

[![Image 189: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort")**Forts** can be built in territories to block the advance of enemy armies and protect important cities and strategic points from being easily captured. The size of a fort is determined by its **fort level**, where **1** is the minimum and 0 indicates the lack of a fort, and every [![Image 190: Fortress](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Building_fortress.png/28px-Building_fortress.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Fortress "Fortress") fortress building giving **1** fort level. This means that settlements are typically restricted to a fort level of 1, while cities and metropolises are able to support larger forts. The higher the fort level, the harder it is for an enemy army to successfully take the fort.

To fully occupy a province, the attacker must besiege its [![Image 191: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") capital and any forts in the province, after which the rest will be automatically occupied after a few days. An unprotected territory takes only 15 days to occupy, while one with a fort will take multiple months to siege down unless the attacking army launches an [![Image 192: Assault ability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Assault_ability.png/28px-Assault_ability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assault "Assault") assault (usually taking heavy casualties). Each fort level gives **-1** to siege progress and provides the base [![Image 193: Garrison](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/52/Garrison.png/28px-Garrison.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Garrison")**garrison** of **500** per fort level, adjusted by the **garrison size** modifier. A besieging army needs to have at least **2000** men per each fort level to begin a siege, no matter how big the garrison is. Assaulting a fort will damage the garrison, and as soon as it reaches 0, the fort will fall. When it falls (or a new fort level is built), its garrison will start replenishing at a rate of **10%** of the max garrison per month.

In addition, forts exert a **zone of control** (ZoC) over adjacent territories. An army that enters a hostile zone of control will not be able to progress further, and must either proceed to the fort and besiege it or return to the territory they came from. [![Image 194: Flag of Barbarians](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/16/Barbarians.png/20px-Barbarians.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians")[Barbarians](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") ignore all zones of control and can move freely, but territories in the zone of control of a friendly fort will not lose [![Image 195: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value if occupied. Friendly forts also negate [![Image 196: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition from deserts or winter, and will automatically occupy any enemy-controlled territories in its zone of control unless there is a hostile army there.

Every [![Image 197: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort level costs a base of [![Image 198: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**0.40** gold in maintenance each month, adjusted by the [![Image 199: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**fort maintenance** modifier, except for the first capital fort.

Every province has a certain [![Image 200: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort")**fort infrastructure capacity** value, with default capacity of **5**. The first fort in a territory uses up **3** points of capacity, while subsequent fort levels only use **1**, and exceeding the limit gives a [![Image 201: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance")**+25%** maintenance modifier for all forts in the province by for every point over the limit. This means, with the default limit of 5, two level 1 forts in one province would use 6 points and cost ![Image 202: {\displaystyle 0.4*1.25=0.5}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/ea3fac2d9eb930355174274c4ad79b1916c6f7b3) each per month in maintenance ([![Image 203: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") 1 in total), while a single level 2 fort costs [![Image 204: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") 0.8 gold per month to maintain. Capacity can be increased through the [![Image 205: Military province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Military_investment.png/28px-Military_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Military province investment") Fortify Province province investment, several [![Image 206: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, some [![Image 207: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, and a [![Image 208: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonder effect.

Every territory in the province gets [![Image 209: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion")**-0.25** unrest for every used fort infrastructure point, which means spreading forts out over a number of territories is much more efficient at suppressing unrest than concentrating them in a single territory, even if more expensive. As maintaining forts can be costly, it is often a good idea to remove extraneous forts that no longer serve any meaningful defensive and unrest-suppressing purpose.

### Border forts[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Border forts") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Border forts")]

_See also: [Colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization")_

A country that has adopted the [![Image 210: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Castra** military tradition from the [Italic tribe traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions "Italic traditions") tree can have an army build a [![Image 211: Construct border fort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a2/Ability_border_forts.png/28px-Ability_border_forts.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Construct border fort")**border fort** in an owned frontier or neighbouring uncolonized territory. An army can do this action if it meets all the following conditions:

When the ability is activated, the country loses [![Image 212: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**3000** manpower and builds a [![Image 213: Fortress](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Building_fortress.png/28px-Building_fortress.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Fortress "Fortress") fortress in the territory. The country then gains ownership of the province if it was previously uncolonized, in which case the territory will also gain a [![Image 214: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman pop of the primary culture and religion. Finally, one random cohort becomes [![Image 215: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to the commander, unless the general is the ruler of a non-republic, in which case the country gains [![Image 216: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**1** tyranny instead. This ability can notably be used to [colonize](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization") territories even if there is no neighbouring territory with a dominant integrated culture and state religion.

## Ports[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Ports") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Ports")]

[![Image 217: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port")**Ports** are buildings that can be built in any [![Image 218: Coastal Sea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Terrain_coastal.png/28px-Terrain_coastal.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Coastal Sea") coastal territories, as well as certain inland territories on [![Image 219: River](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Terrain_river.png/28px-Terrain_river.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "River") major rivers (those that begin the game with a [![Image 220: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port). They allow [![Image 221: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ship "Ship") ships to dock (including quickly embarking and disembarking troops), be built, repair, and supply, and are important for any nation that seeks to maintain a significant navy. The number of ports in a territory is the territory's **port level**, which in addition to the effects from the port buildings themselves also determines what types of ships it can build and repair: the light [![Image 222: Unit liburnian.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Unit_liburnian.png/28px-Unit_liburnian.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Liburnian "Liburnian") liburnians and [![Image 223: Unit trireme.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d5/Unit_trireme.png/28px-Unit_trireme.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trireme "Trireme") triremes only require a level 1 port, the medium [![Image 224: Unit tetrere.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Unit_tetrere.png/28px-Unit_tetrere.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tetrere "Tetrere") tetreres and [![Image 225: Unit hexere.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/87/Unit_hexere.png/28px-Unit_hexere.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Hexere "Hexere") hexeres require at least level 3 ports, while the very heavy [![Image 226: Unit octere.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a1/Unit_octere.png/28px-Unit_octere.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Octere "Octere") octeres and [![Image 227: Unit mega-polyreme.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/27/Unit_mega-polyreme.png/28px-Unit_mega-polyreme.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mega-polyreme "Mega-polyreme") mega-polyremes can only be built and repaired at ports that are at least level 5.

In addition to effects from the building itself, every [![Image 228: Coastal Sea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Terrain_coastal.png/28px-Terrain_coastal.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Coastal Sea") coastal territory with at least one port gets the following modifiers:

For each territory in a province that has at least one port, the province also gets the following modifier:

*   [![Image 229: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed")**+2.5%** Pop Migration Speed

Note that neither of these effects stack if territory has more than one port.

### Pirate havens[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Pirate havens") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Infrastructure&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Pirate havens")]

Certain ports are home to [![Image 230: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate")**pirate havens** where groups of pirates are based from. These pirate havens are present in certain ports at the beginning of the game, and in addition to giving an economic boost of [![Image 231: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**+20%** Local Tax and [![Image 232: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**+0.01%** Local Population Growth to their territory they host **pirate [fleets](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy")**, always composed of all light ships, which can rented out as naval mercenaries at times of war. When not being hired pirate fleets will eventually grow restless and go on raids, at which point the fleet while become hostile to all other navies and travel to nearby unfortified ports to **plunder** them, giving [![Image 233: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**-25%** Local Tax and [![Image 234: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion")**+10%** Demotion Speed to the plundered territories.

If pirates grow to be too much of a problem, their havens can be destroyed using the [![Image 235: Root out pirates](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4b/Ability_root_out_pirates.png/28px-Ability_root_out_pirates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Root out pirates")**Root Out Pirates** army ability on any controlled territory once the anti-pirate [law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") has been passed, including those belonging to other countries that have been occupied in a [![Image 236: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war. This will remove the modifier (including its beneficial modifiers) and destroy the associated pirate fleet, even if it is currently out on a raid. Alternatively, pirates can be actively encouraged by passing the pro-pirate law, which will create a new [![Image 237: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate") pirate haven in one of the country's ports to increase its prosperity and provide more available naval mercenaries at the expense of the nearby coasts.

Destroying all ports in a pirate haven will also force any local pirate/mercenary fleets to disband, though this will not remove the pirate haven itself. If the port is ever rebuilt, the pirate fleet will eventually reappear.
