![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/bvd-KC7U67E/hqdefault.jpg)

Characters and Inventions Revisited

Load video

Imperator: Rome - Characters & Inventions (inventions section starts at **4:36**).

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Technology_screen.png/700px-Technology_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Technology_screen.png)

The main technology interface

[![Image 3: Menu technology.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Menu_technology.png/28px-Menu_technology.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")[Technology](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Research_points) represents the discovery, improvement, and implementation of new tools, techniques, and customs that give a wide variety of powerful bonuses across every field. Technologically advanced [nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State "State") will field a more effective military, make more efficient use of their pops and resources, and be able to create a more stable and loyal realm, while those that fall behind will find it increasingly difficult to keep up with their peers. All technologies fall into one of four fields - [![Image 4: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") martial, [![Image 5: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") civic, [![Image 6: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") oratory and [![Image 7: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") religious - and can be divided between generic **advances** that gain progress from generation of [![Image 8: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**research points** by [![Image 9: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 10: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, and the more specific [![Image 11: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")**inventions** that can be adopted by spending the [![Image 12: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovations that advances grant. The main technology interface can be accessed though the [![Image 13: Menu technology.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Menu_technology.png/28px-Menu_technology.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**technology** tab in the side menu bar.

## Advances[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Advances") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Advances")]

**Advances** represent the general level of knowledge and technology that the country has reached. They provide a baseline level of modifiers from increased technology and each advance grants [![Image 14: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") 1 innovation that can be used to unlock an [invention](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"). There is no cap in the maximum level of advances that can be achieved, though ahead-of-time penalties will effectively give a soft cap to the level of advances that is realistically possible to achieve. Advances are slowly researched over time based on the amount of [![Image 15: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points that a country produces in proportion to its [![Image 16: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture population, subject to many different modifiers that can further increase or decrease the speed at which research progress is accumulated.

### Starting advances level[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Starting advances level") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Starting advances level")]

Each country starts with a predetermined level of technological advances, usually determined by their culture and civilization level, along with a number of unused [![Image 17: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovations corresponding to its starting advances level (if applicable).

The following countries/cultures start with all advances at level **2** (with [![Image 18: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")**8** starting innovations):

The following countries/cultures start with all advances at level **1** (with [![Image 19: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")**4** starting innovations):

All other countries start with no advances (level **0**) or innovations.

## Research[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Research") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Research")]

### Research points[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Research points") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Research points")]

[![Image 20: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**Research points** represent the contribution of pops towards the technological and intellectual advancement of the country, and are summed up over every pop in every owned territory every month to get the total research points for the state. Research points are generated exclusively by [![Image 21: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 22: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, who produce a base output of **0.5** and **0.2** research points, respectively, each month at [![Image 23: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") 100% happiness. Therefore, research point production is also affected by [![Image 24: Noble output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Pop_noble_output.png/28px-Pop_noble_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Noble output") noble output and [![Image 25: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output") citizen output as well as [![Image 26: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research point modifiers, and is also heavily dependent on the [![Image 27: Noble ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9e/Pop_noble_ratio.png/28px-Pop_noble_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Noble ratio")[![Image 28: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") proportion and [![Image 29: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness")[![Image 30: Citizen happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") happiness of nobles and citizens in the country. This means that in addition to directly maximizing research point production (such as by building [![Image 31: Library](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Building_library.png/28px-Building_library.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Library "Library") libraries and increasing overall [![Image 32: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") population output with [![Image 33: Foundry](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Building_foundry.png/28px-Building_foundry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Foundry "Foundry") foundries and raising [![Image 34: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization level), increasing [![Image 35: Noble ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9e/Pop_noble_ratio.png/28px-Pop_noble_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Noble ratio") noble ratio and [![Image 36: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") citizen ratio directly through laws and [![Image 37: Academy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Building_academy.png/28px-Building_academy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Academy "Academy") academies and [![Image 38: Court of Law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Building_court.png/28px-Building_court.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Court_of_Law "Court of Law") courts of law or indirectly by building more and larger [![Image 39: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City")[cities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cities "Cities") (as nobles and citizens do not exist in appreciable numbers in settlements) are also an effective longer-term solution towards increasing research point production. Large cities and [metropolises](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") are usually the largest source of research points in the middle to late game, as the stacking effects of [![Image 40: Academy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Building_academy.png/28px-Building_academy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Academy "Academy") academies and [![Image 41: Court of Law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Building_court.png/28px-Building_court.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Court_of_Law "Court of Law") courts of law as well as general [![Image 42: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization level increases from buildings means that they usually have both the highest proportion of nobles and citizens and the highest happiness and research point production per noble/citizen, though population capacity means that larger empires will still need a significant number of cities to produce enough research.

The following inventions give percentage increases to the amount of research points produced:

### Monthly research[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Monthly research") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Monthly research")]

**Monthly research** is the base value of research progress for all four fields and is obtained by dividing research efficiency by **192**, corresponding to a base time of 192 months (16 years) for each advance. The baseline research efficiency cap of 125% initially caps monthly research at **0.65%**, with the maximum possible research efficiency of 250% giving a highest possible base monthly research of **1.30%**.

### Research progress[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Research progress") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Research progress")]

**Research progress** is the actual monthly progress in each field of advances. The base value is the monthly research, which is then modified by the field-specific **research speed** modifier. The [![Image 43: Technology speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ed/Technology_speed.png/28px-Technology_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology speed")**technology speed** modifier affects the research speed in all fields, while the field-specific technology modifiers ([![Image 44: Military tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tech_investment.png/28px-Military_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")**military tech investment**, [![Image 45: Civic tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Civic_tech_investment.png/28px-Civic_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")**civic tech investment**, [![Image 46: Oratory tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Oratory_tech_investment.png/28px-Oratory_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")**oratory tech investment**, and [![Image 47: Religious tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e3/Religious_tech_investment.png/28px-Religious_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")**religious tech investment**) are applied only to the research speed of one specific field. Modifiers to research speed can come from various [deity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") bonuses and omens, country [heritages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritages "Heritages"), [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods"), and event/mission modifiers, but the main component of research speed for each field will typically be the attribute of the field's researcher corresponding to the field. The behind/ahead of time modifier is applied multiplicatively after all the other modifiers, and gives a **-0.75%** malus for each year ahead and **+0.75%** bonus for each year behind the country is compared to the benchmark of 1 advance every **16** years after the start of the game. Certain events can also directly increase the amount of research progress accumulated in a particular field.

An advance will be researched once the total research progress for that field reaches **100%**, with any overflow carried over for the next advance. The progress and effects (including unlocked inventions) of the next advance in each field can be viewed in the tooltip of the corresponding progress bar.

### Research efficiency[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Research efficiency") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Research efficiency")]

**Research efficiency** is the ratio of research points produced per year to [![Image 48: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture population and the main component of the actual research progress each month, meaning that larger countries generally need higher research point generation in order to keep up with research. It is calculated according to the following formula:

![Image 49: {\displaystyle {\text{Research efficiency}}={\frac {{\text{Research Points}}\cdot 12}{\text{Integrated Culture Population}}}\cdot 100\%}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/2fecfacf5a2b7c96761cc8b4b0ec860ff50bc86b)
Generally, it is desirable to keep research efficiency at at least 80% in order to research advances at a reasonable pace. Research efficiency has a baseline cap of **125%**, modified by the [![Image 50: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**maximum research efficiency** modifier. Each of the following inventions gives [![Image 51: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**+25%** maximum research efficiency, giving a maximum of **250%** research efficiency if all of them are adopted:

Even though research efficiency is capped, it can still be useful to try to push the research points to population ratio higher in order to provide a buffer when assimilating pops or integrating new territory. Small, highly urbanized city-states will generally have a much higher research efficiency than sprawling empires with many rural pops, though it is possible to have a high research efficiency even with the largest empires so long as the major cities are sufficiently large and research-focused, of which growth through [enslavement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement"), [migration](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration"), and continued [![Image 52: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") urbanization will typically be needed to keep up.

As the denominator of research efficiency is based on the [![Image 53: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture population, not the total population, it is also possible to get high research efficiency by keeping a relatively small core of integrated culture pops compared to the empire's total output - especially useful is conquering foreign areas with significant numbers of [![Image 54: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 55: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, as they will produce [![Image 56: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points but not count against the denominator for research efficiency. However, the lower happiness of unintegrated culture pops, and particularly those of the higher classes that produce research, generally means that more care has to be taken to ensure their [![Image 57: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness and keep [![Image 58: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest under control compared to integrating them.

### Researchers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Researchers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Researchers")]

Each technology field can have one **researcher** assigned to it, who leads the research efforts in that field and has a significant influence on how quickly advances in that field progress, giving a **+10%** research speed boost for each attribute point corresponding to the field ([![Image 59: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial for military advances, [![Image 60: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse for civic advances, [![Image 61: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") charisma for oratory advances, and [![Image 62: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal for religious advances). Like all other jobs and offices, holding the researcher position counts towards [![Image 63: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence, [![Image 64: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, [![Image 65: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base, and the [![Image 66: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") number of positions that a major family expects to hold.

## Fields[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Fields") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Fields")]

The modifiers for a single level of advances in each field are listed below. Each level provides an additional modifier on top of the previous ones already accumulated.

### [![Image 67: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") Martial Advances[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Martial Advances") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Martial Advances")]

### [![Image 68: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Civic Advances") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Civic Advances")]

### [![Image 69: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") Oratory Advances[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Oratory Advances") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Oratory Advances")]

*   [![Image 70: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**+0.5%** Country Civilization Value

### [![Image 71: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") Religious Advances[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Religious Advances") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Religious Advances")]

*   [![Image 72: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion")**+1%** Omen Power

## Inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Inventions")]

_Main article: [Invention](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invention "Invention")_

[![Image 73: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")**Inventions** represent specific technologies or practices that can be adopted by the state in order to get a specific military, economic, or political effects and bonuses. Every invention is associated with a specific field of advances and is structured as part of a tree, with most inventions requiring prerequisites that must be adopted first before the next invention can be taken. Inventions are adopted by spending [![Image 74: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation")**innovations**, gained primarily from technological advances (which grant one innovation for each level) as well as from a few [![Image 75: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, [![Image 76: Menu mission.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Menu_mission.png/28px-Menu_mission.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") mission tasks, and events.

## References[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: References") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Technology&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: References")]


============================================================
# Researcher
============================================================
Title: Position - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Researcher

Published Time: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 00:49:48 GMT

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position")[Positions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Researcher) consist of the major jobs and offices involved in the command and administration of a country and its military. They are filled by the [characters](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters "Characters") of a realm and are one of the main mechanics by which the country and its characters interact, as not only the means by which characters can contribute their skills and attributes towards the betterment of the realm but also the most important source and easily controlled source of [![Image 2: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base and [![Image 3: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. Proper character and position management requires a fine balance between choosing office holders who are the best and most competent at their jobs and the requirements of family loyalty and realm stability.

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

[![Image 4](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5a/General_appointement_screen.png/500px-General_appointement_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:General_appointement_screen.png)

Appointing a new general

Most positions are filled by appointment and can generally be filled and dismissed freely by the player. Appointing a character to a position gives the loyalty modifier **Granted Office**, increasing [![Image 5: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty by **+15** for **5** years, while revoking an office gives the **Revoked Offices** modifier decreasing [![Image 6: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty by **-5** for **2.5** years. In general, a character can only hold one position at any given time, with the exception of certain ruler-related positions, and cannot be dismissed or replaced from their position if they become unloyal. Only adult citizens can be appointed to a position, with any gender restrictions also applied if relevant to the country.

Characters in positions are usually paid a certain percentage of the country's [![Image 7: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income as their [![Image 8: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**wage**, which is by modified country-wide with the [![Image 9: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**Monthly Wages for Characters** modifier and per character by the [![Image 10: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**Monthly Wage** modifier. Notably, each point of a character's [![Image 11: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption increases the wage paid by **2.5%**, which can make wages unmanageably large if allowed to spiral out of control. Depending on the exact position, position holders may also get a certain amount of [![Image 12: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, [![Image 13: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence, [![Image 14: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, and/or [![Image 15: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship each month. All positions also give their holder [![Image 16: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+2** power base, which depending on the position can then be increased based on the size of armies and navies commanded or regions governed.

Each major [family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family "Family") will expect that their members hold a certain proportion of all currently held assignable positions, calculated as:

![Image 17: {\displaystyle {\text{Expected Positions}}={\text{round}}\left({\frac {{\text{Total Number of Positions}}\cdot 0.7}{\text{Number of Major Families}}}\right)}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/d48c721cedd8d1885e1898abc3db27deb33d370c)
This includes positions such as government offices, governors, generals, and researchers, but not those that are chosen or elected outside of direct player control such as rulers or party leaders.

Any family that does not hold its expected number of positions is considered [![Image 18: Scorned family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Scorned_family.png/28px-Scorned_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Scorned family")**scorned** and receives the following stacking modifiers to all its members for each missing expected position:

For instance, a family that expects to get 4 positions but only has 1 will get [![Image 19: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-15** Loyalty, [![Image 20: Party optimates.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/37/Party_optimates.png/28px-Party_optimates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Optimates "Optimates")**-0.06** Monthly Optimates Conviction, and [![Image 21: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs")**-0.06** Monthly Oligarchs Conviction.

A family that instead holds at least **double** the number of expected positions is [![Image 22: Grateful family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/65/Grateful_family.png/28px-Grateful_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Grateful family")**grateful** and receives the following modifiers to all its members:

It is usually important to balance positions between families, even if this results in suboptimal office holders, in order to ensure that families are not scorned. Alternatively, it may also be feasible to completely sideline a family and ensuring that it has no positions and little power base in order to appease the other families and more easily give them enough positions to get the grateful family bonuses.

Here is a table showing the maximum amount of happy families possible per number of families while still meeting the expectations of all other families.:

Total Families Max Happy Families Total Jobs(Job per family breakdown)
5 3 32(8+8+8+4+4)
4 2 24(8+8+4+4)
3 2 18(8+8+4)

This table assumes that you have enough potential employees.

## Ruler[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ruler") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ruler")]

[![Image 23](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/22/Tribal_ruler_interface.png/500px-Tribal_ruler_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Tribal_ruler_interface.png)

The standard interface for the ruler

[![Image 24: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Each state is headed by a **ruler**, prominently visible on the **Government** tab of the [![Image 25: Government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government panel, and their [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes"), and [relationships](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Relationships "Characters") have a significant effect on state modifiers, [![Image 26: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty, and [![Image 27: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Senate approval") Senate support, among other things. Rulers with high attributes can be a considerable boon to their country, significantly strengthening the state and allowing the nation to punch well above its weight, while corrupt, low attribute rulers can inflict major penalties that a state must struggle through. However, depending on the government form, it may be possible to cover weak areas with a co-ruler or consort that has appropriately complementary attributes.

Unlike most positions, rulers are not appointed and dismissed at the whim of the player. The [![Image 28: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession")**succession** of power is instead determined by the type of [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") - dynastic inheritance in a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), Senatorial election in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), and rotation between Clan Chiefs in [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations, with rulers either reigning until death (as in monarchies and tribes) or for a certain fixed term (as in republics). Monarchies and Tribes tend to stay with the same ruler for much longer and so while the nation will enjoy prosperity under great rulers for a much longer period of time they will also have to suffer for extended periods of misrule by poor rulers, while the relatively rapid elections of Republics mean that their ruler attributes are on the whole more even. The lessened level of control over the choice of ruler, particularly in monarchies, means that countries must be prepared to deal with incompetent or problematic rulers more often than with other positions.

The ruler is always considered the governor of the [![Image 29: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region") and thus will [![Image 30: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command any [![Image 31: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy raised in that region, but cannot be appointed to any other position (in particular, rulers cannot ever command [![Image 32: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions or [![Image 33: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") navies). Despite this, rulers do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds. Many events also take the point of view of the ruler, and different options may become available or unavailable based on the ruler's traits and attributes. Rulers of other states are prominently displayed in the Diplomacy screen, and building friends with foreign rulers can be a useful way to improve relations with another country.

Ruler / Co-Ruler Modifiers | Stat/Attribute | Bonuses |
| --- | --- |
| [![Image 34: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") Martial | * [![Image 35: Manpower recovery speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Manpower_recovery_speed.png/28px-Manpower_recovery_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower_recovery_speed "Manpower recovery speed")**+1.00%** Manpower Recovery Speed * [![Image 36: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery")**+1.00%** Army Morale Recovery |
| [![Image 37: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") Finesse | * [![Image 38: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")**-1.00%** Build Cost * [![Image 39: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade")**+2.00%** National Commerce Income |
| [![Image 40: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") Charisma | * [![Image 41: Claim fabrication speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Fabricate_claim_speed.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim#Fabricating_claims "Claim fabrication speed")**+0.20** Claim Fabrication Speed * [![Image 42: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**-0.01** Monthly Tyranny |
| [![Image 43: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") Zeal | * [![Image 44: Monthly war exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "Monthly war exhaustion")**-0.01** Monthly War Exhaustion * [![Image 45: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**+0.01** Monthly Stability Change |
| [![Image 46: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") Popularity | For every point of popularity above **50**: * [![Image 47: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**+0.0025** Monthly Legitimacy * [![Image 48: Monthly tyranny.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_tyranny.png/28px-Monthly_tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_tyranny.png)**-0.001** Monthly Tyranny * [![Image 49: Clan chief loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Monthly_clan_chief_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_clan_chief_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief loyalty")**+0.5** Loyalty of Clan Chiefs For every point of popularity below **50**: * [![Image 50: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-0.10%** Integrated Culture Happiness * [![Image 51: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**-0.005** Monthly Legitimacy * [![Image 52: Clan chief loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Monthly_clan_chief_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_clan_chief_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief loyalty")**-1** Loyalty of Clan Chiefs |
| [![Image 53: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") Corruption | * [![Image 54: Monthly political influence modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f5/Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png)**-0.50%** Monthly Political Influence * [![Image 55: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**-0.001** Monthly Legitimacy * [![Image 56: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed")**+0.05%** Pop Migration Speed |

Note that does not include modifiers on the capital region and levies that a ruler gives by virtue of being the governor of the capital region.

In addition, the ruler gets the following character modifiers:

Note that in addition to the loyalty modifier, a ruler's loyalty is always fixed to [![Image 57: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**100** regardless of any other modifiers.

### Co-Ruler[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Co-Ruler") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Co-Ruler")]

[![Image 58](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Republic_ruler_interface.png/500px-Republic_ruler_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Republic_ruler_interface.png)

The aristocratic republic interface for the ruler, including the co-ruler

[Aristocratic Republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#aristocratic_republic "Government") are distinguished by power-sharing between two rulers who collectively co-head the government, the second of which is represented ingame by the **co-ruler**. Co-rulers are considered equivalent to the ruler for most ingame mechanics, contributing any of their four main attributes that they are more skilled in than the ruler (effectively providing a bonus to ruler attributes), averaging ruler [![Image 59: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity between the ruler and co-ruler, and adding their [![Image 60: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption to that of the ruler for the purposes of country modifiers. Unlike rulers, the co-ruler does not always have [![Image 61: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") 100 loyalty, but instead gives a [![Image 62: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**-1%** political influence gain malus for every point of [![Image 63: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") below **50**. The co-ruler is elected alongside the ruler in each election at the end of the term, with the second place candidate being elected with the same term and requirements as the main ruler. It is possible and common for the two co-rulers to be of different [factions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Faction "Faction").

Like rulers, co-rulers do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds unless they also hold another position, of which they can only be appointed as army commanders (legates, tribunes, generals) and admirals.

Co-rulers get the following character modifiers:

### Consort[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Consort") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Consort")]

[![Image 64](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d1/Monarchy_ruler_interface.png/500px-Monarchy_ruler_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monarchy_ruler_interface.png)

The monarchy interface for the ruler, including the consort

In [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), the ruler can instead be supported by the **consort**, who is the spouse of the ruler. Like co-rulers, consorts will replace a ruler's attributes with their own for the purposes of running the country in any category where they are more skilled, and give a [![Image 65: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**-1%** political influence gain malus for every point of [![Image 66: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty below **50**. It can therefore be very useful to marry rulers and potential heirs to spouses whose attributes can compensate for any weak points in their attributes. Unlike in a co-rulership, a consort's [![Image 67: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity and [![Image 68: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption do not count towards ruler popularity and corruption for the purposes of calculating country modifiers.

Like rulers and co-rulers, consorts do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds unless they also hold another position. However, unlike them, consorts can be assigned to any other position as long as the gender rules allow for it. In addition, by marrying into the royal family, consorts expect their children or descendants will eventually take the throne; if a consort has any living children, they will get a [![Image 69: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-15** loyalty malus if the primary heir is not one of their children or descendants.

Consorts get the following character modifiers:

### Primary Heir[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Primary Heir") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Primary Heir")]

[![Image 70](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/ed/Primary_heir_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Primary_heir_interface.png)

The monarchy interface for the primary heir

A [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") also has a special title for the **primary heir** who is first in line to inherit the throne. There are no mechanics or country modifiers associated with the primary heir, but the heir is still paid a monthly wage and gets bonuses to popularity, prominence, statesmanship, and loyalty. As with consorts, primary heirs do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds unless they also hold another position, but they can be assigned to any other position as long as the gender rules allow for it.

Primary Heirs get the following character modifiers:

Consider giving the primary heir positions that come with a large [![Image 71: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base as they are more loyal than most characters. Examples include a governorship over a large region, or a general commanding many cohorts. A position that gives extra [![Image 72: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, such as command over a [![Image 73: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion, is particularly useful as high popularity increases [![Image 74: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession") succession support for the heir and reduces the [![Image 75: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty loss on succession.

## Government offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government offices")]

[![Image 76](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Republic_Rome_government_offices_screen.png/500px-Republic_Rome_government_offices_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Republic_Rome_government_offices_screen.png)

The government offices interface

Every country has a set of **eight** major **government offices** that can be filled, representing important posts in the administration of the country. Every office is associated with a particular country modifier, the strength of which depends on the **effective rating** of the office holder. The effective rating is determined by multiplying the attribute of the office holder that is associated with the office by the holder's [![Image 77: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")[statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship"), which means that more experienced characters may be more effective at an office than a younger neophyte even if their innate skill is not as high. Furthermore, there are [various](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Architect_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Architect events (page does not exist)")[events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Stability_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stability events (page does not exist)") that can fire regularly affecting [![Image 78: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") ruler popularity and [![Image 79: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability depending on the [![Image 80: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship of various office holders. This means that maintaining an officer for longer periods of time is more effective than constantly switching them out for newer characters with better attributes (but lower statesmanship), and as the main source of increasing statesmanship is itself being employed in an office this encourages appointing new up-and-coming characters to less important posts (including other offices but also research and governor posts, as their effectiveness does not depend on statesmanship) first in order to build up their experience and statesmanship before promoting them to higher and more crucial offices. Office holders also get bonuses to [![Image 81: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence and [![Image 82: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, which makes them more favoured to be elected to ruling office.

Government offices are also the main source of [![Image 83: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") monthly political influence generation. Every office holder generates [![Image 84: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**+0.25** monthly political influence, scaled by their [![Image 85: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty as a fraction out of 100; this means that filling offices highly loyal officers will ensure a high level of political influence to use on all sorts of different projects, while a administration that is constantly working to block and undermine itself will find it difficult to gain the capital to do anything at all. Therefore, while officers do not have the increased power base of governors or generals that makes maintaining their loyalty important for avoiding civil wars, it is still important to keep an eye on the loyalty of office holders to ensure an adequate supply of political power for government actions.

With the exception of the consort and primary heir positions, government offices are mutually exclusive with all other positions. Office holders can generally be freely appointed and dismissed, but like all other appointed positions officers will refuse to leave their post if they are unloyal.

The offices available to a country depend on the government type. All governments have an office dedicated to [![Image 86: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion change, [![Image 87: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience, [![Image 88: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") tax income, [![Image 89: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omen power, and [![Image 90: Health.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Health.png/28px-Health.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Health "Health") health, while the other three vary, but usually have an office that affects happiness directly or indirectly, a second office with military bonuses, and a modifier for a government-specific mechanic. Note that only the generic names are given below; many cultures have unique localisations for each of the offices.

### Republic offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Republic offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Republic offices")]

In addition to the usual three offices, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") have offices for [![Image 91: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval") party approval, [![Image 92: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods") divine sacrifice cost, and [![Image 93: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change.

### Monarchy offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Monarchy offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Monarchy offices")]

The unique [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") offices give modifiers for [![Image 154: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy, a military bonus for [![Image 155: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance") mercenary army maintenance, as well as [![Image 156: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change, but have a weaker [![Image 157: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifier office than other government types.

| Office | Attribute | State Modifier (per effective attribute) | Character Modifiers | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### Tribal offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Tribal offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Tribal offices")]

[Tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") offices give a military bonus in [![Image 214: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower recovery speed and have happiness modifiers for [![Image 215: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen and [![Image 216: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens. Notably, their [![Image 217: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion office is weaker than in republics or monarchies, which is compensated for by a stronger [![Image 218: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifier.
