| [Subject nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") | [![Image 173: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations")[Mercenary state](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_state "Mercenary state") | Mercenary Army Maintenance: **-3%** per [![Image 174: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations")[mercenary state](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_state "Mercenary state") |
| [Country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank") | _see_[country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank") |  |

## Commanders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Commanders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Commanders")]

_See also: [Position#Army commanders](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Army\_commanders "Position")_

Most armies are led by a [![Image 175: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander")**commander** that organizes and leads the army at a tactical level and can give various bonuses - and maluses - to its ability to fight, maneuver, and manage its logistics. The most important attribute for a commander is their [![Image 176: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial skill; for each point of its commander's martial, an army will gain [![Image 177: Assault ability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Assault_ability.png/28px-Assault_ability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assault "Assault")**+5%** assault ability, [![Image 178: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")**+1%** enslavement efficiency, and [![Image 179: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")**+0.10** cohort loyalty gain chance as well as a bonus to dice rolls in combat, allowing well-lead armies to punch significantly above their weight in [combat](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare"). An army without a commander will instead get a [![Image 180: Land morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b8/Land_morale.png/28px-Land_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Land_morale.png)**-15%** land morale and [![Image 181: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/28px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay")**+1%** experience decay modifier, making them very vulnerable to a properly led army; even a weak, low martial commander is much better than not having a commander at all. A commander's [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), particularly any command traits, can also have effects on [![Image 182: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") discipline, [![Image 183: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale, [![Image 184: Reinforcement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Reinforcement_speed.png/28px-Reinforcement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Reinforcement "Reinforcement") reinforcement speed, and more.

### Assigning commanders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Assigning commanders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Assigning commanders")]

The commander of a [![Image 185: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy is usually the [![Image 186: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governor of the region the levy was raised from (even if the levy is split into multiple units, the governor will remain in command of all of them); as governors also have an important role in the administration of the region, this often requires balancing between a potential governor's [![Image 187: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse skill for administering the region and increasing its revenues and [![Image 188: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial for leading levied armies during times of war, at least for heavily levy-dependent countries. In [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") countries, levies from all regions are instead split between the control of the [![Image 189: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs. Being more specialized, each [![Image 190: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion can instead have up to **4** dedicated commanders that can be assigned to legion, consisting of 1 **Legate** (who will be the legion's default commander) and up to 3 **Tribunes**. Each legion commander can only lead a single army of the legion at a time, but can be freely swapped out for another one of the legion's commanders (though this gives a temporary [![Image 191: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-5** loyalty malus). Like legions, [![Image 192: Start migration cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration") migratory armies can simply have any character that does not already hold a [![Image 193: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") position appointed to be their commander, and [![Image 194: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries come with their own commander when hired that cannot be replaced.

### Loyalty[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Loyalty") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Loyalty")]

_See also: [Cohort loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort\_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")_

As the army's leader, a commander exerts a great degree of control over their commanded armies, which has significant implications for [![Image 195: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. The commander of a raised army will get [![Image 196: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+0.3** power base for every percentage of the country's currently raised armies (including raised [![Image 197: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, but not including unraised levies) they command, up to a maximum of [![Image 198: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+1.2** power base per cohort. Over time, and particularly when fighting in battles, each cohort will also have a chance to become [![Image 199: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to its commander, scaling based on the general's [![Image 200: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") charisma, [![Image 201: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial, [![Image 202: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") wealth, [![Image 203: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, number of already loyal cohorts, and [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"). A loyal cohort is tied even more closely to its sponsor than its commander, with their sponsor paying **33%** of their maintenance (if relevant) and giving an even larger [![Image 204: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base bonus ([![Image 205: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+1** power base for each percentage of the country's army which is loyal to a character - even if that character is no longer the cohort's commander). If a loyal cohort is disbanded, they will become loyal [veterans](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Veteran "Veteran") attached to their sponsor and continue to provide a smaller amount of [![Image 206: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base. In addition to power base, both loyal cohorts and veterans also increase a character's [![Image 207: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, [![Image 208: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, [![Image 209: Change governor policy cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Change_governor_policy_cost.png/28px-Change_governor_policy_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Successors "Government") attraction as heir, and [![Image 210: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value") succession value - the last two particularly important for [![Image 211: Leader pretender.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/11/Leader_pretender.png/28px-Leader_pretender.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pretender "Pretender") pretenders.

Note that a cohort can only give [![Image 212: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base to a character once - if a cohort is loyal to a character who is also its current commander, that cohort will only count towards the proportion of loyal cohorts the character has, not commanded. This typically makes commanders one of the more powerful characters in a country, and [![Image 213: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty can become a significant issue if they are already predisposed to become disloyal, whether through particular [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits") or from a pre-existing [![Image 214: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base. Unless a character is very loyal, it is generally dangerous to allow a single character to take command over a large proportion of a country's army (except for the [![Image 215: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler, generally).

If an army has a [![Image 216: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") disloyal commander, like other office holders they will refuse to give up their command (including any attempts to reorganize or disband the army) or follow any orders from the government. They will instead march around and (if relevant) engage in battles and sieges of their own accord, typically not actively sabotaging a country's war efforts but generally following their own interests and refusing to coordinate with other armies. This will usually significant decrease the overall effectiveness of the army in the war effort and can be especially problematic if at war with a dangerous enemy where focus and coordination is key. While a single disloyal commander is generally not a significant threat, if a [![Image 217: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war breaks out they will join the opposing side, along with all the cohorts under their command and turning their army against the country. Even if they are no longer a commander, characters siding with the rebellion will also bring along any [![Image 218: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal cohorts and veterans. A threatening commander can be removed from their post (including their governorship, if they are a governor) at any time using the **Hold Triumph** interaction if they have won a battle in the last 2 years at the cost of [![Image 219: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**30** political power, which can be useful for dealing with disloyal commanders (note that this only applies for appointed positions - the ruler cannot be given a triumph to remove them from the capital levy, and similarly [![Image 220: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs cannot be removed from their tribal retinues this way).

### Other effects[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Other effects") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Other effects")]

Commanders can gain [![Image 221: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") wealth and [![Image 222: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity from winning battles and sacking cities and capitals; the latter is particularly useful with armies under the command of the ruler, as the loot is instead accrued directly to the [![Image 223: Treasury.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7e/Treasury.png/28px-Treasury.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy") treasury and [![Image 224: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity can easily be converted into [![Image 225: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence using the [influence character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Interactions "Characters") interaction. Commanders also gain [![Image 226: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") monthly family prestige based on the proportion of the country's army that are currently under their command, which altogether means that particularly successful commanders are often strong-performing candidates in [republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") elections, though the needs of being away from the capital to campaign means that they have a vastly reduced [![Image 227: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence.

## Reinforcement[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Reinforcement") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Reinforcement")]

Cohorts that have taken casualties through [combat](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare") or [![Image 228: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition can restore their [![Image 229: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength by drawing from a country's [![Image 230: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower pool through **reinforcement**. As long as there is [![Image 231: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower available, every cohort in an army can reinforce a certain baseline percentage of its total (maximum) strength each month depending on its circumstances:

*   **+10%** (**+50**) per month in owned territories
*   **+5%** (**+25**) per month if in enemy or uncolonized territory but adjacent to an owned territory, in occupied territory, or in neutral territory
*   **+3.75%** (**+18**) per month if in enemy territory but adjacent to an occupied (but not owned) territory
*   **+2.5%** (**+12**) per month for migratory armies in uncolonized territory
*   **+0.5%** (**+2**) per month if otherwise in enemy territory, or for normal armies in an uncolonized territory that is not adjacent to an owned territory

The rate of reinforcement is then further modified by the country's, [![Image 232: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander's, and army's [![Image 233: Reinforcement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Reinforcement_speed.png/28px-Reinforcement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Reinforcement "Reinforcement")**reinforcement speed** modifier. Reinforcing armies do not cost any extra [![Image 234: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, only [![Image 235: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower; if a country does not have enough [![Image 236: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower to meet all of that month's reinforcement needs, only some cohorts will be able to be reinforced that month.

## Attrition[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Attrition") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Attrition")]

_See also: [Supply limit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply\_limit "Supply limit"), [Food#Army supply](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food#Army\_supply "Food")_

[![Image 237: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition")**Attrition** is the loss of troops from non-combat related reasons, whether from disease, starvation, or simply desertion, and is a constant factor to keep in mind in warfare. Armies moving through hostile territory, or in general areas unable to properly support their needs, may suffer as many casualties as those directly involved in combat.

The amount of food and other resources an army needs is represented by its [![Image 238: Army weight modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Army_weight_modifier.png/28px-Army_weight_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army_weight_modifier "Army weight modifier")**unit weight**, which is the sum of the weights of each individual cohorts; each cohort has a base unit weight of **1**, which is then adjusted by the **Weight for Supply Limits** value for its cohort type (less for light units such as [![Image 239: Unit light infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Unit_light_infantry.png/28px-Unit_light_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Infantry "Light Infantry") light infantry and [![Image 240: Unit archers.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8e/Unit_archers.png/28px-Unit_archers.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Archers "Archers") archers and more for heavier units such as [![Image 241: Unit heavy infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Unit_heavy_infantry.png/28px-Unit_heavy_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heavy_Infantry "Heavy Infantry") heavy infantry and [![Image 242: Unit war elephants.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Unit_war_elephants.png/28px-Unit_war_elephants.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_Elephants "War Elephants") war elephants), which is then further modified by the [![Image 243: Army weight modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Army_weight_modifier.png/28px-Army_weight_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army_weight_modifier "Army weight modifier")**army weight modifier**. The army will then take **1** point of [![Image 244: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition for every point of unit weight it has above the [![Image 245: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit of the territory it is currently in.

Some territories may also have a certain amount of [![Image 246: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition")**base attrition**, which is added onto any attrition from being over the supply limit (even if the army is not taking any attrition otherwise). Base attrition is given mostly by [![Image 247: Desert](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Terrain_desert.png/28px-Terrain_desert.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Desert") desert terrain and winter modifiers, as well as for besieging armies. The country [![Image 248: Hostile attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d4/Hostile_attrition.png/28px-Hostile_attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Hostile_attrition.png)**hostile attrition** modifier similarly gives attrition to all hostile armies inside a country's unoccupied territory. This means that it may occasionally be better to take a longer route that passes through more favourable terrain, particularly if the army has a high food demand and is not well-supplied. The automatic path-finding will prefer shorter movement times and low attrition when possible, but at times it might be better to cross that desert directly to reach the battlefield more quickly.

The attrition that a country's armies take is modified country-wide by the [![Image 249: Land unit attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/de/Land_unit_attrition.png/28px-Land_unit_attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Land_unit_attrition.png)**army attrition** modifier and finally capped by the army's [![Image 250: Max attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/32/Max_attrition.png/28px-Max_attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Max_attrition.png)**maximum attrition**, with a base of **5%** that can be increased up to **9%** during harsh winters.

An army does not lose soldiers immediately upon taking attrition; instead, each point of attrition that the army is taking on the monthly tick will increase the army's [![Image 251: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food consumption by **+10%**. Only when an army runs out of food will it begin to take losses every month, with each cohort taking a certain amount of damage based on their **Monthly Damage from Attrition** modifier as a proportion of their total/potential (notably, not current) strength. For instance, a [![Image 252: Unit heavy infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Unit_heavy_infantry.png/28px-Unit_heavy_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heavy_Infantry "Heavy Infantry") heavy infantry cohort has **5%** Monthly Damage from Attrition, which means that if its army is out of food it will lose **25** soldiers each month, regardless of whether it is at full strength or has already been heavily depleted. This means that losses from running out of food are much more significant, relative to the army's current strength, for armies that are already partly depleted (whether from combat or previous losses to attrition), and for those that are disproportionately composed of heavier unit types.

## Army movement[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Army movement") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Army movement")]

Armies move from one territory to another. Adjacent territories have a calculated distance between them, based largely on their size, and armies move a base distance per day (subject to modifiers). The [![Image 253: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost")**movement cost** modifier increases the amount of time needed for an army to move from the territory to another territory, effectively increasing the distance; many terrain types, particularly [![Image 254: Mountains](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/81/Terrain_mountain.png/28px-Terrain_mountain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Mountains") mountains, [![Image 255: Marsh](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Terrain_marsh.png/28px-Terrain_marsh.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Marsh") marshes, and [![Image 256: Jungle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Terrain_jungle.png/28px-Terrain_jungle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Jungle") jungles give significant modifiers to movement cost, while [![Image 257: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road "Road") roads will signficiantly reduce it. The time to move from one territory to another then depends on the army's speed and the effective distance between the territories, but is always at least 1 day. As long as an army has not yet moved more than halfway towards the other territory, the movement order can be cancelled and the army will immediately stop moving (with all movement progress lost), but once an army has moved more than halfway from one territory to another, it will be considered to be [![Image 258: Locked.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Locked.png/28px-Locked.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Locked.png)**movement locked** and movement to the other territory can no longer be cancelled. If there is an enemy army that will be in the territory an army is moving to once it gets there, there will be an indicator showing the expected outcome of the battle.

Each cohort type has a specific **movement speed**, which is the baseline value of quickly it normally moves from one territory to another. A cohort's movement speed is then modified by the [![Image 259: Army movement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b2/Army_movement_speed.png/28px-Army_movement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army_movement_speed "Army movement speed")**army movement speed** modifier of its country, commander, and army, as well as on a cohort type-specific basis by the individual **unit type movement speed** modifiers, all given mainly by [![Image 260: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and [![Image 261: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions. The [![Image 262: Army movement without roads](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Movement_speed_if_no_road.png/28px-Movement_speed_if_no_road.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Army movement without roads")**army movement without roads** modifier (used for armies that are [![Image 263: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") building roads) is also applied, but only if there is no road connecting the two territories the cohort's army is moving between. The movement speed of an army is then the movement speed of its slowest cohort.

## Army actions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Army actions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Army actions")]

Besides moving to different locations, armies can also be ordered to do a large number of actions, some military, some political, and some economic, and there are various toggles and settings that can be changed to tailor the behaviour of the army. Note that many actions have restrictions, and no actions can be done on armies with [![Image 264: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") disloyal commanders.

### Organization[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Organization") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Organization")]

None of these actions, except for disbanding, can be used on [![Image 265: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries.

### [[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Menus") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Menus")]

*   Select Objective – Can select an [objective](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Unit_objectives) for this unit, which will allow it to act mostly independently while trying to follow a broad objective given by the player.
*   Select Tactics – Change the [combat tactic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Combat_tactic "Combat tactic") of the unit, which affects combat effectiveness based on the unit composition and the combat tactic used by enemy armies.
*   Select Preferred Flank Size - Select the preferred size of the flanks, where the flanking cohorts will be placed.
*   Select Primary Cohort - Select the unit type that will be preferentially placed in the front row during [battle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare"), where they will be the first to engage the enemy.
*   Select Secondary Cohort - Select the unit type that will be preferentially placed in the second row during [battle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare"), where they will engage only after the primary cohorts (first row) have fled or been destroyed.
*   Select Flanking Cohort - Select the unit type that will be preferentially placed in the flanks during [battle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare"), where they will try to encircle and cut down enemy cohorts.

### Toggles[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Toggles") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Toggles")]

*   [![Image 266: Allow attach.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f3/Allow_attach.png/28px-Allow_attach.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Allow_attach.png) Allow Attachment – Allows other units to attach to the current army, which will make them automatically follow the army as closely as possible.
*   [![Image 267: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") Build Road – Instructs the army to build [![Image 268: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road "Road") roads between owned, subject, or uncolonized territories while moving, improving [![Image 269: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") movement speed and giving small economic bonuses. Building roads costs a base of [![Image 270: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**50** gold (reduced to [![Image 271: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold if the army has at least [![Image 272: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") 1 engineer cohort) and gives significant penalties to [![Image 273: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale, [![Image 274: Army movement without roads](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Movement_speed_if_no_road.png/28px-Movement_speed_if_no_road.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Army movement without roads") movement speed (when not on roads), and [![Image 275: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") maintenance cost. Requires [![Image 276: Civic faction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Party_civic.png/28px-Party_civic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senatorial_factions "Civic faction") Civic Advances to be greater than **4**, and that the army has at least **10** cohorts.
*   [![Image 277: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army") Drill Army – Gives [![Image 278: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience")**+2.50%** monthly experience gain in exchange for [![Image 279: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**+33%** legion maintenance cost and [![Image 280: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")**+1** cohort loyalty gain chance, allowing an army to improve or maintain its [![Image 281: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") level during peacetime for improved efficacy in combat and [![Image 282: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience gain. Requires a commander and at least normal default army maintenance in [![Image 283: Menu economy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Menu_economy.png/28px-Menu_economy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy") economic policies, and the army cannot move, fight or siege while drilling. Can only be done with [![Image 284: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions.
*   [![Image 285: Force march](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/05/Ability_force_march.png/28px-Ability_force_march.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Force march") Force March – Gives [![Image 286: Army movement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b2/Army_movement_speed.png/28px-Army_movement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army_movement_speed "Army movement speed")**+50%** army movement speed in return for [![Image 287: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit")**+50%** army weight modifier and [![Image 288: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery")**−100%** army morale recovery. Useful for quickly moving an army to where it is needed, particularly if it is already at full [![Image 289: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale, but will almost completely stop an army from recovering [![Image 290: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale if low. Requires the [![Image 291: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Quick March [![Image 292: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") Martial Invention.
*   [![Image 293: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") Build Military Road – An improved version of the standard Build Road unit ability that costs only [![Image 294: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**25** gold without an [![Image 295: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") engineer cohort (remains [![Image 296: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold the army does have one) and with no maluses to army [![Image 297: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale or [![Image 298: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") maintenance cost. Requires the [![Image 299: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Roman Roads** tradition in the [Roman Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions#Roman_traditions "Italic traditions") tree (no advances level requirement) and that the army has at least **5** cohorts, and is available only to [![Image 300: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions.
*   [![Image 301: Unit reorganization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b9/Ability_unit_recovery.png/28px-Ability_unit_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Unit reorganization") Unit Reorganization – Gives [![Image 302: Reinforcement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Reinforcement_speed.png/28px-Reinforcement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Reinforcement "Reinforcement")**+25%** reinforcement speed and [![Image 303: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery")**+10%** army morale recovery in exchange for [![Image 304: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**+100%** maintenance and [![Image 305: Army movement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b2/Army_movement_speed.png/28px-Army_movement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army_movement_speed "Army movement speed")**−33%** movement speed, allowing an army to quickly recover from losses. Particularly useful for shortening the time for mercenaries to become battle ready, if it can be afforded. Can only be done in friendly (owned, allied or subject) owned and controlled territory.

### Attachment[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Attachment") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Attachment")]

### Unit abilities[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Unit abilities") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Unit abilities")]

Most unit abilities are quite specialized and have either [![Image 306: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government or [![Image 307: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military tradition requirements.

*   [![Image 308: Anabasis](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e0/Ability_anabasis.png/28px-Ability_anabasis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Anabasis") Anabasis – The [![Image 309: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler, as the [commander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") of an army, goes on the tour of the local [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), increasing [![Image 310: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty instantly by **5** and then by [![Image 311: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**+0.25** for **2** years at the cost of [![Image 312: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**15** political influence. Only available to [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") if the [![Image 313: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler is commanding an army in the [![Image 314: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital, and can be done every 2 years per province.
*   [![Image 315: Construct border fort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a2/Ability_border_forts.png/28px-Ability_border_forts.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Construct border fort") Construct Border Fort – Builds a [![Image 316: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") level 1 fort in border territory or neighbouring uncolonized territory for [![Image 317: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**1500** manpower. Can be used to [colonize](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization") neighbouring territories, which also creates a [![Image 318: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen pop of primary culture and religion. Requires the [![Image 319: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Castra** military tradition in the [Italic Tribe Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions#Italic_tribe_traditions "Italic traditions") tree, an army with at least [![Image 320: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohorts "Cohorts") 5 cohorts, and that the territory has no neighbouring owned forts.
*   [![Image 321: Desecrate holy site](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Ability_desecrate_holysite.png/28px-Ability_desecrate_holysite.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Desecrate holy site") Desecrate Holy Site – Destroys a [![Image 322: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") holy site in a controlled (not necessarily owned) territory for [![Image 323: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion")**2** aggressive expansion, angering followers of its religion and weakening its owner. Any [sacred treasures](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treasure "Treasure") in the [![Image 324: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") holy site will be seize and transferred to the army's owner's [reliquary](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Reliquary "Reliquary"). Gives [![Image 325: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**10** stability if the country has the [![Image 326: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Militant Epicureanism [![Image 327: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") Religious invention.
*   [![Image 328: Military colonies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7f/Ability_military_colonies.png/28px-Ability_military_colonies.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Military colonies") Military Colonies – Creates a [![Image 329: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman pop of primary culture and religion in a territory with no more than [![Image 330: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") 5 pops located inside a loyal province, at the cost of [![Image 331: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**1500** manpower, [![Image 332: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**15** province loyalty, and [![Image 333: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyalty for a random cohort in the army (or [![Image 334: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**1** tyranny if the commander is the ruler of a non-republic). Requires either the [![Image 335: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Military Colonies** ([Greek Kingdom Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Greek_traditions#Greek_kingdom_traditions "Greek traditions")), [![Image 336: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Kleruchoi** ([Persian Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Persian_traditions#Persian_traditions "Persian traditions")), or [![Image 337: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")**Colonial Integration** ([Levantine Kingdom Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levantine_traditions#Levantine_kingdom_traditions "Levantine traditions")) military traditions and an army with at least [![Image 338: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohorts "Cohorts") 5 cohorts.
*   [![Image 339: Order full retreat](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Shattered_retreat.png/28px-Shattered_retreat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Order full retreat") Order Full Retreat – Gives the order for the unit to perform a shattered retreat from foreign territory or a battle, at the cost of [![Image 340: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength")**50%** of the army's current strength. This can be useful for armies trapped deep in enemy territory or cut off by hostile forts.
*   [![Image 341: Pillage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3a/Ability_pillage.png/28px-Ability_pillage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Pillage") Pillage – Sack a [![Image 342: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city, [![Image 343: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis, or [![Image 344: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital with at least [![Image 345: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**20** civilization value owned by another country. If the territory has a [![Image 346: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort, it must also be occupied. Gives [![Image 347: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence equal to **1/80** of the territory's [![Image 348: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value multiplied by its [![Image 349: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population (with a minimum of [![Image 350: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**1**) and [![Image 351: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**2.4** gold per pop (up to 2.4 times the [![Image 352: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") cohorts in the army). The pillaged territory gets [![Image 353: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth")**−25%** local population growth and [![Image 354: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax")**−25%** local tax for 5 years, and the owner will lose [![Image 355: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**−50** opinion of the pillaging country with a yearly decay of **2**, stacking up to [![Image 356: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**–200**. If the territory's owner already has less than [![Image 357: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**−100** opinion of the pillaging country before the action was taken, the army's owner will declare [![Image 358: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war on the territory's owner with the **Superiority**[wargoal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wargoal "Wargoal") if they are not already at war. Only available to [![Image 359: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") Migratory Tribes, and only on territories that were not [![Image 360: Pillage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3a/Ability_pillage.png/28px-Ability_pillage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Pillage") pillaged or [![Image 361: Raze city](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Ability_raze_city.png/28px-Ability_raze_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Raze city") razed in the last 5 years.
*   [![Image 362: Raze city](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Ability_raze_city.png/28px-Ability_raze_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Raze city") Raze City – Raze a [![Image 363: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city, [![Image 364: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis, or [![Image 365: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital with at least [![Image 366: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**5** civilization value owned by another country. If the territory has a [![Image 367: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort, it must also be occupied. Halves the [![Image 368: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value in the territory and gives an additional [![Image 369: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**-0.01** monthly civilization change, converting it to [![Image 370: Technology speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ed/Technology_speed.png/28px-Technology_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology speed") research progress equal to 1/8 the civilization value destroyed for each research category where the territory's owner is more advanced than the army's owner. Costs [![Image 371: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion")**2** aggressive expansion and **−50** opinion with the owner with a yearly decay of **2**, stacking up to **−200**. If the territory's owner already has less than [![Image 372: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**−100** opinion of the pillaging country before the action was taken, the army's owner will declare [![Image 373: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war on the territory's owner with the **Superiority**[wargoal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wargoal "Wargoal") if they are not already at war. Only available to [![Image 374: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government")[Tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal"), and only on territories that were not [![Image 375: Pillage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3a/Ability_pillage.png/28px-Ability_pillage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Pillage") pillaged or [![Image 376: Raze city](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Ability_raze_city.png/28px-Ability_raze_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Raze city") razed in the last 5 years.
*   [![Image 377: Root out pirates](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4b/Ability_root_out_pirates.png/28px-Ability_root_out_pirates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Root out pirates") Root Out Pirates – Removes the [![Image 378: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate") Pirate Haven modifier from an controlled (not necessarily owned) province that has it. Requires the [![Image 379: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") Anti-Piracy Edict (for [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy")), Anti-Piracy Statutes (for [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")), or Lex Claudia (for [![Image 380: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")) laws, and is not available to [![Image 381: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government")[tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal").
*   [![Image 382: Settle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Ability_settle.png/28px-Ability_settle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Settle") Settle – Settles [![Image 383: Start migration cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration")[migratory armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#migratory_armies) in an owned, controlled, or uncolonized territory, converting each migratory cohort into a [![Image 384: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesman of the primary culture and religion. Will [colonize](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization") the territory if uncolonized or seized from another country if occupied, and requires that there be more migratory cohorts in the army than pops in the territory if the territory is currently unowned. If colonizing or seizing a territory, only the minimum number of cohorts required to match the current population will be settled. Only available for [![Image 385: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") migratory tribes.

## Unit objectives[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Unit objectives") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Unit objectives")]

Unit objectives allow a player to automate individual armies and issue broad orders that they will try to follow under the control of the AI, outsourcing the management of some (or all) armies using the same processes that the AI of other countries uses to control its armies and navies. An objective can be selected for each army or navy under player control (or several units at once, in which case the objective is applied to each of the selected armies/navies), which enables AI control over the selected units and allows it automatically move around, sieging territories, engaging enemies, or avoiding hostile forces depending on the selected object. Armies with a selected objective can also be assigned any number of [regions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region"), which it will try to restrict its operations to. While usually not as effective or efficient as manual country of armies, automated armies are generally sufficient to deal with regions or fronts where they already have local superiority.

### Available objectives[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Available objectives") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Available objectives")]

### Special objectives[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Special objectives") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Special objectives")]

There are also many unit objectives that cannot be picked but that may still be encountered, especially in AI behaviour.


============================================================
# Land_units
============================================================
Title: Land units - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units

Published Time: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 00:50:15 GMT

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

The smallest military unit is called a _cohort_. A cohort is comprised of 500 men, and multiple cohorts can be grouped to form an [army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army").

## Basic stats[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Basic stats") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Basic stats")]

All units types have the same basic stats:

## List of unit types[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: List of unit types") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: List of unit types")]

There are 11 types of land units. For [![Image 1: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, these are automatically assigned based on the [culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") of the levied [pops](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop "Pop"). [![Image 2: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") Legions have their cohorts individually selected and trained at the cost of manpower and money. Note that [![Image 3: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") engineers can only be raised in Legions.
