### Explanations[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Explanations") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Explanations")]

### Modifiers to unit types[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Modifiers to unit types") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Modifiers to unit types")]

There are many [modifiers](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Modifiers "Modifiers") to the available unit types. Most of them are unlocked from [Military Traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_Traditions "Military Traditions") and [Technology](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology").

Each cohort may also become [![Image 46: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to a specific character, which gives [![Image 47: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline")**+10%** Discipline as well as [![Image 48: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base to the sponsor.

### Support units[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Support units") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Support units")]

Certain types of cohorts are **support units**. Support units are very ineffective in combat generally only make up a small proportion of the army, but have important roles and bonuses for most of an army's other functions and needs, such as with logistics, movement, and sieging.

#### Supply trains[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Supply trains") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Supply trains")]

[![Image 49: Unit supply train.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Unit_supply_train.png/28px-Unit_supply_train.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_Train "Supply Train")**Supply trains** carry by far the most [![Image 50: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food of any cohort type while consuming very little themselves, and for most armies will make up the majority of their food storage. As the rate at which an army resupplies in friendly territory is based on its maximum food capacity, supply trains also indirectly increase their rate of resupply. In [![Image 51: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, 1 extra supply train is raised for every 9 cohorts in the levy, over and on top of all cohorts raised normally (this means that larger levies will always be a little bit larger than their base [![Image 52: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength") strength).

In general, supply trains are required for most armies to operate in enemy territory for more than a few months at a time without taking heavy losses from attrition, which is particularly important when engaging in prolonged sieges.

#### Engineers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Engineers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Engineers")]

[![Image 53: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers")**Engineers** represent units specialized in sieges and the construction of military facilities such as siege towers, roads, temporary bridges, and more. While expensive, they provide significant support for sieges and army movement and can significantly speed up the prosecution of a war. Engineer cohorts are available only to [![Image 54: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions.

Each engineer cohort in a besieging army gives **+1** to the siege roll, divided by the territory's [![Image 55: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_level "Fort level") fort level plus 1, rounded down. This is described by the following formula:

![Image 56: {\displaystyle {\text{Siege Bonus from Engineers}}=floor\left({\frac {\text{Number of Engineer Cohorts}}{{\text{Fort Level}}+1}}\right)}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/ac2814e6bef4090fba6e16bcee8b125acb59f766)
For instance, 4 engineer cohorts will give **+2** to the siege roll against a territory with [![Image 57: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_level "Fort level") 1 fort level, but only 1 against a territory with [![Image 58: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_level "Fort level") 2 fort levels. Engineers generally become less efficient as the strength of a fort increases, but can reduce smaller and weaker forts quickly.

Having at least one engineer cohort in an army reduces the cost of [![Image 59: Build road](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Build road") building roads (including military roads) to [![Image 60: Cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**10** gold (from a base of [![Image 61: Cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**50** for normal roads and [![Image 62: Cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**25** for military roads). This cost is not affected by any modifiers or by the number of engineer cohorts, as long as there is at least 1. The reduction in cost is very significant, even for military roads, and any legions that are assigned to building roads would benefit greatly from an engineer cohort.

An army that has at least 1 engineer cohort for every **10** cohorts in the army will also not take any combat penalties when crossing a [![Image 63: River](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Terrain_river.png/28px-Terrain_river.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "River") river or strait, which improves the army's ability to maneuver and seek out favourable opportunities for battle.

## Unit type strategy[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Unit type strategy") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Unit type strategy")]

Based on the various bonuses and penalties, each unit type seems to fall into certain categories.

### Supply trains[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Supply trains") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Supply trains")]

These are not combat units. Their only ability is to have a lot of food capacity, in case attrition is a major issue. But, they seem to count for calculations of Cohorts for sieges. When looking at units' strengths, there is no point including this one. It takes **+100%** and deals **−90%** damage towards everything. If it is deployed to the battle, you may need more units to put in the front line.

Their high price means they should be deployed in moderation, but the long duration for them to fill up with food means they should not be deployed too late either.

### Light infantry[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Light infantry") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Light infantry")]

There are only a few good things you can say about light infantry. While every other unit has some unit they counter, light infantry only has some units they are not _too heavily_ countered by:

*   They are cheap, with the same price Level as archers and chariots. They have a incredibly low attrition weight. Both this and the gold price makes them ideal if bodies rather then quality are needed, like for sieges. However even here, archers might be better in all but the most extreme cases.
*   They have a lot of buffs from [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") (leather and base metals) and a fair share of [military traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions").
*   A decent amount of [combat tactics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units#combat_tactics) also have a high efficiency for them.
*   While they take **−25%** morale damage, that barely offsets them taking around **+25%** morale and strength damage from every enemy. That also means they churn through more manpower in battle than perhaps any other unit.
*   They can be used to "pad out" the side of the front line, to avoid the enemy being able to fully use flanking. This is a rare case where the reduced morale damage could be useful, despite the cost in manpower.
*   They also have a lot of damage penalties. Their only hope seems to be to outlast the enemy on the morale front. They could also outnumber the enemy on sheer cohort count 1:2 at comparable prices − except manpower of course.
*   Camel cavalry are the only thing they can claim to counter somewhat.

### Archers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Archers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Archers")]

The first step up from light infantry and a mainstay of many armies.

*   Their increased morale damage makes them more likely to flee, but such a behaviour can conserve manpower and make room for the 2nd tier cohorts − ideally things countering whatever drove off the archers. They are supposed to deal damage, but fall back to let others deal damage if they are countered.
*   They do well against heavy infantry, at a fraction of the cost, as long as they outnumber the enemy.
*   Not a lot of [combat tactics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units#Combat_tactics) make good use of them.
*   Heavy and light cavalry in turn counter them heavily, but again they can quickly disengage due to extra morale damage without taking too many losses.

### Chariots[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Chariots") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Chariots")]

Old technology from the Bronze Age, they are largely outdated by the time of the game and accordingly only used by few:

*   Only available to barbarians and a few other military traditions.
*   The poor man's heavy infantry. If you lack the resources or the supply for those but have them available, they are a viable alternative.
*   Strong versus archers and light infantry, extra countered by heavy infantry and heavy cavalry. Cannot [assault](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assault "Assault") in a siege.
*   Can deal a lot of damage, but also take a lot.
*   As cheap as light infantry and archers.

### Heavy infantry[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Heavy infantry") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Heavy infantry")]

The mainstay of civilised and barbarian field armies of the period, but often hard to get:

*   Requires Iron, a rather rare trade-good in some areas. Chariots are usually an alternative in those cases.
*   They also cost a lot of supply and money, making them primarily useful in very favourable terrain with high Civilisation.
*   Archers and horse archers counter them, along with elephants. Most other unit types have a poor match-up.

### Elephants[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Elephants") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Elephants")]

The ultimate melee unit, with a very high price to match. In almost everything the antithesis of the light infantry:

*   Extremely expensive in money, supply, food and even time
*   The ultimate first line in the game
*   They barely take any strength damage and deal a lot of damage in return
*   Strategies that affect them tend to have more effect then on any other unit type, with Shock Action giving a unmatched 200%.
*   One-on-one, there is no unit that has any hope of countering them.
*   The main counter is to outnumber them, especially with horse archers.
*   Heavy infantry. light infantry and archers can also be counter options, since they don't have as high penalties to attacking elephants as others.
*   Supply might be their worst enemy.

### Light cavalry[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Light cavalry") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Light cavalry")]

Coming out of the Bronze Age, riding horses became a major factor in warfare:

*   Almost as cheap as archers and light infantry.
*   Very high maneuver, with easily available horses. Usually the first flanking option one can find.
*   Quite deadly against archers and the only counter to horse archers, making them a possible 1st Cohort option.
*   Very good and very available flanker.
*   Best movement speed in the game, making them useful for quick reaction forces.

### Camel cavalry[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Camel cavalry") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Camel cavalry")]

The light cavalry of the desert regions.

*   Usage is almost identical to light cavalry; refer to section for the basics.
*   More expensive in gold.
*   Inferior combat bonuses, but also penalties.
*   Comparatively good supply, food storage/consumption and high maneuver.

### Heavy cavalry[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Heavy cavalry") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Heavy cavalry")]

Putting armor on horses and riders results in totally different usages:

*   Available along with light cavalry, but uses are utterly different.
*   Works nothing like any other cavalry units, more like a heavy infantry variant.
*   Worse maneuver than light cavalry, well suited for the front line.
*   More expensive in supplies and money.
*   Stronger than heavy infantry against archers and most cavalry.
*   The primary counter is heavy infantry, but horse archers are able to outlast them.
*   Pretty good movement speed can give them a place in reaction forces.

### Horse archers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Horse archers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Horse archers")]

Combines the best parts of cavalry and archers:

*   In bonuses, penalties and stats, it uses the better of archers or light cavalry.
*   In money and supply, it is the same as heavy infantry.
*   Counters heavy infantry, but countered by light cavalry, camels and, potentially, elephants.
*   The ultimate maneuver/flanking unit.

## Migratory units[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Migratory units") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Migratory units")]

These are a special unit type, composed by Light infantry, available only to [migratory tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government"). Migratory units are created in the territory tab at the cost of [![Image 64: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**8** stability (this value can be modified by laws and by lowering centralization level). Migratory units can move across borders without military access and can settle in uncolonized lands or in occupied enemies' territories.

Migratory units, beyond settling, can perform two specific actions in non-owned colonized territories where they are standing:

If these actions are performed multiple times against the same country, reducing their opinion to -200, the migratory tribe will automatically declare war on them with the Show Superiority [casus belli](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Casus_belli "Casus belli").

Migratory units can be merged with regular units but can't be raised during war.

## Non-military implications[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Non-military implications") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_units&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Non-military implications")]

Cohorts are not politically neutral in _Imperator_. In fact, they have many important implications in a state's politics.

*   Cohorts can become loyal to a general personally, rather than to the state. While this decreases their maintenance costs (as the general is paying part of the expenses), loyal cohorts can be regarded as the general's "private" army. This is especially crucial if the general is disloyal and the state is in danger of civil war. 
    *   In tribal states, clan retinues are always loyal to their respective clan chiefs. Clan retinue size decreases as centralization increases.

*   Generals and clan chiefs gain power base and prestige based on the number of cohorts commanded _and_ loyal to them personally.


============================================================
# Mercenaries
============================================================
Title: Army - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") An **army** represents a group of armed soldiers fighting on behalf of a nation, composed of individual [cohorts](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units") and represented by a pawn on the map that can be moved around on its own. [![Image 2: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") War is waged primarily with armies, whose main role is to occupy and siege enemy [![Image 3: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") territories and [fight](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare") other armies to deny them the ability to occupy and pillage a country's own lands.

Generally, armies are raised from a country's [![Image 4: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture population and divided into two main types. At game start, each country may raise soldiers from their integrated culture pops to fight as [![Image 5: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, which while can be quite large relative to a country's population are by their nature hard to organize and cannot be maintained for too long without stressing the social fabric of the nation. As a country expands and develops, they may transition to be able to recruit [![Image 6: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions, which while more expensive are permanent, professional military forces whose organization and composition can be more carefully controlled. If a country needs more men than can be raised from its population and is wealthy enough to afford it, [![Image 7: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries offer a third option, if somewhat more fickle.

Armies are typically led by a [![Image 8: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander who, while subservient to the government, ultimately is the person who exerts direct control over the army. A well-led army can be highly effective in battle and punch well above its weight to turn the tide of a war, but the command of an army is also a [![Image 9: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") powerful position that has a significant impact on the [![Image 10: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty of its holder. A disloyal commander will refuse any orders from above and follow their own objectives that may well not be that of the country as a whole, and may at some point decide to side against the government - along with all the troops under their command - in the event of a [![Image 11: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war.

## Name[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Name") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Name")]

All armies have a **name** that allows for easy identification, which by default is generally based on the type of the army and the region that it was raised from. Any owned army, except for [![Image 12: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries, can be renamed at any time by double-clicking on the unit name on the top of the unit panel.

Every [![Image 13: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion also has a name and can be renamed to the player's liking by double clicking on its name in the Legion tab of the [![Image 14: Military](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Menu_military.png/28px-Menu_military.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Military") military panel. The names of every army in the legion will by default include the name of its legion, and if the legion's name is changed, the name of the cohort will be automatically updated as well.

## Army types[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Army types") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Army types")]

### Levies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Levies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Levies")]

[![Image 15: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy")**Levies** can be raised from free (non-[![Image 16: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave) [![Image 17: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture pops in every owned [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region"). The [![Image 18: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength")**strength** of a region's levy, the number of pops that are actually raised in the levy, is determined by country's [![Image 19: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy")**levy size** modifier, with a base of **7.50%** of eligible pops that can be raised; the size of levies can be modified by various [![Image 20: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, [![Image 21: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, [![Image 22: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government types, [heritages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritages "Heritages"), and more, and additionally always has a minimum of **4** pops that can be raised per region. Every pop that is raised becomes a single 500-man [cohort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort "Cohort"), with the overall [unit type composition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units") of the levy depending on the [![Image 23: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture")[culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#List_of_cultures "Culture") that it is raised from. For every **9** cohorts that are raised, the levy will also get an additional [![Image 24: Unit supply train.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Unit_supply_train.png/28px-Unit_supply_train.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_Train "Supply Train") supply train support unit.

Raising a region's levy does not cost any [![Image 25: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, but instead costs [![Image 26: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion")**0.5** war exhaustion. Levies will be raised by default in their governorship's capital, but can be raised from anywhere in their governorship by clicking the appropriate territory in the **Levies** mapmode. Levies can be freely split and merged back into themselves, but a levy cannot be merged with an army of a different type or a levy raised from a different region. A levy is always commanded by the [![Image 27: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") governor of its region (including the [![Image 28: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler for levies raised from the [![Image 29: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital region), including if a levy is split up; the region's governor will be considered the commander of all the different levy components simultaneously.

In [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") governments, levies are instead led by [![Image 30: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs, and levies from a single region will often be split up such that each clan chief gets at least one army to take command of. Even if they were raised from the same region, levies led by different clan chiefs cannot be merged together. Tribal nations also have a significant bonus to [![Image 31: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy size, to compensate for their generally smaller populations and the fact that they cannot raise [![Image 32: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions.

Once raised, levies do not require any maintenance, but raised pops do not have any production which can have a significant impact on [![Image 33: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income, [![Image 34: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points, and [![Image 35: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower production. Levies that are raised for longer than **6** months will also produce additional [![Image 36: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion, on top of any accumulated from [![Image 37: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition or occupied territories. If a levy cohort is overrun or destroyed in battle, there is also a chance that its associated pop will be killed, which can make it quite damaging to lose large numbers of levies in a war. If a levy cohort's pop is lost or is no longer eligible to be raised as a levy, the cohort will no longer be able to reinforce and will slowly lose [![Image 38: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength each month and will eventually disappear as it is no longer being supplied.

Levies can only be disbanded 4 months after they were raised, during peacetime, are not moving, and if their governor is loyal. Levies cannot be disbanded normally and must be dismissed from the Levies tab of the [![Image 39: Military](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Menu_military.png/28px-Menu_military.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Military") military screen on a governorship-wide basis. If a levy has been raised for at least **8** months, every levy cohort that is disbanded will grant [![Image 40: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**0.015** military experience for each point of [![Image 41: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") cohort experience. This compensates for the fact that they cannot [![Image 42: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army") drill and are not meant to be raised permanently, and so tend not to accumulate much passive [![Image 43: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience gain. Once a levy has been disbanded, it will take between **4** and **24** months for the region's levy to be ready to be raised again, depending on the strength of the levy when it was disbanded. However, they can be instantly raised during a defensive war.

Following is a complete list of [![Image 44: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy size modifiers.

| --- | --- | --- |
| Base | Base | **+7.5%** |

| [![Image 47: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") In Defensive War | **+1%** |
| [Country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank") | [![Image 48: Country rank 2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Country_rank_2.png/28px-Country_rank_2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City_state "City state") City State | **+12%** |

| [![Image 54: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Military Service](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Military_Reforms "Laws") ([Monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy")) | **+10%** |
| [![Image 55: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Citizen Militia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Military_Reforms "Laws") ([Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")) | **+5%** |

| [![Image 57: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Provisioning Act](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Military_Reforms "Laws") ([Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")) | **+2.5%** |

| [![Image 60: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Republican Levy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Military_Reforms "Laws") ([![Image 61: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")) | **+7.5%** |
| [![Image 62: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Punic Reforms](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Military_Reforms "Laws") ([![Image 63: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")) | **+2.5%** |
| [Inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") | [![Image 64: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")_Auxiliary Recruitment_ ([![Image 65: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") Religious invention) | **+2%** |

| [![Image 75: Flag of Maurya](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5b/Maurya.png/20px-Maurya.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maurya "Maurya") Mauryan Decline Heritage ([Maurya](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maurya "Maurya")) |

### Legions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Legions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Legions")]

In [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") and [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), certain [![Image 76: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") military reform laws allow for the recruitment of permanent armies of professional forces called [![Image 77: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion")**legions** (tribal nations cannot recruit legions until they reform into a republic or monarchy). Where a levy consists of an assortment of units based on the [![Image 78: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") culture of the pops being called into service, the troop composition of legions can be much more precisely controlled. Like levies, each legion is associated with a particular [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region") and shares the same pool of [![Image 79: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength") strength, drawn from free [![Image 80: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture pops with the proportion determined by the country's [![Image 81: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy size modifier; this means that for every cohort recruited into a legion, one less cohort can be raised from that region's levy, and the size of a region's legion is capped by the region's base levy size (not including [![Image 82: Unit supply train.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Unit_supply_train.png/28px-Unit_supply_train.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_Train "Supply Train") support units). If all of a region's [![Image 83: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength") levy strength is used up in its legion, no levy can be raised from that region. Laws that allow for the raising of legions have a lower [![Image 84: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy size modifier than those focused on levies, which generally leads to a choice between a larger but more disorganized levy, or a smaller but higher quality legion. Notably, [![Image 85: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") engineers cannot be raised as levies and can only exist as legion subunits.

Creating a new legion costs [![Image 86: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**25** gold. Once a legion has been created, cohorts can be added to any of their units as long as it is in an owned and controlled territory. Each new cohort costs [![Image 87: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**500** manpower and a variable amount of [![Image 88: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold per cohort depending on its type, from [![Image 89: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**8** gold for [![Image 90: Unit archers.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8e/Unit_archers.png/28px-Unit_archers.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Archers "Archers") archers and [![Image 91: Unit light infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Unit_light_infantry.png/28px-Unit_light_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Infantry "Light Infantry") light infantry to [![Image 92: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**40** gold for engineers. Once built, cohorts will be immediately added to the unit without any travel time.

Up to [![Image 93: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander")**4** commanders can be assigned to each legion. The main commander of a legion is known as the **Legate**, and will be the default commander of the main army. If a new unit is created from a legion, one of lesser commanders, called **Tribunes**, will take command of that unit. The command of any of a legion's units can be freely swapped between its 4 commanders (even the legate), though a character will lose [![Image 94: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**5** loyalty if their command is revoked. A legion can be split into different armies and freely merged or reorganized within the legion, but like levies legions from different regions cannot be merged together. There is no limit on the number of armies that a legion can be split into as long as there are enough cohorts to do so, but as each commander can only lead one army at a time if there are more than 4 armies in a legion, at least one of them will necessarily not have a commander.

Any units of a legion can be disbanded at any time as long as it is not in battle or in retreat, which will return **25%** of the cohort's manpower back to the pool as well as removing any need for maintenance, but has an upfront [![Image 95: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold cost of **15%** of the recruitment cost, increasing to **20%** for cohorts that are [![Image 96: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to a character. Unlike levies, legions do not give any [![Image 97: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience when disbanded, but as legions are persistent units that are maintained even during peacetime and can [![Image 98: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army") drill to improve their [![Image 99: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") cohort experience outside of battle, they generally contribute much more to passive [![Image 100: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience generation.

#### Legion maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Legion maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Legion maintenance")]

As a professional army, legions have a monthly [![Image 101: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**maintenance cost**, with a base value of **1/48** (around **2.08%**) of its total recruitment cost (corresponding to **25%** of the total recruitment cost each year). The maintenance cost is reduced to **66%** of normal (1/72 or 1.38% of total recruitment cost monthly, 16.5% of total recruitment cost yearly) for cohorts that are [![Image 102: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to a character, and removed entirely for units commanded by [![Image 103: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") disloyal characters. Legion maintenance is then further modified by the [![Image 104: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance")**legion maintenance cost** modifier, which can be toggled in the [![Image 105: Menu economy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Menu_economy.png/28px-Menu_economy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy") economy tab for either [![Image 106: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") higher morale or [![Image 107: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") saving on expenses as well as from a large variety of other more permanent modifiers.

#### Distinctions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Distinctions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Distinctions")]

_Main article: [Distinction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction")_

As legions fight in wars, they may accumulate [![Image 108: Phalera helmetman.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Phalera_helmetman.png/28px-Phalera_helmetman.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction")**distinctions**, permanent modifiers to their fighting capabilities that apply to all of their units. Distinctions are generally accumulated by a legion over time and apply to all of a legion's armies and commanders, and can give strong bonuses that will generally make a legion significantly stronger than a levy of equivalent size. A legion's important victories and defeats will also be recorded in their legion history.

Most distinctions are available only with the **[Heirs of Alexander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heirs_of_Alexander "Heirs of Alexander")** DLC.

### Migratory armies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Migratory armies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Migratory armies")]

_Main articles: [Land units#Migratory units](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land\_units#Migratory\_units "Land units"), [Tribal migration](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal\_migration "Tribal migration")_

[![Image 109: Start migration cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration")**Migratory armies** are a special army type available to [![Image 110: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") Migratory Tribes, composed of [migrant cohorts](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units#Migratory_units "Land units") that can be raised by uprooting the entire population of a territory. Starting a migration in a territory requires that the region not have any [![Image 111: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies raised and costs [![Image 112: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**8** stability per territory, converting each pop in the territory into a single [![Image 113: Unit light infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Unit_light_infantry.png/28px-Unit_light_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Infantry "Light Infantry") light infantry cohort that, like levies, do not cost any maintenance. While not very powerful and potentially quite costly as migrating entails abandoning territory and the loss of any migratory cohorts equates to the loss of a whole pop, the ability to raise units out of every pop in a territory means that even relatively small countries can raise a truly large host if they are willing to take the risks.

Migratory armies are unique in that they do not require [![Image 114: Has military access.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Has_military_access.png/28px-Has_military_access.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_access "Military access") military access to move onto or across other countries' territories, and a country can continue to exist even without owning any [![Image 115: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") territories as long as it has at least migratory armies. They also have the unique unit abilities that allows them [![Image 116: Raze city](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Ability_raze_city.png/28px-Ability_raze_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Raze city") Raze Cities to gain [![Image 117: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research progress from damaging the [![Image 118: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities of more advanced countries, as well as [![Image 119: Settle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Ability_settle.png/28px-Ability_settle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Settle") settle on controlled or uncolonized territories to take control of the territory and turn the migratory cohorts back into [![Image 120: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen pops.

### Mercenaries[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Mercenaries") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Mercenaries")]

_Not to be confused with [![Image 121: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject\_nations "Subject nations")[mercenary states](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary\_state "Mercenary state"), a special subject type._

[![Image 122: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries")**Mercenaries** are soldiers willing to fight for any country in return for [![Image 123: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold. While a significant expense that should not be taken lightly, mercenaries can play a decisive role in wars by allowing wealthy countries to augment their military forces beyond what their population and [![Image 124: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower can support, as well as coming with skilled [![Image 125: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanders that can give a decisive edge in battle - as long as their employer was willing and able to pay them, and nobody else could come with a better offer.

Mercenaries are organized into individual mercenary companies, each with its own [![Image 126: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander (that all of its cohorts are [![Image 127: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to) and based from a particular territory from which it draws its culture, size, composition, and unit graphics, and stands while unhired; importantly, larger cities will typically support larger mercenary compaies. Mercenary companies appear all over the map and are typically found in major [![Image 128: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and other population centers; disenfranchised heirs and princely adventurers may also decide to seek fortune by forming their own mercenary companies. All mercenary companies can be viewed in the [![Image 129: Menu mercenary.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dd/Menu_mercenary.png/28px-Menu_mercenary.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_mercenary.png)**Mercenaries** screen, along with their strength, composition, maintenance costs, current employer, and more. From this interface, countries can also hire and otherwise interact with mercenary companies.

Mercenaries are affected by army modifiers such as discipline, morale and damage.

#### Hiring Mercenaries[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Hiring Mercenaries") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Hiring Mercenaries")]

While unhired, all mercenary companies belong to the special [![Image 130: Flag of Mercenaries](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Mercenaries.png/20px-Mercenaries.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries")[Mercenaries](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") faction, which is neutral to all countries, and will stand in their home territory waiting for a contract. They do not count towards the [![Image 131: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit of the territory where they are located and will not fight other armies, in effect not interacting with anything else at all.

Any mercenary company can be hired as long as it is within [![Image 132: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range, it is not already employed, and the country can afford the upfront cost of [![Image 133: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**100** gold, modified by the country's [![Image 134: Recruit mercenary cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png/28px-Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_cost "Mercenary cost")**recruit mercenary cost** modifier. It is not possible to hire only part of a mercenary company; a company must be hired all at once. Once a mercenary company is hired, it immediately comes under the control of its employer, but is not automatically moved from its base and must be moved to where the country plans to use it. Hired mercenary armies outside of a country's territory will begin in a state of exile and must be moved to controlled territory before they can used in battle. This means that hiring an army far from the conflict zone could get expensive, as maintenance is paid from the day they are hired.

Mercenary armies also start with [![Image 135: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") 0 morale, so they will not be ready for battle for a number of months and should generally be hired ahead of time. Using the [![Image 136: Unit reorganization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b9/Ability_unit_recovery.png/28px-Ability_unit_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Unit reorganization") Unit Reorganization ability once it is in controlled territory can speed up this process, though it will be even more expensive than using on a normal army. Once no longer exiled, the mercenary army will act as any other controlled army, except for the fact that their leader cannot be removed and that they cannot be merged, split, or otherwise reorganized. In particular, mercenary armies do benefit from all military modifiers that a country has, and as they have no interest in a country's politics they will always have maximum [![Image 137: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty - unless they are not paid, or get a better offer. Almost as importantly, mercenaries do not reinforce from the [![Image 138: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower pool - this means that they do not cost [![Image 139: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower to reinforce, and will always reinforce even if the country is out of [![Image 140: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower.

#### Mercenary limit[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mercenary limit") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mercenary limit")]

Every country has a maximum number of mercenary armies that they can hire at a time, determined by their [![Image 141: Recruit mercenary cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png/28px-Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maximum_mercenary_armies "Maximum mercenary armies")**maximum mercenary armies** modifier that is increased largely through [![Image 142: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and [country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank"). Once a country has reached or exceeded its number of maximum mercenary armies, it will be unable to recruit any more until they either raise their cap or disband one of their currently employed mercenaries (though this does not apply to bribing mercenaries employed by enemy countries). Note that each mercenary company, regardless of size or composition, counts the same amount towards the cap; this means that even after reaching the mercenary limit, a country can still increase the strength of its mercenary armies by disbanding weaker employed companies and hiring larger, stronger armies.

#### Mercenary maintenance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Mercenary maintenance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Mercenary maintenance")]

Maintaining a mercenary company is somewhat more expensive than a [![Image 143: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion with an equivalent size and composition, with a base multiplier of **150%** the normal legion maintenance cost (i.e. a 50% increase over the base maintenance cost). The maintenance cost of a mercenary company is then modified multiplicatively by the both the standard [![Image 144: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") legion maintenance cost and [![Image 145: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance")**mercenary army maintenance** modifiers, which gives the following formula for each the maintenance cost of each mercenary army cohort:

![Image 146: {\displaystyle {\text{Monthly Maintenance}}={\text{Base Maintenance Cost}}\cdot 1.50\cdot (1+{\text{Mercenary Army Maintenance}})\cdot (1+{\text{Legion Maintenance Cost}})}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/5850841a17c67a8de35e5b19926feb0970ed7a82)
Note that this means that if a country's [![Image 147: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance") mercenary army maintenance modifier is less than **-34%**, mercenaries will in fact be cheaper than legions.

#### Disbanding Mercenaries[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Disbanding Mercenaries") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Disbanding Mercenaries")]

As with most armies, a mercenary army can also be disbanded at any time, as long as it is not in battle. Disbanding mercenaries means that that the country will no longer have to pay for their maintenance has no cost, other than losing use of their commander and soldiers. A mercenary army will also be automatically disbanded if it is completely destroyed in battle.

Once disbanded, the mercenary army will again be considered available for hire, and it will begin its journey back to its home location. Once it has returned, it will slowly reinforce back to full strength.

#### Desertion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Desertion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Desertion")]

As a mercenary's loyalty is dependent entirely on the ability of its employer to pay them, being unable to pay hired mercenaries is problematic and can create rather dangerous situations. If a country goes into [![Image 148: Bankruptcy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Bankruptcy.png/28px-Bankruptcy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deficit "Deficit") deficit and becomes unable to properly pay its mercenaries, mercenary armies will become [![Image 149: Objective disloyal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Objective_disloyal.png/28px-Objective_disloyal.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Objective_disloyal.png) disloyal and refuse to listen to orders or actively engage the enemy. If the deficit goes below [![Image 150: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**-50**, hired mercenary companies may take more drastic action; they may decide that the contract is over and desert its current employer while sacking the local territory, defect and try to find a better contract with an enemy nation, or even decide to take their payment in land and seize control of the local [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province").

A country can also attempt to **bribe** an enemy nation's hired mercenaries if they have at least [![Image 151: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**24** gold for every cohort in the mercenary company, and the mercenary company has not been bribed in the last year. The mercenary's current employer will then either have to match the offer or face the mercenary switching allegiance to their enemy. Adopting the [![Image 152: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Mercenary Benefits [![Image 153: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Invention will prevent enemy countries from bribing a country's employed mercenaries.

#### Modifiers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Modifiers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Modifiers")]

The Following is a list of mercenary modifiers:

| Type | Modifier | [![Image 154: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") Mercenary Modifier |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") | [![Image 155: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")_Professional Sailors_ ([![Image 156: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") Martial Invention) | Maximum Mercenary Armies **+1** |

## Commanders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Commanders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Commanders")]

_See also: [Position#Army commanders](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Army\_commanders "Position")_

Most armies are led by a [![Image 175: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander")**commander** that organizes and leads the army at a tactical level and can give various bonuses - and maluses - to its ability to fight, maneuver, and manage its logistics. The most important attribute for a commander is their [![Image 176: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial skill; for each point of its commander's martial, an army will gain [![Image 177: Assault ability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Assault_ability.png/28px-Assault_ability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assault "Assault")**+5%** assault ability, [![Image 178: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")**+1%** enslavement efficiency, and [![Image 179: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")**+0.10** cohort loyalty gain chance as well as a bonus to dice rolls in combat, allowing well-lead armies to punch significantly above their weight in [combat](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare"). An army without a commander will instead get a [![Image 180: Land morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b8/Land_morale.png/28px-Land_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Land_morale.png)**-15%** land morale and [![Image 181: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/28px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay")**+1%** experience decay modifier, making them very vulnerable to a properly led army; even a weak, low martial commander is much better than not having a commander at all. A commander's [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), particularly any command traits, can also have effects on [![Image 182: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") discipline, [![Image 183: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale, [![Image 184: Reinforcement speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Reinforcement_speed.png/28px-Reinforcement_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Reinforcement "Reinforcement") reinforcement speed, and more.

### Assigning commanders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Assigning commanders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Assigning commanders")]

The commander of a [![Image 185: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy is usually the [![Image 186: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governor of the region the levy was raised from (even if the levy is split into multiple units, the governor will remain in command of all of them); as governors also have an important role in the administration of the region, this often requires balancing between a potential governor's [![Image 187: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse skill for administering the region and increasing its revenues and [![Image 188: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial for leading levied armies during times of war, at least for heavily levy-dependent countries. In [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") countries, levies from all regions are instead split between the control of the [![Image 189: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs. Being more specialized, each [![Image 190: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion can instead have up to **4** dedicated commanders that can be assigned to legion, consisting of 1 **Legate** (who will be the legion's default commander) and up to 3 **Tribunes**. Each legion commander can only lead a single army of the legion at a time, but can be freely swapped out for another one of the legion's commanders (though this gives a temporary [![Image 191: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-5** loyalty malus). Like legions, [![Image 192: Start migration cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration") migratory armies can simply have any character that does not already hold a [![Image 193: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") position appointed to be their commander, and [![Image 194: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries come with their own commander when hired that cannot be replaced.

### Loyalty[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Loyalty") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Army&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Loyalty")]

_See also: [Cohort loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort\_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")_

As the army's leader, a commander exerts a great degree of control over their commanded armies, which has significant implications for [![Image 195: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. The commander of a raised army will get [![Image 196: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+0.3** power base for every percentage of the country's currently raised armies (including raised [![Image 197: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, but not including unraised levies) they command, up to a maximum of [![Image 198: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+1.2** power base per cohort.
