| [Ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") | [![Image 408: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") Charisma | **-0.01** per point |
| [![Image 409: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") Popularity | **-0.001** per point above 50 |
| [![Image 410: Merciful](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Merciful.png/28px-Merciful.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Merciful")[Merciful](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) | * **-0.05** |
| [![Image 411: Forgiving](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e3/Forgiving.png/28px-Forgiving.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Forgiving")[Forgiving](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) |
| [![Image 412: Trusting](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/44/Trusting.png/28px-Trusting.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Trusting")[Trusting](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) |
| [![Image 413: Cruel](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Cruel.png/28px-Cruel.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Cruel")[Cruel](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) | **+0.02** |
| [![Image 414: Deify ruler cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png/28px-Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Apotheosis "Apotheosis") Apotheosis | **+0.01** per [Deified Ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Apotheosis "Religion") in Pantheon |
| [Heritage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritage "Heritage") | [![Image 415: Flag of Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Athens.png/20px-Athens.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens") Athenian Heritage ([Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens")) | **-0.02** |

While nominally a negative modifier, [![Image 416: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny is generally not particularly problematic at lower levels and it is not very important to keep it low unless the nation is already experiencing issues with [![Image 417: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty. The [![Image 418: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") invoke devotio action in particular can be used to essentially gain [![Image 419: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") in return for reducing [![Image 420: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion, effectively converting [![Image 421: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") population unhappiness (and corresponding [![Image 422: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest) into [![Image 423: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty, which is generally easier to deal with, especially if the country does not have much other tyranny to begin with.

It is worth noting that since Tyranny decay does not scale according to its current value and there are no easy ways to quickly lower it, high Tyranny can be problematic if the state is not prepared, especially if the ruler has low [![Image 424: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") Charisma and so the state has low base tyranny decay - if this becomes the case, the loyalty of powerful characters needs to be assured at all costs, or the country could easily spiral into [![Image 425: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war. The significant [![Image 426: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty and [![Image 427: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold maluses together can be dangerous if not kept under control by other means, as even a single disloyal family head might be enough to trigger a civil war. [![Image 428: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") Republics in particular need to pay close attention to tyranny levels, as tyranny reduces [![Image 429: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") Democrat support which can be highly problematic if they make up a significant portion of the Senate - especially if that in turn lowers overall [![Image 430: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Senate approval") senate support to the point where taking any actions accrues even more tyranny. On the flip side, tyranny pleases the [![Image 431: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs, making high tyranny an option to consider if they make up a significant portion of the Senate.

In the later game, if and when the player has accumulated enough [![Image 432: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty bonuses to handle the penalties, high Tyranny is much more useful and players can take full advantage of its bonuses, particularly its effects on [![Image 433: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion change. In conjunction with further reductions from other sources and growing Aggressive Expansion Impact bonuses, a country with high Tyranny can handle much larger conquests and [![Image 434: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion values in the lategame as opposed to what is feasible early on. The [![Image 435: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National Slave Output can also be quite useful once all the loyalty problems can be properly dealt with, making for a powerful tyrannical empire.

### War Exhaustion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: War Exhaustion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: War Exhaustion")]

[![Image 436: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:War_exhaustion.png)**War exhaustion** represents anger and discontent in the general population from the human and economic cost of war. Raising a region's [![Image 437: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies costs [![Image 438: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion")**0.5** war exhaustion, and keeping levies raised for more than **6** months will give an additional monthly war exhaustion penalty that increases the longer the levy is raised. Casualties, blockades, and enemy occupation can also contribute significantly to increasing a country's war exhaustion.

The effects of war exhaustion can be significant, decreasing [![Image 439: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of all pops and impacting [![Image 440: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") ruler popularity, but also increasing [![Image 441: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience gain and so can some amount of war exhaustion can be useful if the country is sufficiently stable. High War Exhaustion will increase the AI's willingness to sign a more favourable peace, and conversely may force a player to sign a less favourable peace treaty rather than let their nation be consumed by unrest. War Exhaustion ticks down slowly while at peace and based on the ruler's current [![Image 442: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal, which can be increased through various omens, laws, and ideas. If War Exhaustion becomes too high, the [![Image 443: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") Invoke Devotio action in the religion panel can be taken to increase War Exhaustion decay at the cost of [![Image 444: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") Tyranny. War exhaustion generally has a value between **0** and **20**, but can rise to **30** for the attacker in an offensive war.

Each point of war exhaustion has the following effects:

## State rank[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: State rank") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: State rank")]

A country's **rank** is a measure of how large and influential a particular state is, based largely on the number of [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") that it owns. Country rank is one of the primary determinants of the number of major families/clan chiefs, [![Image 445: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range, and [![Image 446: Integrate speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/31/Integrate_speed.png/28px-Integrate_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Integration_speed "Integration speed") integration speed as well as significantly impacting [![Image 447: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence generation, [![Image 448: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness, and [![Image 449: Warscore cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Warscore_cost.png/28px-Warscore_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_score_cost "War score cost") warscore cost but higher ranked nations are also more vulnerable to civil war and rebellions as they have lower [![Image 450: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/General "General loyalty")[![Image 451: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Admiral "Admiral loyalty")[![Image 452: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor loyalty") office loyalty as well as a [![Image 453: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty malus and a lower [![Image 454: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold.

* [![Image 485: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") Subject nations are additionally capped at [![Image 486: Country rank 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Country_rank_3.png/28px-Country_rank_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Local_power "Local power") local power status, regardless of how big they get.

Country rank is also particularly important for diplomacy, as certain diplomatic actions are available only to countries of a certain rank.

| Rank | [![Image 487: Defensive league.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Defensive_league.png/28px-Defensive_league.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Defensive_league "Defensive league") Defensive League | [![Image 488: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") Alliance | [![Image 489: Guaranteeing.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Guaranteeing.png/28px-Guaranteeing.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Guarantee "Guarantee") Guarantee | [![Image 490: Threaten war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bc/Threaten_war.png/28px-Threaten_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Threaten_war.png) Threaten War | [![Image 491: Casus belli.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Casus_belli.png/28px-Casus_belli.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Casus_belli.png) Support Rebels | [![Image 492: Enforce peace.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7f/Enforce_peace.png/28px-Enforce_peace.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Enforce_peace.png) Enforce Peace | [![Image 493: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") Intervene in War |

## Maximum civilization value[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Maximum civilization value") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Maximum civilization value")]

_Main article: [Civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization\_value "Civilization value")_

Every country has a base [![Image 542: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**country civilization level**, visible on the ![Image 543: Overview](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f1/Menu_overview.png/28px-Menu_overview.png)[Nation Overview](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Beginner%27s_guide#Interacting_with_Your_Empire "Beginner's guide") panel, that determines the baseline maximum possible civilization level of every territory that the country owns. This value can be increased by a number of different modifiers, such as country's [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") type, positive [![Image 544: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization for [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") (up to **+10%** at [![Image 545: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") 100), [![Image 546: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")[Oratory Advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") (**+0.5%** per level), the national idea [![Image 547: Idea state religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/32/Idea_state_religion.png/28px-Idea_state_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Idea_state_religion.png)[State Religion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas#Religious "National ideas") (**+5%**), the [![Image 548: Gemstones](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/70/Goods_gems.png/28px-Goods_gems.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Gemstones "Gemstones") gemstones and [![Image 549: Glass](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Goods_glass.png/28px-Goods_glass.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Glass "Glass") glass capital surplus bonuses (**+5%** each), and various other more specific modifiers.

Note that the country civilization level is only the baseline maximum civilization; individual province and territory modifiers such as [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings"), [treasures](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treasure "Treasure"), [terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain "Terrain"), special modifiers, and capital bonuses awarded when creating a [formable nation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations") can push the maximum civilization value of any individual territory higher than the country civilization value, often much higher for large, built up cities.


============================================================
# Land_warfare
============================================================
Title: Land warfare - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/R9EnG_iUs5E/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator: Rome - Warfare Reformed

Load video

_**Imperator: Rome - Warfare Reformed**_. The art of warfare and how your armies can win wars in Imperator: Rome. Use strategy and tactics to overcome your enemies and conquer the known world!

**Land combat** in Imperator occurs when at least two [![Image 2: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army")**[armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army")** of [states](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/States "States") currently [![Image 3: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") at war (or otherwise hostile) with each other are in the same [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories"), and forms one of the main pillars of warfare in the game. Once engaged in battle, both sides will fight and deal damage to each other's [![Image 4: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength and [![Image 5: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale until one side is either forced to retreat or completely destroyed. The outcome of a battle depends on many factors, including the [![Image 6: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength") strength and [![Image 7: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale of each army, their [composition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"), [![Image 8: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander martial and ability, the local [terrain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_type "Terrain type"), modifiers such as [![Image 9: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") discipline and [![Image 10: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") experience, pure luck, and more.

Victorious armies accrue [warscore](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warscore "Warscore"), inflict greater losses, and deny the enemy the ability to [![Image 11: Siege.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Siege.png/28px-Siege.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege") besiege and occupy friendly territory (or initiate sieges and occupation of enemy territory). Typically, the side that can more consistently win battles and inflict proportionally greater losses on the enemy will emerge victorious in the war.

## Initiating a battle[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Initiating a battle") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Initiating a battle")]

A battle begins when two armies that are hostile to each other - typically because they belong to countries that are at [![Image 12: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, but also including circumstances such as a country's armies engaging invading [![Image 13: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") barbarian hordes - are in the same territory. If there are multiple armies in the territory that have the same owner, are on the same side of a war, or are at least co-belligerent with a hostile army that they are engaging with, they will be considered on the same **side** of the battle, and will fight together in the battle as though they were a single army. This is particularly relevant when fighting in an alliance where multiple allied armies need to join together to engage the enemy armies, or for larger countries with multiple [![Image 14: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy and [![Image 15: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion armies that cannot be merged together. [Attaching](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Actions "Army") armies to a leading allied or owned armies, which will make the attached army following the leader as closely as possible (though this may not work perfectly if the two armies have a different movement speed), can be a helpful aid in this.

In the rare event that there are three hostile sides with armies in the same territory, the first two sides to arrive will engage first, ignoring the third side. Once that battle is over, the side that won the first battle will immediately engage the third side in a new battle, while the defeated side retreats.

### Imminent battle[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Imminent battle") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Imminent battle")]

When there is an **imminent battle** that involves one of the country's armies - that is, there is an owned, allied, or enemy army that is currently moving into a territory where a battle will start assuming that it (as well as all the other nearby armies) is not given a new order - an alert will appear on the map indicating the imminent battle, as well as the estimated outcome of the battle given the circumstances and the relative strength of the potentially involved armies. The estimated outcome will give one out of five possible predictions:

Note that while the imminent battle alert takes into account all armies that are currently in or moving into the territory, it does not take into account any other nearby armies that are not currently moving into the territory but are close enough to realistically intervene. For instance, if the enemy has a large army in the area that might join the battle, engaging could be more risky than the alert indicates.

## Combat[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Combat") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Combat")]

### Battle chess board[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Battle chess board") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Battle chess board")]

In Imperator: Rome, the **battle chess board** is divided into squares, grouped into 2 rows, one for each side. The width of the rows depends on the territory's [![Image 16: Local combat width modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dd/Local_combat_width_modifier.png/28px-Local_combat_width_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Combat_width "Combat width")**combat width**, with a base value ranging from **16** to **40** depending on the territory's [terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_type "Terrain type") that can be reduced by permanent, pre-placed gate and mountain pass territory modifiers as well as [![Image 17: Earthworks](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Building_fortress_ramparts.png/28px-Building_fortress_ramparts.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Earthworks "Earthworks") Earthworks buildings that can be built in [![Image 18: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities. One [cohort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort "Cohort") fills one square, so the combat width of the territory controls how many cohorts can actually engage at a given time. If one of the armies has more cohorts than can fit into the combat width, then they will have to wait in reserve, unable to participate in battle unless a spot opens up (from a cohort breaking and retreating or being destroyed); this means that battles in territories with a smaller combat width will have more limited opportunities for flanking and somewhat blunt the advantages of having a larger army.

### Deployment[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Deployment") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Deployment")]

_Main article: [Deployment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deployment "Deployment")_

At the start of a battle, [cohorts](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohorts "Cohorts") from both armies deploy on the battlefield. Deployment may also happen during the battle when [cohorts](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohorts "Cohorts") retreat and are replaced by reserves or new armies join the battle (often referred as **reinforcement**).

The order and layout of deployment can be customized for each army by selecting preferred unit types and flank sizes in the army interface.

### Morale[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Morale") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Morale")]

[![Image 19: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**Morale** represents the willingness of an army's soldiers to fight. Every cohort in an army has an individual morale value, which can range from 0 to a base maximum of **3** (adjusted by the [![Image 20: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**Morale of Armies** modifier), which is then averaged over all the cohorts to give the army's overall morale. [![Image 21: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levies are raised at full morale, but [![Image 22: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions and [![Image 23: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries start at 0 morale when raised and will take some time before they are ready for battle. If a cohort has less than its maximum possible morale, it will gain a certain percentage of its maximum morale each month based on its [![Image 24: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery")**Army Morale Recovery** modifier. The army morale recovery modifier is highly dependent on the [![Image 25: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler's [![Image 26: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial skill which can be problematic for countries with a low martial, but can be increased at the cost of higher [![Image 27: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") maintenance with the [![Image 28: Unit reorganization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b9/Ability_unit_recovery.png/28px-Ability_unit_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Unit reorganization") Unit Reorganization unit ability.

When in battle, cohorts inflict damage to both morale and strength. Cohorts that lose all of their morale withdraw from the battle and will no longer fight, and if all of an army's cohorts have lost their morale it will be forced to concede the battle and retreat. Most battles are decided by which army loses all its morale and retreats first; it is rare for an army or cohort to fight all the way to the point of annihilation unless it is already weak or is completely overwhelmed. This can allow an army to emerge victorious over another army that is larger or nominally stronger if it has higher morale or is otherwise more effective at inflicting morale damage, and can be particularly decisive in the early game.

### Battle[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Battle") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Battle")]

_Main article: [Damage formula](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Damage\_formula "Damage formula")_

Each day, each [cohort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohorts "Cohorts") in the first battle row of each side will attack one other cohort within its range. A unit type's range in squares is equal to its maneuver value, and the opposing cohorts inflict damage to both each other's [![Image 29: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength and [![Image 30: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale.

Casualties and morale depletion are applied at the end of each day to each cohort taking part in the battle. The _Attacker-role_ and _Defender-role_ are calculated the same way, but may be subject to some [modifiers](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Damage_modifiers).

Each cohort has separate base values for strength-damage and morale-damage. These are subject to a random [![Image 31: Chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Chance.png/28px-Chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Chance.png) dice roll from **1-6** every **5** days, which is then combined by a large variety of [other modifiers](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Damage_modifiers) to determine how much damage each cohort gives and takes. Because of this chance element, the exact outcome of every battle is to some extent unpredictable, though there is a limit to how much impact luck can have (if the difference in power is large enough, the stronger army will always triumph over the weaker one).

At the end of each day, units with less than [![Image 32: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**0.25** Morale disengage until the end of the battle. On the next day, other units or reserves fill in the ranks to take their place. The day after the last available unit from either side disengages, combat ends and the defeated army is forced into a [![Image 33: Retreating.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Retreating.png/28px-Retreating.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Retreat "Retreat") shattered retreat, unless it has been completely destroyed. There is no pursuit phase where the victors run down and inflict heavy casualties upon the broken forces - once an army or cohort has retreated, it will no longer take any losses (unless the entire army is subsequently [overrun](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Stack_wiping)).

#### Ordered retreat[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Ordered retreat") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Ordered retreat")]

After a few days of combat have passed, it becomes possible to order a [![Image 34: Retreating.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Retreating.png/28px-Retreating.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Retreat "Retreat") retreat by either ordering an army to move to another territory or using the [![Image 35: Shattered retreat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Shattered_retreat.png/28px-Shattered_retreat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Order full retreat") order full retreat unit ability. At that point, the battle is considered lost with a corresponding loss of [warscore](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warscore "Warscore") and [![Image 36: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") commander popularity. Ordering the army to move will force it to retreat to that territory - even if it is very far away (but still accessible) - while using the [![Image 37: Shattered retreat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Shattered_retreat.png/28px-Shattered_retreat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Order full retreat") order full retreat unit ability will cause the army retreat to an automatically chosen location (similar to if it was forced to retreat from lack of morale) and will additionally cause the retreating army to lose **50%** of its current [![Image 38: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength. This harsh penalty means that [![Image 39: Shattered retreat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Shattered_retreat.png/28px-Shattered_retreat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Order full retreat") ordering a full retreat should generally only be used if a normal retreat is not possible, and the alternative is the complete annihilation of the army.

Each army in a combat engagement can be separately ordered to retreat by selecting it from the outliner. Any remaining armies continue fighting and deploying reserves to fill in abandoned positions. Because of this possibility it might prove advantageous to group [![Image 40: Unit archers.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8e/Unit_archers.png/28px-Unit_archers.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Archers "Archers") archers (**+25%** morale damage) or [![Image 41: Unit light infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Unit_light_infantry.png/28px-Unit_light_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Infantry "Light Infantry") light infantry (**-25%** morale damage) into armies of their own and have them retreat prematurely.

The base number of days before a retreat can be ordered is **5**, which can be reduced by the [![Image 42: Shattered retreat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Shattered_retreat.png/28px-Shattered_retreat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Retreat_delay "Retreat delay")**Retreat Delay** modifier (given by the [![Image 43: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Tactical Withdrawal [martial invention](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") and [![Image 44: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonders with the [![Image 45: Gw effect retaliation doctrine.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Gw_effect_retaliation_doctrine.png/28px-Gw_effect_retaliation_doctrine.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Gw_effect_retaliation_doctrine.png) Retaliation Procedures effect) to a capped minimum of **1** day.

### Stack wiping[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Stack wiping") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Stack wiping")]

Stack wiping means that one side of a battle gets totally wiped out, losing all of its men. This usually happens when one side is much stronger than the other. The exact rules are:

*   Total manpower is greater than 10x of the enemy manpower. This is checked at start and also at end of the battle.
*   Enemy can't deploy any cohorts. This happens when all cohorts have less than 0.25 morale.
*   Enemy is defeated before retreat is possible and total manpower is greater than 2x of the enemy manpower.

If both sides would stack wipe then only the defender stack wipes.

### Tips: Largest impact[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Tips: Largest impact") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Tips: Largest impact")]

In summary, the variables that impact the combat the most are:

*   For morale damage - current morale. A unit at full morale does 5x morale damage of a unit that is on low maintenance.
*   With small armies - flanking/maneuver. Flanking with 5 horse archers on each side makes enemy side units take 6x damage, while horse archers themselves stay at full morale and manpower.
*   General skill and terrain. A 5 pip advantage (e.g. 11 skill general vs 1 skill general) can increase the base damage by as much as 100%, while harsh terrain can reduce an attacker's damage by as much as 80%.
*   Heavily countering unit types. E.g. Light Cavalry vs Heavy Infantry does only 50% normal damage.
*   Low manpower. A battered non-consolidated 250-man cohort will only do 50% of the damage of a full 500-man cohort, while still taking a full space on the front line.

## Damage modifiers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Damage modifiers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Damage modifiers")]

_Main article: [Damage formula](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Damage\_formula "Damage formula")_

In addition to the random [![Image 46: Chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Chance.png/28px-Chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Chance.png) dice roll for each side, there are a large number of modifiers that affect the damage that each cohort inflicts and takes in battle. Modifiers that affect the dice roll itself generally combine additively, while modifiers that modify damage generally combine multiplicatively. In general, most of these multipliers can be simply thought of as increasing or decreasing a cohort's effectiveness in battle, with the distinctions between these modifiers being largely about which cohorts and circumstances they are applicable in and how a country or army can acquire them.

### Commander martial[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Commander martial") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Commander martial")]

During a battle, the army with the higher [![Image 47: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") martial [![Image 48: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander gets a **+1** bonus to the dice roll for every **2** points of difference in martial skill, rounded down. If the difference is less than 2 or the two commanders have the same martial, then there is no bonus (or penalty) for either side.

### Terrain modifiers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Terrain modifiers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Terrain modifiers")]

_Main article: [Terrain types](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain\_types "Terrain types")_

When engaging in battle, certain terrain types and features are favourable for the defender and give penalties for the attacker. This penalty is applied directly to the [![Image 49: Chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Chance.png/28px-Chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Chance.png) dice roll that determines the [base amount of damage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Damage_formula#Base_damage "Damage formula") that the attacking army inflicts. Note that terrain effects are at best zero and cannot be positive, and that the defender can never suffer terrain penalties. Normally the defender is the army that arrived the territory first, but this is inverted if the army that is already in the territory is currently [besieging](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege") an enemy [![Image 50: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") fort - in this case, the army that is arriving in the territory is considered the defender, and the army that is currently undertaking the siege will take any attacker penalties. This, for instance, makes besieging mountain forts dangerous as an enemy army can sweep in at any time to attack the besieging army and force them to engage in combat with a significant penalty.

Certain terrain types always have combat penalties for the attacker:

The following penalties stack on top of any terrain effect:

Note that the river and strait crossing penalties can be negated if the attacking army has at least [![Image 51: Engineer cohort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Engineer_cohort.png/28px-Engineer_cohort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Engineers "Engineers") 1 engineer cohort for every 10 cohorts in the army.

Thus, the maximum possible penalty is **-4** (e.g. a naval landing into mountains).

### Discipline[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Discipline") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Discipline")]

[![Image 52: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") A cohort's **discipline** represents how well organized its soldiers are and how well it can hold its formation against enemy attack. Discipline increases the overall damage a cohort does against enemy soldiers, and so cohorts with higher discipline are more effective in battle. Along with [![Image 53: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale, discipline is one of the most important modifiers that determines the quality of a cohort and army. Note that while discipline increases the damage done by a cohort against other cohorts, it does not decrease the damage that the cohort takes from the enemy army.

A general country-level discipline modifiers apply to all of a country's cohorts, and these come primarily from [![Image 54: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, [deity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deity "Deity") modifiers, and [![Image 55: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omens, as well as various more specific script modifiers. Certain countrywide discipline modifiers, particularly those from [![Image 56: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions and [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_good "Trade good"), are applied only to a specific [unit type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"). Finally, there are army-specific modifiers from [![Image 57: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trait "Trait"), [![Image 58: Phalera helmetman.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Phalera_helmetman.png/28px-Phalera_helmetman.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction") distinctions, and army modifiers more generally that may modify the discipline of all the cohorts in the army or only those of a certain [unit type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"). All discipline modifiers that are currently applied country-wide can be viewed in the Cohorts tab of the [ledger](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Ledger&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ledger (page does not exist)"), and the total discipline modifier of any individual cohort can be viewed by hovering over its name in the unit view.

Discipline has a base value of **100%**, which is adjusted by the sum of all discipline modifiers that apply to that cohort before being applied to the [damage calculation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Damage_formula "Damage formula") - for instance, a cohort with a [![Image 59: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline")**+20%** discipline will have an overall discipline of **120%**, and the amount of damage that it inflicts in battle will be multiplied by **120%** (i.e. multiplied by **1.20**). Most sources of discipline stack additively - for instance, two sources of **+10%** discipline will give a final modifier of **+20%** discipline. The exception to this rule is the **+10%** modifier from [![Image 60: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") cohort loyalty, which is applied multiplicatively to the entire discipline value (i.e. the discipline value is multiplied by **1.10**) after all the other modifiers have been applied. For instance, a cohort that is loyal to a character and has a **+20%** discipline modifier from other sources will have a total discipline of **120% x 1.10 = 132%**, not 130% as might otherwise be expected.

### Combat bonus[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Combat bonus") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Combat bonus")]

[![Image 61: Combat bonus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/47/Combat_bonus.png/28px-Combat_bonus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Combat_bonus "Combat bonus") Certain [![Image 62: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, [![Image 63: Phalera helmetman.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Phalera_helmetman.png/28px-Phalera_helmetman.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction") distinctions, and a few other modifiers give a **combat bonus** for a certain terrain type, which can be either a country or commander/army modifier and apply to either all cohorts or only cohorts of a specific [unit type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"). A combat bonus acts as a multiplier on the damage done by cohorts against enemy cohorts when engaged in battle in a territory that has that terrain type - for instance, a **+15%** combat bonus on [![Image 64: Hills](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Terrain_hills.png/28px-Terrain_hills.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Hills") hills terrain will multiply the damage done by the cohort by **1.15** whenever fighting in a battle on a hills terrain territory. Note that, like [![Image 65: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline"), a combat bonus does not decrease the amount of damage a cohort takes on that terrain type.

### Unit type damage[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Unit type damage") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Unit type damage")]

_Main article: [Land units#Unit type combat effectiveness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land\_units#Unit\_type\_combat\_effectiveness "Land units")_

Many unit types are stronger or weaker against certain other unit types, which is represented by a multiplier to damage inflicted by all cohorts of that unit type to all cohorts of the other unit type (represented ingame by the **Vs [unit type]** modifier); for instance, all [![Image 66: Unit heavy infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Unit_heavy_infantry.png/28px-Unit_heavy_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heavy_Infantry "Heavy Infantry") heavy infantry cohorts get a **+20%** modifier to damage against all [![Image 67: Unit light infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Unit_light_infantry.png/28px-Unit_light_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Infantry "Light Infantry") cohorts regardless of circumstance. Unlike most other cohort damage modifiers, the modifier to the damage a unit type inflicts or takes against other unit types is fixed and cannot be changed by any country or army modifiers.

### Combat tactics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Combat tactics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Combat tactics")]

_"Combat tactics" redirects here. For naval combat tactics, see [Naval warfare § Combat tactics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Naval\_warfare#Combat\_tactics "Naval warfare")._

Every army can be assigned a particular **combat tactic** that they will attempt to use in battle against other armies. The available combat tactics add a [rock-paper-scissors-Spock-lizard](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock%E2%80%93paper%E2%80%93scissors#Additional_weapons "wikipedia:Rock–paper–scissors")-mechanic to battles - each tactic is strong against two other tactics, and poor against two others. Combat tactics have the following properties:

*   The modifier applies to the damage done.
*   The positive modifier applies only to certain [unit types](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units#List_of_unit_types "Land units"), and can be different for each type. The displayed "total effectiveness" of a tactic is the average of the tactic's effectiveness over each of the army's cohorts.
*   The negative modifier applies fully regardless of army's composition.
*   The casualties modifier affects the strength damage done in the battle applies to both armies.
*   Every army always has a combat tactic chosen from the available list, with the default being [![Image 68: Shock action](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Tactic_shock_action.png/28px-Tactic_shock_action.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Combat_tactics "Shock action") Shock Action.
*   The combat tactic can be changed at any time prior to a battle at no cost or cooldown.
*   An army's combat tactic cannot be changed once a battle has been started, but can be overridden if a larger allied army subsequently joins the battle.

The effect of any specific tactic is not overwhelmingly powerful. See [Tips: Largest impact](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Tips:_Largest_impact) above to compare the magnitude of other combat modifiers.

#### General tactics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: General tactics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: General tactics")]

These five tactics are available to all countries at all times.
