### Offense and defense[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Offense and defense") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Offense and defense")]

[![Image 188: Offense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Offense.png/28px-Offense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Offense "Offense")**Offense** modifies the overall damage inflicted by a cohort in battle (similar to [![Image 189: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") discipline), while [![Image 190: Defence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/41/Defence.png/28px-Defence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Defense "Defense")**defense** modifies the overall damage that a cohort takes in battle (similar to [![Image 191: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") experience). Both offense and defense modifiers are always specific to a certain [unit type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units"), and like most damage modifiers are typically given by [![Image 192: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, [![Image 193: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, [![Image 194: Phalera helmetman.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Phalera_helmetman.png/28px-Phalera_helmetman.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction") distinctions. Note that the total offense of the attacking cohort combines additively, not multiplicatively, to the total defense of the defending cohort. For instance, if a cohort with **+20%** offense is attacking a cohort with **+10%** defense, the overall modifier on damage will be **20% - 10% = 10%** (not **(120% x 90%) - 100% = 8%** if they combined multiplicatively).

### Experience[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Experience") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Experience")]

_"Experience" redirects here. For the related nationwide value used to purchase military traditions, see [![Image 195: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military\_experience.png/28px-Military\_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military\_experience "Military experience")[Military experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military\_experience "Military experience")._

[![Image 196: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") Every cohort has an **experience** value out of 100% that represents how well trained the cohort is. Every point of experience gives **-0.3%** damage reduction, for a maximum of **-30%** damage taken in combat at 100% experience. Archers have a slightly increased damage reduction because of extra morale damage taken. Similarly Heavy Infantry, Light Infantry and War Elephants have a slightly decreased damage reduction.

The [![Image 197: Global cohort start experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Global_cohort_start_experience.png/28px-Global_cohort_start_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_starting_experience "Cohort starting experience")**Cohort Starting Experience** and [![Image 198: Starting experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Starting_experience.png/28px-Starting_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Starting experience")**Starting Experience** modifiers increase the experience that newly raised levies and newly recruited legion cohorts start with (with the [![Image 199: Starting experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Starting_experience.png/28px-Starting_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Starting experience") starting experience modifier also increasing the experience of newly built [![Image 200: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") ships), and when focused on can add up to a substantial amount of experience.

After a cohort is raised, one of the main way it accumulates experience is through fighting battles. After each battle, every cohort involved will gain experience based on the number of casualties their side took, giving the following formula:

![Image 201: {\displaystyle {\text{Experience Gain}}=40\cdot {\frac {\text{Allied Losses}}{\text{Total Allied Soldiers in Battle}}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/f32c84a3fd110154c3f34381757b4c9b5ed137d4)
Cohorts can also passively gain experience through the [![Image 202: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience")**Monthly Experience Gain** modifier, which gives a flat modifier to the change in experience per month. The most common way to increase cohort experience this way is to use the [![Image 203: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army")**[Drill Army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Army")** action for [![Image 204: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions, which gives a **+2.50%** monthly experience gain bonus (at the cost of increased [![Image 205: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") army maintenance and [![Image 206: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") cohort loyalty gain chance).

Every month, cohort experience also decays according to the cohort's total [![Image 207: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/28px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay")**Experience Decay** modifier, with a base of [![Image 208: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/28px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay")**+4.50%** experience decay per month. This decay can be reduced by various [ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Idea "Idea"), [deities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deity "Deity"), [![Image 209: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, and [![Image 210: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, but is capped at **0%** (it is not possible for cohorts to gain experience through negative decay). Note that unlike the [![Image 211: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") monthly experience gain modifier, which is a flat increase or decrease in experience points per month, [![Image 212: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/28px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay") experience decay reduces cohort experience by a certain percentage of its current experience per month. For instance, a cohort with [![Image 213: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") 30% experience will have a base experience decay of 4.50% × 30 = 1.35, and assuming no other changes will have its experience reduced to [![Image 214: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") 28.65% at the beginning of the next month. This means that generally, cohort experience will decay much faster at higher values than lower values. There is no extra experience decay when cohorts are reinforcing.

Cohort experience is converted into [![Image 215: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience in two different ways. The average cohort experience across all armies increases the country's passive monthly [![Image 216: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience gain by **+0.006** per point (up to a maximum of [![Image 217: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**+0.60** military experience if all cohorts have [![Image 218: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") 100% experience), which is mostly relevant for [![Image 219: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions (especially during peacetime). As well, [![Image 220: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies that have been raised for at least **8** months will grant [![Image 221: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**0.015** military experience per point of [![Image 222: Experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") cohort experience for each disbanded cohort.

### Strength and morale damage[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Strength and morale damage") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Strength and morale damage")]

While most of the other modifiers described here apply equally to both [![Image 223: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale and [![Image 224: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength damage, certain [unit types](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units") have modifiers that affect the amount of damage they take that apply to only one or the other, through the [![Image 225: Morale damage taken.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/Morale_damage_taken.png/28px-Morale_damage_taken.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_damage_taken "Morale damage taken")**Morale Damage Taken** and [![Image 226: Strength damage taken.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a5/Strength_damage_taken.png/28px-Strength_damage_taken.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Strength_damage_taken "Strength damage taken")**Strength Damage Taken** modifiers respectively. For instance, [![Image 227: Unit war elephants.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Unit_war_elephants.png/28px-Unit_war_elephants.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_Elephants "War Elephants") war elephants have a **-50%** Strength Damage Taken modifier and a **+20%** Morale Damage Taken modifier, which means that they lose less strength in battle than other unit types but lose morale more quickly. Similarly, the [![Image 228: Morale damage done.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1c/Morale_damage_done.png/28px-Morale_damage_done.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_damage_done "Morale damage done")**Morale Damage Done** and [![Image 229: Strength damage done.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0e/Strength_damage_done.png/28px-Strength_damage_done.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Strength_damage_done "Strength damage done")**Strength Damage Done** modifiers affect the amount of morale and strength damage a cohort inflicts, respectively.

## Post-battle effects[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Post-battle effects") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Post-battle effects")]

Besides the damage inflicted on each army, battles also have direct political and personal consequences for army's commanders and the war as a whole.

### Retreat[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Retreat") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Retreat")]

When an army is defeated in battle or is ordered to abandon the battlefield, it will attempt to [![Image 230: Retreating.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Retreating.png/28px-Retreating.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Retreat "Retreat")**retreat** to a nearby friendly territory. When retreating, an army will not respond to any commands and cannot be diverted from moving to its target retreat destination, but will not engage any enemy armies (even if it passes through the same territory as one). As well, an army will recover [![Image 231: Morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Morale.png/28px-Morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") morale while retreating, but will not reinforce any lost [![Image 232: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_strength "Cohort strength") strength. A retreat ends once the retreating army has reached its chosen destination - note that if there is an enemy army at that destination, it will immediately engage the army.

A retreating army will usually choose to retreat at least 7-8 territories away, but is still subject to movement rules. This means that it is possible for an army to be trapped on small islands or behind forts and be unable to retreat more than a short distance, which leaves them vulnerable to being hunted down by nearby armies and destroyed right after their retreat ends, as they will be weakened from the battle and not have enough time to regain much strength or morale.

### Warscore[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Warscore") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Warscore")]

_Main article: [Warscore](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warscore "Warscore")_

The side that wins the battle - either by forcing the other side to retreat first, or completely destroying all the enemy armies involved in the battle - gains warscore depending on how many casualties each side took in the battle as a proportion of the number of soldiers involved. The exact formula can be calculated as:

![Image 233: {\displaystyle {\text{Warscore}}=3\cdot \left({\frac {\text{Allied Losses}}{\text{Total Allied Soldiers in Battle}}}+{\frac {\text{Enemy Casualties}}{\text{Total Enemy Soldiers in Battle}}}\right)}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/fe6e81418fba5c9c38efa67854c27dd40dfaa726)
Counterintuitively, this formula means that the winning side gets more warscore if they take more casualties (as well as from inflicting more casualties on the enemy side). In particular, it means that a stackwipe with zero losses on one side will give that side **+3** warscore, while a battle where both sides are nearly wiped out will give up to **+6** warscore.

The maximum possible warscore from all battles (including both land and naval battles) is **25**. Superiority [wargoals](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wargoal "Wargoal") will also give additional ticking warscore to the side that has a net total of at least **+10** warscore from battles.

### War exhaustion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=26 "Edit section: War exhaustion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: War exhaustion")]

_Main article: [War exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War\_exhaustion "War exhaustion")_

Once a battle concludes, each side takes [![Image 234: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion based on their number of casualties as a proportion of their total maximum army strength. The total maximum army strength is calculated as how large the country's army would be (not including [![Image 235: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries or other special armies) if all of its [![Image 236: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions and [![Image 237: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies were at full strength, which is equivalent to the following formula:

![Image 238: {\displaystyle {\text{War Exhaustion Gain}}=3\cdot {\frac {\text{Casualties}}{500\cdot ({\text{Number of Legion Cohorts + Total Levy Strength}})}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/821864ffa1800cb126ec6fa02835aa32a0563a1a)
Note that the total levy strength is equal to how many levy cohorts the country would have if they were all raised at that moment. This is not necessarily the same as the number of levy cohorts that the country actually has currently raised, since population growth or decline in the region means that the strength of the levy may have changed since the levy was raised. Overall, this means that less populous countries and those with a smaller total army strength take more war exhaustion from the same amount of casualties than more populous country would.

### Commander popularity[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&veaction=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Commander popularity") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Land_warfare&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Commander popularity")]

_Main article: [Popularity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")_

An army's [![Image 239: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander is considered personally responsible for the outcome of any battles, and will gain or lose [![Image 240: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity depending on the outcome of battles they are involved in. After any battle ends, the commander of the winning side will gain popularity and the commander of the losing side will lose popularity based on the casualties that they have inflicted on the other side, according to the following formula:

![Image 241: {\displaystyle {\text{Popularity Change}}=4\cdot {\frac {\text{Enemy Casualties}}{\text{Total Enemy Soldiers in Battle}}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/f3deb75968133eed5531acdfbb1253c0af7f194d)
Note that for the losing side, the result of the formula is multiplied by **-1** (i.e. made negative) in order to get the amount of popularity that the losing commander loses. As well, note that while the winning side gains popularity depending on how many casualties they inflicted on the losing side, the losing commander loses popularity depending on how many casualties the winning side (not the losing side) took. As with warscore, this somewhat counterintuitively means that a defeated commander will lose more popularity if their side inflicts more casualties, as long as they still end up losing the battle in the end.


============================================================
# Government
============================================================
Title: Government - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government

Published Time: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 00:50:40 GMT

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/AKy1Ngodf4I/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator Tutorial - Governments - pt 6

Load video

Imperator: Rome tutorial #6 - Governments.

[![Image 2: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") The **government** of a [country](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country "Country") defines its basic political framework and power structure, including how the [![Image 3: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler of the country is selected, the possible [![Image 4: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws that can be enacted, the amount and category of [national ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas") that can be adopted, the available [![Image 5: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") offices that characters can be appointed to, and more. Each government form has its own set of modifiers and characteristics and falls into one of three basic types - **republic**, **monarchy**, or **tribe**, each with its own set of distinctive mechanics. Over the course of the game, it is possible to change the government of the country from one form to another by [decision](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions") or [mission task](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions").

## Republics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Republics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Republics")]

[![Image 6](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e7/Government_republic_screen.png/500px-Government_republic_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Government_republic_screen.png)

The government interface for republics

**Republics** are defined first and foremost by the sharing of power between its citizens or upper class, depending on the type. Rulers are chosen by election based on a character's popularity and prominence and serve only for a fixed, usually relatively short term, meaning that republics will tend to cycle through different characters quite quickly and are not quite as involved in playing dynastic games. Republics are also marked by the presence of a Senate whose approval is required for most government actions, made up of a number of factions who may give various bonuses but also seek to advance their goals and interests.

Republican governments are most common among the Greek city-states as well as the Italic and Punic realms of the Western Mediterranean, but can also be found in the emporiums of the Horn of Africa and the Persian Gulf as well as the warrior confederations of Punjab.

### Republican governments[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Republican governments") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Republican governments")]

Unless specified otherwise, all Republics have the following base characteristics:

| Republic type | Idea slots | Base modifiers | Government bonus | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Aristocratic Republic | [![Image 7: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 2 Military, [![Image 8: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | **Has co-ruler** | * [![Image 9: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") Character loyalty: **+5** * [![Image 10: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National freeman happiness: **+8%** | _Whilst outwardly democratic, an Aristocratic Republic limits leadership to those who enjoy high status._ |
| Democratic Republic | [![Image 11: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 12: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 13: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | [![Image 14: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**National [civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Civilization_Value "Population"):****+15%**, **Minimum election age 25** | * [![Image 15: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National freeman happiness: **+8%** | _This system of government gives all eligible citizens a voice, through the election of representatives to a senate, or council._ |
| Oligarchic Republic | [![Image 16: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 17: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 2 Oratory | **Minimum election age 40** | * [![Image 18: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") Monthly wages: **−10%** * [![Image 19: Pop citizen happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") National citizen happiness: **+8%** | _An Oligarchic Republic restricts positions of power to a wealthy or powerful minority._ |
| Plutocratic Republic | [![Image 20: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 2 Civic, [![Image 21: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 22: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") Capital import routes: **+1** * [![Image 23: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") National commerce income: **+10%** | _Primarily concerned with the acquisition of wealth, Plutocratic Republics can be found ruling many of the coastal City States throughout the world._ |
| Theocratic Republic | [![Image 24: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 25: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 2 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 26: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") Omen power: **+15%** * [![Image 27: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness") State religion happiness: **+10%** | _The priesthood will enshrine democracy into the very tenets of our faith!_ |
| Athenian Republic | [![Image 28: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 29: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 30: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 31: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | [![Image 32: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**National [civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Civilization_Value "Population"):****+25%**, **Minimum election age 25** | * [![Image 33: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Changing_Laws "Enact law cost") Enact law cost: **-25%** * [![Image 34: Pop citizen happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") National citizen happiness: **+8%** | _The Athenian republic is known far and wide for its integrity and strength._ |

### The Senate[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Senate") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Senate")]

The **Senate** (which may go by a number of other names, such as the **Adirim**, **Ekklesia**, **Gerousia**, **Sangha**, or **Assembly**) consists of **100** seats with each seat belonging to one of **three** factions. Every adult character in a republic is part of one of the republic's factions based on which faction they have the highest [conviction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conviction "Conviction") for, and each faction has a [leader](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Party_leader "Party leader"), generally the most [![Image 35: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominent character that supports that faction. Each character in a faction contributes a certain amount of [![Image 36: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence")**senatorial influence**, calculated as each character's [![Image 37: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base adjusted by their [![Image 38: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence")**senate influence** modifier. The amount of seats each faction has, their [![Image 39: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control")**senate control**, which determines how strong and influential the faction, will then gradually drift over time to the proportion of the total [![Image 40: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence in the country that the faction members hold, meaning that the strength of factions will swing significantly based on the power of its membership. Notably, [![Image 41: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence is significantly decreased for [![Image 42: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanders and [![Image 43: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governors, which means that assigning those offices to powerful members of a faction can be a good way to significantly decrease the power of a disapproving faction and swing the Senate in the government's favour.

#### Ruler elections[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ruler elections") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ruler elections")]

When the ruler (and co-ruler) of a republic dies or finishes their term, his or her successor is elected by the Senate, with the eligible character with the highest [![Image 44: Succession](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession")**succession support** becoming the new ruler (and the eligible character with the second highest [![Image 45: Succession](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession") succession support elected co-ruler, for government types that have that position).

Every character is supported by a certain number of seats from each faction, which is a proportion of the [![Image 46: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control") total number of seats the faction holds in the Senate calculated according to the following factors:

A character's total succession support is then the sum of the seats that support them across all three factions. As factions strongly favour their faction members, especially their leaders, and the total succession support a faction gives depends on their [![Image 47: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control") number of seats, factions with stronger control in the Senate will tend have more of their faction members elected as [![Image 48: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler. Electing faction member as the [![Image 49: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") gives a particular countrywide bonus depending on the faction, but the ruling faction will also expect to have its [objectives](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Faction_objectives) fulfilled during the term, which may have severe consequences if their agenda is not passed.

#### Senate approval[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Senate approval") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Senate approval")]

Every faction has an [![Image 50: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**approval** value that represents how much support there is within the faction for the government's current policies. Every character in a faction will get [![Image 51: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**+0.1** loyalty for every point of approval above **50**, and [![Image 52: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-0.3** loyalty for every point of approval below **50**. Faction approval will change each month according to a wide variety of factors, different for each faction, as well as the [![Image 53: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval")**party approval** modifier. Many actions and [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), as well as completing [![Image 54: Faction objective](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Political_parties.png/28px-Political_parties.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Faction_objectives "Faction objective") faction objectives, can also immediately change faction approval.

The overall [![Image 55: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**Support in the Senate** is sum of all faction approvals weighted by their [![Image 56: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control") number of controlled seats, and determines how many seats will vote in favour of the government's actions. A faction's support will slowly change from month to month depending on a wide variety of factors differing for each faction, and is also impacted by changing [![Image 57: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, many [character interactions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_interaction "Character interaction"), and certain [diplomatic actions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy "Diplomacy").

There are three different categories of actions which require senate approval, largely the same as those that impact approval:

*   Changing [![Image 58: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")
*   Many character interactions, such as banishing, imprisoning, etc.
*   All diplomatic interactions with other nations

The senate will completely block an action if fewer than [![Image 59: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**30** senate seats support it, and will pass it without issue if there is at least [![Image 60: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**51** Senate support. If between 30 and 51 senators back the action, it can be forced through at the cost of around [![Image 61: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**+0.67** tyranny for each point of support less than 51 (to a maximum of [![Image 62: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**14** tyranny), which can quickly become debilitatingly high if actions are forced through too often.

#### Faction objectives[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Faction objectives") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Faction objectives")]

Every ruler term, each faction will have its own [![Image 63: Faction objective](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Political_parties.png/28px-Political_parties.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Faction_objectives "Faction objective")**objective** or **agenda** that it wishes to have passed. These objectives vary widely from changing [![Image 64: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, appointing certain characters to [![Image 65: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") office, declaring [![Image 66: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, developing certain [provinces](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), increasing [![Image 67: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability, and more. Completing a faction's objectives is generally the fastest way to gain [![Image 68: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") Senate approval, though they may be costly to do.

If a faction's objective is completed, it will gain either [![Image 69: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**10** or **20** approval by the faction (beyond any approval gained from making the actions required for the objective), after which the faction will have no objective until the next ruler election. The ruling faction will expect to have its objectives completed during while in government, and if their objective has not yet been met and the country has at least [![Image 70: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**50** Senate approval they will eventually pass their demands through the Senate anyways, forcing the country to choose between accepting the fulfillment of the objective or using the **consular veto** to stop it and lose [![Image 71: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**50** approval from the ruling faction. Either way, once the faction's agenda is forced it will be considered either completed or aborted and the faction will have no objective until the next election.

All possible objectives and the factions that can have them are listed in the table below.

| Objective | [![Image 72: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs | [![Image 73: Party traditionalist.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4a/Party_traditionalist.png/28px-Party_traditionalist.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traditionalists "Traditionalists") Traditionalists | [![Image 74: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") Democrats | [![Image 75: Party optimates.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/37/Party_optimates.png/28px-Party_optimates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Optimates "Optimates") Optimates | [![Image 76: Party boni.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0a/Party_boni.png/28px-Party_boni.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Boni "Boni") Boni | [![Image 77: Party populares.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Party_populares.png/28px-Party_populares.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Populares "Populares") Populares | Notes |

| [![Image 111: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") Reconcile | [![Image 112: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | [![Image 113: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | [![Image 114: Yes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/77/Yes.png/28px-Yes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Yes.png "Yes") | [![Image 115: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | [![Image 116: Yes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/77/Yes.png/28px-Yes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Yes.png "Yes") | [![Image 117: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | Will only fire if the country has at least [![Image 118: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**20** tyranny for [![Image 119: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") democrats, or [![Image 120: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**10** for [![Image 121: Party boni.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0a/Party_boni.png/28px-Party_boni.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Boni "Boni") boni. Requires tyranny to be reduced by **20** for [![Image 122: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") democrats, or **10** for [![Image 123: Party boni.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0a/Party_boni.png/28px-Party_boni.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Boni "Boni") boni. |

#### General factions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: General factions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: General factions")]

These factions are present in all countries except for [![Image 267: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome"), who has its own special set.

protecting the interests of citizens and non-citizens alike to their own advantage. They are a foil to the domination of the entrenched Oligarchs, though the motives of their politicians are often equally devious and ambitious._ |

#### Roman factions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Roman factions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Roman factions")]

### Dictators[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Dictators") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Dictators")]

In times of [![Image 351: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, a republic can appoint their ruler as a temporary **dictator** in order to deal with the expediencies of war more quickly and efficiently without having to seek Senatorial approval for their actions. Using the **Appoint Dictator** character interaction requires at least [![Image 352: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**75** Senate support and costs [![Image 353: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**10** tyranny, giving **+5%** Morale of Armies and [![Image 354: Succession](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Election term duration")**+1** Election Term Duration as well as bypassing the need for Senate approval while the dictatorship is active.

When the war or the ruler's term ends, the dictator is expected to return his or her powers and restore control to the Senate. However, depending on the dictator's faction, [![Image 355: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trait "Trait"), and the country's size, the dictator may decide to retain their powers anyways and extend their dictatorship and ruler term beyond its normal limit, with temporary penalties to [![Image 356: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty and [![Image 357: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny. As the extended dictatorship proceeds, the dictator will eventually push for the enacting the [![Image 358: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") Princeps Civitas or [![Image 359: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") Lifetime Elections law, essentially making them dictator for life if accepted. If an extended dictatorship is becoming problematic, the dictator can be forced to step down with the **Return Dictatorship** character interaction if their [![Image 360: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity falls low enough or the country's [![Image 361: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability is high enough at the cost of [![Image 362: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**25** stability. Alternatively, the **Murder Dictator** interaction can be used to remove an illegal dictator at any time, with the same [![Image 363: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny and [![Image 364: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion costs as a usual assassination plus an additional cost of [![Image 365: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**30** stability.

While the republic is under a dictatorship - whether legally granted or illegally extended - the [dictatorship invention tree](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions#Dictatorship_tree "Oratory inventions") becomes available, allowing the country to take [![Image 366: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions that entrench the power of the dictator by revising the constitution, extending their term, and finally instituting a hereditary line of succession to reform the republic into a [monarchical](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy")**Dictatorship**, either by pushing through reforms with sufficient [![Image 367: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs or [![Image 368: Party populares.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Party_populares.png/28px-Party_populares.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Populares "Populares") Populares support or forcing through demands with a [![Image 369: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war.

### Offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Offices")]

_Main article: [Position#Republic\_offices](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Republic\_offices "Position")_

In addition to the usual three offices, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") have offices for [![Image 370: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval") party approval, [![Image 371: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods") divine sacrifice cost, and [![Image 372: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change.

### Interactions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Interactions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Interactions")]

Republics can empower any of their three factions for a certain bonus at the cost of [![Image 433: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence and either [![Image 434: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny, [![Image 435: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, or [![Image 436: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") as well as increasing that faction's [![Image 437: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") approval at the expense of the other factions, but this will also decrease the [![Image 438: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of a certain pop class or group. Depending on the law enacted in the Legislative Body/Assembly Endorsement category, there are may also be available interactions to get improve governor attributes or get claims on neighbouring states.

## Monarchies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Monarchies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Monarchies")]

[![Image 525](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Government_monarchy_screen.png/500px-Government_monarchy_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Government_monarchy_screen.png)

The government interface for monarchies

In a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), authority lies entirely in the hands of the ruler who controls the government and can generally do as he or she wishes, so long as the rest of the realm remains loyal. Monarchs sit for life with a consort to assist them and succession runs entirely along familial lines, with the throne inherited from parent to child or from one sibling to the next depending on the exact succession law. This concentration of power in a single person, however, means that much more attention must be paid to the legitimacy of the current ruler as the country sees it as well as fending off the threats of pretenders and claimants who would drive the realm into civil war in order to claim the throne for themselves.

Monarchies are common throughout the east, from the many realms of the Diadochi to the Indian dynasties spanning its north and south, as well as the smaller states of Sogdiana and the Tarim Basin and the ancient kingdoms of Nubia and Ethiopia. Some, such as [![Image 526: Flag of Sparta](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Sparta.png/20px-Sparta.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sparta "Sparta")[Sparta](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sparta "Sparta") or [![Image 527: Flag of Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c0/Syracusae.png/20px-Syracusae.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae")[Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae"), can also be found further to the west among the more predominant republics of the region.

### Monarchy governments[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Monarchy governments") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Monarchy governments")]
