| Dictatorship | [![Image 551: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 552: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 553: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 554: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 555: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National freeman happiness: **+12%** * [![Image 556: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output") National citizen output: **+12%** | _A form of totalitarian government, a dictatorship places political power in the hands of a single individual._ |
| Empire | [![Image 557: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 558: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 559: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 560: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 561: Freeman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Pop_freemen_output.png/28px-Pop_freemen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Freeman output") National freemen output: **+12%** * [![Image 562: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National slave output: **+12%** * [![Image 563: Warscore cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Warscore_cost.png/28px-Warscore_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_score_cost "War score cost") War score Cost: **-10%** | _An Emperor wields absolute power over the state, and usually holds sovereignty over lesser kings and nobles._ |
| Imperial Cult | [![Image 564: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 565: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 566: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 567: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 568: Freeman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Pop_freemen_output.png/28px-Pop_freemen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Freeman output") National freemen output: **+12%** * [![Image 569: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National slave output: **+12%** * [![Image 570: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") Omen power: **+15%** | _In some cultures, an Emperor or Empress holds such authority that they are considered to be a messenger of the divine, or even a deity themselves._ |

### Legitimacy[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Legitimacy") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Legitimacy")]

While a monarch typically has the authority to act without asking for approval their subject characters will still react to their actions if they do not approve of them. [![Image 571: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**Legitimacy** is a value between **−100** and **+100** and models the perceived right for the monarch to rule their country. Legitimacy generally changes by a small amount from month to month, but can also be affected by various [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [mission tasks](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions").

For the monarchies that exist in Imperator: Rome at the start of the game this was highly relevant as they were almost all established in this generation. None of them have a firm number of supporters, and many of them (like Egypt or the Seleucid Empire) have a population that considers them to be foreigners.

For each point of positive legitimacy (above **0**), the country will get:

For each point of negative legitimacy, the country will get:

Legitimacy is gained from acting as a good monarch, most importantly:

*   [![Image 572: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") Ruler popularity
*   [![Image 573: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") High stability
*   [![Image 574: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") The skill of your currently employed Court Philosopher

Legitimacy is reduced by anything that threatens popular support for the monarchy, most importantly:

To help increase legitimacy, there is a government action to [![Image 575: Button endorse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Button_endorse.png/28px-Button_endorse.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Button_endorse.png)**Strengthen Legitimacy**, which gives a monthly legitimacy increase of **+0.05** for **5** years at a cost of [![Image 576: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**10** political influence and a monthly increase of [![Image 577: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**+0.02** tyranny while the modifier is running. Legitimacy can be strengthened multiple times with the modifiers stacking on each other, but each use of the action increases the (political influence) cost of further strengthening legitimacy by **+50%** while it is running. There are also various indirect ways, such as Holding Games, that increase popularity and therefore indirectly legitimacy.

### Succession[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Succession") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Succession")]

The personal nature of monarchical rule means that monarchies are generally the most vulnerable during a **succession**, when the monarch dies, is deposed, or abdicates and is replaced by his or her heir, usually a child or other close relative. A strong, well-supported, popular heir will likely have few problems taking control of the realm, while a young, inexperienced monarch with little support could find themselves at the mercy of scheming pretenders and face a [![Image 578: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war.

During a succession, the country will always lose [![Image 579: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**10** stability, and all provinces will lose between **3** and **10**[![Image 580: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") loyalty depending on the [![Image 581: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity of the heir. If any of the pretenders are disloyal, the country will lose an additional [![Image 582: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**20** stability as a succession crisis breaks out.

#### Successors and pretenders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Successors and pretenders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Successors and pretenders")]

Successions are not always as easy as the described laws would imply. There are many examples of conflicts over who would inherit, sometimes tearing even great and otherwise stable kingdoms apart. In the government view, the **four** characters highest in line to inherit will be displayed at all times (starting with the **[primary heir](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Primary_heir "Primary heir")** who is currently designated to inherit when the ruler dies), together with their [![Image 583: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty and [![Image 584: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value") succession value (the overall strength of their claim). Every character in a monarchy also has a preferred heir out of these four. Most of the time, this will be the current heir, but depending on factors like friendships, skills, or lack of loyalty, they may prefer one of the other heirs. It is possible to influence the choice of preferred heir towards the primary heir using the [![Image 585: Law agnatic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/01/Law_agnatic.png/28px-Law_agnatic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Successors_and_pretenders "Primary heir attraction")**Primary Heir Attraction** modifier, which will make characters more likely to prefer the primary heir.

The other [![Image 586: Leader pretender.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/11/Leader_pretender.png/28px-Leader_pretender.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pretender "Pretender") three **pretenders** (the three characters with the highest succession value after the primary heir) will have a negative modifier to their [![Image 587: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty and will normally do what they can to assemble money and supporters for the day the current monarch dies. Apart from increasing loyalty and attacking the causes for someone preferring another heir, you can ask directly ask characters to support your preferred heir as long as their loyalty is at least 50, which drastically increases their support for your current heir at the cost of [![Image 588: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**2** tyranny.

Upon succession, the current heir will become the new monarch with the starting [![Image 589: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy determined by the [![Image 590: Next ruler legitimacy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Next_ruler_legitimacy.png/28px-Next_ruler_legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Successors "Next ruler legitimacy")**Next Rulers Legitimacy** modifier, with a base of **60**, plus **20** times the country's religious unity, then reduced by **2** for each employed character that supported another heir.

#### Succession value[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Succession value") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Succession value")]

A character's claim to the throne, including which characters are designated as the [primary heir](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Primary_heir "Primary heir") and the [![Image 591: Leader pretender.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/11/Leader_pretender.png/28px-Leader_pretender.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pretender "Pretender") pretenders, is determined by his or her [![Image 592: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value")**succession value**. The method for determining a character's succession value, and therefore position in the line of inheritance, depends on the country's currently enacted [succession law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Succession_Law "Laws"), though it is also possible for the choices of the current ruler and certain other factors to bypass the normal line of succession to some extent.

*   **Agnatic:** Succession value is based on descent, with the children of the monarch preferred over their siblings or other, more faraway relatives. Elder children and their descendants are preferred over younger children, and females are excluded from the succession.
*   **Agnatic-Cognatic:** Succession value is based on descent, with children of the monarch preferred over their siblings or other, more faraway relatives. Elder children and their descendants are preferred over younger children, with males preferred over females of the same relationship to the ruler unless gender equality is enabled.
*   **Agnatic Seniority:** Male line descendants of the ruling family's founder get succession value based on age, without regard to how closely related they are to the current ruler. This often means that the male siblings of the monarch will inherit before any children, and rulers will usually be older on succession.
*   **Elective Succession:** Succession value is based on how many [![Image 593: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") position holders prefer them as heir. Attributes such as [![Image 594: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") charisma, [![Image 595: Age](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2d/Age.png/28px-Age.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Secondary "Age") age, [![Image 596: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, [![Image 597: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence, and being of the same family are all important to the electors. Any character in the realm is eligible, though members of the ruling family are still to some extent preferred. Women (unless gender equality is enabled) and children are disfavoured.
*   **Egyptian Succession:** Succession value is based on descent, with children of the ruler are preferred over their siblings or other, more faraway relatives. Elder children and their descendants are preferred over younger children, regardless of gender. Members of the royal family are permitted to marry their own family members (including sibling to sibling).

All succession laws that are based on descent assign succession value on the basis of **primogeniture**. Under primogeniture, a character's children always take precedence over any siblings that the character is ahead of, no matter where the heir is in the line of succession (or if he/she is dead). In principle, this is the same as the character's eldest child (after gender considerations) always inheriting, and then either immediately passing the throne on to their eldest child if they are already dead by the time of succession or passing back up to the parent the crown was inherited from if there are no living descendants. This means, for instance, that a son of a ruler's first son takes precedence over the ruler's second son, even if the second son is technically more closely related to the ruler. Similarly, the second son and all of his descendants would be favoured over any siblings of the ruler or their descendants. When sorting between the children of a single character, gender restrictions or preferences are applied first, followed by age; this means that, under Agnatic-Congatic (without gender equality), a son who is the 4th child (and his descendants) would be behind a son who is the 3rd child (and his descendants), but ahead of a daughter who is the 1st child (and her descendants).

In all cases (except for elective succession), if there are no eligible direct relatives, the family members of the current ruler will always always preferred over non-family members.

If the country either has at least [![Image 598: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**90** legitimacy or the [![Image 599: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") Divinity Statute law, the ruler can also at any time **anoint** a new heir from among the three current pretenders (if they are part of the ruling family) at the cost of [![Image 600: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**15** political power and [![Image 601: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**25** legitimacy. This gives [![Image 602: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value")**+1000** succession value which ensures that they become the new [primary heir](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Primary_heir "Primary heir"), though the old displaced heir will not be happy in will lose [![Image 603: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**10** loyalty in addition to the normal pretender loyalty malus. In an elective monarchy, anointing only gives [![Image 604: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value")**+5** succession value which, while significant, is not necessarily enough to guarantee their election, but the favoured pretender does not necessarily have to be done on a member of the ruling family and the current primary heir will not be angered.

Succession value is also increased by the proportion of [![Image 605: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal cohorts or veterans compared to the overall size of the army, as well as being the commander of a legion with the [![Image 606: Phalera eagle.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8e/Phalera_eagle.png/28px-Phalera_eagle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction") Regnatrix distinction, which can be especially significant in elective monarchies.

#### Succession crisis[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Succession crisis") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Succession crisis")]

At the time of succession, if any of the pretenders that did not gain the throne are unloyal (have less than [![Image 607: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**33** loyalty), a **succession crisis** will break out. The country will lose an additional [![Image 608: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**20** stability, along with the normal hit from a succession, and each pretender will begin assembling an army of as many loyal troops as they can afford. As this army will be loyal to the disloyal pretender, the army cannot be given orders and the pretender cannot be removed from command, giving a significant increase the pretender's [![Image 609: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base and a further malus to [![Image 610: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. The presence of these armies is likely to drive the country towards a [![Image 611: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war, either immediately or in the long run.

In addition to the normal interactions, there are also some other special character interactions that can be used on pretenders to try to alleviate the threat:

Meanwhile, foreign countries will have also have access to the **Intervene in Crisis** character interaction to spend [![Image 612: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Cost") gold and [![Image 613: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence on increasing the size of the pretender army, and the **Support Pretender** interaction to lower the pretender's [![Image 614: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty and promise support in terms of [![Image 615: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower or an [![Image 616: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") alliance in the event of a civil war.

### Offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Offices")]

_Main article: [Position#Monarchy\_offices](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Monarchy\_offices "Position")_

The unique [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") offices give modifiers for [![Image 617: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy, a military bonus for [![Image 618: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance") mercenary army maintenance, as well as [![Image 619: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change, but have a weaker [![Image 620: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifier office than other government types.

| Office | Attribute | State Modifier (per effective attribute) | Character Modifiers | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Chancellor | [![Image 621: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") | [![Image 622: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Monthly aggressive expansion: **−0.025** | * [![Image 623: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+2%** Monthly Wage * [![Image 624: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+0.05** Monthly Character Prominence * [![Image 625: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**+0.10** Monthly Family Prestige * [![Image 626: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+30** Prominence * [![Image 627: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")**+0.80%** Monthly Statesmanship | _This office is the monarch's chief diplomat responsible for the reputation of the ruler and state in foreign courts._ |

### Interactions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Interactions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Interactions")]

As well as the general war council interaction for granting claims, monarchies have three government-specific interactions for demanding general support for the primary heir, holding games to boost ruler popularity, and patronizing the arts for integrated culture happiness and civilization level.

|  | Interaction | Requirements | Effects |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 677: War council.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/89/War_council.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:War_council.png) | Summon War Council | * There are at least 3 adult characters, excepting the ruler, in the current country * Has not used this interaction in the last **10** years * There is a neighbouring country that is not an [![Image 678: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") ally or [![Image 679: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject of the current country | * Tooltip: Summons a selection of our most prestigious senators to consider a focus for our next conquest. This can be used to provide a claim on a province, and may be used once every 10 years. * Get the event [**War Council**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=War_council_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "War council events (page does not exist)") |
| [![Image 680: Demand oaths of allegience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/b/bb/Demand_oaths_of_allegience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Demand_oaths_of_allegience.png) | Demand Oaths of Allegiance | * Has at least [![Image 681: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence, modified by the [![Image 682: Price oaths of allegiance button cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2f/Price_oaths_of_allegiance_button_cost_modifier.png/20px-Price_oaths_of_allegiance_button_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_oaths_of_allegiance_button_cost_modifier.png)**Oaths of Allegiance Cost** modifier * Does not have the modifier **Oaths of Allegiance** * Has a primary heir of the ruling family | * Lose [![Image 683: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence, modified by the [![Image 684: Price oaths of allegiance button cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2f/Price_oaths_of_allegiance_button_cost_modifier.png/20px-Price_oaths_of_allegiance_button_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_oaths_of_allegiance_button_cost_modifier.png)**Oaths of Allegiance Cost** modifier * Get the modifier **Oaths of Allegiance** for **5** years, giving: * [![Image 685: Law agnatic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/01/Law_agnatic.png/28px-Law_agnatic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_agnatic.png)**+25** Primary Heir Attraction * Tooltip: The continuation of a dynasty brings strength and security to a realm. [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRuler.GetName], whilst a fearsome and honorable [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRulerTitle], cannot rule forever - we must rouse support for the future of the [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRuler.GetFamilyDisplayAdjective] line! |
| [![Image 686: Hold games.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/e2/Hold_games.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Hold_games.png) | Hold Games | * The current ruler does not have the modifier **Ongoing Games** * The current ruler has at least [![Image 687: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income of the current country * The current ruler is an adult * Has at least [![Image 688: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income | * Lose [![Image 689: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income * The current ruler loses [![Image 690: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income of the current country * The current ruler gets the modifier **Ongoing Games** for **5** years, giving: * [![Image 691: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**+0.10** Monthly Popularity * Tooltip: Your ruler can gain extra popularity from competing in the games. * If the current ruler does not have a [nickname](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Nickname "Nickname"), 5% chance of getting the cognomen **Catilina** if the ruler is in the Italic culture group or the nickname '**The Beneficent'** otherwise * Get the event [**The Upcoming Games**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Games_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Games events (page does not exist)") in **10-30** days * Tooltip: The common folk love gladiators, chariot races and athletics, even more than they love [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRuler.GetPraenomen] - luckily [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRuler.GetPraenomen] knows this all too well. |
| [![Image 692: Patronize arts.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/8b/Patronize_arts.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Patronize_arts.png) | Patronize the Arts | * Has at least [![Image 693: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence * Does not have the modifier **Cultural Patron** | * Lose [![Image 694: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence * Get the modifier **Cultural Patron** for **5** years, giving: * [![Image 695: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+5%** Integrated Culture Happiness * [![Image 696: Global monthly civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Global_monthly_civilization.png/28px-Global_monthly_civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_monthly_civilization.png)**+0.01%** Monthly Civilization Change * Tooltip: [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetPrimaryCulture.GetName] ways have safeguarded the development of our nation for aeons. Let us bring these ideals to all within our realm. |

## Tribal[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Tribal") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Tribal")]

[![Image 697](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Government_tribal_screen.png/500px-Government_tribal_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Government_tribal_screen.png)

The government interface for tribes

**Migratory Tribes**, **Settled Tribes**, and **Federated Tribes** make up the great majority of the countries in Imperator. Tribal countries exist in all locations, from Europe to Arabia and the Caucasus to the interior of India and Tibet all the way to the Burmese border.

The base premise behind tribal governments is that authority is shared between the clans, represented by clan chiefs who command and maintain their own retinues of personally loyal troops. They have a significant say in the government and when a ruler dies the new ruler will be elected from among the other clan chiefs, ensuring a rotation of power between the clans. Tribes are also distinguished by their lower level of development and civilization, choosing between increasing centralization and adopting a more sedentary lifestyle that increasingly approaches that of a republic or monarchy or decentralizing the country to put a greater emphasis on a migratory lifestyle and the traditional clan structure, whose hordes will be the terror of all civilized peoples.

### Tribal governments[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Tribal governments") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Tribal governments")]

Unless specified otherwise, all Tribes have the following mechanics and modifiers:

| Tribe type | Idea slots | Base modifiers | Government bonus | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Migratory Tribe | [![Image 698: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 2 Military | [![Image 699: Migration cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration") Allows migration [![Image 700: Tribesman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Tribesman happiness") National tribesmen happiness: **+8%** [![Image 701: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy size multiplier: **+10%** | [![Image 702: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output") National tribesmen output: **+12%** | _Elected by a council of tribesmen, a tribal chief rules for life, and is entrusted with the safety and security of his or her tribe. The most important authority vested in the Chief is the decision of when to settle and when to migrate to another, more favorable location._ |
| Settled Tribe | [![Image 703: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 704: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | [![Image 705: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Country civilization level: **+5%** [![Image 706: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy size multiplier: **+5%** | [![Image 707: Tribesman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Tribesman happiness") National tribesmen happiness: **+12%** | _Tribal leaders are elected for life, by a council of the clan leaders. This is a society that has come to settle permanently in its region, embracing a sedentary lifestyle._ |
| Federated Tribe | [![Image 708: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 709: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 710: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | [![Image 711: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Country civilization level: **+10%** [![Image 712: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy size multiplier: **+10%** | [![Image 713: Tribesman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Pop_tribesmen_output.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Tribesman output") National tribesmen output: **+20%** [![Image 714: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") Pop promotion speed: **+25%** | _Representing the wisdom of a great number of united clans, the council of a Federated Tribe resembles the beginnings of a democratic state._ |

### Centralization[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Centralization") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Centralization")]

All tribes make use of a [![Image 715: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization")**centralization** scale that goes from **−100** to **+100**. Centralization has a monthly base rate of change that is largely determined by the country's [![Image 716: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, and can also be changed through various events, such as dealing with the other clan chiefs. There is however no direct way to change the centralization value, using political influence or money for instance; it is only adjusted as a result of your actions and over time. The vast majority of countries are Settled Tribes at start, and some are part of larger tribal identities that can form into larger Federated Tribes.

The modifiers from centralization scale linearly, with negative centralization giving more military-focused bonuses and positive centralization focused more on economic modifiers and development. The table below shows examples.

| [![Image 717: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Centralization level | [![Image 718: Migration cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Migrating_and_settling "Migration cost") Migration cost | [![Image 719: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") National tribesman output | [![Image 720: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale") Morale of Armies | [![Image 721: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier | [![Image 722: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") Research Points | [![Image 723: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") Pop Promotion Speed | [![Image 724: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Country civilization level |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **−100%** | **−50%** | **+25%** | **+25%** | **+15%** |  |  |  |
| **−50%** | **−25%** | **+12.50%** | **+12.5%** | **+7.5%** |  |  |  |
| **0** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **+50%** |  |  |  |  | **+25%** | **+1.25** | **+5%** |
| **+100%** |  |  |  |  | **+50%** | **+2.50** | **+10%** |

A higher [![Image 725: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value will make [![Image 726: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen unhappy (while [![Image 727: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles, [![Image 728: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens and [![Image 729: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen will feel more and more at home), meaning that the [![Image 730: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen will start being less productive and be more prone to generate [![Image 731: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest. At the start of the game the centralization levels of tribal countries all around the map will differ; they start well into the negatives for the Germanic tribes for instance, while others, like [![Image 732: Flag of Turdetania](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/44/Turdetania.png/20px-Turdetania.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Turdetania "Turdetania")[Turdetania](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Turdetania&action=edit&redlink=1 "Turdetania (page does not exist)") in Hispania, start with significant positive centralization.

### Clan chiefs and retinues[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Clan chiefs and retinues") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Clan chiefs and retinues")]

_Main article: [Clan chief](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan\_chief "Clan chief")_

[![Image 733: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") A tribal government shares its power between **clan chiefs**, which are the heads of the major families, or **clans**, of the tribe. The [![Image 734: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler is always one of the clan chiefs, and when the current chief dies, the most skilled of the other clan chiefs is elected as the new ruler, which means that the chiefdom will always rotate between the clans to at least some degree. As the leader of a significant component of the tribe, each clan chief will always control a portion of a country's [![Image 735: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies as their personal retinues.

[![Image 736: Clan retinue.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0e/Clan_retinue.png/28px-Clan_retinue.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_retinue "Clan retinue") When a tribal country raises its [![Image 737: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies from a region, the levy will be divided between each of clan chiefs (including the [![Image 738: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler) as their personal **clan retinue**. Each retinue from a region will always be at least **1000** men (2 cohorts); this means that regions with a especially small levy size will only provide retinues for some of the clan chiefs. Clan retinues are always [![Image 739: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") led by and [![Image 740: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to their clan chief, and cannot be merged with levies led by other clan chiefs (even if they come from the same region). Note that the clan retinue allocated to the ruling family will not be [![Image 741: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to the ruler, but will still be [![Image 742: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") led by the ruler and will generally be larger than, or at least as large as, the clan retinues raised by the other chiefs. Since retinue troops are always loyal to their clan chief, these armies will contribute significantly to their clan chief's [![Image 743: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base and [![Image 744: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value") succession value as long as they are raised, which can have a significant impact on the [![Image 745: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty of clan chiefs as well as the outcome of tribal elections during wartime as they will always be a significant portion of the army.

### Tribal elections[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Tribal elections") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Tribal elections")]

When a tribal ruler dies or is deposed, his or her successor is elected from the other [![Image 746: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs according to the weighted sum of their [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes"):

Modifiers that affect [![Image 747: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Succession_value "Succession value") succession value - in particular, [![Image 748: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal cohorts or veterans, particularly relevant when [![Image 749: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies are raised - are also taken into account when determining a clan chief's succession support. As the succession value from normal sources are generally much lower in a tribe as opposed to a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), succession value from other sources is particularly relevant in tribal elections. The next ruler is then the clan chief that has the highest total succession support.

Only clan chiefs at the time of succession are eligible to be elected. As the clan chief of the ruling family is always the current ruler, who is not eligible to succeed him/herself, this means that the next ruler will always come from a different family compared to the current ruler.

### Migrating and settling[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Migrating and settling") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Migrating and settling")]

_See also: [Colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization")_

Migratory Tribes have the ability to [![Image 750: Migration cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration")**migrate**, and all tribal governments can become a Migratory Tribe if they reach a sufficient degree of decentralization. Migration can be initiated in any territory that has at least [![Image 751: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population")**3** pops where the primary culture and religion are dominant if no [![Image 752: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies have been raised from the region, by clicking on the [![Image 753: Migration cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration")**Migrate** button in the territory section of the province interface. Beginning a migration in a territory has a base cost of [![Image 754: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**8** stability (reduced by the [![Image 755: Migration cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Migrating_and_settling "Migration cost")**migration cost** modifier, given by negative centralization and the [![Image 756: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion")[Tuistic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tuistic "Tuistic") religion).

Migrating will turn up to **20** of the pops in the territory into special **Migrant Cohorts** of [![Image 757: Unit light infantry.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/40/Unit_light_infantry.png/28px-Unit_light_infantry.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Light_Infantry "Light Infantry") light infantry, with each pop becoming a single cohort. This creates a special army that has no maintenance, is not tied to any existing pop or region (unlike [![Image 758: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies and [![Image 759: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions), and neither requires [![Image 760: Has military access.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Has_military_access.png/28px-Has_military_access.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_access "Military access") military access to cross foreign lands, nor ever becomes exiled, even if for example declaring war on the owner of the territory it is standing in. All types of pops can be used to create a migration cohort, but once settled (see below) always turn into [![Image 761: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen – to migrate is to let go of any specialized roles they may have had in their original location. Migration is the only way to raise all of a country's pops into an army, and allows even relatively small tribes to raise a large host if they are willing to take the risks and costs.

Any army that has at least as many migration cohorts as there are pops in its current location can [![Image 762: Settle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Ability_settle.png/28px-Ability_settle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Settle")**settle** in that location. This will turn all migrant cohorts in the army into [![Image 763: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen of your culture and religion (even if the original pops were another class, culture, or religion) and settles them in this territory, taking ownership of it. In order to be able to settle the territory, it must also either be uncolonized or controlled (i.e. owned or occupied in a war). Like with normal [colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization") and annexation, all pre-existing pops are kept. It is also possible to resettle migrant cohorts into already owned territory, as long as it is controlled, which will simply add the pops into the territory.

Even if a country loses its last territory it still remains playable as long as it still has migrant cohorts, meaning it is possible to quite literally uproot an entire country and resettle it somewhere else.

### Offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Offices")]

_Main article: [Position#Tribal\_offices](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Tribal\_offices "Position")_

[Tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") offices give a military bonus in [![Image 764: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower recovery speed and have happiness modifiers for [![Image 765: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen and [![Image 766: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens. Notably, their [![Image 767: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion office is weaker than in republics or monarchies, which is compensated for by a stronger [![Image 768: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifier.

### Interactions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Interactions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Interactions")]

As well as the general war council interaction for granting claims, tribal nations have three government-specific interactions for increasing enslavement efficiency, holding games to boost ruler popularity, and encouraging migration in order to reduce tribal centralization.

|  | Interaction | Requirements | Effects |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 825: War council.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/89/War_council.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:War_council.png) | Summon War Council | * There are at least 3 adult characters, excepting the ruler, in the current country * Has not used this interaction in the last **10** years * There is a neighbouring country that is not an [![Image 826: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") ally or [![Image 827: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject of the current country | * Tooltip: Summons a selection of our most prestigious senators to consider a focus for our next conquest. This can be used to provide a claim on a province, and may be used once every 10 years. * Get the event [**War Council**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=War_council_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "War council events (page does not exist)") |
| [![Image 828: Raiding parties.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/c/c3/Raiding_parties.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Raiding_parties.png) | Assemble Raiding Parties | * Has at least [![Image 829: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence, modified by the [![Image 830: Price assemble raiding party button cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/81/Price_assemble_raiding_party_button_cost_modifier.png/20px-Price_assemble_raiding_party_button_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_assemble_raiding_party_button_cost_modifier.png)**Assemble Raiding Party Cost** modifier * Does not have the modifier **Raiding Parties Assembled** | * Lose [![Image 831: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence, modified by the [![Image 832: Price assemble raiding party button cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/81/Price_assemble_raiding_party_button_cost_modifier.png/20px-Price_assemble_raiding_party_button_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_assemble_raiding_party_button_cost_modifier.png)**Assemble Raiding Party Cost** modifier * Get the modifier **Raiding Parties Assembled** for **5** years, giving: * [![Image 833: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement")**+10%** Enslavement Efficiency * Tooltip: The lure of plunder and wealth is enough to steel the resolve of even the most faint-hearted. |
| [![Image 834: Hold games.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/e2/Hold_games.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Hold_games.png) | Hold Games | * The current ruler does not have the modifier **Ongoing Games** * The current ruler has at least [![Image 835: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income of the current country * The current ruler is an adult * Has at least [![Image 836: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income | * Lose [![Image 837: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income * The current ruler loses [![Image 838: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**4.2** times the monthly income of the current country * The current ruler gets the modifier **Ongoing Games** for **5** years, giving: * [![Image 839: Games**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Games_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Games events (page does not exist)") in **10-30** days * Tooltip: The common folk love gladiators, chariot races and athletics, even more than they love [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRuler.GetPraenomen] - luckily [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetRuler.GetPraenomen] knows this all too well. |
| [![Image 840: Tribal migration.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/88/Tribal_migration.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Tribal_migration.png) | Encourage Tribal Migration | * Does not have the modifier **Migration Encouraged** * Has a territory with at least [![Image 841: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") 5 tribesmen of the state culture and religion * There is an unowned territory in the capital region or any region neighbouring the capital region | * If the current country has a territory with at least [![Image 842: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") 5 tribesmen of the state culture and religion: * Move [![Image 843: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") 5 tribesmen of the state culture and religion from a random owned territory that has at least 5 to a random unowned territory in the capital region or any region neighbouring the capital region (this does NOT colonize the territory) * Get the modifier **Migration Encouraged** for **5** years, giving: * [![Image 844: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization")**-0.03** Monthly Centralization * [![Image 845: Pop citizen happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Pop_citizen_happiness.png)**-12%** National Citizen Happiness * Tooltip: Our people are becoming too sedentary for their own good. While we may lose some of our brethren in the short term, it is right that we should encourage all [SCOPE.GetRootScope.GetCountry.GetPrimaryCulture.GetName] peoples to wander freely, that we might embrace the old ways some day. |

## Government reform and conversion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Government reform and conversion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Government reform and conversion")]

_Main article: [Decisions#Government\_conversion\_decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions#Government\_conversion\_decisions "Decisions")_

Apart from the [Tribal Reform missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions#Tribal_Reform "Missions") and various country-specific events and mission tasks, all government changes are done through specific decisions. Swapping between government types of the same group can generally be done freely given the country meets the requirements, but switching between different groups is significantly more difficult (if possible at all). The possible government conversions are as follows:

*   Settled Tribes and Migratory Tribes can always convert between each other. Federated Tribes can also become Migratory Tribes, but the only way to form a Federated Tribe is through certain formable nation decisions.
*   Settled and Federated Tribes can reform into either an Autocratic Monarchy or a Democratic Republic.
*   An Autocratic Monarchy can reform into one of the more advanced monarchy types (Aristocratic, Stratocratic, Plutocratic, or Theocratic Monarchy). These four can interconvert between each other, but cannot go back to becoming an Autocratic Monarchy.
*   Similarly, a Democratic Republic can reform into one of the more advanced republic types (Aristocratic, Oligarchic, Plutocratic, or Theocratic Republic). These four can also interconvert between each other, but cannot go back to becoming an Democratic Republic.
*   Any of the four advanced republic types can become a Dictatorship to adopt a monarchical government form (through the Line of Succession [![Image 846: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Oratory Inventions).
*   Any of the five base monarchy types (Autocratic, Aristocratic, Stratocratic, Plutocratic, or Theocratic Monarchy), as well as Dictatorships, can become an Empire.
*   All Monarchical governments except for Autocratic monarchies and Dictatorships can become an Imperial Cult.

Other conversions are possible through special country-specific events or missions. These decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/conversions.txt.

_Times are changing and this land can no longer support our great tribe. It is time to once more embrace the traditions of our ancestors, and prepare for migrations to more peaceful and fertile lands._

_We have found a good home, it is time we settle down and adopt a more sedentary lifestyle. Let us proclaim ourselves a kingdom, separating ourselves from the migratory tribes to which we once belonged._

_[COUNTRY.GetRuler.GetName], inspired by the Kings and Tyrants of foreign lands, might exert greater authority by discarding the tribal trappings of our ancestors._
