============================================================
# Laws
============================================================
Title: Laws - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[Laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws) define the military, economic, social, and political structure and policies of the country at a more granular level below the [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") form, allowing countries to choose between a wide array of various possible modifiers and bonuses. Every law is part of a category of related laws, and only one law in each category can be enacted at a time, with the first law in each category usually enacted by default at the start of the game; many law categories are locked at start, with governmental or technological advancement required to enact the new laws and get their often increasingly powerful bonuses.

## Changing Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Changing Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Changing Laws")]

Enacting a new law costs a base of **15**[![Image 1: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")[Stability](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") and **35**[![Image 2: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")[Political Influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_Influence "Political Influence"), which can be reduced through the [![Image 3: Enact law cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Changing_Laws "Enact law cost")**Enact Law Cost** modifier. While [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") can pass laws at any time provided enough stability and political influence, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Republics "Government") must seek the approval of the [Senate](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Republics "Government"), requiring at least [![Image 4: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**51** Senate support for full approval or at least [![Image 5: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**30** support to force it through at the cost of around [![Image 6: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**0.67** tyranny for every point of support below 51 (up to a maximum of [![Image 7: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**14**). Tribes must instead seek the approval of their [![Image 8: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief")[clan chiefs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief"); if any of them are disloyal, no laws can be passed. Additionally, some laws are locked behind various requirements, such as technology level, adopted [![Image 9: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, [![Image 10: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization (for tribes), [ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler")[faction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Faction "Faction") and [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes"), and more.

## Types of Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Types of Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Types of Laws")]

Each type of government has its own distinct set of laws. Additionally, [![Image 11: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome") also has its own unique laws if it is a republic, though apart from the names and descriptions it is almost entirely identical to the standard republican laws.

### Republic Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Republic Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Republic Laws")]

[![Image 12](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/79/Rep_laws.png/500px-Rep_laws.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Rep_laws.png)

The law interface for republics

While half of the law categories are available from the start, the last 4 categories (Religious Laws, Integration Laws, Citizenship laws and Land Reforms) are unlocked by Civic Advancement technology as the game progresses.

The main change to Republican laws however is that any choice you make will increase the influence over one faction in senate over the others over time. Often the faction that grows will do so in a reactive manner, empowering one faction will allow its opponents to grow.

Forcing laws through the senate, if the government does not have at least [![Image 13: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Senate approval")**51** Senate support, will cost around [![Image 14: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**0.67** tyranny for every point of support below 51 (up to a maximum of [![Image 15: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**14** tyranny). If senate support is less than [![Image 16: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**30**, laws cannot be passed at all.

#### Military Reforms[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Military Reforms") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Military Reforms")]

We must create a permanent and modern army so that we can defend ourselves and project power where we need to. This act proposes a drastic overhaul of the logistical and administrative divisions within our armies._ |

#### Religious Law[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Religious Law") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Religious Law")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 41: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 3

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Traditional Observance | * [![Image 42: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness") State Religion Happiness: **+4%** | _Since times immemorial, our ancestors have practiced the old ways in their own manner. We should encourage traditional observance of our sacred duty._ |
| Priestly Status | * [![Image 43: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") Omen Power: **+15%** | _Priests have always held a position of authority over spiritual matters in our nation. Extending this to invite their authorized political participation on state matters, is logically, the next step._ |
| Deny Priests in Senate | * [![Image 44: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") Pop Conversion Speed: **+20%** | _Religious interference on political matters is no longer desired; our holy men should be out amongst the people, bolstering them in times of need._ |
| Senatorial Veto | * [![Image 45: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods") Divine Sacrifice Cost: **-33%** | _Giving the senate last say on candidates for religious offices, allows us to use these important roles as political stepping stones to higher positions. Sadly, this often encourages the less pious to seek appointment._ |

#### Integration Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Integration Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Integration Laws")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 46: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 6

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Occupation Rights | * [![Image 47: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National Freemen Happiness: **+4%** | _It should be the right of every free man to decide where to settle, and in what manner to subsist. We should promote those ideals in our very constitution._ |
| Relocation Policy | * [![Image 48: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") Pop Demotion Speed: **+6** * [![Image 49: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness") National Noble Happiness: **+2%** | _Control over where our people settle, is vital in maintaining the long-term health of our state. A series of laws, duties and enticements should make this easier, although those with more nomadic backgrounds may be displeased._ |
| Rights of Nobility | * [![Image 50: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output") National Citizen Output: **+20%** | _For too long, we have remained undecided on the subject of the conflict between nobility and common folk. It is time we rejected the arguments in favor of the commoners, who are ignorant enough to see our historic state fail._ |
| Devolved Administration | * [![Image 51: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") Integrated Culture Happiness: **+5%** | _A decentralized state is often an effective way of keeping rebellious behavior localized. Whilst it may affect certain aspects of our government negatively, the benefits of this path are sure to outweigh them._ |

#### Citizenship Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Citizenship Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Citizenship Laws")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 52: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 9

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Status Quo | * [![Image 53: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") National Tax: **+5%** | _The struggle between the lower and upper classes has long been a defining feature of our civilization. Whilst it may be a mere platitude, confirming the rights of all free men as regards trial, conviction and debt slavery, will appease many._ |
| Slave Treatment Sanctions | * [![Image 54: Slave ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Pop_slaves_ratio.png/28px-Pop_slaves_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Slave ratio") Desired Slaves Ratio in Cities: **+15%** | _It is our duty to ensure that slaves are in receipt of at least the very basic amenities needed to survive - better working conditions promote productivity._ |
| Cultural Primacy | * [![Image 55: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") Desired Citizen Ratio in Cities: **+10%** | _As our nation grows, so does too the number of foreign cultures, unusual practices, and mysterious rites. It is becoming, then, that we appease our increasingly concerned free folk, regardless of their status in our society._ |
| Manumittance Policy | * [![Image 56: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") Desired Freemen Ratio in Cities: **+10%** | _It is time we admitted those less fortunate, into the higher strata of society. The balance to be struck, however, is reassuring the nobles that their superiority in status and autonomy, is guaranteed._ |

#### Land Reforms[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Land Reforms") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Land Reforms")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 57: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 12

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Interference Policy | * [![Image 58: Agressive expansion impact.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Agressive_expansion_impact.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_impact.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_impact.png) Aggressive Expansion Impact: **-10%** | _The primitive ways of our neighbours are well known. By proclaiming our superiority openly, it will hardly come as a surprise that we wish to take lesser peoples under our wing. For protection, of course._ |
| Military Settlement Policy | * [![Image 59: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") Pop Promotion Speed: **+6** * [![Image 60: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") Pop Migration Speed **+1.5** | _We must assign a portion of all conquered land to the veterans of the campaign who were responsible for acquiring it. As such, our hardy soldiers will continue to act as a means of peacekeeping, and no longer overburden our home territories._ |
| Land Seizure Protocol | * [![Image 61: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") Slaves Needed For Surplus: **-2** | _For too long, wealthy citizens have been in control of excessive amounts of valuable land. By ordering the forfeiture of farms, mines and strategic logistical supplies, we can redistribute them to better serve the state._ |
| Seizure Amendments | * [![Image 62: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National Slave Output: **+25%** | _The extreme measures of Land Seizure Protocols may not be quite as necessary as previously thought. By reaching a compromise with the nobility, they will grudgingly provide support, whilst retaining control of their own affairs._ |

### Republic Laws (Roman version)[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Republic Laws (Roman version)") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Republic Laws (Roman version)")]

[![Image 63](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c9/Rome_Laws.png/500px-Rome_Laws.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Rome_Laws.png)

The law interface for [![Image 64: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome"), if it is a republic

The laws of the [![Image 65: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome") differ from the ordinary laws of the republic in terms of names and descriptions, as well as a few scattered modifiers.

#### Military Reforms[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Military Reforms") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Military Reforms")]

Requirements:

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Servian Levy | * [![Image 66: Manpower recovery speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Manpower_recovery_speed.png/28px-Manpower_recovery_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower_recovery_speed "Manpower recovery speed") Manpower Recovery Speed **+5%** * [![Image 67: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier **+5%** | _Since the day of the Roman Kingdom all citizens with more than a certain degree of property must answer the call to be levied into armies when Rome so requires. The wealthier the citizen the more they are expected to contribute, and the more say they will have in the matters of the Republic._ |
| Republican Levy | * [![Image 68: Monthly war exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "Monthly war exhaustion") Monthly War Exhaustion: **-0.04** * [![Image 69: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier **+7.5%** | _As the Republic grows so does the need for a larger and more well trained levy. The Census is a good base for how to alot roles in the levied citizens in the army but age should play an almost as important role._ |
| Punic Reforms | * [![Image 70: Global manpower modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Global_manpower_modifier.png/28px-Global_manpower_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_manpower_modifier.png) National Manpower: **+10%** * [![Image 71: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier **+2.5%** * Enables raising a [![Image 72: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion in the capital region. | _Levied citizens are at the core of Roman Republican society and this system must remain the main part of our armies. Nonetheless wars against Carthage and Macedon highlights the need to lighten the old recruitment rules, allowing even those with little to no property to serve. Likewise the levy must to be suplemented by a more lasting force, always ready to hurry to do the senate's bidding._ |
| Marian Reforms | * [![Image 73: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") Discipline: **+5%** * Enables raising a [![Image 74: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion in any region. | _The Marian reforms drastically overhauled the organization and recruitment policies of the Roman republic. Requirements for entry into the army were largely abolished, and the now ineffective manipular system was restructured, resulting in permanent cohorts consisting of 'centuries' of soldiers._ |

#### Electoral Reforms[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Electoral Reforms") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Electoral Reforms")]

Requirements:

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Leges Genuciae | * [![Image 75: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Divine_sacrifice "Divine sacrifice") Divine Sacrifice Cost: **-5%** | _The Genucian laws had a variety of errata, however, the keystone tenets held that any politician was forbidden from possessing more than one office simultaenously, and furthermore, that at least 10 years must have passed after the date of his first election, until a magistrate was able to hold the same office again._ |
| Consultum Ultimum | * [![Image 76: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Election_term.png) Election Term Duration: **+5** | _Intended to safeguard the republic in times of desperate need, the 'Final Decree of the Senate', was a reform of the older system of dictatorship, whereby one man would receive absolute authority over matters of state. This backfired disastrously when the senate invoked this decree against Caesar, who, ironically, claimed to view the decree as proof that republican ideals were in danger of being overthrown._ |
| Lex Antonia | * [![Image 77: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Election_term.png) Election Term Duration: **-3** | _Proposed by Mark Antony, in the aftermath of Caesar's assassination, this law aimed to abolish dictatorship altogether, though was ultimately unsuccessful; perhaps paving the way for the dawn of the Empire._ |
| Princeps Civitatis | * [![Image 78: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Election_term.png) Election Term Duration: **+45** | _After the bitter Triumvirate conflicts, the adopted son of Caesar, Gaius Octavius, later known as the first Emperor of Rome, reformed the very nature of the republic. Whilst in practice he held authoritarian control over a state in turmoil, he referred to himself as Princeps Civitatis, or, 'First Citizen', as he set about creating the legal framework for an empire that spanned nearly 500 years._ |

#### Assembly Endorsement[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Assembly Endorsement") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Assembly Endorsement")]

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Tribal Assembly | * [![Image 79: Monthly corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/70/Monthly_corruption.png/28px-Monthly_corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_corruption.png) Monthly Corruption: **-0.10** | _The 35 tribes of the Tribal Assembly were composed of citizens residing in distinct geographic areas. It voted, like the Curiate assembly, on matters legislative; providing endorsement and authority on many decisions._ |
| Centuriate Assembly | * [![Image 80: Claim fabrication cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim#Fabricating_claims "Claim fabrication cost") Fabricate Claim Cost: **-50%** | _The military arm of the Roman democratic system, the Centuriate assembly was weighted by the material wealth of the soldiers of which it was comprised. The practical result was a particularly elitist legislative body._ |
| Curiate Assembly | * [![Image 81: Monthly tyranny.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_tyranny.png/28px-Monthly_tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_tyranny.png) Monthly Tyranny: **-0.02** * Enables the [Summon Curiate Assembly](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Summon_Curiate_Assembly "Summon Curiate Assembly") government interaction | _The Curiate assembly was a legislative body open to all those of patrician status within Roman society. Whilst it lost much power to the establishment of other assemblies, the symbolic votes it cast could carry much weight on matters of state._ |
| Senatorial Endorsement | * [![Image 82: Monthly political influence modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f5/Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png) Monthly Political Influence: **+5%** * Enables the [Senatorial War Council](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senatorial_War_Council "Senatorial War Council") government interaction | _The powers of the people grow too great; we must limit their influence in the political sphere by increasing Senatorial powers even further..._ |

#### Maritime Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Maritime Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Maritime Laws")]

Requirements:

*   Has at least [![Image 83: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") 1 Port

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Merchant Exemptions | * [![Image 84: Manpower recovery speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Manpower_recovery_speed.png/28px-Manpower_recovery_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower_recovery_speed "Manpower recovery speed") Manpower Recovery Speed: **+5%** | _The burden of safeguarding a growing state requires special attention to the supply of food for the people. Instituting tax and legal exemptions for foreign merchants willing to supply grain should encourage a healthy trade in vital supplies._ |
| Lex Gabinia | * [![Image 85: Export value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/64/Commerce_from_export.png/28px-Commerce_from_export.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Export value") Export Value: **+25%** | _Proposed as an emergency measure, the Lex Gabinia granted huge powers to the ruling consul, Pompeius Magnus, in order to combat the plague of piracy in the Mediterranean. The law was heavily opposed by traditionalists in the Senate, who were terrified of placing too much power in the hands of any individual._ |
| Lex Caec. de Vectigalibus | * [![Image 86: Import value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Global_import_commerce_modifier.png/28px-Global_import_commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Import value") Import Value: **+25%** * Creates a [![Image 87: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate") Pirate Haven in a random owned [![Image 88: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port territory when enacted | _A minor but popular law regarding the collection of port duties, the Lex Caecilia abolished the taxes associated with importing goods in Italy._ |
| Lex Claudia | * [![Image 89: Anti piracy cb.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Anti_piracy_cb.png/28px-Anti_piracy_cb.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Anti_piracy_cb.png) Anti-Piracy Casus Belli: **Yes** | _Possibly aimed at curbing the private wealth of senators, the Lex Claudia prohibited senatorial families from owning trade ships above a certain capacity. The effects of this law were considerable, and played a large part in regulating profiteering behavior amongst wealthy citizens._ |

#### Religious Law[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Religious Law") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Religious Law")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 90: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 3

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Avoid Religious Mandates | * [![Image 91: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness") State Religion Happiness: **+4%** | _Officially interfering in the rites and practices of the state religion, can cause some dissatisfaction amongst the pious. By abstaining from doing so, we might at least avoid awkward predicaments._ |
| Lex Ogulnia | * [![Image 92: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") Omen Power: **+15%** | _The status of the Plebeians was a cause of great concern in republican Rome. By admitting them as valid members of the priesthood despite the concerns of the priests themselves, a clear message of inclusion was sent._ |
| Lex Aelia Et Fufia | * [![Image 93: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") Pop Conversion Speed: **+20%** | _A simple limitation of the ratio of owned to freed slaves that any man might possess, ensured that the a slave economy was still viable in the late Republic._ |
| Lex Domitia de Sacerdotiis | * [![Image 94: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods") Divine Sacrifice Cost: **-33%** | _This law took the responsibility of appointing the Pontifex Maximus firmly out of the hands of the college of priests; resulting in the senate wielding greater power over state religion._ |

#### Integration Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Integration Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Integration Laws")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 95: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 6

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lex Canuleia | * [![Image 96: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National Freemen Happiness: **+4%** | _Passed centuries previously, the Lex Canuleia confirmed the right for patricians and plebs to intermarry. In an early example of a political faux-pas, Consul Curtius, who opposed the law, suggested that the children of such marriages might displease the gods themselves, resulting in overwhelming support for the bill._ |
| Lex Papia de Peregrinis | * [![Image 97: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") Pop Demotion Speed: **+6** * [![Image 98: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness") National Noble Happiness: **+2%** | _This law set out to challenge false claims of citizenship, with the ultimate aim of deporting foreigners from Rome. Archias, the mentor of Cicero, was famously tried under this law, an act which caused Cicero to write his work, 'Pro Archia Poeta', in Archias' defense._ |
| Lex Licinia Mucia | * [![Image 99: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output") National Citizen Output: **+20%** | _This law instigated the investigation of Italian allies registered as Roman citizens, largely due to a populist trend in xenophobia. The passing of the act caused uproar amongst the Roman Allies, effectively banning them from acting as citizens._ |
| Lex Hortensia | * [![Image 100: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") Integrated Culture Happiness: **+5%** | _This drastic law put an end to the Conflict of Orders, ensuring that Plebeians and Patricians alike, were subject to the same rules._ |

#### Citizenship Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Citizenship Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Citizenship Laws")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 101: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 9

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lex Aternia Tarpeia | * [![Image 102: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") National Tax: **+5%** | _Enabling magistrates to impose fines upon nobles and citizens was an early, but effective way of limiting the power of the nobility. Fines in the early republic were levied in livestock, but later amended to require payment in bronze, as stipulated by the Lex Julia Papiria._ |
| Lex Fufia Caninia | * [![Image 103: Slave ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1f/Pop_slaves_ratio.png/28px-Pop_slaves_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Slave ratio") Desired Slaves Ratio in Cities: **+15%** | _A simple limitation of the ratio of owned to freed slaves that any man might possess, ensured that the a slave economy was still viable in the late Republic._ |
| Lex Plautia Papiria | * [![Image 104: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") Desired Citizen Ratio in Cities: **+10%** | _The Lex Plautia was passed during the bitter Social War, granting citizenship to those who had risen up against Rome, and restoring peace in the Italian peninsula._ |
| Lex Aelia Sentia | * [![Image 105: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") Desired Freemen Ratio in Cities: **+10%** | _The manumission of slaves became so increasingly prevalent in Rome, that a law was enacted to put limitations on how manumitted slaves were to act, and what heights they might climb to by denying freed slaves the right of citizenship._ |

#### Land Reforms[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Land Reforms") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Land Reforms")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 106: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Advances is greater or equal to 12

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cassian Reform | * [![Image 107: Agressive expansion impact.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Agressive_expansion_impact.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_impact.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_impact.png) Aggressive Expansion Impact: **-10%** | _The Cassian reform was passed in the early days of the republic, setting the stage for Roman policy for years to come. By seizing all conquered land acquired during conquest, and selling it to wealthy patricians, war became a lucrative business._ |
| Lex Servilia Glaucia | * [![Image 108: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") Pop Promotion Speed: **+6** * [![Image 109: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") Pop Migration Speed **+1.5** | _Providing plots of land for military veterans, the Lex Servilia was simultaneously a method of rewarding retiring soldiers, and exerting control over conquered foreign territory._ |
| Lex Sempronia Agraria | * [![Image 110: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") Slaves Needed For Surplus: **-2** | _Proposed by Tiberius Gracchus, the Lex Sempronia imposed fines upon landowners who held more than an allotted portion of land, going so far as to demand the seizure and redistribution of this 'illegal' territory to smallholders. The circumstances surrounding the creation of this law were tense, even by the standards of Roman politics, resulting in extreme civil unrest, and ultimately, the untimely demise of Tiberius._ |
| Gracchus Reform | * [![Image 111: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National Slave Output: **+25%** | _Wary of his brother's fate, Gaius Gracchus continued to pass a wide measure of social and land reforms, focusing on domestic justice and the appropriation of conquered land. The famous 'Bread Dole', instituted by Gaius, was the subject of popular acclaim._ |

### Monarchy Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Monarchy Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Monarchy Laws")]

[![Image 112](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/71/Monarchy_Laws.png/500px-Monarchy_Laws.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monarchy_Laws.png)

The law interface for monarchies

Just like in Republics, there are a number of Law Categories that are unlocked by technology (Taxation Laws, Domestic Laws, Conversion Laws, Authority Laws and Contract Law) but requiring specific [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") rather than simply Civic Advancement.

#### Succession Law[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Succession Law") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Succession Law")]

Requirements:

*   Familial Marriage - The current country has a [primary heir](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Primary_heir "Primary heir"), and there is a member of the ruling family that is the opposite gender of the ruler

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 113: Law agnatic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/01/Law_agnatic.png/28px-Law_agnatic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_agnatic.png) Agnatic Primogeniture | * Eldest male child of the ruler is the primary heir, followed by younger sons and more distant male line relatives. Women are excluded. | _Agnatic succession dictates that the eldest male child always inherits the throne. This is a simple and ancient system, but can often result in young monarchs, bloody politics, and a lack of viable successors._ |
| [![Image 114: Law agnatic cognatic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Law_agnatic_cognatic.png/28px-Law_agnatic_cognatic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_agnatic_cognatic.png) Agnatic-Cognatic | * Same as Agnatic Primogeniture, with females included behind males that are as related to the current ruler unless gender equality is enabled. | _The principle of cognatic succession, is to allow female children of a monarch to succeed to the throne if there are no valid male heirs. This can affect the perceived legitimacy of a dynasty, but will greatly decrease the chances of a failed succession._ |
| [![Image 115: Law agnatic seniority.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Law_agnatic_seniority.png/28px-Law_agnatic_seniority.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_agnatic_seniority.png) Agnatic Seniority | * The eldest descendant of the ruling family's founder inherits, with uncles and brothers typically taking priority over sons. | _Agnatic Seniority is rarer form of patrilineal succession, in which the reigning monarch's younger brothers are higher in the order of succession than his sons. This can often result in huge webs of rivalry and myriad claimants to the throne._ |
| [![Image 116: Law elective.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Law_elective.png/28px-Law_elective.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_elective.png) Elective Succession | * The primary heir is elected from among all the characters in the realm based on the support of [![Image 117: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") position holders. Members of the ruling family are favoured, but not overly so. * No [![Image 118: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty penalty from pretenders. | _None alive remember the age of elected kings, yet rumors reach us of a system rich in equity and fairness once championed by the Greeks. With a system of elective succession, we might avoid the bitter power struggles that plague our neighbors._ |
| [![Image 119: Law familial marriage.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/32/Law_familial_marriage.png/28px-Law_familial_marriage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_familial_marriage.png) Familial Marriage | * The eldest child of the ruler, no matter its gender, will be the heir, followed by more distant relatives regardless of gender. * Sibling marriage is allowed. | _In this manner of succession, the eldest child of the ruler will be the primary heir, regardless of gender. In order to secure a strong, legitimate bloodline, however, it becomes commonplace for royal siblings to intermarry, thus avoiding any chance for the lineage to be sullied._ |

#### Military Reforms[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Military Reforms") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Military Reforms")]

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Noble Retinues | * [![Image 120: Starting experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Starting_experience.png/28px-Starting_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Starting experience") Cohort Starting Experience: **+5%** * [![Image 121: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier: **+5%** | _The nobles of the realm are the font upon which the kingdom is built. Be they great Magnates or minor Barons, they are all obligated to answer our call to arms, levying troops from the peasants as well as calling upon their own retainers._ |
| Military Service | * [![Image 122: Unintegrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6c/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture "Unintegrated culture happiness") Unintegrated Culture Happiness: **-4%** * [![Image 123: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") Integrated Culture Happiness: **+5%** * [![Image 124: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier: **+10%** | _The privileged cultures in our kingdom are given many privileges and enjoy representation in government. In return they are obligated to all do military service when the state so requires. In return for their service any soldier will recieve an alotment of land to support them._ |
| Mercenary Contract Law | * [![Image 125: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance") Mercenary Maintenance: **-10%** * [![Image 126: Recruit mercenary cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d8/Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png/28px-Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Recruit_mercenary_cost_modifier.png) Recruit Mercenary Cost: **-25%** | _Our nation, rolling in wealth, has become a haven for foreign soldiers looking for work. Why should we risk our own people in defense of our home, when others will fight for us?_ |
| Royal Guard | * [![Image 127: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline") Discipline: **+5%** * [![Image 128: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") Levy Size Multiplier: **+2.5%** * Enables raising a [![Image 129: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion in the capital region | _A great kingdom such as ours cannot rely solely on levied troops to support the expansive foreign policies of the realm. A permanent and well drilled royal guard is not just a luxury that a monarch might use to impress lesser kings, it is instrumental to bending the world to our will._ |
| Royal Army | * [![Image 130: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") Legion Maintenance Cost: **-15%** * Enables raising [![Image 131: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions in all regions | _For a great empire it is necessary to maintain not just one but several permanent forces, both to support our foreign policy and to police and defend the realm against intruders._ |

#### Maritime Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Maritime Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Maritime Laws")]

We have no need of a more centralized maritime policy._ |
| Non-Interference Edict | * [![Image 133: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") National Commerce Income: **+15%** * [![Image 134: Diplomatic reputation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Diplomatic_reputation.png/28px-Diplomatic_reputation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_reputation "Diplomatic reputation") Diplomatic Reputation: **-5** * Creates a [![Image 135: Pirate haven.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f2/Pirate_haven.png/28px-Pirate_haven.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pirate "Pirate") Pirate Haven in a random owned [![Image 136: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port territory when enacted | _The seas bring fortune, as well as misery. By declaring that our state rescinds all protection to traders, we may see in an increase in piracy, but also in profits. We don't care who brings us trade, only that it gets here._ |
| Anti-Piracy Edict | * [![Image 137: Anti piracy cb.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Anti_piracy_cb.png/28px-Anti_piracy_cb.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Anti_piracy_cb.png) Anti-Piracy Casus Belli: **Yes** * [![Image 138: Citizen happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") National Citizen Happiness: **+4%** | _Piracy has, for long, been a tolerated evil. Now that our naval capability has reached a tipping point, we should aim to protect shipping in our waters, by providing state protection._ |

#### Taxation Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Taxation Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Taxation Laws")]

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No Taxation Laws |  | _We have no taxation law selected._ |
| Infrastructure Policy | * [![Image 139: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") National Tax Income: **-15%** * [![Image 140: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth") National Population Growth: **+0.05%** | _It may be a significant investment, but the improvements offered by a detailed program of public works, including sanitation, living conditions, and meeting places, will boost growth in our urban centers._ |
| Exemption for Nobility | * [![Image 141: Noble output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Pop_noble_output.png/28px-Pop_noble_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Noble output") National Noble Output: **-3%** * [![Image 142: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness") National Noble Happiness: **+6%** | _Taxes are a drain on the lifestyle of our most loyal and prominent subjects. It will not be popular, but abolishing all forms of tax on the nobility will surely earn us gratitude in some most unexpected places._ |

#### Domestic Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Domestic Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Domestic Laws")]

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No Domestic Laws |  | _We have no Domestic Laws selected._ |
| Relax Citizenship Status | * [![Image 143: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") Desired Citizen Ratio in Cities: **+10%** | _As our state grows ever larger, it becomes apparent that we are unable to rely solely on ancient and noble families to make up the lofty echelons of society. Relaxing the requirements to obtain full citizenship, however, may anger those lucky enough to already possess it._ |
| Courts for Landowners | * [![Image 144: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") Desired Freeman Ratio in Cities: **+10%** | _A common practice to appease smallholders, involved setting up a series of district courts to handle domestic disputes. Known as dicasteries to the Greeks, these were often established in foreign lands as a matter of course._ |

#### [[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Authority Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Authority Laws")]

Requirements:

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No Authority Laws |  | _We have no Authority Law selected._ |

### Tribal Laws[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Tribal Laws") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Tribal Laws")]

[![Image 165](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5b/Tribal_Laws_Screen.png/500px-Tribal_Laws_Screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Tribal_Laws_Screen.png)

The law interface for tribal countries

Tribes are different in many ways from other countries, focusing primarily on [![Image 166: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization and how sedentary their society is. Instead of unlocking from [technology](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology"), Tribal laws are separated into two distinct paths: one that up more laws the more negative the country's centralization is, and one whose progression is based on getting higher centralization. Passing laws as a tribal country also requires that all [![Image 167: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs are [![Image 168: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyal.

Laws themselves will also all either increase or decrease [![Image 169: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization over time, making laws an integral part of making a Tribe more migratory or in turning it more sedentary and ultimately a Republic or Monarchy. The choice in each group is typically between a stronger law that has a smaller effect on centralization, and a weaker one that changes centralization more quickly.

The additive effects of multiple laws also means that while it is slow at start the reformation of the country's society will tend to pick up pace over time as more laws are enacted.

#### Religious Integration[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Religious Integration") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Religious Integration")]

The freedom to choose how and whom to worship should remain a core tenet of our society._ |
| Encourage Syncretism | * [![Image 171: Unintegrated culture group happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture_group "Unintegrated culture group happiness") Unintegrated Culture Group Happiness: **+12%** * [![Image 172: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Monthly Centralization: **-0.05** | _Foreign Gods are of much interest to us. Myriad methods of faith exist, and we can only benefit from experiencing them all._ |
| Adopt Human Sacrifices | * [![Image 173: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Monthly Centralization: **-0.10** * [![Image 174: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") Omen Power: **+10%** | _The Power of an omen, our holy ones tell us, is directly related to the value and power of the sacrifice made in its name. By offering up those we capture in battle, we are sure to reap rewards eternal._ |
