| Word of Mouth |  | _We expect our edicts and laws to be passed about by word of mouth. Sometimes, the old ways are the best ways._ |
| Tribal Representatives | * [![Image 206: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Monthly Centralization: **+0.05** * [![Image 207: Monthly political influence modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f5/Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_political_influence_modifier.png) Monthly Political Influence: **+10%** | _By appointing representatives in each of our population centers; each with the power of officiate on local matters, we extend our authority to a great degree._ |
| Sedentary Bureaucracy | * [![Image 208: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Monthly Centralization: **+0.10** * [![Image 209: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") Pop Promotion Speed: **+2** | _Formalizing a static framework of officials will encourage our people to work within the confines of our decree._ |

#### War Philosophy[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=41 "Edit section: War Philosophy") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: War Philosophy")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 210: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Centralization is less or equal to -80

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Roving Warbands | * [![Image 211: Manpower recovery speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Manpower_recovery_speed.png/28px-Manpower_recovery_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower_recovery_speed "Manpower recovery speed") Manpower Recovery Speed: **+5%** | _The promise of loot and pillage is often enough to attract warriors of all stations._ |
| Rightful Domination | * [![Image 212: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") Enslavement Efficiency: **+10%** * [![Image 213: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National Slave Output: **+12%** | _Our society must make a place for itself through the industrious work of those less fortunate than ourselves. The taking of slaves must be made a priority._ |
| Honor in Battle | * [![Image 214: Land morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b8/Land_morale.png/28px-Land_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Land_morale.png) Morale of Armies: **+5%** * [![Image 215: Monthly war exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "Monthly war exhaustion") Monthly War Exhaustion: **-0.05** | _The only true honor comes from the defeat of a strong opponent. In war, we build a tradition and spirit the likes of which the world has never seen._ |

#### Code of Rights[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&veaction=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Code of Rights") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Laws&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Code of Rights")]

Requirements:

*   [![Image 216: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") Centralization is greater or equal to 80

| Law | Effects | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ad-Hoc Justice System | * [![Image 217: Tribesman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Tribesman happiness") National Tribesmen Happiness: **+4%** | _Justice is performed by elders, chiefs, and local ethnarchs, leading to a system over which we have little control._ |
| Rights of Man | * [![Image 218: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Country Civilization Level: **+5%** * [![Image 219: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") Desired Freemen Ratio in Cities: **+20%** | _Each one of our people should have an unalienable right to exist free of molestation from other within our tribe. These right must be set down in stone, that we may advance as a society._ |
| Rights of Birth | * [![Image 220: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Country Civilization Level: **+5%** * [![Image 221: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") Desired Citizen Ratio in Cities: **+20%** | _Those with an ancestry of nobility must be guaranteed a place of greater standing within our society. Only by acknowledging their superiority shall we grow to match our potential._ |


============================================================
# Characters
============================================================
Title: Characters - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/bvd-KC7U67E/hqdefault.jpg)

Characters and Inventions Revisited

Load video

Imperator: Rome - Characters & Inventions.

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a2/UI_character_view.png/500px-UI_character_view.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:UI_character_view.png)

The character view, provides ways to interact with character as well as showing summary view of family, rivals and friends.

In addition to and separate from [pops](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population"), Imperator: Rome defines individual **characters**. The characters in _Imperator_ are deeply detailed, and together with the pops and the politics are part of what makes a vibrant living world. They have portraits that age gradually, with lots of different ethnicities covering the world. Characters occupy [![Image 3: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position")[positions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position "Position") and serve as the rulers, advisors, governors, generals, and more who run the country and its administration, but also have fickle [![Image 4: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty and must be kept either loyal or in check lest they become discontent and use their considerable resources to start a [![Image 5: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war.

## Name[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Name") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Name")]

Every character has a **name** by which they are normally identified, partly assigned at birth, partly inherited from the character's parents, and partly given over the course of a character's life. A character's name can be divided into 4 distinct parts, usually arranged in the following structure:

**[Given name]****[Regnal Number]****[Nickname]****[Family name]**

Italic characters have a special naming system that reflects Roman naming conventions, with the cognomen (nickname) coming last and simplified names for females. Male Italic characters then use following naming structure:

**[Given name]****[Regnal Number]****[Family name]****[Nickname]**

Female Italic characters use following naming structure:

**[Family name]****[Given name (Number)]****[Regnal Number]****[Nickname]**

### Given name[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Given name") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Given name")]

A character's primary name is their **given name**, or **praenomen**, which is assigned at birth (or when created) and is usually randomly chosen from a list of names determined by the character's culture group, but can also be manually set or changed for children born to the current [![Image 6: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler. This is the main name that characters are usually referred to by.

Italic female characters are not assigned a usual praenomen. Instead, they are given a Latin **number** based on their order of birth, with the first daughter being "Prima", the second being "Secunda", the third being "Tertia", and so forth. Their given name is also placed after the family name, unlike with Italic male characters.

#### Regnal name[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Regnal name") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Regnal name")]

Certain countries and dynasties have a tradition of rulers taking on a specific **regnal name** when they ascend the throne instead of retaining their original personal names, typically in honour of the dynasty's founder. Under some circumstances, anointed heirs will also be given the choice to take on a regnal name before they fully ascend to the throne.

### Family name[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Family name") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Family name")]

When a character is born, he or she will typically take on the **family name** of his or her father, identifying the family that the character comes from. Depending on the culture, the family name may differ slightly between male and female forms. Children of minor characters will not receive a family name, and are known only by their given names.

Randomly generated minor characters will instead take a random name from the family name list of their culture group. Due to the relatively small number of possible family names for many culture groups, it is common to see unrelated characters with the same family name.

### Nickname[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Nickname") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Nickname")]

_Main article: [Cognomen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cognomen "Cognomen")_

Characters may gain a **nickname** or **cognomen** (for Italic-cultured characters) that references one of the characteristics or accomplishments, typically given to victorious [![Image 7: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") generals after a triumph or granted to a [![Image 8: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") governor, as well as through certain events. Characters of the Italic culture group have a more sophisticated system where cognomen can be gained when reaching adulthood and inherited from father to son, as well as being placed at the end of the name.

A character can only have one nickname at a time; if a character acquires a new nickname, the old one will be removed.

### Regnal Number[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Regnal Number") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Regnal Number")]

Rulers of [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") have a **regnal number** that distinguishes them from previous (and future) monarchs with the same name. Regnal numbers are in Roman numerals and usually counted starting from the beginning of the game, with the starting ruler receiving the regnal number **I**, though certain countries start with with a list of already used regnal names and numbers to ensure a historical naming scheme.

## Stats[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Stats") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Stats")]

Every character has a large range of stats that determine their skills and standing across all sorts of different fields, each with a different effect.

### Primary[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Primary") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Primary")]

_Main article: [Attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes")_

The 4 main stats, or **attributes**, represent the character's skills and ability in some of the most important facets of serving an office and ruling a country. Attributes are mostly fixed and generally will not vary much over the course of the game after a character reaches adulthood, unless they are seriously injured.

### Secondary[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Secondary") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Secondary")]

Secondary stats are more diverse and represent everything about a character from how well known or liked they are to their current health or loyalty to the state. Most of these are generally less important than a character's primary attributes, but should still be taken into consideration when appointing a character to an office or deciding whether or not they might become a threat.

*   [![Image 9: Age](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2d/Age.png/28px-Age.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Secondary "Age")**Age** is a requirement for many parts of public life, from education to taking office. A character reaches adulthood at **16**, though many types of [republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") have age requirements significantly above that for their rulerships, and older characters will become increasingly susceptible to terminal illnesses.
*   [![Image 10: Health.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Health.png/28px-Health.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Health "Health") The **Health** stat of a character ranges from 0 to 100, and is increased or decreased by various traits (particularly illnesses). If the health of a character reaches 0, that character will die. Using the **Seek Treatment** character interaction will increase the health of the character with a chance of permanently injuring the character. Unlike all other stats, the health of a character is not shown exactly - instead, the interface gives a general indication of how healthy any particular character is.
*   [![Image 11: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")**Statesmanship** represents how experienced the character is in the matters of government. It is accumulated over time by holding jobs and offices as well as the [![Image 12: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse attribute and some traits, and determines how effectively attributes are applied towards [![Image 13: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") government offices.
*   [![Image 14: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**Prominence** represents the fame of the character. Jobs and titles help bring characters to public attention, and make it more likely that they will be elected in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic").
*   [![Image 15: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**Popularity** is a measure of how the people see the character. In [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") high popularity characters are more likely to be elected leader of the republic and will have higher [![Image 16: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence. However even monarchies cannot ignore popular people - and monarchs must be mindful of their own popularity.
*   [![Image 17: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**Loyalty** is a measure of a character's loyalty to the state. Disloyal characters are more likely to cause problems to a ruler than loyal ones, will refuse to listen to orders, and can even drive a realm towards [![Image 18: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war. However even the most loyal of characters has their limit.
*   [![Image 19: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**Corruption** is a measurement of this character’s willingness to engage in underhanded practices. Greed, bribery and the bending of rules come hand-in-hand with high corruption. Corrupt characters will take more than their [![Image 20: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") wage would normally be and are likely to anger provinces under their control.
*   [![Image 21: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding")**[Holdings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding")** provide [![Image 22: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income and [![Image 23: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base to a character, and are usually concentrated among the family heads. However characters may also invest in territories they have a personal connection to.
*   [![Image 24: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Cost")**Wealth**: Every character can accumulate money based on their jobs and holdings, which can be used to further their personal ambitions.
*   [![Image 25: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**Power Base** represents how much influence a character has in a country, and is accumulated from many different sources, such as [![Image 26: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") jobs and offices, [![Image 27: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings, [![Image 28: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") commanded and loyal cohorts, governorships, [![Image 29: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige (applied to family heads only), and more.
*   [![Image 30: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Election_term.png)**Succession Support** determines who will become ruler when the current ruler dies or finishes their term. How this is calculated depends on the ruler type: [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") determine this entirely by familial relationships, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") by popularity, prominence, family prestige, and faction support, and [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") by the value of the character's attributes.
*   [![Image 31: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs")[![Image 32: Party traditionalist.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4a/Party_traditionalist.png/28px-Party_traditionalist.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traditionalists "Traditionalists")[![Image 33: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats")[![Image 34: Party optimates.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/37/Party_optimates.png/28px-Party_optimates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Optimates "Optimates")[![Image 35: Party boni.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0a/Party_boni.png/28px-Party_boni.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Boni "Boni")[![Image 36: Party populares.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Party_populares.png/28px-Party_populares.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Populares "Populares")**Faction Conviction**: In a republic, every adult character has a predilection for each of the 3 senatorial factions, with the current value the sum of the total accumulated predilection so far. The amount gained (or lost) per month depends largely on the character's traits, attributes, and other characteristics, and they will join whichever faction they have the highest conviction for.
*   [![Image 37: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence")**Senatorial Influence**: The number of seats that a faction controls is determined by the total senatorial influence of its members. Senatorial influence is largely determined by a character's [![Image 38: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base, but can also be increased by [![Image 39: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity and a number of traits, while governors and generals will find it hard to effectively exert their influence far away from the capital.

## Relationships[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Relationships") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Relationships")]

Most characters will have different relationships with various other characters, whether inherited through blood and marriage or developed through interactions. These relationships are important in determining how characters interact with each other, and the ruler's relationships are particularly important for [![Image 40: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty and the functioning of the realm.

### Spouse[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Spouse") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Spouse")]

_See also: [Consort](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Consort "Consort")_

Every adult character can have one **spouse** of the opposite gender. Characters will generally only marry within their own country, although it is possible for a marriage to be arranged between the ruling families of two different [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"). A character will usually only have children with their spouse and will not reproduce if unmarried, although there are some events where bastards can appear, mostly firing for the ruler. Marriage of the ruling character and close relatives in a player-controlled realm is handled through the **Arrange Marriage** interaction. Incestuous marriages between close family members (parents, siblings, children, and grandchildren) are forbidden unless the [![Image 41: Law familial marriage.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/32/Law_familial_marriage.png/28px-Law_familial_marriage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Law_familial_marriage.png)[Familial Marriage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Succession_Law "Laws") monarchy law is enabled.

In [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), the choice of spouse is particularly important as the consort is an official position that can contribute their [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes") over that of the ruler if they are higher.

### Relatives[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Relatives") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Relatives")]

Characters have **relatives** as determined by their familial relations, with the parents, siblings, and children displayed directly in the interface. The main importance of relatives are in [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), where succession is determined by family relations with the exact rules depending on the [Succession Law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Succession_Law "Laws"). Close family members of the ruler - parents, siblings, children, and grandchildren, even if not of the ruler's family - can be married off with the **Arrange Marriage** interaction. Bloodline traits can also only be passed down from parent to child, with the exact rules depending on the trait. If they are currently minor characters, it is also significantly cheaper to adopt certain close relatives - children and siblings - into the ruling family.

### Friends[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Friends") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Friends")]

Every character can have a certain number of **friends**, controlled by the [![Image 42: Max friends](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/66/Max_friends.png/28px-Max_friends.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Friend "Max friends")**max friends** modifier. Friends tend to be loyal and supportive of each other, such as supporting the government if friendly with the ruler and moving to support the other side in a [civil war](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") if one of their friends goes into rebellion.

Friends of the ruler get the following modifiers:

It is generally useful for the ruler to make friends with other powerful characters in the realm, such as family heads and strong generals and governors, especially those with a somewhat lower [![Image 43: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty who could use the buffer. In particular, making friends with family heads may give you opportunities to revoke their most populous holdings, reducing their income and power base.

Friendships between the rulers of two different countries will increase mutual relations by [![Image 44: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**25** opinion, making it useful for increasing opinion a little higher for diplomatic action approval if relations have already been fully improved and gifts sent. Foreign characters can also be recruited to join a country's court if they are friends with the ruler, at the cost of [![Image 45: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion.

[![Image 46: Friend event.jpg](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/87/Friend_event.jpg/500px-Friend_event.jpg)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Friend_event.jpg)

The ruler can make friends using the **Make Friends** character interaction, as long as they have friend slots remaining. When you start befriending someone, you will be presented with several events, where each option provides different amounts of progress. You need to choose either:

*   2 large options
*   3 moderate
*   1 large, 1 moderate, 1 small

This process might cost you up to 100 gold or 5000 manpower or increase tyranny by 5.

Friendships can be ended at any time with the **Remove Friend** character interaction.

### Rivals[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Rivals") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Rivals")]

Every character can have a certain number of **rivals**, controlled by the [![Image 47: Max rivals](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1c/Max_rivals.png/28px-Max_rivals.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rival "Max rivals")**max rivals** modifier. Rivals dislike each other and will tend to take aggressive actions and schemes against each other, up to and including assassinations.

Rivals of the ruler get the following modifiers:

Rivalry does not have as strong a loyalty impact as friendship does, but it is nonetheless recommended to generally avoid rivalries with powerful characters.

If two country rulers are rivals, each country will get [![Image 48: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**-50** opinion of each other, significantly impairing relations.

In addition to as an effect of various events, rivalries can be started at any time with the **Make Rival** interaction as long as there are rivalry spots remaining. Making a domestic rival costs [![Image 49: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**5** popularity, while making a rival of a foreign ruler requires the [![Image 50: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion") relations be below **0**. A domestic rival can be removed at any time with the **Remove Rival** interaction at the cost of [![Image 51: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**30** political influence, and a foreign rival if [![Image 52: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion") relations are above **0**.

## Family[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Family") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Family")]

Most characters in a country are part of a **major family**. Members of a family are usually related to each other and children will inherit the family of their primary parent (usually the father), but it is also possible for characters to join a major family through adoption.

Characters in a major family will automatically marry shortly after they reach adulthood, with the main exception of the ruling family of a player-controlled country. Members of the ruling family will usually not marry except through the Arrange Marriage interaction; as only close relatives of the ruler can marry using this interaction, this means that more distantly related family members will generally not marry. This is generally most problematic in [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") as the ruling family rotates in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") and [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal"), and means that without intervention, the ruling family will typically shrink to become much smaller than the other families, limiting the talent pool that can be drawn from when giving out [positions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position "Position") to appease the ruling family. This can be dealt with either by constantly adopting promising minor characters or marrying close relatives, particularly sisters, daughters, or granddaughters to more distantly related members of the family to ensure they reproduce.

Characters that are part of the ruling family get [![Image 53: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+0.01** monthly prominence and [![Image 54: Primary heir attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/01/Law_agnatic.png/28px-Law_agnatic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Successors_and_pretenders "Primary heir attraction")**+10** primary heir attraction.

### Expected number of positions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Expected number of positions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Expected number of positions")]

_Main article: [Position#Mechanics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Position")_

Major families expect to have their members hold a certain proportion of all currently held assignable positions in order to be **content**. Any family that does not hold its expected number of positions is considered [![Image 55: Scorned family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Scorned_family.png/28px-Scorned_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Scorned family")**scorned**, which gives a significant loyalty malus, while a family that instead holds at least double the number of expected positions is [![Image 56: Grateful family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/65/Grateful_family.png/28px-Grateful_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Grateful family")**grateful**, which gives a number of bonuses. Ensuring that all families are content, even if this requires appointing less-skilled but more politically connected characters to important positions, is an important part of character and family management.

### Family prestige[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Family prestige") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Family prestige")]

[![Image 57: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") Every family has an associated **prestige** value, which represents how prominent the family has been throughout the country's history. Prestige is accumulated passively from all the [positions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position "Position"), wealth and cohorts controlled by the family's members; prestige can also be increased from various events. Family prestige gives bonuses to [![Image 58: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base, [![Image 59: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence, [![Image 60: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence, [![Image 61: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship, and more to the [head of the family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family"), with the effects scaled according to how large a family's prestige is compared to the total prestige of all the families in the country.

[![Image 62: Deify ruler cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png/28px-Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Apotheosis "Apotheosis") Apotheosis with the [![Image 63: Magna Graecia (DLC).png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Magna_Graecia_%28DLC%29.png/28px-Magna_Graecia_%28DLC%29.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Magna_Graecia_(DLC) "Magna Graecia (DLC)") Magna Graecia DLC also requires the family of the deified ruler to have at least [![Image 64: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**800** prestige.

### Head of family[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Head of family") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Head of family")]

Each major family has a **head**, usually the oldest member of the family; the main exception is the ruling family of [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), which is always helmed by the ruler. The head of each major family gets the following modifiers for each percentage of the total [![Image 65: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige in the country that the family has:

[![Image 66: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief")[Clan chiefs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") in [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations have a somewhat different set of modifiers.

Additionally, all [holdings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") pass to the head of the family when a character dies, which means that heads of families tend to have the highest [![Image 67: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") wealth and [![Image 68: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base in the country, even before considering positions. It is therefore dangerous to appoint them as governors or generals, as the [![Image 69: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty impact of their very high power base means that it will be difficult to keep them consistently loyal. Appointing them as a [researcher](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Researcher "Researcher") or to a government [office](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") is recommended if they need to be given a position, as those do not increase power base and matter less if they become disloyal.

When a country is conquered or annexed, three family heads from the conquered state can be invited to join the conqueror's court as minor characters, along with a few of their family members. All other characters will be executed or disappear into obscurity.

### Number of families[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Number of families") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Number of families")]

The maximum **number of major families** in a country depends on its [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank"), ranging from 3 for [![Image 70: Country rank 2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Country_rank_2.png/28px-Country_rank_2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City_state "City state") City States and [![Image 71: Country rank 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Country_rank_3.png/28px-Country_rank_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Local_power "Local power") Local Powers to 5 for [![Image 72: Country rank 6.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Country_rank_6.png/28px-Country_rank_6.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Great_power "Great power") Great Powers. In tribes, the number of families is instead the number of [![Image 73: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief")[clan chiefs](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief"), which is generally the same as the number of families for other government forms, except for [![Image 74: Country rank 5.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Country_rank_5.png/28px-Country_rank_5.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Major_power "Major power") Major Powers.

If the number of major families is less than the maximum - usually after rising in rank - then an [event](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Rise_of_the_family_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rise of the family events (page does not exist)") will trigger creating a new family from one of the 3 most powerful minor characters in the country. Similarly, if the number of major families is higher than the maximum, then one of them will [fall from grace](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Rise_of_the_family_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rise of the family events (page does not exist)") and all of its members will be demoted to minor characters.

### Minor characters[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Minor characters") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Minor characters")]

Characters that are not part of a major family are considered **minor characters**. Minor characters do not have a family head and do not benefit from the effects of [![Image 75: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, which generally means that they are unlikely to accumulate a large [![Image 76: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base unless granted a particularly large army or region to govern. They will also not marry automatically and usually do not have a family, although it is possible to manually marry relatives of the ruler to them like with any other unmarried adult character.

Minor characters will automatically appear in the court from time to time and might be of any existing [culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") or [religion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") in the country, though they will still generally be of the primary culture and religion. In [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") and [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") they can be **adopted** into the ruling family at any time with a base cost of [![Image 77: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**10%** of the family's current prestige and [![Image 78: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)**10** legitimacy (more if the character has a high [![Image 79: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base); other families may also adopt minor characters if their size becomes too small.

## Positions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Positions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Positions")]

_Main article: [Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position "Position")_

One of the main roles of characters are to be assigned to various [![Image 80: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position")**positions** in a country. Besides being the ruler of a country, they could be assigned to govern a province, command an army, handle research, or be given a role in the government, providing various bonuses depending on their traits and attributes and the position they are assigned to in exchange for [![Image 81: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") pay, [![Image 82: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence, and [![Image 83: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") increased power base. Major families will always expect to hold a certain percentage of all positions in the realm, and can become grateful if they are given much more than expected. Managing the family composition, loyalty, and competence of position holders is an important part of internal management and ensuring a stable, prosperous realm.

Positions in the government include:

*   Ruler
*   Co-Ruler and Consort
*   Government Offices
*   Researcher
*   Governor
*   Commander
*   Admiral

## Power base[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Power base") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Power base")]

[![Image 84: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") A character's **power base** represents the weight of influence a character has, though his/her wealth, connections, commanded and loyal soldiers, and level of authority. Every unit of [![Image 85: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base decreases a character's [![Image 86: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty by **0.55**, making power base one of the most important factors decreasing [![Image 87: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty, and if the proportion of [![Image 88: Disloyal power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3c/Disloyal_power_base.png/28px-Disloyal_power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Disloyal power base")**disloyal power base** is higher than the [![Image 89: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold, the country will progress towards a [![Image 90: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war. In [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), a character's [![Image 91: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base is also the base from which [![Image 92: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senatorial influence is calculated. Generally, a close eye should always be kept on characters with a high [![Image 93: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base, keeping them as [![Image 94: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyal as is feasible (or at least above the disloyalty threshold with some buffer to spare) while being sure not to give them any positions that would result in even higher [![Image 95: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base (as that can bring the [![Image 96: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty penalty from high [![Image 97: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base up to unmanageable levels).

The main sources of power base are:

*   **+2** from all [![Image 98: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") positions (including [![Image 99: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") rulers, co-rulers, consorts, primary heirs, offices, researchers, governors, generals, admirals, and party leaders)
*   **+1.2** to [heads of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") for each percentage of the total [![Image 100: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige in the country their family has
*   **+0.2** to the [![Image 101: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler for each percentage of the country's [![Image 102: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population in the [![Image 103: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital region
*   **+0.7** to [![Image 104: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governors for each percentage of the country's [![Image 105: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population in their governorship
*   **+0.3** to [![Image 106: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanders for each percentage of the country's [![Image 107: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") army under their command, not including any [![Image 108: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") cohorts loyal to the commander, up to a maximum of **+1.2** per commanded cohort (applies for both [![Image 109: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion legates and [![Image 110: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") governors with levies raised)
*   **+1** to for each percentage of the country's [![Image 111: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") army that are [![Image 112: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") cohorts loyal to the character, even if under the command of another character (does not apply for the cohorts loyal to the [![Image 113: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler), up to a maximum of **+4** per loyal cohort
*   **+0.05** for the character's number of [![Image 114: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal veteran cohorts as a percentage of the country's [![Image 115: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") army size, up to a maximum of **+0.2** per loyal veteran cohort
*   **+0.1** for each percentage of the country's [![Image 116: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") ships commanded by an admiral, up to a maximum of **+1** per commanded ship
*   **+0.5** for each percentage of the country's [![Image 117: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") territories held as [![Image 118: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings, up to a maximum of **+10** per holding

## Traits[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Traits") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Traits")]

_Main article: [Trait](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trait "Trait")_

**Traits** represent different facets of a character's personality, lifestyle, abilities, conditions, and more, and have a significant effect on a character's [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes") and ability to perform in [![Image 119: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") positions, as well as affecting how they respond to events (particularly for the AI).

Most traits have base effects on character attributes and stats that are always active, but some also give modifiers that are only active when the character holds a position like being the [![Image 120: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler or a [![Image 121: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander. These position-specific modifiers affect the target of the position - the army or navy for [![Image 122: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanders and [![Image 123: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") admirals, the region for [![Image 124: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governors, and the entire country for [![Image 125: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") rulers.

Traits can be categorized into one of four categories:

*   **Personality**
*   **Military**
*   **Health**
*   **Status**

Some traits, particularly personality traits, have **opposites** that they are mutually exclusive with. A character will never have two traits that are opposite with each other at the same time; if a character gets a new trait whose opposite it currently has, the opposite trait will be immediately removed.

## Schemes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Schemes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Schemes")]

_Main article: [Scheme](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Scheme "Scheme")_

Every character has an ongoing **scheme**, or **ambition**, at any time that represents their current objectives and activities apart from any currently held positions. These schemes vary widely, from learning and growing up or investing in existing [![Image 126: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings to increasing [![Image 127: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence and plotting against other characters. Schemes are generally the main mechanic by which non-ruler characters have agency and interact with other characters and their holdings. While the schemes of most characters are fairly unimportant and can for the most part be ignored, some, particularly those of powerful characters or those otherwise important to the ruler, can matter significantly, whether positively or negatively. Most schemes can be stopped by using the [![Image 128: Persuade to drop scheme.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a2/Persuade_to_drop_scheme.png/28px-Persuade_to_drop_scheme.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Persuade_to_drop_scheme.png)**Persuade to Drop Scheme** interaction, which costs [![Image 129: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**10** political influence.

## Interactions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Interactions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Interactions")]

_Main article: [Character interaction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character\_interaction "Character interaction")_

Most actions on characters can be performed through **character interactions**, available either on the right of the character screen or by right clicking the character's portrait. In a [republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), many of these interactions will require [![Image 130: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") Senate approval.

## References[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&veaction=edit&section=26 "Edit section: References") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Characters&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: References")]

*   There is a [Character Finder mod](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1855664157) that does all the searching for you. Not compatible with ironman.


============================================================
# Position
============================================================
Title: Position - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position")[Positions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position) consist of the major jobs and offices involved in the command and administration of a country and its military. They are filled by the [characters](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters "Characters") of a realm and are one of the main mechanics by which the country and its characters interact, as not only the means by which characters can contribute their skills and attributes towards the betterment of the realm but also the most important source and easily controlled source of [![Image 2: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base and [![Image 3: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. Proper character and position management requires a fine balance between choosing office holders who are the best and most competent at their jobs and the requirements of family loyalty and realm stability.

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

[![Image 4](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5a/General_appointement_screen.png/500px-General_appointement_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:General_appointement_screen.png)

Appointing a new general

Most positions are filled by appointment and can generally be filled and dismissed freely by the player. Appointing a character to a position gives the loyalty modifier **Granted Office**, increasing [![Image 5: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty by **+15** for **5** years, while revoking an office gives the **Revoked Offices** modifier decreasing [![Image 6: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty by **-5** for **2.5** years. In general, a character can only hold one position at any given time, with the exception of certain ruler-related positions, and cannot be dismissed or replaced from their position if they become unloyal. Only adult citizens can be appointed to a position, with any gender restrictions also applied if relevant to the country.

Characters in positions are usually paid a certain percentage of the country's [![Image 7: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income as their [![Image 8: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**wage**, which is by modified country-wide with the [![Image 9: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**Monthly Wages for Characters** modifier and per character by the [![Image 10: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**Monthly Wage** modifier. Notably, each point of a character's [![Image 11: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption increases the wage paid by **2.5%**, which can make wages unmanageably large if allowed to spiral out of control. Depending on the exact position, position holders may also get a certain amount of [![Image 12: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, [![Image 13: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence, [![Image 14: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, and/or [![Image 15: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship each month. All positions also give their holder [![Image 16: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+2** power base, which depending on the position can then be increased based on the size of armies and navies commanded or regions governed.

Each major [family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family "Family") will expect that their members hold a certain proportion of all currently held assignable positions, calculated as:

![Image 17: {\displaystyle {\text{Expected Positions}}={\text{round}}\left({\frac {{\text{Total Number of Positions}}\cdot 0.7}{\text{Number of Major Families}}}\right)}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/d48c721cedd8d1885e1898abc3db27deb33d370c)
This includes positions such as government offices, governors, generals, and researchers, but not those that are chosen or elected outside of direct player control such as rulers or party leaders.

Any family that does not hold its expected number of positions is considered [![Image 18: Scorned family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Scorned_family.png/28px-Scorned_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Scorned family")**scorned** and receives the following stacking modifiers to all its members for each missing expected position:

For instance, a family that expects to get 4 positions but only has 1 will get [![Image 19: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-15** Loyalty, [![Image 20: Party optimates.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/37/Party_optimates.png/28px-Party_optimates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Optimates "Optimates")**-0.06** Monthly Optimates Conviction, and [![Image 21: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs")**-0.06** Monthly Oligarchs Conviction.

A family that instead holds at least **double** the number of expected positions is [![Image 22: Grateful family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/65/Grateful_family.png/28px-Grateful_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Grateful family")**grateful** and receives the following modifiers to all its members:

It is usually important to balance positions between families, even if this results in suboptimal office holders, in order to ensure that families are not scorned. Alternatively, it may also be feasible to completely sideline a family and ensuring that it has no positions and little power base in order to appease the other families and more easily give them enough positions to get the grateful family bonuses.

Here is a table showing the maximum amount of happy families possible per number of families while still meeting the expectations of all other families.:

Total Families Max Happy Families Total Jobs(Job per family breakdown)
5 3 32(8+8+8+4+4)
4 2 24(8+8+4+4)
3 2 18(8+8+4)

This table assumes that you have enough potential employees.

## Ruler[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ruler") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ruler")]

[![Image 23](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/22/Tribal_ruler_interface.png/500px-Tribal_ruler_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Tribal_ruler_interface.png)

The standard interface for the ruler

[![Image 24: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Each state is headed by a **ruler**, prominently visible on the **Government** tab of the [![Image 25: Government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government panel, and their [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes"), and [relationships](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Relationships "Characters") have a significant effect on state modifiers, [![Image 26: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty, and [![Image 27: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Senate approval") Senate support, among other things. Rulers with high attributes can be a considerable boon to their country, significantly strengthening the state and allowing the nation to punch well above its weight, while corrupt, low attribute rulers can inflict major penalties that a state must struggle through. However, depending on the government form, it may be possible to cover weak areas with a co-ruler or consort that has appropriately complementary attributes.

Unlike most positions, rulers are not appointed and dismissed at the whim of the player. The [![Image 28: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession")**succession** of power is instead determined by the type of [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") - dynastic inheritance in a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), Senatorial election in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), and rotation between Clan Chiefs in [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations, with rulers either reigning until death (as in monarchies and tribes) or for a certain fixed term (as in republics). Monarchies and Tribes tend to stay with the same ruler for much longer and so while the nation will enjoy prosperity under great rulers for a much longer period of time they will also have to suffer for extended periods of misrule by poor rulers, while the relatively rapid elections of Republics mean that their ruler attributes are on the whole more even. The lessened level of control over the choice of ruler, particularly in monarchies, means that countries must be prepared to deal with incompetent or problematic rulers more often than with other positions.

The ruler is always considered the governor of the [![Image 29: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region") and thus will [![Image 30: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command any [![Image 31: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy raised in that region, but cannot be appointed to any other position (in particular, rulers cannot ever command [![Image 32: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions or [![Image 33: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") navies). Despite this, rulers do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds. Many events also take the point of view of the ruler, and different options may become available or unavailable based on the ruler's traits and attributes. Rulers of other states are prominently displayed in the Diplomacy screen, and building friends with foreign rulers can be a useful way to improve relations with another country.

Ruler / Co-Ruler Modifiers | Stat/Attribute | Bonuses |
| --- | --- |
