Note that does not include modifiers on the capital region and levies that a ruler gives by virtue of being the governor of the capital region.

In addition, the ruler gets the following character modifiers:

Note that in addition to the loyalty modifier, a ruler's loyalty is always fixed to [![Image 57: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**100** regardless of any other modifiers.

### Co-Ruler[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Co-Ruler") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Co-Ruler")]

[![Image 58](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Republic_ruler_interface.png/500px-Republic_ruler_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Republic_ruler_interface.png)

The aristocratic republic interface for the ruler, including the co-ruler

[Aristocratic Republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#aristocratic_republic "Government") are distinguished by power-sharing between two rulers who collectively co-head the government, the second of which is represented ingame by the **co-ruler**. Co-rulers are considered equivalent to the ruler for most ingame mechanics, contributing any of their four main attributes that they are more skilled in than the ruler (effectively providing a bonus to ruler attributes), averaging ruler [![Image 59: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity between the ruler and co-ruler, and adding their [![Image 60: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption to that of the ruler for the purposes of country modifiers. Unlike rulers, the co-ruler does not always have [![Image 61: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") 100 loyalty, but instead gives a [![Image 62: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**-1%** political influence gain malus for every point of [![Image 63: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") below **50**. The co-ruler is elected alongside the ruler in each election at the end of the term, with the second place candidate being elected with the same term and requirements as the main ruler. It is possible and common for the two co-rulers to be of different [factions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Faction "Faction").

Like rulers, co-rulers do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds unless they also hold another position, of which they can only be appointed as army commanders (legates, tribunes, generals) and admirals.

Co-rulers get the following character modifiers:

### Consort[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Consort") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Consort")]

[![Image 64](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d1/Monarchy_ruler_interface.png/500px-Monarchy_ruler_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monarchy_ruler_interface.png)

The monarchy interface for the ruler, including the consort

In [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), the ruler can instead be supported by the **consort**, who is the spouse of the ruler. Like co-rulers, consorts will replace a ruler's attributes with their own for the purposes of running the country in any category where they are more skilled, and give a [![Image 65: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**-1%** political influence gain malus for every point of [![Image 66: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty below **50**. It can therefore be very useful to marry rulers and potential heirs to spouses whose attributes can compensate for any weak points in their attributes. Unlike in a co-rulership, a consort's [![Image 67: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity and [![Image 68: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption do not count towards ruler popularity and corruption for the purposes of calculating country modifiers.

Like rulers and co-rulers, consorts do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds unless they also hold another position. However, unlike them, consorts can be assigned to any other position as long as the gender rules allow for it. In addition, by marrying into the royal family, consorts expect their children or descendants will eventually take the throne; if a consort has any living children, they will get a [![Image 69: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-15** loyalty malus if the primary heir is not one of their children or descendants.

Consorts get the following character modifiers:

### Primary Heir[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Primary Heir") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Primary Heir")]

[![Image 70](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/ed/Primary_heir_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Primary_heir_interface.png)

The monarchy interface for the primary heir

A [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") also has a special title for the **primary heir** who is first in line to inherit the throne. There are no mechanics or country modifiers associated with the primary heir, but the heir is still paid a monthly wage and gets bonuses to popularity, prominence, statesmanship, and loyalty. As with consorts, primary heirs do not count towards the number of positions that a family holds unless they also hold another position, but they can be assigned to any other position as long as the gender rules allow for it.

Primary Heirs get the following character modifiers:

Consider giving the primary heir positions that come with a large [![Image 71: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base as they are more loyal than most characters. Examples include a governorship over a large region, or a general commanding many cohorts. A position that gives extra [![Image 72: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, such as command over a [![Image 73: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion, is particularly useful as high popularity increases [![Image 74: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession") succession support for the heir and reduces the [![Image 75: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty loss on succession.

## Government offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government offices")]

[![Image 76](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Republic_Rome_government_offices_screen.png/500px-Republic_Rome_government_offices_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Republic_Rome_government_offices_screen.png)

The government offices interface

Every country has a set of **eight** major **government offices** that can be filled, representing important posts in the administration of the country. Every office is associated with a particular country modifier, the strength of which depends on the **effective rating** of the office holder. The effective rating is determined by multiplying the attribute of the office holder that is associated with the office by the holder's [![Image 77: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")[statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship"), which means that more experienced characters may be more effective at an office than a younger neophyte even if their innate skill is not as high. Furthermore, there are [various](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Architect_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Architect events (page does not exist)")[events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Stability_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stability events (page does not exist)") that can fire regularly affecting [![Image 78: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") ruler popularity and [![Image 79: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability depending on the [![Image 80: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship of various office holders. This means that maintaining an officer for longer periods of time is more effective than constantly switching them out for newer characters with better attributes (but lower statesmanship), and as the main source of increasing statesmanship is itself being employed in an office this encourages appointing new up-and-coming characters to less important posts (including other offices but also research and governor posts, as their effectiveness does not depend on statesmanship) first in order to build up their experience and statesmanship before promoting them to higher and more crucial offices. Office holders also get bonuses to [![Image 81: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence and [![Image 82: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige, which makes them more favoured to be elected to ruling office.

Government offices are also the main source of [![Image 83: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") monthly political influence generation. Every office holder generates [![Image 84: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**+0.25** monthly political influence, scaled by their [![Image 85: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty as a fraction out of 100; this means that filling offices highly loyal officers will ensure a high level of political influence to use on all sorts of different projects, while a administration that is constantly working to block and undermine itself will find it difficult to gain the capital to do anything at all. Therefore, while officers do not have the increased power base of governors or generals that makes maintaining their loyalty important for avoiding civil wars, it is still important to keep an eye on the loyalty of office holders to ensure an adequate supply of political power for government actions.

With the exception of the consort and primary heir positions, government offices are mutually exclusive with all other positions. Office holders can generally be freely appointed and dismissed, but like all other appointed positions officers will refuse to leave their post if they are unloyal.

The offices available to a country depend on the government type. All governments have an office dedicated to [![Image 86: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion change, [![Image 87: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience, [![Image 88: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") tax income, [![Image 89: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omen power, and [![Image 90: Health.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Health.png/28px-Health.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Health "Health") health, while the other three vary, but usually have an office that affects happiness directly or indirectly, a second office with military bonuses, and a modifier for a government-specific mechanic. Note that only the generic names are given below; many cultures have unique localisations for each of the offices.

### Republic offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Republic offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Republic offices")]

In addition to the usual three offices, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") have offices for [![Image 91: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval") party approval, [![Image 92: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods") divine sacrifice cost, and [![Image 93: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Censor | [![Image 94: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") | [![Image 95: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval") Party approval: **+0.02** * Note: The Party approval is applied to the faction that the Censor is part of | * [![Image 96: Monthly wage ### Monarchy offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Monarchy offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Monarchy offices")]

The unique [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") offices give modifiers for [![Image 154: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy, a military bonus for [![Image 155: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance") mercenary army maintenance, as well as [![Image 156: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change, but have a weaker [![Image 157: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifier office than other government types.

### Tribal offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Tribal offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Tribal offices")]

[Tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") offices give a military bonus in [![Image 214: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower recovery speed and have happiness modifiers for [![Image 215: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen and [![Image 216: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens. Notably, their [![Image 217: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion office is weaker than in republics or monarchies, which is compensated for by a stronger [![Image 218: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") national tax modifier.

| Magistrate | [![Image 261: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") | [![Image 262: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") National tax: **+2%** | * [![Image 263: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+2%** Monthly Wage * [![Image 264: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+0.10** Monthly Character Prominence * [![Image 265: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**+0.10** Monthly Family Prestige * [![Image 266: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+10** Prominence * [![Image 267: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")**+0.80%** Monthly Statesmanship | _This office is in charge of the tribe's treasury and the collection of tribute in the name of the chief._ |
| Apothecary | [![Image 268: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") | [![Image 269: Health.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Health.png/28px-Health.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Health "Health") Health: **+0.005%** | * [![Image 270: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+1%** Monthly Wage * [![Image 271: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+0.05** Monthly Character Prominence * [![Image 272: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**+0.10** Monthly Family Prestige * [![Image 273: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+5** Prominence * [![Image 274: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship")**+0.50%** Monthly Statesmanship | _This office supervises the medical treatment and health of the chief and tribal nobility._ |

## Governors[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Governors") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Governors")]

[![Image 275](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/1/17/Governor_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Governor_interface.png)

The governor in the territory interface

**Governors** are appointed to lead the administration of provinces too far from the capital to be effectively controlled from the capital, effectively ruling a particular [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region") on behalf of the central government. A governor can be appointed to any non-capital region that the country owns territories in, though they may only administer a single region at a time. Typically, [![Image 276: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse is the most important attribute for determining a governor's skill, giving a [![Image 277: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Local output")**+1.5%** population output modifier for each point. Changing the [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") of a province requires that its region has a governor; a policy will be automatically selected and set or reset each time a new governor is appointed, and the effects of a governor policy are also scaled to the governor's [![Image 278: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse according to the following formula:

![Image 279: {\displaystyle {\text{Effective Policy Modifier}}={\frac {{\text{Governor Finesse}}+1}{10}}\cdot {\text{Base Policy Modifier}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/01ae0533664bc0c5ecedbe5ac5b38997f98e7f74)
In addition to heading the regional administration, governors also [![Image 280: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command any [![Image 281: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies raised from their governorships. Just as with all other commanders, governors that are currently commanding levies will contribute their [![Image 282: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial to the army's modifiers and gain [![Image 283: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base based on the proportion of all cohorts in all the armies they have under their command, which means that [![Image 284: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial skill is also an important consideration for governorships with a large levy that is expected to make up a significant part of the country's army. Note that governors will always command all levies raised from their governorships and cannot be replaced as commander (unless the position of governor itself is revoked), even if they are split; this is the only case where a single character can have command over multiple different armies.

Governors also have a significant impact on provincial loyalty. Every province in a governorship will get [![Image 285: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**+0.004** local provincial loyalty for each point of governor [![Image 286: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty above **50** (to a maximum of **+0.20**), so it is quite useful to appoint more loyal governors to troublesome regions (note that there is no loyalty malus for having an unloyal governor). There are also a number of [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits") governors can have that give various bonuses or maluses to territories in their governorship.

A region that does not have a governor appointed will have a severe malus for each of its territories:

In addition, any levies raised from a region with no governor will have no commander, significantly reducing their effectiveness in battle.

Unlike most other positions, a governor is usually paid only a very small [![Image 287: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") wage directly from the treasury. Instead, governors are permitted to collect a portion of the region's income as the main part of their wage, with the exact amount adjusted by the country's [![Image 288: Governor wages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Change_governor_policy_cost.png/28px-Change_governor_policy_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor wages")**governor wage** modifier. A governor's wage is therefore mostly proportional to the overall wealth of their region, and every territory in a governor's region will get a base malus of [![Image 289: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax")**-25%** local tax and [![Image 290: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade")**-25%** local commerce modifier. Note that governor corruption only affects the wage they draw from the treasury, not the wage they draw from their region, which means that corrupt governors generally do not have as significant an impact on income as other office holders. However, governor corruption does decrease [![Image 291: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty by **-0.0025** per point up to **-0.25** loyalty at [![Image 292: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") 100 corruption, which means that highly corrupt governors can be very detrimental to the stability of the realm if not handled properly - either by [imposing sanctions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Interactions "Characters") or removing them from their post altogether.

In addition to the [![Image 293: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+2** power base from the governor office, all governors will also gain [![Image 294: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+0.7** power base for every percentage of the country's [![Image 295: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population in the governor's region, and if their governorship's [![Image 296: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies are raised they will gain further power base by virtue of the cohorts they [![Image 297: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command. Governors will therefore take up a significant portion of the country's total power base, particularly if the country is relatively small and/or expanded beyond its capital region without consolidating that region first; those of large, populous regions especially must be kept (or appointed to be) content and loyal. However, in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") the needs of being away from the capital to run the provincial administration means that governors have a vastly reduced [![Image 298: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence which could be used to sway the balance of power in the Senate, though appointing a powerful character as a governor could easily become a problem in of itself. The [![Image 299: Admiral loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor loyalty")**loyalty of governors** modifier applies [loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") modifiers specifically to governors, and is largely given as a bonus by a few [![Image 300: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonder effects, [national ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas"), [![Image 301: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, and [![Image 302: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions that can help keep governors under control. Note that governors are not affected by the [![Image 303: Admiral loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/General "General loyalty") loyalty of generals modifier, even when leading a levy army.

Like all other appointed positions, governors can be assigned and dismissed at will (unless they are disloyal, at which point they will refuse to leave their post). The post of governor is mutually exclusive with most other positions, though there are some cases where there is overlap between the responsibilities of governors and other position holders. Rulers are considered the governor of the capital region for all intents and purposes, including pop output bonuses from [![Image 304: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse, unrest from [![Image 305: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption, and most importantly [![Image 306: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanding the [![Image 307: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy of the [![Image 308: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital region. The main exception is that rulers do not collect their wage from the capital region (as it is collected directly from the treasury), and so territories in the capital region do not get the **-25%** income malus that all other regions do. Note that this automatic governorship is not considered an assignable office that counts towards a family's number of held positions.

Each governor gets the following character modifiers:

While commanding [![Image 309: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, a governor gets an additional modifier that increases the wage they are paid from the treasury:

*   [![Image 310: Monthly wage on character modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_on_character_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")**+100%** Monthly Wage

## Military positions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Military positions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Military positions")]

### Army commanders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Army commanders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Army commanders")]

_See also: [Commander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander")_

While [![Image 311: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies are [![Image 312: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanded by [governors](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor"), permanent standing armies are instead led by characters holding dedicated military positions, without any need to pay attention to administrative matters as well. All characters holding these types of positions are affected by the [![Image 313: Admiral loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/General "General loyalty")**loyalty of generals** modifier if they are currently in [![Image 314: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command of an army (which is not necessarily always the case).

#### Legates[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Legates") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Legates")]

[![Image 315](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/a/ae/Legate_tribune_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legate_tribune_interface.png)

Legates and tribunes in the military interface

Each of a country's [![Image 316: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions can have a **legate** appointed to head the legion and and take [![Image 317: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command of its main army in the Legion tab of the [![Image 318: Menu military.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Menu_military.png/28px-Menu_military.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_military.png) military interface. Unlike governors, a legate can only command one army at a time (with any split off armies commanded by tribunes), but can be detached from his or her command and swapped between armies (within the legion) freely as long as the army is not in combat (though this will incur a temporary [![Image 319: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-5** loyalty malus) - this includes leaving the legate without any command at all, with all of the legion's armies led by tribunes (or even unled). However, a legate cannot be dismissed from his or her position for the first **12** months after their appointment, and so cannot be easily swapped between legions.

As the commanders of one of the country's stronger and more important armies, [![Image 320: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial is by far the most important attribute to consider for legates, with a commander's martial skill giving bonuses to [![Image 321: Assault ability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Assault_ability.png/28px-Assault_ability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assault "Assault") assault ability, [![Image 322: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") enslavement efficiency, and dice rolls in combat, allowing well-lead armies to punch significantly above their weight in [combat](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare"). In addition to the default power base from holding a position legates will also usually get boosts to [![Image 323: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base from commanding cohorts, and as the commanders of semi-permanent [![Image 324: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions in particular are one of the main characters that would be expected to gain [![Image 325: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal cohorts and veterans, which usually makes legates among the more powerful characters in a realm.

In addition to the power base from commanded or loyal cohorts, legates that are in command are also affected by the [![Image 326: Admiral loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/General "General loyalty")**loyalty of generals** modifier if they are deployed, given by a few disparate bonuses. As with all other positions, a legate will refuse to leave their post if they become disloyal; as with governors, they are particularly prone to this due to the large [![Image 327: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base they can get from the cohorts they command, and their [![Image 328: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty should be watched with care as a disloyal army commander ignoring all orders from above can be highly problematic. As a commander, however, a legate can be removed from their post at any time, even if disloyal, using the **Hold Triumph** interaction if they have won a battle in the last 2 years at the cost of [![Image 329: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political power.

Each legate has the following modifiers:

While legates do not have an especially high wage, it is possible for it to add up to a potentially problematic expense if a country has a large number of legions.

#### Tribunes[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tribunes") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tribunes")]

Every [![Image 330: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion can have up to **3****tribunes** to support their legates and take [![Image 331: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command of armies split off from the main legion army. Like legates, tribunes can be freely detached from their commands and swapped between different armies within their legion (with a temporary [![Image 332: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-5** loyalty malus). Note that tribunes can be appointed regardless of how many armies the legion happens to have at that point in time, even if there are currently no uncommanded armies that the tribune could take command of; it is permitted and common for a tribune (or even a legate) to not be in command of an army. Within a legion, tribunes are ordered by their date of appointment; if the legion's legate dies, is dismissed, or otherwise leaves his or her position for any reason, the highest ranking tribune in the legion will be automatically promoted to legate.

As with legates, [![Image 333: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial skill is by far the most important attribute for tribunes, as commanding an army is their main and essentially only role, and tribunes are essentially identical to legates in every way (except for their modifiers).

Each tribune, if deployed (i.e. in [![Image 334: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") command of an army), gets the following modifiers:

If not deployed, a tribune instead gets the following set of modifiers:

Note that a tribune that is not currently deployed still counts as holding a position, particularly for the purposes of the base [![Image 335: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+2** power base and calculating the number of positions that a family holds, despite the fact that they do not get a [![Image 336: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") wage and have no responsibilities. This means that tribune slots are especially useful for appointing unskilled and even disloyal characters as a meaningless sinecure position to inflate the number of positions a family holds, as long as they are not actually needed for legions that might need to be split up into multiple armies.

#### Generals[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Generals") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Generals")]

[![Image 337](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/32/General_interface.png/200px-General_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:General_interface.png)

A general in the army interface

[![Image 338: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") Mercenaries, [![Image 339: Start migration cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_migration "Tribal migration") migratory armies, and other event armies that are neither [![Image 340: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies nor [![Image 341: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions are [![Image 342: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanded by a **general**. Unlike legates and tribunes, a general cannot be replaced or dismissed for the first **12** months after their appointment, as the position is tied directly to the command of the army (with the except of mercenary generals, who cannot be replaced at all). Otherwise, generals are much like the other army positions.

Every general has the following modifiers:

### Admirals[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Admirals") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Admirals")]

[![Image 343](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/d/d1/Admiral_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Admiral_interface.png)

A admiral in the navy interface

**Admirals** are the naval counterparts to army commanders, as the commanders of a nation's [fleets](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy"), and most mechanics apply analogously (particularly to appointed commanders such as legates and generals). Any adult character (following the country's gender restrictions) that does not already hold another appointed position can be directly appointed to command a single fleet as an admiral (note that this includes the co-ruler and primary heir, but not the [![Image 344: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler as the ruler is considered the governor of the capital region and commands its levies). Like generals, there is a **12** month cooldown before an admiral can be replaced or dismissed, as well as another **12** month cooldown before a dismissed or replaced admiral can be assigned another command. As with armies, a fleet in the middle of battle also cannot have its admiral changed.

An admiral's [![Image 345: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial skill gives [![Image 346: Ship capture chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2d/Ship_capture_chance.png/28px-Ship_capture_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Naval_warfare#Combat "Naval warfare")**+0.5%** ship capture chance per point and gives a bonus to dice rolls in combat, potentially significantly increasing their fleet's effectiveness during engagements. An navy without a admiral will instead get a [![Image 347: Naval morale.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Naval_morale.png/28px-Naval_morale.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy_morale "Navy morale")**-15%** naval morale and [![Image 348: Experience decay](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Experience_decay.png/28px-Experience_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience decay")**+1%** experience decay modifier, which similarly makes an unled navy highly vulnerable.

As naval commanders admirals also gain [![Image 349: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base proportional to how much of the country's navy they have under their command (scaling to a maximum of **+10** power base), though the power base of commanded fleets is much less than that of armies. There is no loyalty mechanic for ships or fleets analogous to the [![Image 350: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") cohort loyalty mechanic. Like with army commanders, there is a [![Image 351: Admiral loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Admiral "Admiral loyalty")**loyalty of admirals** modifier that gives [![Image 352: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty modifiers specifically to admirals.

Each admiral has the following modifiers:

Like with legates, tribunes, and generals, admirals are also paid a significant amount of the nation's income. This can be problematic if a country appoints too many admirals, though this is usually somewhat less of a problem than with army commanders as a navy's high mobility means that it is rare that more than 2 or 3 are needed. Admirals will also gain [![Image 353: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity from winning battles, though as there are generally fewer naval battles in a war and no cities for admirals to sack, they generally will not reach the same level of popularity as successful army commanders. Unlike governors and legion commanders, however, admirals do not have reduced [![Image 354: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence.

## Researchers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Researchers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Researchers")]

[![Image 355](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Researcher_interface.png/250px-Researcher_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Researcher_interface.png)

Researchers in the technology interface

**Researchers** oversee the progress of [technological](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") discovery and advances inside the country. One researcher can be appointed for each of the **four** fields of advances, providing a [![Image 356: Military tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tech_investment.png/28px-Military_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")[![Image 357: Civic tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Civic_tech_investment.png/28px-Civic_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")[![Image 358: Oratory tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Oratory_tech_investment.png/28px-Oratory_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")[![Image 359: Religious tech investment.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e3/Religious_tech_investment.png/28px-Religious_tech_investment.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Progress "Technology")**+10%** tech investment bonus to that field for every point of the researcher's corresponding [attribute](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes"). In addition, researchers that have the [![Image 360: Obsessive](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2d/Obsessive.png/28px-Obsessive.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Obsessive") obsessive, [![Image 361: Intelligent](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Intelligent.png/28px-Intelligent.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Intelligent") intelligent, [![Image 362: Polymath](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Polymath.png/28px-Polymath.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Polymath") polymath, or [![Image 363: Scholar](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c5/Scholar.png/28px-Scholar.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Scholar") scholar traits also have a small chance each month to generate an extra [![Image 364: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovation, on top of the innovations that the country normally gains through advances.

As the main source of tech investment modifiers, a good researcher can considerably speed up the rate of technological advances in the country (and vice versa), and lack of penalties from having an unloyal researcher means that it is generally preferable to appoint the characters with the highest attribute in each field as researcher. However, the traits that let a researcher discover [![Image 365: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") extra innovations should also be taken into consideration, and in many circumstances it may be better to appoint a character that has one (or more) of those traits over another character with somewhat higher skill but no ability to generate extra innovations. Since the extra innovations are generated at a relatively constant rate regardless of how quickly or slowly the country is progressing technologically otherwise, focusing on getting researchers with these traits is particularly useful for countries that do not expect to progress quickly through advances, either because of a [![Image 366: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") low research efficiency or high ahead of time penalties from already being highly advanced. Note, as well, that each researcher can only be appointed to a single field at a time (and cannot currently hold any other assignable position).

Each researcher gets the following modifiers:

Notably, researchers gain [![Image 367: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship from their position but their bonuses are not affected by their statesmanship. This makes it possible to appoint high attribute but low statesmanship characters into the researcher office in order to accumulate statesmanship for another more important office later, though depending on how this is used this may come at the cost of optimizing technological progression.

## Construction Leaders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Construction Leaders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Construction Leaders")]

[![Image 368](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Construction_leader_interface.png/500px-Construction_leader_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Construction_leader_interface.png)

A construction leader in the great wonder interface

**Construction leaders** oversee the progress of [![Image 369: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") great wonders that are under construction. One construction leader can be appointed for each great wonder that the country currently has under construction, providing a [![Image 370: Total workpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ce/Total_workpower.png/28px-Total_workpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Workpower "Workpower")**+5%** overall workpower bonus for every point of the construction leader's [![Image 371: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse, increasing the speed at which the pops in the territory build the wonder. Certain traits can further modify the amount of workpower that every pop contributes to wonder construction through the general and pop-specific [![Image 372: Total workpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ce/Total_workpower.png/28px-Total_workpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Workpower "Workpower") Great Wonder Construction modifiers, as well as increasing or decreasing the wonder's prestige once finished through the [![Image 373: Wonder prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Prestige_workpower.png/28px-Prestige_workpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Great_wonder#Construction_leader "Wonder prestige") Great Wonder completion Prestige modifier. A great wonder that does not have any construction leader assigned will instead get a **25%** malus to construction work speed. Once the wonder is finished, the associated construction leader office will be removed.

Each construction leader gets the following modifiers:

Like researchers, construction leaders gain [![Image 374: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship from their position but their bonuses are not affected by their statesmanship, which makes it possible to use the position to train up inexperienced but highly-skilled characters for promotion to government offices (possibly at the expense of making the great work construction slower).

## Party Leaders[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Party Leaders") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Party Leaders")]

[![Image 375](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ce/Republic_faction_interface.png/500px-Republic_faction_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Republic_faction_interface.png)

Party leaders in the government interface

In [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), each of the 3 [Senate](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate") factions has a **party leader** who heads the faction and wields a large amount of influence among its members. Party leaders get a [![Image 376: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession")**+100%** succession support bonus from their faction, ensuring that they always their faction's full support when calculating succession support. This means that party leaders of larger factions are usually prime candidates to be elected as ruler or co-ruler, as long as they are eligible. Family members of the party leader also get a small [![Image 377: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession")**+10%** succession support bonus from the party, even if they are not actually part of the faction themselves.

When a party leader dies or leaves the country, he or she will be immediately replaced, typically with the most next most [![Image 378: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominent member of the faction. Members of other parties may also join the faction and become the leader if a party has too few remaining candidates for party leader. The faction conviction of a party leader does not change and a party leader will never switch factions, even if they do somehow get higher conviction in a faction other than the one they currently lead.

Each party leader has the following modifiers:

As it is not an appointed position, a party leader title by itself does not count towards the number of held positions that a major family expects to have, but party leaders may be elected or appointed to any other position as long as they meet the requirements. As high [![Image 379: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominence characters are more likely to be chosen as party leaders, they will tend to already hold some sort of position in the government.

## Clan Chiefs[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Clan Chiefs") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Position&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Clan Chiefs")]

[![Image 380](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Clan_chief_interface.png/500px-Clan_chief_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Clan_chief_interface.png)

Clan chiefs in the government interface

[![Image 381: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief")[Tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations have a number of **clan chiefs** that lead their clan/family and represent its interests in the country, taking the place of [family heads](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") in other government types. The ruler of a tribal nation is always one of the clan chiefs; when the ruler dies, the next ruler will be elected from the clan chief with the next highest [![Image 382: Election term.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Election_term.png) succession support, as determined by their attributes. When a clan chief dies, the title will be assigned to the next head of the family on the next monthly tick, typically the next oldest member of the family. The number of clan chiefs (and clans) in a tribal country is determined by the value of its [![Image 383: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief")**Number of Clan Chiefs** modifier, increasing with the country's [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank").

As the representatives of the main clans of the tribe, clan chiefs represent significant interests within the tribe and get a say some of the internal affairs of the country - in particular, all clan chiefs must be loyal in order to change [![Image 384: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws and enact certain decisions, particularly those relating to changing government form. In addition, when [![Image 385: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies are raised from one of a tribe's regions, they are not put under the command of the local governor, but rather partitioned between each of the clans (including the ruling clan) as [![Image 386: Clan retinue.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0e/Clan_retinue.png/28px-Clan_retinue.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_retinue "Clan retinue") clan retinues with the clan chief as [![Image 387: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander. These clan retinues are always personally [![Image 388: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal to the clan chief (except for those under the [![Image 389: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler) and contribute to their clan chief's [![Image 390: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base correspondingly, which usually significantly increases their power base. However, as they may be called up to command their retinues at any time, unlike heads of family clan chiefs cannot be appointed to any other positions.

Additionally, unlike family heads, clan chiefs do not get [![Image 391: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base from their clan's share of the total [![Image 392: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige within the country, but instead get [![Image 393: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence")**+1** prominence for each percentage of the country's total [![Image 394: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige") family prestige that belongs to their clan. This means that clan chiefs generally have less [![Image 395: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base than heads of family in republics and monarchies, save for when their retinues are raised. In addition to the usual loyalty modifiers, the [![Image 396: Clan chief loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Monthly_clan_chief_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_clan_chief_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief loyalty")**loyalty of clan chiefs** modifier will apply loyalty modifiers specifically to clan chiefs only, which is affected by some laws, event modifiers, and [![Image 397: Ruler popularity gain.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Ruler_popularity_gain.png/28px-Ruler_popularity_gain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Ruler_popularity_gain.png) ruler popularity.

Like all other non-appointed positions, the clan chief position does not count towards the number of positions a family holds, even when the [![Image 398: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies are raised and the chiefs are commanding their retinues.

All clan chiefs have the following modifiers:

# Culture

From Imperator Wiki

[Jump to navigation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#mw-head)[Jump to search](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#searchInput)

This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

Load video

YouTube

YouTube might collect personal data. [Privacy Policy](https://www.youtube.com/howyoutubeworks/user-settings/privacy/)

Continue Dismiss

Imperator: Rome - Culture and Religion Remade.

[![Image 1: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture")**Culture** is a representation of the ethnicity and customs of a [state](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State "State"), [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters "Characters"), or [pop](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population"), with cultural tensions and differences being one of the main components determining the [![Image 2: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of pops as well as affecting [![Image 3: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty. The civic rights and privileges that are granted to a culture determine how closely they are integrated into the country and its military and administration, and how much discrimination there is against pops and characters of that culture affect their propensity to become unhappy, produce [![Image 4: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest, and [revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion"). Most cultural interactions and information can be accessed through the [![Image 5: Menu culture.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Menu_culture.png/28px-Menu_culture.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_culture.png) culture screen.

- [x]
