## Primary culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Primary culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Primary culture")]

Every country begins the game with a predefined **primary culture**, which can be seen at the top right corner of the [![Image 6: Menu diplomacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Menu_diplomacy.png/28px-Menu_diplomacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_diplomacy.png) Diplomacy screen. Outside of a few specific events, decisions, and mission tasks, such as the startup events for [![Image 7: Flag of Heraclea Pontica](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a4/Heraclea_Pontica.png/20px-Heraclea_Pontica.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heraclea_Pontica "Heraclea Pontica")[Heraclea Pontica](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heraclea_Pontica "Heraclea Pontica"), the [![Image 8: Flag of Parthia (Formable)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Placeholder_Flag.png/20px-Placeholder_Flag.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Parthia_(Formable) "Parthia (Formable)")[Parthia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Parthia_(Formable) "Parthia (Formable)") formation decision, the final tasks of the [**Hellenistic Empire**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diadochi_missions "Diadochi missions") mission tree, and events during the Yuezhi migrations, the primary culture generally cannot be changed except for during a [barbarian takeover](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians").

Pops of the primary culture are typically the happiest and most satisfied pops in the realm, and are fixed to the [![Image 9: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble [civic right](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Civic_rights). Most often the [culture of the state population](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Population_culture_and_population_religion "Population") is more diverse, but pop culture can be gradually changed to match that of the state during the playthrough via [![Image 10: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilation, as pops of a different culture and especially culture group have a significant happiness malus that can become very difficult to manage if [![Image 11: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability begins to drop. [![Image 12: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") Assimilation is also slowed if the dominant province culture and/or religion is not the same as that of the state, all of which makes the conquest of wrong culture (especially wrong culture group) and wrong religion areas significantly more difficult, especially in the early game. Outside of events pops will always assimilate and convert to the primary culture. As an alternative to assimilation, cultures can be [![Image 13: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated to be given the same rights as the primary culture, at the expense of the happiness of already integrated cultures.

The primary culture also has an effect on diplomacy. States of a different culture (in the same culture group) get a [![Image 14: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**-10** opinion malus, while those in a different culture group get a [![Image 15: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**-20** opinion malus. The [![Image 16: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject types available also depend on primary culture; another state can only be made a [feudatory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations#Feudatory "Subject nations") if it is in the same culture group as its new overlord, and [satrapies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations#Satrapy "Subject nations") can only be made of states with [Persian military traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Persian_traditions "Persian traditions"), which consist generally of countries in the Iranian, Caucasian, Anatolian, Scythian, Bactrian, and Aramaic culture groups.

A country's starting [![Image 17: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[military tradition trees](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_Traditions "Military Traditions") is also determined based on its primary culture/culture group, which affects what military and economic bonuses can be accrued by spending [![Image 18: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience, at least initially. However, it is possible to unlock other tradition trees by integrating their cultures and adopting the appropriate traditions.

## Integrated cultures[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Integrated cultures") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Integrated cultures")]

Cultures with [![Image 19: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen or [![Image 20: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble civic rights are considered an [![Image 21: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture")**integrated culture**, forming an integral part of the court, administration, and military with the highest level of cultural privileges. Integrated cultures do not [![Image 22: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilate, count towards the number of pops for [![Image 23: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research speed, are allowed to [colonize](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization"), and generally have the highest [![Image 24: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of all cultures. Perhaps most importantly, integrated culture pops form the backbone of a country's military, with the size of its army - the number of [![Image 25: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies that can be raised and the number of cohorts that can be recruited to [![Image 26: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions - based directly on how many non-slave integrated pops there are in the country, which makes integrating a large culture one of the most effective ways to grow a country's military capabilities. The integration of a sufficiently large culture can also allow the country to unlock and adopting its associated [![Image 27: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military tradition trees. Certain cultures, when integrated, also allow for unique [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invention "Invention").

For most nations, the only integrated culture at the start of the game is the primary culture, but existing cultures in the country can be integrated by changing its civic rights. There is no limit on the number of possible integrated cultures, but each integrated culture beyond the primary culture gives [![Image 28: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-4%** Integrated Culture Happiness, which means that having more integrated cultures will result in each culture having a smaller bonus. Therefore, it is generally preferable to prioritize integrating cultures that form a large part of the country in order to maximize the usefulness of each integrated culture, particularly those in more developed and urbanized areas (in order to compensate towards counting against [![Image 29: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research speed).

### Cultural integration[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cultural integration") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cultural integration")]

A culture can be [![Image 30: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture")**integrated** by changing its civic right to a integrated class ([![Image 31: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen or [![Image 32: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble). Unlike all other civic right changes, the integration process is not instantaneous and takes a certain amount of time based on how large the culture is relative to the rest of the country. The rate of progress increases with the size of the culture relative to the rest of the country and decreases with the absolute size of the pop, and is further modified by the [![Image 33: Global cultural integration speed modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png/28px-Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_integration "Cultural integration")**Cultural Integration Speed** modifier. Notably, cultures of other culture groups have [![Image 34: Global cultural integration speed modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png/28px-Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_integration "Cultural integration")**-50%** Cultural Integration Speed. Integration progress has an minimum of **0.05%** daily progress and a maximum of **0.4%**, meaning the integration will take between **250** and **2000** days (about 8.3 months to 5.5 years), though integration progress can also be gained or lost by events.

While integration is ongoing, there are events can trigger where the culture being integrated will demand more rights and power, pops and characters might try to assimilate to the new culture, or the current establishment becomes discontent at the attempts to integrate this new group. Siding with the integrated culture will usually increase [![Image 35: Global cultural integration speed modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png/28px-Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_integration "Cultural integration") integration progress and their [![Image 36: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") cultural happiness while angering currently integrated cultures, and vice versa.

One of the following will happen at **25%** progress:

*   With a weight of **50**, nothing
*   With a weight of **30**, get the event [**Acceptance Through Marriage**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**A Governor of Their Own**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**Governor Adopts Local Mannerisms**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**[target_culture.GetCulture.GetName] Culture Embraced**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**[target_culture.GetCulture.GetName] Recruitment Drive**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**Societal Unrest**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**Prominent [target_culture.GetCulture.GetName] Family Demands Recognition**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")

One of the following will happen at **75%** progress:

*   With a weight of **50**, nothing
*   With a weight of **30**, get the event [**Acceptance Through Marriage**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**A Governor of Their Own**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**Governor Adopts Local Mannerisms**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**[target_culture.GetCulture.GetName] Culture Embraced**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**[target_culture.GetCulture.GetName] Recruitment Drive**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**Societal Unrest**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **10**, get the event [**Prominent [target_culture.GetCulture.GetName] Family Demands Recognition**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **50**, get the event [**A New Center of Power**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")
*   With a weight of **50**, get the event [**Army Mutinies**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Cultural_integration_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cultural integration events (page does not exist)")

Multiple cultures can be integrating at the same time, but each integrating culture gives a [![Image 37: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**-0.20** Monthly Stability Change modifier, which can be highly destabilizing if too many cultures are integrating at the same time. Only once the integration progress is finished will the civic right be increased and the [![Image 38: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness penalty apply. Once started, integration cannot be cancelled and the culture's civic right cannot be changed while the process is ongoing.

Note that while pops of integrated cultures will not assimilate, any assimilation that started _before_ the pop's culture finished integrating will not stop once the culture has been integrated. As assimilation of a even a single pop can sometimes take many years, this means that it is possible to see an integrated culture pop assimilating for a long time even after integration.

#### Cultural inventions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Cultural inventions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Cultural inventions")]

_See also: [Invention](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invention "Invention")_

Many culture groups give access to [![Image 39: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") special inventions if a country has a primary or integrated culture in that culture group. If possible, it is often advantageous to integrate at least one culture from each of these groups in order to get access to these inventions, which can be quite strong; in particular, among these inventions are those which more than make up for the decrease in integrated culture happiness, such as:

*   Indian cultures (the Aryan, Dravidian and Pracyan culture groups) have _Brahmi Script_ ([![Image 40: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+2.5%** Integrated Culture Happiness) and _Cashmere Wool_ ([![Image 41: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+5%** Integrated Culture Happiness); both are under [Oratory inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions").
*   Hellenstic cultures have _Stoicism_ and _Epicureanism_; both give [![Image 42: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**+5%** Integrated Culture Happiness each and are under [Civic inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions").

### Cultural unintegration[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Cultural unintegration") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Cultural unintegration")]

Any integrated culture except the primary culture can be unintegrated by changing its civic right to a non-integrated class ([![Image 43: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman, [![Image 44: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesman, or [![Image 45: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave). Unintegrating a culture has a greater penalty than reducing civic rights normally and instead gives the **Citizenship Lost** modifier to the culture for **10** years, giving:

*   [![Image 46: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-20%** Cultural Happiness
*   [![Image 47: Citizen output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Citizen output")**-10%** Culture Citizen Output
*   [![Image 48: Noble output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Pop_noble_output.png/28px-Pop_noble_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Noble output")**-10%** Culture Noble Output

Unintegrating a culture is generally not recommended for larger cultures, but can be useful for cultures that have become sufficiently small relative to the rest of the empire that the happiness malus is better used on integrating another, larger culture. Note that the cumulative effects of losing the [![Image 49: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness modifiers and losing citizenship means that there will be a significant swing in happiness (and therefore loyalty) that should be taken to account, especially if the culture's provinces are not well developed.

## Territory culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Territory culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Territory culture")]

Every territory has a **dominant culture**, which is the culture with the highest number of pops in the territory; in the case of ties, [![Image 50: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated cultures are always chosen to be dominant, if one of the choices. This is the culture as displayed for the territory in the culture map mode and is generally considered the culture of the territory for most purposes, particularly for [colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization"), moving the [![Image 51: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital, and many [event](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [mission task](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") checks.

Most importantly, there are also territory modifiers applied if the dominant culture is not an [![Image 52: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture, which decreases the output of more foreign, outlying provinces and makes them harder to [![Image 53: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") convert and [![Image 54: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilate. Such a territory where the **Dominant Culture is not Integrated Culture** gets the following modifiers:

*   [![Image 55: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**-50%** Local Research Points
*   [![Image 56: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus")**+2** Slaves needed for Local Surplus
*   [![Image 57: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output")**-30%** Population Output
*   [![Image 58: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion")**-10%** Pop Conversion Speed
*   [![Image 59: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation")**-25%** Pop Assimilation Speed
*   [![Image 60: Local base trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_base_trade_routes.png/28px-Local_base_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route")**-25%** Local Base Trade Routes

## Character culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Character culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Character culture")]

Characters' culture determines their names and initial physical appearance (the latter can change through intermarriage to be different from culture in subsequent generations). Character culture is generally fixed, though there are a few events by which a character's culture can be changed - governors may switch to the culture of the regional capital if the [Cultural Assimilation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Governor_policies "Province") policy is not being used, office holders may take on the culture of the state that employs them, and the heir of a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") may switch to the culture of the [Royal Tutor](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Offices_2 "Government"). Newborn children will often take the nation's primary culture, regardless of the culture of their parents.

Characters have a [![Image 61: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty modifier based on the [![Image 62: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness modifier of their culture, based on the formula:

$\text{Character Loyalty Modifier} = 0.3 \text{Cultural Happiness} 9$![Image 63: {\displaystyle {\text{Character Loyalty Modifier}}=0.3\cdot {\text{Cultural Happiness}}-9}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/f552f1b4d360eb7d2669834228f63e1724656b1a)
The break-even point is at **30%**, which means that for instance, characters of a different, unintegrated culture group will have a base [![Image 64: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-5.4** loyalty modifier, while without any modifiers a primary culture character will have no loyalty modifier from cultural happiness. Positive cultural happiness modifiers are therefore still useful even if the population is already content, as it is generally rare that all characters are sufficiently loyal that further loyalty is not relevant. Note that this loyalty modifier is only applied for characters of a culture that exists in the country (i.e. is either the primary culture or has at least one pop in the country - can be checked by looking at the list of cultures in the [![Image 65: Menu culture.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Menu_culture.png/28px-Menu_culture.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_culture.png) culture screen) - characters of cultures not in the country do not get any loyalty modifier, even if they would if the country had at least one pop of their culture.

Character culture is also somewhat relevant for [![Image 66: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governor positions, as governors not of a primary or integrated culture will never use the [Cultural Assimilation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Governor_policies "Province") policy by default, and they will generally be hesitant to use it on provinces of the same culture.

## Civic rights[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Civic rights") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Civic rights")]

Every culture in a country has a specific level of **civic rights**, which defines the highest class that pops of that culture can be [![Image 67: Local pop promotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Local_pop_promotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_promotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Promotion "Promotion") promoted to. A pop that has a higher class than it civic right allows will not automatically change class; however, if it is ever demoted, it will not be able to promote back up beyond its allowed civic rights limit again. Different levels of civic rights also have different [cultural decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_decisions "Cultural decisions") available, with integrated levels and non-integrated levels having their own distinct sets of decisions and possible privileges.

Changing pop rights costs [![Image 68: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**5** political influence and is usually instantaneous, except for changing from a non-integrated level ([![Image 69: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman, [![Image 70: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesman or [![Image 71: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slave) to an integrated level ([![Image 72: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble or [![Image 73: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen), which will start the [![Image 74: Global cultural integration speed modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png/28px-Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_integration "Cultural integration") cultural integration process and take a number of years. Increasing a pop's civic rights gives the **Rights Increased** modifier for **10** years giving [![Image 75: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**+5%** Culture Happiness, while decreasing it typically gives the **Disenfranchisement** modifier for **10** years, giving [![Image 76: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-10%** Culture Happiness. The **Disenfranchisement** modifier is not given when converting from [![Image 77: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble rights to [![Image 78: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen rights (which has no penalties at all), while converting from an integrated right to a non-integrated right gives the larger **Citizenship Lost** modifier. Tribesmen and slaves are considered on the same tier and so converting between the two do not give the **Rights Increased** or **Disenfranchisement** modifiers. All cultural modifiers that no longer apply with the new culture's civic right are also removed when changing civic right levels.

### Noble[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Noble") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Noble")]

[![Image 79: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") Noble civic rights are the highest civic rights that can be granted to a culture, with the primary culture fixed to this level of rights at all times. Every other culture that has noble rights will give [![Image 80: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-5%** Primary Culture Happiness; importantly, this is applied only to the primary culture, not other integrated cultures.

As a civic right that grants integrated culture status, every culture with noble civic rights beyond the primary culture gives [![Image 81: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-4%** Integrated Culture Happiness.

### Citizen[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Citizen") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Citizen")]

[![Image 82: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") Citizen civic rights also give integration status and are the standard civic right for integrated cultures at the start of the game.

As a civic right that grants integrated culture status, every culture with citizen civic rights beyond the primary culture gives [![Image 83: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**-4%** Integrated Culture Happiness.

### Freeman[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Freeman") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Freeman")]

[![Image 84: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") Freeman civic rights are the default rights given to most non-primary cultures at the beginning of the game, as well as any newly acquired cultures, with no special modifiers or mechanics.

### Tribesman[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Tribesman") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Tribesman")]

[![Image 85: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") Tribesman civic rights are considered lower than freemen and on par with slaves, but do not have any special modifiers or mechanics.

### Slave[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Slave") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Slave")]

[![Image 86: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") Slave civic rights are the lowest tier of civic rights, considered on par with tribesmen and typically with the lowest happiness. Demoting a culture to slave status gives **Slavery Imposed** modifier, giving:

*   [![Image 87: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-10%** Culture Happiness
*   [![Image 88: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output")**-10%** Culture Slave Output

## Cultural decisions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Cultural decisions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Cultural decisions")]

_Main article: [Cultural decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural\_decisions "Cultural decisions")_

**Cultural decisions** can be enacted for each culture in a country under the culture screen. These decisions usually allow a country to grant various privileges to a culture, increasing their [![Image 89: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") cultural happiness and sometimes [![Image 90: Global cultural integration speed modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png/28px-Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_integration "Cultural integration") cultural integration speed at the expense of [![Image 91: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability and usually either [![Image 92: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") cultural output or a temporary malus to [![Image 93: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness or [![Image 94: Citizen happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") national citizen happiness. There are different sets of decisions for the primary culture, other integrated cultures, and non-integrated cultures and generally most of these privileges are permanent and generally cannot be revoked unless the culture is no longer eligible for the privilege at all (particularly when changing integration status).

## Cultural happiness modifiers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Cultural happiness modifiers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Cultural happiness modifiers")]

Along with pop type and religion, the culture of a pop is one of the main factors determining its [![Image 95: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")[happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Happiness_of_pops "Population") and which happiness modifiers it is affected by. Culture-based happiness modifiers are applied to a pop based on its relationship to the the nation's primary culture - whether the pop is of the primary culture, in the same group, or in a different culture group altogether. The sum of all happiness modifiers modifiers for a particular culture can be viewed in that culture's entry in the [![Image 96: Menu culture.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Menu_culture.png/28px-Menu_culture.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_culture.png) culture screen, which is the number used to determine the impact of cultural happiness on [![Image 97: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty.

### Culture happiness[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Culture happiness") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Culture happiness")]

[![Image 98: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**Culture Happiness** modifiers are applied to individual cultures and affect only the pops and characters of that specific culture. Cultural happiness modifiers are usually given by cultural rights and interactions, as well as their affiliated events and decisions.

### Integrated culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Integrated culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Integrated culture")]

[![Image 99: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness")**Integrated Culture Happiness** modifiers are applied to pops of all fully integrated cultures, including the primary culture. Pops of integrated cultures usually have the highest happiness and generally are unlikely to be problematic unless [![Image 100: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability becomes too low or [![Image 101: Monthly war exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "Monthly war exhaustion") war exhaustion becomes too high.

### Unintegrated culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Unintegrated culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Unintegrated culture")]

[![Image 168: Unintegrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6c/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture "Unintegrated culture happiness")**Unintegrated Culture Happiness** modifiers are applied to pops that are in the same culture group as the primary culture, but not of the primary culture itself and are not integrated. Note that despite the name, unintegrated culture happiness modifiers are **not** applied to pops who are in a different culture group from the primary culture. While usually not as happy as primary culture pops, pops within the same culture group are also generally easy to deal with and are rarely the source of serious problems unless [![Image 169: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability becomes too low.

### Unintegrated culture group[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Unintegrated culture group") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Unintegrated culture group")]

[![Image 190: Unintegrated culture group happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_wrong_culture_group_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Unintegrated_culture_group "Unintegrated culture group happiness")**Unintegrated Culture Group Happiness** modifiers are applied to pops of an unintegrated culture that are in a different culture group from the primary culture. Pops in the wrong culture group have a significantly lower base happiness, making them particularly susceptible to increasing [![Image 191: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest, decreasing [![Image 192: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") provincial loyalty, and threatening [![Image 193: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion") rebellions - especially in provinces with a high proportion of [![Image 194: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 195: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, with their high political weight and low base happiness. For this reason, maximizing unintegrated culture group happiness is particularly important for large, expansionist empires for whom most of their pops will inevitably fall into this category.

## Assimilation[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Assimilation") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Assimilation")]

A pop's culture can change to the nation's primary culture via [![Image 233: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation")**assimilation**. For every [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories"), a single randomly chosen pop will always be assimilating as long as that territory holds pops that are not of an integrated culture and are not otherwise busy (e.g. converting or promoting). Assimilation progress builds each month, with the exact progress determined by the sum of all [![Image 234: Assimilation speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Assimilation_speed.png/28px-Assimilation_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Assimilation_speed.png)**Pop Assimilation Speed** modifiers. Once progress reaches 100%, the pop will be assimilated to the primary culture and another pop will begin to assimilate, if there are any other pops that can be assimilated. [![Image 235: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") Integrated cultures will never assimilate.

Given that pops of unintegrated pops cannot be raised in a country's army and usually have significant happiness penalties, it is generally beneficial to assimilate any non-integrated culture pops in the country. Using the [![Image 236: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Policy_cultural_assimilation.png) Cultural Assimilation [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy"), [founding colonies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_decisions#found_colonia_cultural_decision "Cultural decisions"), building [![Image 237: Theater](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4d/Building_theater.png/28px-Building_theater.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Theater "Theater") grand theaters, and passing the laws with assimilation bonuses for [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribe "Tribe") and [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") will speed up the assimilation, but in general provinces - especially more populated settlements, as [![Image 238: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities generally have disproportionately faster assimilation - may take decades before they are significantly assimilated. However, as integrated pops count against [![Image 239: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research efficiency while non-integrated pops do not, integrating a large amount of pops - particularly those that are primarily of the lower classes - can have a significant impact on research progression if additional measures in that area are not taken.

## Graphical culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Graphical culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Culture&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Graphical culture")]

The graphical culture of a culture group determines the style of unit and city models. Most graphical cultures have their own unit models, used by all the nations of the associated culture groups which don't have unique unit models. City models are shared among several graphical cultures, and their style depends on the majority culture of the city's [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories").

| Graphical culture | City style |
| --- | --- |
| german_gfx | Tribal |
| celtic_gfx |
| iberian_gfx |
| roman_gfx | Hellenistic |
| greek_gfx |
| carthaginian_gfx | Punic[[1]](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#cite_note-1) |
| numidian_gfx[[2]](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#cite_note-2) | African |
| egyptian_gfx |
| nubian_gfx |
| anatolian_gfx | Anatolian |
| arabian_gfx | Persian |
| persian_gfx |
| scythian_gfx | Steppe |
| north_indian_gfx | Indian |
| south_indian_gfx |

============================================================
# Religion
============================================================
Title: Religion - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/ntS8eA9MwuI/hqdefault.jpg)

Culture and Religion Remade

Load video

Imperator: Rome - Culture and Religion Remade (religion section starts at **2:10**).

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/2.0_religion_menu.jpg/500px-2.0_religion_menu.jpg)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:2.0_religion_menu.jpg)

The religion interface

[![Image 3: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion")**Religion** represents the belief systems of the ancient world, usually polytheistic and encompassing a wide range of gods, and important to everything, from daily life to the highest political echelons. In the game, every [state](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State "State"), [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters "Characters"), and [pop](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") belongs to a particular religion, with their interactions playing a significant role in loyalty and pop happiness. The choice of pantheon gods and omens in the state religion forms an important part of the modifiers and bonuses that a country has, and is the one of the main interactions that a player will have with religion, with the systems of holy sites and sacred treasures providing more auxiliary goals and flavour. Most religion interactions can be managed through the [![Image 4: Menu religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Menu_religion.png/28px-Menu_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_religion.png)**Religion** tab.

## State religion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: State religion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: State religion")]

Every country starts with a predefined **state religion**, which can be viewed just above the pantheon deities in the [![Image 5: Menu religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Menu_religion.png/28px-Menu_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_religion.png) Religion tab. Every religion has bonuses that are applied to the entire country, and also determines the base deities that are always available regardless of the population or holy sites of the country. State religion also has a small effect on diplomacy—countries of the same religion get a [![Image 6: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**+10** opinion bonus with each other. There is no malus for having a different state religion.

There is no default happiness bonus or penalty for pop religion, but pops will receive a [![Image 7: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**+4%** happiness bonus for each deity in the state pantheon that matches their religion, making it useful for polytheistic states to take on pantheon gods of religions that are widespread inside their country - though spreading out the deity happiness bonus between deities of different religions means a smaller happiness modifier for each individual religion. The happiness of state religion pops specifically is also be increased with the [![Image 8: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness")**state religion happiness** modifier, which is given by various omens and deities, [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"), [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), [heritages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritages "Heritages"), and more.

Additionally, the percentage of pops following the state religion determines the nation's [![Image 9: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal")**Religious Unity** value, which is the main component of [![Image 10: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omen power.

### State Conversion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: State Conversion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: State Conversion")]

_Main article: [Decisions#Religious Conversion decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions#Religious\_Conversion\_decisions "Decisions")_

The state religion can be changed by decision if there is at least **1** character of the new religion in the country, and either **50%** of the non-slave pops in the [![Image 11: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital, or at least **20%** of all the pops of the country and at least one [![Image 12: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") pantheon deity, follow the new religion the country intends to convert to. Switching religions can only be done once every 10 years and costs [![Image 13: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**200** political influence and [![Image 14: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**30** stability, also giving a temporary drop of [![Image 15: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**−15** Loyalty for all characters that are followers of the old faith.

## Character religion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Character religion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Character religion")]

Each character also has their own **character religion**, usually but not always the same as that of the state. Characters that do not follow the state religion have a [![Image 16: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**−10** maximum loyalty modifier, and pops have a [![Image 17: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-6%** happiness penalty if the governor of their region is not of their religion - notably, this includes even state religion pops, if their governor is not of the state religion.

Characters can be forced to convert if their [![Image 18: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty is at least 60, at the cost of [![Image 19: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**+5.00** Tyranny and a temporary drop of [![Image 20: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**−30** loyalty for **5** years. There are also a few events by which a character's religion can otherwise be changed — governors may switch to the religion of the regional capital if the Religious Conversion policy is not being used, office holders may take on the religion of the state that employs them, the heir of a monarchy may switch to the religion of the Royal Tutor, and rulers may get an event to switch to the state religion if they do not follow it.

## Territory religion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Territory religion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Territory religion")]

Every territory has a **dominant religion**, which is the religion with the highest number of pops in the territory. This is the religion as displayed for the territory in the religion map mode and is generally considered the religion of the territory for most purposes, particularly for [colonization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization"), moving the [![Image 21: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital, and many [event](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [mission task](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") checks.

Most importantly, there are also territory modifiers applied if the dominant religion is not the state religion, which makes them harder to [![Image 22: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") convert and [![Image 23: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilate. Such a territory where the **Dominant Religion is not State Religion** gets the following modifiers:

## State religion happiness[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: State religion happiness") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: State religion happiness")]

[![Image 24: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness")**State Religion Happiness** modifiers are applied to all pops of the state religion, regardless of what religions the pantheon deities are of. Unlike with [![Image 25: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated cultures, there is no base state religion happiness modifier, though modifiers from other sources can still add up to a significant amount that makes conquering provinces with the same religion significantly easier.

## Pop Conversion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Pop Conversion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Pop Conversion")]

[![Image 84: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") In every [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories"), a single pop can undergo **conversion** to the state religion at a time, as long as there are any pops in that territory that can convert and are not otherwise busy (e.g. assimilating or promoting). Outside of events, pops will always convert to the state religion. Conversion progress builds each month, with the exact progress determined by the sum of all [![Image 85: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion")**Pop Conversion Speed** modifiers. Once progress reaches 100%, the pop will be converted to the state religion and another pop will begin to convert, if there are any other pops that can be converted. The speed of conversion is influenced by many factors, particularly the [![Image 86: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Policy_religious_conversion.png) Religious Conversion [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") and the dominant province [![Image 87: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture")[culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") and [![Image 88: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religion being different from the state culture and religion. Note that it is possible for conversion to be completely stalled.

All else being equal, it is generally preferable to convert pops to the state religion, though the differences in happiness are much less than with unintegrated [culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") or culture group pops and can be ameliorated by adopting deities of the pop's religion in the pantheon (though this will necessarily come at the expense of the happiness of pops of other religions), which generally makes conversion less important than [assimilation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation"). Under some circumstances it may also be beneficial to retain pops of other religions in order to have continued access to their deities, though the strong effects of religious unity on [![Image 89: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omen power means that it is still generally undesirable to have too many other pops of a non-state religion.

## Pantheons[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Pantheons") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Pantheons")]

[![Image 137: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") Every nation has a **state pantheon** consisting of four deities (also called **prophets**, **yazatas**, or **paradigms**, depending on the religion), one for each category: **War**, **Culture**, **Economy**, and **Fertility**. Every nation can select from a fixed list of their religion's deities to fill the slots, with some deities available to all countries of that religion and some with stricter requirements such as culture, tag, or finishing a certain [mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mission "Mission") task. In addition, polytheistic nations can also select deities from other polytheistic religions if they own that deity's [![Image 138: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") holy site, or a high enough percentage of their pops follows a given religion. The percentage required depends on the deity's rarity: **Very Common** deities require **5%** of the nation's total pops to follow the deity's religion, **Common** deities require **10%**, **Rare** deities require **20%**, and **Very Rare** deities require **40%**.

Every deity that is not part of the state religion gives a stacking [![Image 139: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion")**−20%** conversion speed malus for all pops in the nation (regardless of whether or not their gods are currently represented in the pantheon).

Each deity has a fixed **passive modifier** which is in effect as long as that deity is part of the pantheon. Additionally, every 5 years (adjusted by the [![Image 140: Omen duration](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4b/Omen_duration.png/28px-Omen_duration.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Omen duration")**Omen Duration** modifier) one can invoke an **omen** from one of the pantheon's deities, which confers an additional, usually more powerful, bonus. The current omen's effects are multiplied by the nation's current [![Image 141: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion")**Omen Power**, with the base effects being reached at **100%** Omen Power - note that omen power is uncapped and often will rise above 100% as the game progresses. Owning the deity's [![Image 142: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") holy site increases the effect of both its passive and omen modifiers (if enabled) by **25%**.

Switching a deity in the pantheon costs a base of [![Image 143: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**−15** stability and prevents that deity's omen from being invoked for **3** years. The stability penalty is temporarily reduced by **-75%** for 10 years after converting to another religion, through the [![Image 144: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon")**Cost to Change Pantheon Deity** modifier. Deities whose omens are currently being invoked cannot be replaced.

Each deity and its requirements and bonuses are listed on the page of the religion it belongs to.

### Omen power[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Omen power") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Omen power")]
