## Holy sites[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Holy sites") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Holy sites")]

_See also: [Treasure](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treasure "Treasure")_

[![Image 187: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") A **holy site** is a temple, sanctuary, or other sacred site that is particularly important to the worship of a specific deity. Each deity can have one holy site anywhere in the world, and owning a deity's holy site while worshiping the deity in your pantheon increases the strength of both their passive and omen effects by **+25%**. There are many number of holy sites present at the start of the game, and a new one can be consecrated for a pantheon deity in an owned territory at a cost of [![Image 188: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**−300** Gold and [![Image 189: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**−50** Political Influence, adjusted by the [![Image 190: Holy site cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Enact_holy_site_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_holy_site_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site cost")**Enact Holy Site Cost** modifier.

Holy sites confer the following modifiers to the territory they are located in, regardless of its size, status, and whether it is dedicated to a deity in the state religion/ pantheon or not:

Holy sites can hold [![Image 191: Treasure chest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Treasure_chest.png/28px-Treasure_chest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Treasure_chest.png)**sacred treasures**, which confer bonuses to the entire province the holy site is located in - it is therefore typically useful to bring as many sacred treasures as possible to the capital province in order to maximize the effects of their bonuses. Any treasure can be placed in any holy site regardless of its religion, but each holy site has a limited number of **altars** where sacred treasures can be placed, depending on the rank of its territory: one for [![Image 192: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement")[settlements](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement"), two for [![Image 193: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City")[cities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cities "Cities"), and three for [![Image 194: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis")[metropolises](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis"). There is a fixed amount of sacred treasures in the world, with new ones only being created as a result of certain missions and events. Sacred treasures can be removed from an owned holy site at any time at the cost of [![Image 195: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion")**+1.00** Aggressive Expansion.

Unplaced sacred treasures are held in the nation's [![Image 196: National treasure.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cd/National_treasure.png/28px-National_treasure.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Reliquary "Reliquary")**reliquary** and confer no benefits, but cannot be taken through desecration. Upon annexation by conquest, the conqueror is guaranteed to seize at least 2 treasures from the reliquary, if there exist that many; above that, up to 2 treasures in the nation's reliquary will be spirited away to another country within diplomatic range if any exist, with all the rest taken by the conqueror. Diplomatic annexation, will transfer all reliquary treasures to the overlord.

Desecrating a holy site can be done at any time through the [![Image 197: Menu religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Menu_religion.png/28px-Menu_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_religion.png) Religion menu if it is owned, or by using the [![Image 198: Desecrate holy site](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Ability_desecrate_holysite.png/28px-Ability_desecrate_holysite.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Desecrate holy site")**Desecrate Holy Sites** army ability with an army located on a controlled holy site's territory - note that a holy site does not necessarily need to be owned for an army to desecrate it, which means that an army can desecrate a holy site belonging to another nations if it is occupied. Desecrating a holy site destroys it and deposits all of its sacred treasures in the desecrating nation's reliquary, while the territory gets a [![Image 199: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-10%** Local Population Happiness modifier to the territory for **12** months. Using the army interaction also costs [![Image 200: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion")**+2.00** Aggressive Expansion, and if the army is a [![Image 201: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") they may gain either the [![Image 202: Phalera zeus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Phalera_zeus.png/28px-Phalera_zeus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Phalera_zeus.png) Pia or [![Image 203: Phalera zeus dishonor.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/71/Phalera_zeus_dishonor.png/28px-Phalera_zeus_dishonor.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Phalera_zeus_dishonor.png) Impia [distinctions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction") depending on whether or not the holy site's deity belongs to the state religion. Every nation that follows the religion of the holy site's deity will also receive a notification event and might lose [![Image 204: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion") opinion of the desecrating country.

In the holy sites tab in the religion menu, all holy sites in owned territories as well as those for deities currently being worshiped in the pantheon, even if currently unowned, are shown and can be selected in the list. Owned holy sites - even those of deities currently in the pantheon - can be desecrated at any time, with their treasures being returned to the [![Image 205: National treasure.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cd/National_treasure.png/28px-National_treasure.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:National_treasure.png) reliquary, at the cost of [![Image 206: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-10%** Local Population Happiness in the territory for 1 year (as well as the loss of the holy site itself). Treasures in holy sites of deities not in the pantheon can be removed here.

## Apotheosis[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Apotheosis") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Apotheosis")]

[![Image 207: Deify ruler cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png/28px-Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Apotheosis "Apotheosis")**Apotheosis**, or deification, is the process of deifying a past or present ruler of the country. A deified ruler inherits the effects of an existing pantheon deity, which they must be based on, but give an additional **apotheosis effect** bonus each time their omen is invoked. The strength of the apotheosis effect is often affected by the [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes") of the deified ruler, and can include gaining [![Image 208: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, [![Image 209: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, [![Image 210: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") province food, [![Image 211: Technology speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ed/Technology_speed.png/28px-Technology_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology speed") research progress, [![Image 212: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience, [![Image 213: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") pops, [![Image 214: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilation, [![Image 215: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion") conversion, or even [![Image 216: Province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/State_free_investments.png/28px-State_free_investments.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment") province investments.

Monotheistic religions, in particular [Judaism](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Jewish "Jewish"), have pantheons consisting entirely of "deified" characters (sometimes known as prophets), and are unable to deify more. As a counterbalance to the early access to apotheosis bonuses, they can only invoke omens once every **7.5** years (as Judaism has a [![Image 217: Omen duration](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4b/Omen_duration.png/28px-Omen_duration.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Omen duration")**+50%** Omen Duration modifier). All [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") except for Theocratic Republics and Athenian Republics are also unable to deify rulers.

A number of deified characters exist in the game at the start: the Jewish prophets, [Hellenic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Hellenic "Hellenic") Alexander the Great, [Zoroastrian](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zoroastrian "Zoroastrian") Zoroaster, [Buddhist](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buddhist "Buddhist") Siddhartha, and [Jain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Jain "Jain") Mahavira. Certain characters can also be deified through special mission tasks, in particular Antigonus Monopthalmus through one of the [![Image 218: Flag of Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Antigonid_Kingdom.png/20px-Antigonid_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")'s unique mission trees. Deifying a ruler requires the [![Image 219: Magna Graecia (DLC).png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Magna_Graecia_%28DLC%29.png/28px-Magna_Graecia_%28DLC%29.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Magna_Graecia_(DLC) "Magna Graecia (DLC)")[Magna Graecia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Magna_Graecia_(DLC) "Magna Graecia (DLC)") content pack and costs a base of [![Image 220: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**−200** Political Influence, modified by the [![Image 221: Deify ruler cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png/28px-Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Apotheosis "Apotheosis")**Cost to Deify Ruler** modifier (decreased by several [![Image 222: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and increases based on the number of deified rulers currently in the pantheon, making each successive deified ruler more expensive). Deification also also requires the **current** ruler to have at least [![Image 223: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**90** Popularity, and the current ruler's family to have at least [![Image 224: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**800** Prestige.

Every defied ruler in a nation's pantheon gives the following modifiers, stackable up to the four possible deities in the pantheon:

Generally speaking, the most important modifier is generally the pop conversion speed, as the full [![Image 225: Policy religious conversion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Policy_religious_conversion.png/28px-Policy_religious_conversion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conversion "Conversion")**+60%** Pop Conversion Speed modifier if all four deities are deified rulers is very significant. Note that this modifier applies only for rulers deified during the game, and not for existing deified characters available at the beginning of the game; in particular, this means that it is generally not possible for [Jewish](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Jewish "Jewish") countries to get access to the deified ruler modifiers, even if they start with access to the other apotheosis bonuses.

As a separate god, deified rulers are not affected by the [![Image 226: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") holy sites of their parent deity and may have their own holy sites created in a territory - this does mean that if a deified ruler is created out of a deity whose holy site is currently owned, the new deity will not get the **+25%** bonus from owning the holy site until a new holy site is created for the new deity. If a deified ruler is not part of any country's pantheon for more than **20** years, their cult will be forgotten, and the deity, including any holy sites, will no longer be available.

Each deity Apotheosis effect and holy site starting location are listed on the page of the religion it belongs to (links for each religion at the end of this page).

## Divine Sacrifice[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Divine Sacrifice") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Divine Sacrifice")]

[![Image 227: Sacrifice pig.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Sacrifice_pig.png/28px-Sacrifice_pig.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Divine_sacrifice "Divine sacrifice") A **Divine Sacrifice** can be made to appease the people and reassure them that the country will continue to receive the favour and bounty of the gods and higher powers, and is the most reliable way to increase the [![Image 228: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability of the realm. A sacrifice has a base cost of [![Image 229: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**50** political influence and gives the following effects for **5** years:

It is possible to stack the effects of a sacrifice by making another one while the effects of the previous one(s) is ongoing, which has the additional effect of extending the length of the stacked effect by another 5 years. However, as the cost increase of each additional sacrifice stacks quickly if the effects of previous sacrifices are still active, trying to save political influence this way is generally of limited effectiveness unless the country already has a highly reduced sacrifice cost modifier.

The cost of making a sacrifice is modified by the [![Image 230: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods")**Divine Sacrifice Cost** modifier, which can be decreased through various [![Image 231: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")[inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"), [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), [deities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Pantheons "Religion"), and other modifiers. All static sources are listed below.

## Invoke Devotio[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Invoke Devotio") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Invoke Devotio")]

[![Image 258: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") If a war is going badly and [![Image 259: Monthly war exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "Monthly war exhaustion") war exhaustion is high, the country's ruler can **Invoke Devotio** to offer himself as a sacrifice in exchange for victory and strengthen the resolve of the population to fight on. Invoking devotio has a base cost of [![Image 260: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**2** tyranny and gives the following effects for **5** years:

Like with making divine sacrifices, it is possible to stack the effects of invoking devotio by making another invokation while the effects of the previous one(s) is ongoing, which has the additional effect of extending the length of the stacked effect by another 5 years. However, as the amount of tyranny gained is not particularly high and war exhaustion is generally not too problematic unless it is very high, this is generally not as important as with making divine sacrifices.

The cost of making invoking devotio is modified by the [![Image 261: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio")**Invoke Devotio Cost** modifier, which is given only by the [![Image 262: Devout](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Devout.png/28px-Devout.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Devout") Devout trait (**-10%**) and the stacking costs of invoking devotio itself.

============================================================
# Heritages
============================================================
Title: Heritages - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritages

Markdown Content:
**Heritages** are a set of modifiers assigned to each [state](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State "State"), usually consisting of two bonuses and one malus usually reflecting its culture, history, or ambitions. Several [countries](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Countries "Countries") have unique heritages, while the remainder share group heritages. Heritages are pre-assigned and cannot be changed over the course of a game, not even when creating a [formable nation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_Nations "Formable Nations"), outside of a few rare events.

Many major and more notable countries have their own unique heritages, listed below.

[![Image 1: Flag of Acragas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/65/Acragas.png/28px-Acragas.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Acragas "Acragas") Acragantine heritage

[![Image 2: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Build_cost.png)**-5%** Build Cost 

[![Image 3: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Omen.png)**+10%** Omen power 

[![Image 4: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Loyalty_gain_chance.png)**+10%** Cohort loyalty gain chance

_Acragas is the second most powerful Siceliote city, vying with Syracuse for control of the island, but also victim to its own tyrants and demagogues. Her temples are perhaps the most numerous in the entire Greek world._

[![Image 5: Flag of Aeolia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ef/Aeolia.png/28px-Aeolia.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aeolia "Aeolia") Aeolian heritage

[![Image 7: Pop citizen output.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5f/Pop_citizen_output.png/28px-Pop_citizen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Pop_citizen_output.png)**+6%** National citizen output 

[![Image 8: Fort defense.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fd/Fort_defense.png/28px-Fort_defense.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Fort_defense.png)**−10%** Fort defense

_Home to the once great city of Troy, the Greek cities of Aeolia have a strong mercantile background on the along the Hellespont, and boast many philosophers as residents and natives._

[![Image 10: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Discipline.png)**+5%** Discipline

[![Image 12: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Diplomatic_relations.png)**-1** Diplomatic relations

_The fiercely independent Aetolians have survived their northern neighbors advances by tenacity alone, and stand ready to become one of the leading powers of northern Greece._

_One of the oldest cities in the known world, Byblos has been the home of Phoenician maritime kingdoms for longer than most other countries have even been populated and it is from here that many of the settlers that founded the great Punic towns in the western Mediterranean originated._

_Byzantion was fortuitously founded in command of the most important strait in the eastern Mediterranean, growing rich from the passage of trade through and across the Bosporos strait._

_Founded as 'the new city' centuries ago by settlers from Tyre in faraway Phoenicia, Carthage is now the uncontested leader of the Punic, or Western Phoenician world. Her heritage is one of a strong naval and mercantile power, often led to greatness by charismatic generals._

_Croton was a successful Achaean colony and home to the mathematician and sage Pythagoras' and his famous school, which was forced out of the city by revolutionaries afraid of its impious influence and political clout._

_The Greek colony of Cyrene was established by settlers fleeing a famine on the island of Thera. They successfully integrated the native inhabitants and established the famed Pentapolis, or five cities, now a byword for the entire region. A centre of trade and learning, Cyrenaica holds a unique position if they can maintain independence from their powerful neighbors._

Even after the death of Alexander and the rise of the Antipatrids Macedon has an uncontested rule among Greek states, with the Macedonian barons making up the core of a strong military machine._

[![Image 144: Manpower recovery speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Manpower_recovery_speed.png/28px-Manpower_recovery_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Manpower_recovery_speed.png)**−5%** Manpower recovery speed

_Massilia was the leading colony of the Ionian city of Phocaea, whose people abandoned their homes rather than submit to Persian rule. The refugees settled here and in their other colonies, turning the isolated port into one of the foremost trading powers and colonisers in the west, all the while defending their strongholds from the barbarians that surround them._

[![Image 145: Flag of Maurya](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5b/Maurya.png/28px-Maurya.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maurya "Maurya") Heritage of Chandragupta

_While the Maurya empire is a newcomer to the scene of Northern India it is also the carrier of an ancient Dharmic heritage that predates its own conception as well as that of the Nanda and Argead empires it displaced. The state built by Chandragupta is one of institutional strength but it must also harbor a great variety of peoples and faiths._

_**This heritage can be lost after the first generation of Mauryan rulers have died or been deposed.**_

_An ancient rival of Athens for control of Attica, Megara derives its status from its position linking Hellas to the Peloponesos, and is home to the Megarian school of dialectic philosophy._

_When the Spartans were defeated by Epaminondas of Thebes, he founded the city of Messene and gave it to the Helots. We have always been the main population group in both Laconia and Messenia, but for centuries we were slaves under Sparta. As a free nation we can finally benefit from our own hard work. As a free nation we must also never forget that we were once slaves under Sparta, and forcefully be on our guard against those who would enslave us once again._

_Metapontum is one of the oldest Italiote colonies, founded by settlers from Achaea and surviving the intrigues of its close neighbors. The mathematician Pythagoras fled here from Croton, and many still follow his religious teachings._

_Founded many centuries ago Rhodes has risen to be one of the premier naval powers in the Aegean, but is also famous for its great workshops and its magnificent siege works. The most proud achievement of the island state remains being able to withstand the arduous siege of Demetrios Poliorketes, who brought the most advanced siege machinery in the world to the island but failed to breach its fortifications._

[![Image 181: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/28px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome") Roman heritage

_Born where the Greek south meets the Etruscan north, Rome has quickly risen to be the dominant force of central Italy and the leader of the a league of allies that stand ready to do their every bidding. Rome's unfailing capacity to mobilize its population and acquire the loyalty of the city states under its protection has over time grown into a strong imperialistic heritage._

[![Image 185: Flag of Seleukid Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c4/Seleukid_Empire.png/28px-Seleukid_Empire.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Seleukid_Empire "Seleukid Empire") Heritage of Seleukos

_With no direct connection to their Greek homeland the Seleukid rulers have had to foster a Hellenistic culture which can encompass and empower a ruling caste that is caters both to its Greek subjects and immigrants as well as its large Chaldean and Persian population groups. As such it has often come to choose adaption over cultural supremacy and has over time become a shining beacon of the Greek fusion of cultures known as Hellenism._

_Once the dominant power of the Greek world, Sparta remains the carrier of an ancient martial heritage that few can question._

_Syracuse is one of the older Greek colonies in the west and it has on many occasions been a local power contending with the other Italic, Phoenician and Greek powers on equal terms. _Once the leading military and hegemonic power in mainland Greece, Thebes was completely destroyed by Alexander the Great. Kassandros of Macedon however rebuilt the city, inviting Theban exiles to once again establish themselves in their old homes in Boeotia. Eager to reclaim the Heritage of Epaminondas, the Thebans are now ready to rise again._

_As one of Alexander's officers and former bodyguard Lysimachos was always an able general. The lasting heritage he brought to Thrace however was that of a state builder and a diplomat, able to leverage great gains from his victories in the successor wars despite his meager resources._

Certain country-specific heritages will appear only after certain events.

Some countries of the same culture or culture group share a common special heritage, listed below.

All other states without a country or culture/culture group specific heritage get a generic one from the list below, based on the terrain and modifiers of its capital. The first heritage that the country meets the requirements for will be selected.

Pirate heritage

[![Image 218: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Opinion_improvement_active.png)**-15** Opinion of non-Pirate Heritage Countries

_Some would say that the ancestors of this people were pirates and plunderers, always ready to profit from the misfortune of others. That is, however, a matter of perspective. A strong adventuring spirit along with a love for the open sea is the heritage that binds this people together._

Seafaring heritage

**Notes:**_For countries with a starting [![Image 219: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country\_capital.png/28px-Country\_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital in a [![Image 220: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port\_icon.png/28px-Port\_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port")**port**._

_The Heritage of this people has been associated with navigating the open seas for generations. Some say their ancestors came here from faraway lands, others that they have welcomed sailors that sought out their ports._

_While they never became great seafarers this people have always been dependent on what the sea brings them for their wealth and prosperity. A Heritage of trade and bartering is the core around which their culture revolves._

_The fertile plains around great rivers have given birth to many civilizations over the centuries. This people's heritage is closely tied to farming the land of the river plains in which they originated._

_The mountainous landscapes in which this people originated has shaped their Heritage of fierce independence and self determination. Some would say that their strong sense of self defense can even make them see enemies where there were only friends, but their awareness of how they can survive and defend themselves in hostile terrain has served them well over the centuries._

_The ancestors of this people have traversed open deserts for many generations and over time built traditions and an almost instinct based understanding for how to survive and thrive among the hot dunes. _Corinth was one of the largest city-states of Greece until recent years, playing an important role as it was at a highly defensive position in-between the rich states of Attica and the Peleponnese. After the conquests of Macedon, it was left as nothing more than a fortified position for the Macedonian troops in the south, bereft of its own independence._

Released from the [![Image 293: Flag of Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0d/Carthage.png/20px-Carthage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage")[Carthaginian](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage") mission tree [**An End to Sicilian Warfare**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Punic_missions#An_End_to_Sicillian_Warfare "Punic missions")

[![Image 294: Flag of Mithridatic Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Mithridatic_Kingdom.png/28px-Mithridatic_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mithridatic_Kingdom "Mithridatic Kingdom") Heritage of Mithridates

[![Image 295: Build cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e2/Build_cost.png/28px-Build_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Build_cost.png)**-10%** Build cost 

[![Image 296: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Manpower.png)**+5%** National manpower 

[![Image 297: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Diplomatic_relations.png)**−1** Diplomatic relations

_The Persian Mithritadids ruled the Mysian city of Cius until the assassination of Mithridates II drove his heir to forge a new kingdom in Paphlagonia, which eventually became the regional power of northern Asia Minor._

Can be formed through the [![Image 298: Flag of Kios](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Kios.png/20px-Kios.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Kios "Kios")[Kiosian](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Kios "Kios") event [**Paphlagonian Response**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mithridates_events#dhe_mithridates.12 "Mithridates events")

[![Image 299: Flag of Pontus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Pontus.png/20px-Pontus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pontus "Pontus")[Pontus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pontus "Pontus") can also get this heritage through the [![Image 300: Flag of Pontus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Pontus.png/20px-Pontus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pontus "Pontus")[Pontic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pontus "Pontus") event [**Mithridatic Solution**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mithridates_events#dhe_mithridates.6 "Mithridates events")

Religious State heritage

**Notes:**_For countries released through the **[Autonomous Enclave](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic\_missions#shrine\_branch\_4 "Generic missions")** mission task._

============================================================
# National_ideas
============================================================
Title: National ideas - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/National_ideas_interface.png/500px-National_ideas_interface.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:National_ideas_interface.png)

The nation overview tab containing the national ideas sub-section.

Much like in its predecessor, _Europa Universalis: Rome_, a country can be tailored according to its circumstances and ambitions by picking **national ideas** that afford certain bonuses.

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=National_ideas&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=National_ideas&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

There are 36 national ideas the player can choose from. Each idea has its own bonus and belongs to one of four categories: [![Image 2: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") Military, [![Image 3: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic, [![Image 4: Oratory power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") Oratory, and [![Image 5: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") Religion. Some ideas are available from the start, while others are unlocked by [advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Fields "Technology") over the course of the game. Selecting a new national idea, including filling blank slots (such as at the beginning of the game), costs [![Image 6: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**20** political influence and can be done at any time with no cooldown, provided that the country has [![Image 7: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence to spare.

The number of allowed national ideas depends on the country's [![Image 8: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") government type. Generally speaking, [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") governments can have 2 national ideas, while Tribal Federations, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), and [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") have 3. The special Dictatorship, Empire, Imperial Cult, and Athenian Republic government types have the maximum 4 slots.

### Government bonus[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=National_ideas&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Government bonus") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=National_ideas&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Government bonus")]

Each government in the game comes with a base bonus specific to that government. There is also a secondary bonus for each government that is given if the categories of the chosen ideas correspond to what is expected by that government form.

For example, in the screenshot Carthage is an [Aristocratic Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Republics "Government"). This means that they need to pick two [![Image 9: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") military ideas and one [![Image 10: Oratory power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") oratory idea to receive the additional Aristocratic Republic bonus, which will fully coloured when active and faded when inactive. Etruria, which starts as an [Oligarchic Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Republics "Government"), would instead needs one civic idea and two oratory ideas to get its bonus. There is no malus for not having the correct categories of ideas, except for the opportunity cost of not being able to access the government bonus.

The Government bonus of each type of government can be found in the [Government Types](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Government_Types "Government") page.

## Ideas[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=National_ideas&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ideas") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=National_ideas&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ideas")]
