============================================================
# Stability
============================================================
Title: State - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

_This article is about the ingame concept of countries and their associated mechanics. For a list of all countries in the game, see [Countries](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Countries "Countries")._

[![Image 1](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3f/Nation_overview_screen.png/620px-Nation_overview_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Nation_overview_screen.png)

The main country/nation interface. Most national values are listed above the top bar.

A **state**, or a **country**, is an autonomous political structure comprising a community of people organized under some form of [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government"), and is the main playable focus of _Imperator: Rome_. A state has a court of characters, including a [![Image 2: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler; has control over some amount of [territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") and the [population](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") that lives there (except for [![Image 3: Country rank 1.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/18/Country_rank_1.png/28px-Country_rank_1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migrant_horde "Migrant horde")_Migrant Hordes_); a [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") form and [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") that define many aspects of how the country is run; a primary [![Image 4: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") culture and state [![Image 5: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religion that will affect the happiness and civic rights of its pops; and various accumulated values such as [![Image 6: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, [![Image 7: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower, [![Image 8: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence, and [![Image 9: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability that represent the resources available to the country and the current state of its well-being. Countries can interact with each other through [diplomacy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy "Diplomacy"), [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade"), and [![Image 10: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare")[war](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War "War"), and a state may be independent or be a [![Image 11: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations")[subject](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") of a stronger suzerain. The world is populated by hundreds of countries at the start of the game, from minor tribes and city states to the great empires of the east, many of which will likely soon disappear but some who may rise to form one of the great empires that the era was known for.

## Government[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Government") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Government")]

_Main article: [Government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government")_

One of the main defining features of a country is its [![Image 12: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government")**government type**, which can be classified into one of three more general **government forms** - [Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), [Monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") or [Tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal").

Different government types have different modifiers, bonuses, and [National Idea](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas") slots, and it can be useful to switch to one that better synergizes with the current strategy and playstyle. Changing between _government types_ is generally done by [![Image 13: Menu decision.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Menu_decision.png/28px-Menu_decision.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions") decision and the requirements can generally be seen at any time. These requirements may include a minimum [![Image 14: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability level, certain [![Image 15: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")[laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") (or not having certain laws), a minimum [![Image 16: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value in the [![Image 17: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital territory, a specific [![Image 18: Menu technology.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Menu_technology.png/28px-Menu_technology.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") technology level, and more, and will almost always cost [![Image 19: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability. Certain [![Image 20: Menu mission.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Menu_mission.png/28px-Menu_mission.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") mission trees, such as the [![Image 21: Flag of Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0d/Carthage.png/20px-Carthage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage")[Carthaginian](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage") mission tree [**Aftermath of the Revolution**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Punic_missions#Aftermath_of_the_Revolution "Punic missions"), may also allow for changing government type even when if the country does not meet the normal requirements.

The _government form_ defines the method of succession, the available [![Image 22: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), and the makeup of the [![Image 23: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") offices, which give political influence and various bonuses according to the [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character")'s [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes") and [![Image 24: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. Each government form also has different mechanics that a player must deal with - [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") must seek the [![Image 25: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") approval of the [Senate](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#The_Senate "Government") for nearly every major action, [monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") must watch their [![Image 26: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy and deal with angry [![Image 27: Leader pretender.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/11/Leader_pretender.png/28px-Leader_pretender.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pretender "Pretender") pretenders, and [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") must appease their powerful [![Image 28: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs and decide on the direction of [![Image 29: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization. [Government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") offices also provide [![Image 30: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence depending upon the holder's [![Image 31: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty. Changing the government form is usually harder than changing government type; reforming from a Tribal government to a Republic or Monarchy involves going through the [**Tribal Reform**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions#Tribal_Reform "Missions") mission tree, changing from a Republic to a Monarchy requires high [![Image 32: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny and the declaration of a [Dictatorship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Republican_governments "Government") through one of the [![Image 33: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Line of Succession inventions, and changing from a Monarchy to a Republic is not generally possible outside of a few special events or missions (such as the [![Image 34: Flag of Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c0/Syracusae.png/20px-Syracusae.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae")[Syracusan](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae") mission tree [**Death to Tyrants**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Syracuse_Missions&action=edit&redlink=1 "Syracuse Missions (page does not exist)")).

## Ruler[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ruler") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ruler")]

_Main article: [Ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler")_

[![Image 35: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Each state is headed by a **ruler**, prominently visible on the main tab of the ![Image 36: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)[government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") panel, and their [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), [attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes"), and [relationships](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Relationships "Characters") have a significant effect on [![Image 37: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty and many important country modifiers from [![Image 38: Commerce modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/30/Commerce_modifier.png/28px-Commerce_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Commerce_modifier.png) national commerce income to [![Image 39: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale_recovery "Morale recovery") army morale recovery to [![Image 40: Stability monthly change.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/86/Stability_monthly_change.png/28px-Stability_monthly_change.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Stability_monthly_change.png) monthly stability change. Rulers with high attributes can be a considerable boon to their country, significantly strengthening the state and allowing the nation to punch well above its weight, while corrupt, low attribute rulers can inflict major penalties that a state must struggle through. A ruler may be supported by a **co-ruler** or **consort**, who contribute any of their four main attributes if they are higher than those of the ruler, but may also have effects on [![Image 41: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence generation if disloyal and contribute to modifiers for ruler [![Image 42: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity and [![Image 43: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption. Succession varies widely depending on the country's [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") form, ranging between dynastic inheritance in a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), Senatorial election in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic"), and rotation between Clan Chiefs in [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal"); the length of office may vary widely as well, from the shortest possible 2-year term to lifetime rule typical of more autocratic systems.

## Culture[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Culture") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Culture")]

_Main article: [Culture#Primary culture](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Primary\_culture "Culture")_

Every country begins the game with a predefined **primary culture**, which can be seen at the top right corner of the [![Image 44: Menu diplomacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Menu_diplomacy.png/28px-Menu_diplomacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_diplomacy.png) Diplomacy screen. Outside of a few specific events, decisions, and mission tasks, such as the startup events for [![Image 45: Flag of Heraclea Pontica](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a4/Heraclea_Pontica.png/20px-Heraclea_Pontica.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heraclea_Pontica "Heraclea Pontica")[Heraclea Pontica](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heraclea_Pontica "Heraclea Pontica"), the [![Image 46: Flag of Parthia (Formable)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Placeholder_Flag.png/20px-Placeholder_Flag.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Parthia_(Formable) "Parthia (Formable)")[Parthia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Parthia_(Formable) "Parthia (Formable)") formation decision, the final tasks of the [**Hellenistic Empire**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diadochi_missions "Diadochi missions") mission tree, and events during the Yuezhi migrations, the primary culture generally cannot be changed except for during a [barbarian takeover](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians").

Pops of the primary culture are typically the happiest and most satisfied pops in the realm, and are fixed to the [![Image 47: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble [civic right](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability#Civic_rights). Most often the [culture of the state population](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Population_culture_and_population_religion "Population") is more diverse, but pop culture can be gradually changed to match that of the state during the playthrough via [![Image 48: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") assimilation, as pops of a different culture and especially culture group have a significant happiness malus that can become very difficult to manage if [![Image 49: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability begins to drop. [![Image 50: Pop assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2c/Pop_assimilation.png/28px-Pop_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Assimilation "Assimilation") Assimilation is also slowed if the dominant province culture and/or religion is not the same as that of the state, all of which makes the conquest of wrong culture (especially wrong culture group) and wrong religion areas significantly more difficult, especially in the early game. Outside of events pops will always assimilate and convert to the primary culture. As an alternative to assimilation, cultures can be [![Image 51: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated to be given the same rights as the primary culture, at the expense of the happiness of already integrated cultures.

The primary culture also has an effect on diplomacy. States of a different culture (in the same culture group) get a [![Image 52: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**-10** opinion malus, while those in a different culture group get a [![Image 53: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**-20** opinion malus. The [![Image 54: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject types available also depend on primary culture; another state can only be made a [feudatory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations#Feudatory "Subject nations") if it is in the same culture group as its new overlord, and [satrapies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations#Satrapy "Subject nations") can only be made of states with [Persian military traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Persian_traditions "Persian traditions"), which consist generally of countries in the Iranian, Caucasian, Anatolian, Scythian, Bactrian, and Aramaic culture groups.

A country's starting [![Image 55: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[military tradition trees](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_Traditions "Military Traditions") is also determined based on its primary culture/culture group, which affects what military and economic bonuses can be accrued by spending [![Image 56: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience, at least initially. However, it is possible to unlock other tradition trees by integrating their cultures and adopting the appropriate traditions.

## Religion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Religion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Religion")]

_Main article: [Religion#State religion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State\_religion "Religion")_

Every country starts with a predefined **state religion**, which can be viewed just above the pantheon deities in the [![Image 57: Menu religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Menu_religion.png/28px-Menu_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_religion.png) Religion tab. Every religion has bonuses that are applied to the entire country, and also determines the base deities that are always available regardless of the population or holy sites of the country. State religion also has a small effect on diplomacy—countries of the same religion get a [![Image 58: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**+10** opinion bonus with each other. There is no malus for having a different state religion.

There is no default happiness bonus or penalty for pop religion, but pops will receive a [![Image 59: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**+4%** happiness bonus for each deity in the state pantheon that matches their religion, making it useful for polytheistic states to take on pantheon gods of religions that are widespread inside their country - though spreading out the deity happiness bonus between deities of different religions means a smaller happiness modifier for each individual religion. The happiness of state religion pops specifically is also be increased with the [![Image 60: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness")**state religion happiness** modifier, which is given by various omens and deities, [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"), [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), [heritages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritages "Heritages"), and more.

Additionally, the percentage of pops following the state religion determines the nation's [![Image 61: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal")**Religious Unity** value, which is the main component of [![Image 62: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") omen power.

### State Conversion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&veaction=edit&section=T-1 "Edit section: State Conversion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Religion&action=edit&section=T-1 "Edit section: State Conversion")]

_Main article: [Decisions#Religious Conversion decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions#Religious\_Conversion\_decisions "Decisions")_

The state religion can be changed by decision if there is at least **1** character of the new religion in the country, and either **50%** of the non-slave pops in the [![Image 63: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital, or at least **20%** of all the pops of the country and at least one [![Image 64: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") pantheon deity, follow the new religion the country intends to convert to. Switching religions can only be done once every 10 years and costs [![Image 65: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**200** political influence and [![Image 66: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**30** stability, also giving a temporary drop of [![Image 67: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**−15** Loyalty for all characters that are followers of the old faith.

## Heritage[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Heritage") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Heritage")]

_Main article: [Heritage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritage "Heritage")_

Each nation also has a set **heritage**, usually with two bonuses and one malus reflecting its culture, history, or ambitions. Some countries have their own unique heritages, while others have a more generic group heritage depending on its culture or starting position. A country's heritage cannot be changed, even when [forming a new nation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_Nations "Formable Nations"), outside of a few rare events, and its modifiers are applied to the country for the whole game.

## Accumulated points[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Accumulated points") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Accumulated points")]

### Wealth[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Wealth") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Wealth")]

_Main article: [Economy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy")_

[![Image 68: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Cost") The nation's **Treasury** stores all of its currently accumulated **wealth**, or **gold**. The nation's [income](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy") is calculated monthly and comes from [![Image 69: National tax](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "National tax") tax collection, [![Image 70: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") domestic commerce and trade route income, and [![Image 71: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income")[subject](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") tributes. Many events can also give gold into the treasury, often scaled by the nation's current income.

Monthly expenses include [![Image 72: Maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/95/Maintenance.png/28px-Maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maintenance "Maintenance") army and fleet maintenance, [![Image 73: Tribute income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e9/Tribute_income.png/28px-Tribute_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribute_income "Tribute income") tributes to an overlord, [![Image 74: Fort maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Fort_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Fort_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_maintenance "Fort maintenance") fort maintenance and [![Image 75: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage")[character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character") wages. Wealth is also spent on [building](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building "Building") costs, founding [cities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cities "Cities")/metropolises, recruiting [![Image 76: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions and [![Image 77: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries") mercenaries, certain [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character") interactions, [![Image 78: Slave move cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Pop_slaves_move_cost.png/28px-Pop_slaves_move_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_movement "Slave move cost") slave resettlement between [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories"), and much more, particularly [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mission "Mission"). It is sometimes possible for a state to spend more money than it currently has in its treasury, which will put it into [![Image 79: Bankruptcy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Bankruptcy.png/28px-Bankruptcy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deficit "Deficit")**deficit** with impacts on [![Image 80: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability and [![Image 81: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence generation. If a state runs into deficit, events will trigger that grant [![Image 82: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold in exchange for a temporary malus, which generally ensure that countries never stay in deficit for too long.

### Manpower[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Manpower") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Manpower")]

[![Image 83: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**Manpower** represents a pool of ready-to-fight people who are drawn from [![Image 84: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen, [![Image 85: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman, and [![Image 86: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesman pops inhabiting a state. Manpower is used when [recruiting](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_units "Land units") new [![Image 87: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion cohorts and replenishing [![Image 88: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition or combat losses; if manpower becomes low, a nation can become vulnerable to attack. Each point of manpower provides one soldier for a newly recruited or reinforcing cohort; if a country runs out of manpower, they will no longer be able to recruit any more cohorts or reinforce their armies until it replenishes (note that this does not include raising [![Image 89: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, but does include reinforcing them).

The base yearly manpower production of each country is determined by its [![Image 90: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**national manpower**, which is a base of **+125** for most countries with an additional **+500** for [![Image 91: Country rank 1.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/18/Country_rank_1.png/28px-Country_rank_1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migrant_horde "Migrant horde") migrant hordes. This base manpower production is then increased by the output of the [![Image 92: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen, [![Image 93: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freeman, and [![Image 94: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesman pops in the country. Each [![Image 95: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen produces a base of [![Image 96: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**+2** manpower, each [![Image 97: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen a base of [![Image 98: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**+4**, and each [![Image 99: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen a base of [![Image 100: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower")**+3** manpower per pop per year, which is then scaled to the pop's [![Image 101: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness. After applying [![Image 102: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") all population output modifiers, the amount of manpower that a pop produces is further increased country-wide by the [![Image 103: Global manpower modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Global_manpower_modifier.png/28px-Global_manpower_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_manpower_modifier.png)**national manpower** and locally by the [![Image 104: Global manpower modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Global_manpower_modifier.png/28px-Global_manpower_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_manpower_modifier.png)**local manpower** modifiers, which are given by several [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") bonuses (e.g. [![Image 105: Wood](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Goods_wood.png/28px-Goods_wood.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Wood "Wood") wood) and from the [![Image 106: Barracks](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/28/Building_barracks.png/28px-Building_barracks.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Barracks "Barracks") Barracks and [![Image 107: Training Camp](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ec/Building_military.png/28px-Building_military.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Training_Camp "Training Camp") Training Camp [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings"). Note that [![Image 108: Global manpower modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/56/Global_manpower_modifier.png/28px-Global_manpower_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_manpower_modifier.png) global manpower modifiers are only applied to manpower production from pops, not from the base manpower from the [![Image 109: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") national manpower modifier, which is a significant difference for less populous countries. Like all other pop outputs, citizen, freeman, and tribesman happiness and output modifiers will also increase the manpower that they produce.

This gives a final yearly manpower formula of:

![Image 110: {\displaystyle {\text{Yearly Manpower}}={\text{National Manpower}}+\sum _{\text{Territories}}\sum _{\text{Pops}}\left({\text{Base Pop Manpower Production}}\cdot {\frac {\text{Pop Happiness}}{100\%}}\cdot \left(1+{\frac {\text{Pop Output Modifiers}}{100\%}}\right)\cdot \left(1+{\frac {\text{Manpower Modifiers}}{100\%}}\right)\right)}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/8d5a72fbc9b35b8c01d1d97f9aa1b20fb33a401c)
A country's **maximum manpower** is then **11** times the country's total yearly manpower production (including the base [![Image 111: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") national manpower, which gives a base manpower pool of **1375** for each nation). If the current manpower pool is below the cap, it will recover by a base of **1/132nd** each month, so that refilling the pool takes **11** years. For each territory, the part of the manpower gain that comes from pops (i.e. **1/12th** of the local yearly manpower production) is modified by the national [![Image 112: Manpower recovery speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Manpower_recovery_speed.png/28px-Manpower_recovery_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower_recovery_speed "Manpower recovery speed")**manpower recovery speed** modifier before being listed on the territory interface as the **monthly manpower gain**, and this part of the country's manpower production is summed up across all territories as well as with the monthly manpower production from the base [![Image 113: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") national manpower to give the final **manpower gained from pops**. Manpower recovery is also given as the tribute of certain [subject](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") types, such as [feudatories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Feudatory "Feudatory") and [tribal vassals](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal_vassal "Tribal vassal") (note that while the tribute will appear in the total monthly manpower gain for the overlord, it is not explicitly shown in the manpower tooltip for the subject, though the manpower will still be deducted at the beginning of each month regardless).

This can be expressed as the following formula:

![Image 114: {\displaystyle {\text{National Monthly Manpower Gain}}={\frac {{\text{National Manpower}}\cdot 11}{132}}+{\frac {{\text{Yearly Manpower from Pops}}\cdot 11}{132}}\cdot \left(1+{\frac {\text{Manpower Recovery Speed}}{100\%}}\right)+{\text{Monthly Manpower from Tribute}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/cd5627455357216a434e71d443d93199b194e82f)
### Political Influence[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Political Influence") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Political Influence")]

_Main article: [Political influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political\_influence "Political influence")_

[![Image 115: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Political_influence.png)**Political Influence** represents the state's accumulated political capital and is used to perform many actions directly relating to the [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") or administration, such as selecting [national ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas"), implementing [![Image 116: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, increasing [![Image 117: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability, fabricating [![Image 118: Fabricate claim cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim "Claim") claims, founding [![Image 119: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities, making [![Image 120: Province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/State_free_investments.png/28px-State_free_investments.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment") province investments, building [![Image 121: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") wonders, and more.

It is an abstract currency generated by loyal members of your [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") over time, who are chosen as office holders. The total value accrued each month is determined by each [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character")'s [Loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Loyalty "Rebellion"), therefore, it may fluctuate consistently over the year. Each [![Image 122: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") government office provides up to [![Image 123: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**+0.25** per month, scaling by the holder's [![Image 124: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")[loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Loyalty "Rebellion") up to 100. Many [![Image 125: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions increase political influence gain on top of that, while [![Image 126: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") ruler corruption and [![Image 127: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") consort or co-ruler disloyalty can significantly reduce it.

### Military Experience[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Military Experience") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Military Experience")]

[![Image 128: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**Military Experience** is a countrywide value that measures the degree of practical military knowledge and expertise the state has built up. This currency is primarily used to embrace [![Image 129: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, at a cost of **80** military experience for each tradition.

Military Experience increases by a base of [![Image 130: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**+0.30** per month, plus an additional [![Image 131: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**+0.006** for every point of average [![Image 132: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") cohort experience across all armies (for a base maximum of [![Image 133: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**+0.90**). Various other modifiers such as [![Image 134: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion")[war exhaustion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion"), being in a [![Image 135: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") defensive war, and certain deities, [![Image 136: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religions, and [![Image 137: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws give an additional positive modifier to military experience gain, while [![Image 138: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenaries "Mercenaries")[mercenary reliance](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Mercenaries "Army") reduces the rate at which military experience is generated. Other than training up cohorts by fighting battles in war, a country can also use the [![Image 139: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army")**Drill Army** legion ability during peacetime to build up cohort experience in exchange for increased [![Image 140: Army maintenance cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Army_maintenance_cost.png/28px-Army_maintenance_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion_maintenance "Legion maintenance") legion maintenance cost and [![Image 141: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyalty gain chance.

While levies cannot be drilled and so cannot reach particularly high military experience levels, disbanding a levy that has been raised for at least **8** months will grant [![Image 142: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience")**0.015** military experience for each point of [![Image 143: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Experience") cohort experience, which can be very substantial for larger countries that have a significant [![Image 144: Starting experience](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Starting_experience.png/28px-Starting_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare#Experience "Starting experience") starting experience modifier. There are also a number of [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events") and [mission tasks](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions") that give military experience, as well as the [![Image 145: Deify ruler cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png/28px-Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Apotheosis "Apotheosis") apotheosis effect of certain deities when replaced with a deified ruler.

Every point of [![Image 146: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience stored gives a [![Image 147: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**+0.05%** army morale bonus to the country's cohorts, with a maximum bonus of [![Image 148: Land morale recovery.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/00/Land_morale_recovery.png/28px-Land_morale_recovery.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Morale "Morale")**+10%** when the stored military experience reaches its cap at **200** points. The bonus is reduced once the military experience is used on military traditions, mission requirements or in events.

### Research[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Research") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Research")]

_Main article: [Technology#Research\_Points](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research\_Points "Technology")_

[![Image 149: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")**Research Points** represent the progression of techniques, discoveries, and general technology over the course of time. They are produced by [![Image 150: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 151: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens inhabiting a state, and the yearly research output as a proportion of the number of [![Image 152: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture pops in the country determines the rate of progress in technological advances. It is generally recommended that at least 1/3 of a state's [![Image 153: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture population is made up of [![Image 154: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 155: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens in order to achieve at least 100% research efficiency, but how much is actually needed will vary widely depend on the proportion of [![Image 156: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles to [![Image 157: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, as nobles produce much more research per pop, as well as the amount of research produced by unintegrated cultures (who do not count against the denominator of research efficiency). Research point production can be increased directly many modifiers, such as the [![Image 158: Library](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Building_library.png/28px-Building_library.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Library "Library") library city [building](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building "Building") and [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") bonuses (cloth, earthenware, glass and papyrus), and is also sensitive to [![Image 159: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble and [![Image 160: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen happiness and output modifiers. Research point production can be tracked on the [![Image 161: Menu technology.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6f/Menu_technology.png/28px-Menu_technology.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")[technology](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") panel.

Research points are automatically spent towards advancing the four fields of [technology](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Research_Points "Technology"): martial, civil, oratory and religious, modified by [![Image 162: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position")[researcher](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Researcher "Researcher") skill. Each new advance in a field grants a small bonus for each level and grants an [![Image 163: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Innovation "Innovation") innovation, which can then be used to purchase [![Image 164: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")[inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") that give a wide variety of other useful modifiers.

### Stability[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Stability") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Stability")]

[![Image 165: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**Stability** represents the trust that the characters and population of your country has in your government, and its general ability to withstand threats and discontent from within. Stability runs from **0** to **100**, with a base value of **50** that the stability value will naturally decay to. Positive (above 50) has many economic benefits, increasing [![Image 166: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth") population growth, [![Image 167: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") population happiness, [![Image 168: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points, and [![Image 169: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy. Negative stability (below 50) will instead decrease population happiness and legitimacy (in monarchies), making it more likely that a revolt will break out if stability is not quickly raised.

Stability is decreased by certain diplomatic or political actions, such as breaking [![Image 170: Truce.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Truce.png/28px-Truce.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Truce "Truce") truces, changing [![Image 171: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws or [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") form, swapping [![Image 172: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") pantheon deities, or granting many [![Image 173: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") cultural privileges. It is also spent by migratory tribes to start [![Image 174: Migration cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Start_migration_cost_modifier.png/28px-Start_migration_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Migrating_and_settling "Migration cost") migrations. The primary method of increasing stability more quickly is to spend [![Image 175: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence to make [![Image 176: Sacrifice pig.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Sacrifice_pig.png/28px-Sacrifice_pig.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Divine_sacrifice "Divine sacrifice") Divine Sacrifices in the Religion view, which will increase the monthly stability change. Certain omens, [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits"), and [laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") also increase monthly stability change, as does ruler [![Image 177: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal, and many [Queued Events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Event_queue&action=edit&redlink=1 "Event queue (page does not exist)") also offer semi-reliable immediate stability boosts, usually at the cost of spending or forgoing a bonus in some other currency (especially [![Image 178: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold and [![Image 179: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence). High [![Image 180: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion, low [![Image 181: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") senate approval (in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic")), being in [![Image 182: Bankruptcy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Bankruptcy.png/28px-Bankruptcy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Deficit "Deficit") deficit, and [![Image 183: Global cultural integration speed modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png/28px-Global_cultural_integration_speed_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cultural_integration "Cultural integration") integrating cultures are some of the main factors that will decrease stability over time, and should be watched carefully.

A nation is considered **disorganized** if stability is below **20**, which will prevent the country from reassigning [![Image 184: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") government offices or declaring wars.

Generally speaking higher stability is always better, though because decay increases as stability gets further away from 50, it is usually not worth it to intentionally try to keep stability very high. Low stability should always be a cause for concern unless your nation is very well managed, as prolonged instability will lead to widespread unhappiness and [![Image 185: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest, and eventually [![Image 186: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion") rebellions.

Every point of stability above **50** has the following effects:

Every point of stability below **50** has the following effects:

Note that since stability decays towards 50, the stability decay reduction from low stability means that the base change of stability up towards 50 is more slow than the decay down towards 50 from high stability.

### Aggressive Expansion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Aggressive Expansion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Aggressive Expansion")]

[![Image 187: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion")**Aggressive Expansion** (**AE**) represents the resentment that other states and pops in your empire have against conquests and other aggressive actions you take. The primary effect of Aggressive Expansion is to decrease realm [![Image 188: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability and lower the [loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Loyalty "Rebellion") of all [subject states](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations"), which can have a significant impact on [![Image 189: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") population happiness and [![Image 190: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest at higher levels and raise the risk of [rebellions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion") if not handled properly. Higher Aggressive Expansion also significantly decreases the [![Image 191: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion") opinion of nearby states (though allies have a much reduced opinion malus) and makes it more likely that the nation is viewed as a threat, making it more difficult to conduct diplomacy and encouraging other states to form [![Image 192: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance")[alliances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliances "Alliances") or [![Image 193: Defensive league.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Defensive_league.png/28px-Defensive_league.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Defensive_league "Defensive league")[defensive leagues](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treaties#Defensive_Leagues "Treaties") against the expanding country. Very high Aggressive Expansion will have additional effects on [![Image 194: Integrated.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Integrated.png/28px-Integrated.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_cultures "Culture") integrated culture pops as well as reducing political influence generation. Aggressive Expansion has a base value of **0**, where it has no effects, and can become arbitrarily high, but cannot become negative.

Each point of Aggressive Expansion has the following modifiers:

Each point of Aggressive Expansion beyond **50** also has the following effects, in addition to the ones above:

Aggressive Expansion is primarily accrued through conquering new [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") or forcing [vassalization](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") during a war. Several [diplomatic actions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Diplomatic_actions "Diplomacy"), [character interactions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Interactions "Characters"), and unit abilities also increase Aggressive Expansion.

| **-10** | * [![Image 223: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") on adopting the Defending Liberty invention |

The base [![Image 224: Agressive expansion monthly decay.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Agressive_expansion_monthly_decay.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_monthly_decay.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_monthly_decay.png)**Aggressive Expansion Decay** is **-0.20%** of current _Aggressive Expansion_ per month, which is be increased by another **-0.20%** with the [![Image 225: Appeasing stance](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/53/Stance_appeasement.png/28px-Stance_appeasement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_stance "Appeasing stance")[Appeasing Diplomatic Stance](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Diplomatic_Stance "Diplomacy"), **+0.03%** from every point of positive [![Image 226: Diplomatic reputation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Diplomatic_reputation.png/28px-Diplomatic_reputation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_reputation "Diplomatic reputation") diplomatic reputation, and another **-0.20%** if the ruler has the [![Image 227: Righteous](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Righteous.png/28px-Righteous.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Righteous") Righteous trait; however, this decay is active only if your country is not currently on the side of the aggressor in a war. There are also several sources of flat monthly [![Image 228: Agressive expansion monthly change.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png)**Aggressive Expansion Change** that is active at all times, including [![Image 229: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")[Oratory inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Inventions "Technology"), [![Image 230: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office")[government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") offices, and various deities. All static sources of monthly Aggressive Expansion Change are listed below:

[![Image 272: Agressive expansion impact.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Agressive_expansion_impact.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_impact.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_impact.png)**Aggressive Expansion Impact** modifies how much aggressive expansion is accrued from conquering land and vassalizing other states after a war, primarily decreasing the AE cost of conquest. Sources of Aggressive Expansion Impact include [Oratory inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Inventions "Technology"), [Laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), ruler traits, heritages, mission/event modifiers, and high aggressive expansion; the last in particular means that at some point above 50 you will accrue very little additional Aggressive Expansion for any further conquests, effectively providing a soft cap to the modifier. The effects of Aggressive Expansion Impact are capped at **-95.00%**, corresponding to an absolute minimum of **+0.05** aggressive expansion per territory annexed or vassalized. Static sources of Aggressive Expansion Impact are listed below:

| [![Image 273: Agressive expansion impact.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Agressive_expansion_impact.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_impact.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_impact.png) | Type | [Collapse](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **+33.00%** | * [![Image 274: Casus belli.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Casus_belli.png/28px-Casus_belli.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Casus_belli "Casus belli") Demanding territory from [secondary participants](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare#Secondary_Participants "Warfare") (not the warleader) |
