| **-50.00%** | * [![Image 298: Fabricate claim cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim "Claim") Has claim on territory (note: applies when vassalizing as well) |

The impact of [![Image 299: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggressive Expansion is felt most in the early game, when diplomacy is most important and the lack of [![Image 300: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggression Expansion Impact means that taking even a few provinces can rack up a significant amount of Aggressive Expansion. Spending [![Image 301: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence on [![Image 302: Sacrifice pig.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Sacrifice_pig.png/28px-Sacrifice_pig.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Divine_sacrifice "Divine sacrifice") Divine Sacrifices as well as assigning [governor armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Governor_local_troops "Rebellion") to suppress [![Image 303: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest and handle [![Image 304: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty, particularly in already less happy unintegrated culture group provinces, may be necessary to deal with and counteract the loss of [![Image 305: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability, while judicious use of [Improving Relations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Improve_Relation "Diplomacy") can help prevent the diplomatic situation from getting out of hand. An expansionist empire should focus on sources of Aggressive Expansion Change and Impact, while general [![Image 306: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness and [![Image 307: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability modifiers - as well as focusing on stability when taking events from the [Event queue](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Event_queue&action=edit&redlink=1 "Event queue (page does not exist)") - will help deal with the [![Image 308: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability loss from aggressive expansion. In the later game, when the player has built up a large empire that can easily stand against its neighbours, accumulated many of the AE Change and Impact modifiers, and either accrued enough [![Image 309: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness modifiers or have sufficient garrison army capacity to handle the loss of [![Image 310: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability even at relatively high AE values, an empire can take advantage of the [![Image 311: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggressive Expansion Impact for having higher than 50 AE to be able to conquer large amounts of territory at little to no additional aggressive expansion penalty, at which point the main hindrance will be keeping [![Image 312: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability above 30 in order to be able to declare wars (assuming the country is stable enough to survive at low stability).

### Tyranny[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tyranny") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tyranny")]

[![Image 313: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**Tyranny** represents the level of oppression in your country and produces resentment against the government by characters and primary culture pops, but also increases the willingness of pops and factions to accede to the government's demands and centralization of power. The level of Tyranny must be carefully managed, particularly in the early game, in order to avoid large reductions in [![Image 314: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty that can ensue in [![Image 315: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war, but in the later game when [![Image 316: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and growth in power make characters and pops easier to deal with the [![Image 317: Agressive expansion monthly change.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png) aggressive expansion change and [![Image 318: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") national slave output can become quite useful. The base Tyranny values range from the base value of **0**, where it has no effects, to **100**, and cannot be negative.

Each point of Tyranny gives the following modifiers:

Tyranny is primarily increased by various character actions, such as imprisoning, executing, and imposing sanctions on various characters, as well as [![Image 319: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") invoking devotio to reduce [![Image 320: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion and when overriding the will of the Senate in Republics when doing character interactions, changing laws, or conducting diplomacy. There are also many events, particularly those relating to characters and their interactions, that can raise or lower the country's tyranny level. Additionally, country's tyranny enables certain events, and one might find them as impactful as its direct effects.

The actions and interactions that cost or change the national Tyranny value are listed below by their base cost, which can be changed by various interaction-specific modifiers:

| **-5** | * [![Image 358: Bribe character cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/af/Bribe_character_cost.png/28px-Bribe_character_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_interaction "Character interaction") Return Dictatorship [Character Interaction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Interactions "Characters") |

Tyranny is primarily lost through [![Image 359: Monthly tyranny.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_tyranny.png/28px-Monthly_tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_tyranny.png)**Monthly Tyranny** change, which has a base reduction of **-0.02** Tyranny per month. This can be increased and changed by different modifiers, such as certain [![Image 360: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") oratory inventions, various deity and omen bonuses, ruler attributes and traits, and more; sources of monthly tyranny change are listed below:

| Type | Modifier | [![Image 361: Monthly tyranny.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_tyranny.png/28px-Monthly_tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_tyranny.png) Monthly Tyranny | [Collapse](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Base | Base | **-0.02** |
| [Government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") | [![Image 362: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government")[Aristocratic Monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") Government Bonus | **-0.05** |
| [![Image 363: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png)[Strengthening Legitimacy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Legitimacy "Government") | **+0.025** per instance |

| [Heritage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritage "Heritage") | [![Image 415: Flag of Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Athens.png/20px-Athens.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens") Athenian Heritage ([Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens")) | **-0.02** |

While nominally a negative modifier, [![Image 416: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny is generally not particularly problematic at lower levels and it is not very important to keep it low unless the nation is already experiencing issues with [![Image 417: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty. The [![Image 418: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") invoke devotio action in particular can be used to essentially gain [![Image 419: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") in return for reducing [![Image 420: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion, effectively converting [![Image 421: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") population unhappiness (and corresponding [![Image 422: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest) into [![Image 423: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty, which is generally easier to deal with, especially if the country does not have much other tyranny to begin with.

It is worth noting that since Tyranny decay does not scale according to its current value and there are no easy ways to quickly lower it, high Tyranny can be problematic if the state is not prepared, especially if the ruler has low [![Image 424: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") Charisma and so the state has low base tyranny decay - if this becomes the case, the loyalty of powerful characters needs to be assured at all costs, or the country could easily spiral into [![Image 425: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war. The significant [![Image 426: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty and [![Image 427: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold maluses together can be dangerous if not kept under control by other means, as even a single disloyal family head might be enough to trigger a civil war. [![Image 428: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") Republics in particular need to pay close attention to tyranny levels, as tyranny reduces [![Image 429: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") Democrat support which can be highly problematic if they make up a significant portion of the Senate - especially if that in turn lowers overall [![Image 430: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Senate approval") senate support to the point where taking any actions accrues even more tyranny. On the flip side, tyranny pleases the [![Image 431: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs, making high tyranny an option to consider if they make up a significant portion of the Senate.

In the later game, if and when the player has accumulated enough [![Image 432: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty bonuses to handle the penalties, high Tyranny is much more useful and players can take full advantage of its bonuses, particularly its effects on [![Image 433: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion change. In conjunction with further reductions from other sources and growing Aggressive Expansion Impact bonuses, a country with high Tyranny can handle much larger conquests and [![Image 434: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion values in the lategame as opposed to what is feasible early on. The [![Image 435: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National Slave Output can also be quite useful once all the loyalty problems can be properly dealt with, making for a powerful tyrannical empire.

### War Exhaustion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: War Exhaustion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: War Exhaustion")]

[![Image 436: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:War_exhaustion.png)**War exhaustion** represents anger and discontent in the general population from the human and economic cost of war. Raising a region's [![Image 437: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies costs [![Image 438: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion")**0.5** war exhaustion, and keeping levies raised for more than **6** months will give an additional monthly war exhaustion penalty that increases the longer the levy is raised. Casualties, blockades, and enemy occupation can also contribute significantly to increasing a country's war exhaustion.

The effects of war exhaustion can be significant, decreasing [![Image 439: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of all pops and impacting [![Image 440: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") ruler popularity, but also increasing [![Image 441: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience gain and so can some amount of war exhaustion can be useful if the country is sufficiently stable. High War Exhaustion will increase the AI's willingness to sign a more favourable peace, and conversely may force a player to sign a less favourable peace treaty rather than let their nation be consumed by unrest. War Exhaustion ticks down slowly while at peace and based on the ruler's current [![Image 442: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal, which can be increased through various omens, laws, and ideas. If War Exhaustion becomes too high, the [![Image 443: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") Invoke Devotio action in the religion panel can be taken to increase War Exhaustion decay at the cost of [![Image 444: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") Tyranny. War exhaustion generally has a value between **0** and **20**, but can rise to **30** for the attacker in an offensive war.

Each point of war exhaustion has the following effects:

## State rank[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: State rank") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: State rank")]

A country's **rank** is a measure of how large and influential a particular state is, based largely on the number of [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") that it owns. Country rank is one of the primary determinants of the number of major families/clan chiefs, [![Image 445: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range, and [![Image 446: Integrate speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/31/Integrate_speed.png/28px-Integrate_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Integration_speed "Integration speed") integration speed as well as significantly impacting [![Image 447: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence generation, [![Image 448: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness, and [![Image 449: Warscore cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Warscore_cost.png/28px-Warscore_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_score_cost "War score cost") warscore cost but higher ranked nations are also more vulnerable to civil war and rebellions as they have lower [![Image 450: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/General "General loyalty")[![Image 451: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Admiral "Admiral loyalty")[![Image 452: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor loyalty") office loyalty as well as a [![Image 453: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty malus and a lower [![Image 454: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold.

* [![Image 485: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") Subject nations are additionally capped at [![Image 486: Country rank 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Country_rank_3.png/28px-Country_rank_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Local_power "Local power") local power status, regardless of how big they get.

Country rank is also particularly important for diplomacy, as certain diplomatic actions are available only to countries of a certain rank.
