[![Image 272: Agressive expansion impact.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Agressive_expansion_impact.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_impact.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_impact.png)**Aggressive Expansion Impact** modifies how much aggressive expansion is accrued from conquering land and vassalizing other states after a war, primarily decreasing the AE cost of conquest. Sources of Aggressive Expansion Impact include [Oratory inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Inventions "Technology"), [Laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), ruler traits, heritages, mission/event modifiers, and high aggressive expansion; the last in particular means that at some point above 50 you will accrue very little additional Aggressive Expansion for any further conquests, effectively providing a soft cap to the modifier. The effects of Aggressive Expansion Impact are capped at **-95.00%**, corresponding to an absolute minimum of **+0.05** aggressive expansion per territory annexed or vassalized. Static sources of Aggressive Expansion Impact are listed below:

| [![Image 273: Agressive expansion impact.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Agressive_expansion_impact.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_impact.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_impact.png) | Type | [Collapse](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tyranny) |
| --- | --- | --- |

The impact of [![Image 299: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggressive Expansion is felt most in the early game, when diplomacy is most important and the lack of [![Image 300: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggression Expansion Impact means that taking even a few provinces can rack up a significant amount of Aggressive Expansion. Spending [![Image 301: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence on [![Image 302: Sacrifice pig.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Sacrifice_pig.png/28px-Sacrifice_pig.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Divine_sacrifice "Divine sacrifice") Divine Sacrifices as well as assigning [governor armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Governor_local_troops "Rebellion") to suppress [![Image 303: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest and handle [![Image 304: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty, particularly in already less happy unintegrated culture group provinces, may be necessary to deal with and counteract the loss of [![Image 305: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability, while judicious use of [Improving Relations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Improve_Relation "Diplomacy") can help prevent the diplomatic situation from getting out of hand. An expansionist empire should focus on sources of Aggressive Expansion Change and Impact, while general [![Image 306: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness and [![Image 307: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability modifiers - as well as focusing on stability when taking events from the [Event queue](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Event_queue&action=edit&redlink=1 "Event queue (page does not exist)") - will help deal with the [![Image 308: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability loss from aggressive expansion. In the later game, when the player has built up a large empire that can easily stand against its neighbours, accumulated many of the AE Change and Impact modifiers, and either accrued enough [![Image 309: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness modifiers or have sufficient garrison army capacity to handle the loss of [![Image 310: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability even at relatively high AE values, an empire can take advantage of the [![Image 311: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggressive Expansion Impact for having higher than 50 AE to be able to conquer large amounts of territory at little to no additional aggressive expansion penalty, at which point the main hindrance will be keeping [![Image 312: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability above 30 in order to be able to declare wars (assuming the country is stable enough to survive at low stability).

### Tyranny[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tyranny") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Tyranny")]

[![Image 313: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**Tyranny** represents the level of oppression in your country and produces resentment against the government by characters and primary culture pops, but also increases the willingness of pops and factions to accede to the government's demands and centralization of power. The level of Tyranny must be carefully managed, particularly in the early game, in order to avoid large reductions in [![Image 314: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty that can ensue in [![Image 315: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war, but in the later game when [![Image 316: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and growth in power make characters and pops easier to deal with the [![Image 317: Agressive expansion monthly change.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png/28px-Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Agressive_expansion_monthly_change.png) aggressive expansion change and [![Image 318: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") national slave output can become quite useful. The base Tyranny values range from the base value of **0**, where it has no effects, to **100**, and cannot be negative.

Each point of Tyranny gives the following modifiers:

Tyranny is primarily increased by various character actions, such as imprisoning, executing, and imposing sanctions on various characters, as well as [![Image 319: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") invoking devotio to reduce [![Image 320: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion and when overriding the will of the Senate in Republics when doing character interactions, changing laws, or conducting diplomacy. There are also many events, particularly those relating to characters and their interactions, that can raise or lower the country's tyranny level. Additionally, country's tyranny enables certain events, and one might find them as impactful as its direct effects.

The actions and interactions that cost or change the national Tyranny value are listed below by their base cost, which can be changed by various interaction-specific modifiers:

Tyranny is primarily lost through [![Image 359: Monthly tyranny.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_tyranny.png/28px-Monthly_tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Monthly_tyranny.png)**Monthly Tyranny** change, which has a base reduction of **-0.02** Tyranny per month. This can be increased and changed by different modifiers, such as certain [![Image 360: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") oratory inventions, various deity and omen bonuses, ruler attributes and traits, and more; sources of monthly tyranny change are listed below:

| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Base | Base | **-0.02** |

| [Ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") | [![Image 408: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") Charisma | **-0.01** per point |
| [![Image 409: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") Popularity | **-0.001** per point above 50 |
| [![Image 410: Merciful](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/20/Merciful.png/28px-Merciful.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Merciful")[Merciful](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) | * **-0.05** |
| [![Image 411: Forgiving](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e3/Forgiving.png/28px-Forgiving.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Forgiving")[Forgiving](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) |

| [![Image 413: Cruel](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Cruel.png/28px-Cruel.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Cruel")[Cruel](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Personality_traits "Characters") (Ruler Trait) | **+0.02** |
| [![Image 414: Deify ruler cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png/28px-Deify_ruler_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Apotheosis "Apotheosis") Apotheosis | **+0.01** per [Deified Ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Apotheosis "Religion") in Pantheon |
| [Heritage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heritage "Heritage") | [![Image 415: Flag of Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Athens.png/20px-Athens.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens") Athenian Heritage ([Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens")) | **-0.02** |

While nominally a negative modifier, [![Image 416: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny is generally not particularly problematic at lower levels and it is not very important to keep it low unless the nation is already experiencing issues with [![Image 417: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty. The [![Image 418: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") invoke devotio action in particular can be used to essentially gain [![Image 419: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") in return for reducing [![Image 420: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion") war exhaustion, effectively converting [![Image 421: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") population unhappiness (and corresponding [![Image 422: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest) into [![Image 423: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty, which is generally easier to deal with, especially if the country does not have much other tyranny to begin with.

It is worth noting that since Tyranny decay does not scale according to its current value and there are no easy ways to quickly lower it, high Tyranny can be problematic if the state is not prepared, especially if the ruler has low [![Image 424: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") Charisma and so the state has low base tyranny decay - if this becomes the case, the loyalty of powerful characters needs to be assured at all costs, or the country could easily spiral into [![Image 425: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war. The significant [![Image 426: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty and [![Image 427: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold maluses together can be dangerous if not kept under control by other means, as even a single disloyal family head might be enough to trigger a civil war. [![Image 428: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") Republics in particular need to pay close attention to tyranny levels, as tyranny reduces [![Image 429: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") Democrat support which can be highly problematic if they make up a significant portion of the Senate - especially if that in turn lowers overall [![Image 430: Senate approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Senate approval") senate support to the point where taking any actions accrues even more tyranny. On the flip side, tyranny pleases the [![Image 431: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs, making high tyranny an option to consider if they make up a significant portion of the Senate.

In the later game, if and when the player has accumulated enough [![Image 432: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty bonuses to handle the penalties, high Tyranny is much more useful and players can take full advantage of its bonuses, particularly its effects on [![Image 433: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion change. In conjunction with further reductions from other sources and growing Aggressive Expansion Impact bonuses, a country with high Tyranny can handle much larger conquests and [![Image 434: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion values in the lategame as opposed to what is feasible early on. The [![Image 435: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National Slave Output can also be quite useful once all the loyalty problems can be properly dealt with, making for a powerful tyrannical empire.

### War Exhaustion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: War Exhaustion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: War Exhaustion")]

[![Image 436: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:War_exhaustion.png)**War exhaustion** represents anger and discontent in the general population from the human and economic cost of war. Raising a region's [![Image 437: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies costs [![Image 438: War exhaustion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8c/War_exhaustion.png/28px-War_exhaustion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_exhaustion "War exhaustion")**0.5** war exhaustion, and keeping levies raised for more than **6** months will give an additional monthly war exhaustion penalty that increases the longer the levy is raised. Casualties, blockades, and enemy occupation can also contribute significantly to increasing a country's war exhaustion.

The effects of war exhaustion can be significant, decreasing [![Image 439: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of all pops and impacting [![Image 440: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") ruler popularity, but also increasing [![Image 441: Military experience.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Military_experience.png/28px-Military_experience.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_experience "Military experience") military experience gain and so can some amount of war exhaustion can be useful if the country is sufficiently stable. High War Exhaustion will increase the AI's willingness to sign a more favourable peace, and conversely may force a player to sign a less favourable peace treaty rather than let their nation be consumed by unrest. War Exhaustion ticks down slowly while at peace and based on the ruler's current [![Image 442: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal, which can be increased through various omens, laws, and ideas. If War Exhaustion becomes too high, the [![Image 443: Invoke devotio.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c2/Invoke_devotio.png/28px-Invoke_devotio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Invoke_devotio "Invoke devotio") Invoke Devotio action in the religion panel can be taken to increase War Exhaustion decay at the cost of [![Image 444: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") Tyranny. War exhaustion generally has a value between **0** and **20**, but can rise to **30** for the attacker in an offensive war.

Each point of war exhaustion has the following effects:

## State rank[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: State rank") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: State rank")]

A country's **rank** is a measure of how large and influential a particular state is, based largely on the number of [territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") that it owns. Country rank is one of the primary determinants of the number of major families/clan chiefs, [![Image 445: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range, and [![Image 446: Integrate speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/31/Integrate_speed.png/28px-Integrate_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Integration_speed "Integration speed") integration speed as well as significantly impacting [![Image 447: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence generation, [![Image 448: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness, and [![Image 449: Warscore cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Warscore_cost.png/28px-Warscore_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_score_cost "War score cost") warscore cost but higher ranked nations are also more vulnerable to civil war and rebellions as they have lower [![Image 450: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/General "General loyalty")[![Image 451: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Admiral "Admiral loyalty")[![Image 452: Governor loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Monthly_office_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_office_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor loyalty") office loyalty as well as a [![Image 453: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty malus and a lower [![Image 454: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold") civil war threshold.

* [![Image 485: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") Subject nations are additionally capped at [![Image 486: Country rank 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Country_rank_3.png/28px-Country_rank_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Local_power "Local power") local power status, regardless of how big they get.

Country rank is also particularly important for diplomacy, as certain diplomatic actions are available only to countries of a certain rank.

## Maximum civilization value[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Maximum civilization value") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Maximum civilization value")]

_Main article: [Civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization\_value "Civilization value")_

Every country has a base [![Image 542: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**country civilization level**, visible on the ![Image 543: Overview](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f1/Menu_overview.png/28px-Menu_overview.png)[Nation Overview](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Beginner%27s_guide#Interacting_with_Your_Empire "Beginner's guide") panel, that determines the baseline maximum possible civilization level of every territory that the country owns. This value can be increased by a number of different modifiers, such as country's [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") type, positive [![Image 544: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization for [tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") (up to **+10%** at [![Image 545: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") 100), [![Image 546: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")[Oratory Advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") (**+0.5%** per level), the national idea [![Image 547: Idea state religion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/32/Idea_state_religion.png/28px-Idea_state_religion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Idea_state_religion.png)[State Religion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas#Religious "National ideas") (**+5%**), the [![Image 548: Gemstones](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/70/Goods_gems.png/28px-Goods_gems.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Gemstones "Gemstones") gemstones and [![Image 549: Glass](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Goods_glass.png/28px-Goods_glass.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Glass "Glass") glass capital surplus bonuses (**+5%** each), and various other more specific modifiers.

Note that the country civilization level is only the baseline maximum civilization; individual province and territory modifiers such as [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings"), [treasures](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treasure "Treasure"), [terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain "Terrain"), special modifiers, and capital bonuses awarded when creating a [formable nation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations") can push the maximum civilization value of any individual territory higher than the country civilization value, often much higher for large, built up cities.

# Loyalty

From Imperator Wiki

[Jump to navigation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Loyalty#mw-head)[Jump to search](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Loyalty#searchInput)

**Loyalty** can refer to one of three related mechanics:

*   [![Image 2: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")[Character loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty"), how loyal a particular [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character") is toward the government, which at low levels can lead to [![Image 3: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil wars
*   [![Image 4: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")[Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_loyalty "Province loyalty"), how loyal a particular [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") is towards its owner, which at low levels can lead to [![Image 5: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion") rebellions
*   [![Image 6: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty")[Cohort loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty"), the personal loyalty of a cohort towards a particular character beyond their usual commander, which can have a significant impact on [![Image 7: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base

[![Image 8: Disambig grey.png](https://central.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b2/Disambig_grey.png/30px-Disambig_grey.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Disambig_grey.png)This disambiguation page lists articles associated with the same title. 

If an [internal link](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Special:WhatLinksHere/Loyalty "Special:WhatLinksHere/Loyalty") led you here, you may wish to change the link to point directly to the intended article.

## Cookies and targeting

To give you a better and more personal experience we use cookies and third-party tracking tools. We process personal data such as browser information and IP adress for analysing our website and e-commerce performance. To provide you with relevant ads and for measuring our marketing efforts we also share data with 3rd party advertising providers such as Google and Meta. By clicking OK you agree to cookies and targeting.[Privacy policy](https://legal.paradoxplaza.com/)

## Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. 

[Privacy policy](http://legal.paradoxplaza.com/privacy?locale=en)

### Manage Consent Preferences

#### Targeting Cookies

- [x] Targeting Cookies 

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Cookies Details‎

#### Performance Cookies

- [x] Performance Cookies 

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

Cookies Details‎

#### Functional Cookies

- [x] Functional Cookies 

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

Cookies Details‎

#### Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

# Senate
============================================================
Title: Government - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/AKy1Ngodf4I/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator Tutorial - Governments - pt 6

Load video

Imperator: Rome tutorial #6 - Governments.

[![Image 2: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") The **government** of a [country](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country "Country") defines its basic political framework and power structure, including how the [![Image 3: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler of the country is selected, the possible [![Image 4: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws that can be enacted, the amount and category of [national ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas") that can be adopted, the available [![Image 5: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") offices that characters can be appointed to, and more. Each government form has its own set of modifiers and characteristics and falls into one of three basic types - **republic**, **monarchy**, or **tribe**, each with its own set of distinctive mechanics. Over the course of the game, it is possible to change the government of the country from one form to another by [decision](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions") or [mission task](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions").

## Republics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Republics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Republics")]

[![Image 6](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e7/Government_republic_screen.png/500px-Government_republic_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Government_republic_screen.png)

The government interface for republics

**Republics** are defined first and foremost by the sharing of power between its citizens or upper class, depending on the type. Rulers are chosen by election based on a character's popularity and prominence and serve only for a fixed, usually relatively short term, meaning that republics will tend to cycle through different characters quite quickly and are not quite as involved in playing dynastic games. Republics are also marked by the presence of a Senate whose approval is required for most government actions, made up of a number of factions who may give various bonuses but also seek to advance their goals and interests.

Republican governments are most common among the Greek city-states as well as the Italic and Punic realms of the Western Mediterranean, but can also be found in the emporiums of the Horn of Africa and the Persian Gulf as well as the warrior confederations of Punjab.

### Republican governments[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Republican governments") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Republican governments")]

Unless specified otherwise, all Republics have the following base characteristics:

| Republic type | Idea slots | Base modifiers | Government bonus | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Aristocratic Republic | [![Image 7: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 2 Military, [![Image 8: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | **Has co-ruler** | * [![Image 9: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") Character loyalty: **+5** * [![Image 10: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National freeman happiness: **+8%** | _Whilst outwardly democratic, an Aristocratic Republic limits leadership to those who enjoy high status._ |
| Democratic Republic | [![Image 11: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 12: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 13: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | [![Image 14: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**National [civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Civilization_Value "Population"):****+15%**, **Minimum election age 25** | * [![Image 15: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") National freeman happiness: **+8%** | _This system of government gives all eligible citizens a voice, through the election of representatives to a senate, or council._ |
| Oligarchic Republic | [![Image 16: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 17: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 2 Oratory | **Minimum election age 40** | * [![Image 18: Monthly wage modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/93/Monthly_wage_modifier.png/28px-Monthly_wage_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wage "Wage") Monthly wages: **−10%** * [![Image 19: Pop citizen happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") National citizen happiness: **+8%** | _An Oligarchic Republic restricts positions of power to a wealthy or powerful minority._ |
| Plutocratic Republic | [![Image 20: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 2 Civic, [![Image 21: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 22: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") Capital import routes: **+1** * [![Image 23: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") National commerce income: **+10%** | _Primarily concerned with the acquisition of wealth, Plutocratic Republics can be found ruling many of the coastal City States throughout the world._ |
| Theocratic Republic | [![Image 24: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 25: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 2 Religious | (default) | * [![Image 26: Omen.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Omen.png/28px-Omen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Omens "Religion") Omen power: **+15%** * [![Image 27: State religion happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d9/Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_religion_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#State_religion_happiness "State religion happiness") State religion happiness: **+10%** | _The priesthood will enshrine democracy into the very tenets of our faith!_ |
| Athenian Republic | [![Image 28: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 29: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 30: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory, [![Image 31: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | [![Image 32: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**National [civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Civilization_Value "Population"):****+25%**, **Minimum election age 25** | * [![Image 33: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Changing_Laws "Enact law cost") Enact law cost: **-25%** * [![Image 34: Pop citizen happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") National citizen happiness: **+8%** | _The Athenian republic is known far and wide for its integrity and strength._ |

### The Senate[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Senate") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Senate")]

The **Senate** (which may go by a number of other names, such as the **Adirim**, **Ekklesia**, **Gerousia**, **Sangha**, or **Assembly**) consists of **100** seats with each seat belonging to one of **three** factions. Every adult character in a republic is part of one of the republic's factions based on which faction they have the highest [conviction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Conviction "Conviction") for, and each faction has a [leader](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Party_leader "Party leader"), generally the most [![Image 35: Prominence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Prominence.png/28px-Prominence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Prominence "Prominence") prominent character that supports that faction. Each character in a faction contributes a certain amount of [![Image 36: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence")**senatorial influence**, calculated as each character's [![Image 37: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base adjusted by their [![Image 38: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence")**senate influence** modifier. The amount of seats each faction has, their [![Image 39: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control")**senate control**, which determines how strong and influential the faction, will then gradually drift over time to the proportion of the total [![Image 40: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence in the country that the faction members hold, meaning that the strength of factions will swing significantly based on the power of its membership. Notably, [![Image 41: Senate influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fe/Senate_influence.png/28px-Senate_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate influence") senate influence is significantly decreased for [![Image 42: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commanders and [![Image 43: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governors, which means that assigning those offices to powerful members of a faction can be a good way to significantly decrease the power of a disapproving faction and swing the Senate in the government's favour.

#### Ruler elections[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ruler elections") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ruler elections")]

When the ruler (and co-ruler) of a republic dies or finishes their term, his or her successor is elected by the Senate, with the eligible character with the highest [![Image 44: Succession](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession")**succession support** becoming the new ruler (and the eligible character with the second highest [![Image 45: Succession](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Ruler "Succession") succession support elected co-ruler, for government types that have that position).

Every character is supported by a certain number of seats from each faction, which is a proportion of the [![Image 46: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control") total number of seats the faction holds in the Senate calculated according to the following factors:

A character's total succession support is then the sum of the seats that support them across all three factions. As factions strongly favour their faction members, especially their leaders, and the total succession support a faction gives depends on their [![Image 47: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control") number of seats, factions with stronger control in the Senate will tend have more of their faction members elected as [![Image 48: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler. Electing faction member as the [![Image 49: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") gives a particular countrywide bonus depending on the faction, but the ruling faction will also expect to have its [objectives](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate#Faction_objectives) fulfilled during the term, which may have severe consequences if their agenda is not passed.

#### Senate approval[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Senate approval") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Senate approval")]

Every faction has an [![Image 50: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**approval** value that represents how much support there is within the faction for the government's current policies. Every character in a faction will get [![Image 51: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**+0.1** loyalty for every point of approval above **50**, and [![Image 52: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**-0.3** loyalty for every point of approval below **50**. Faction approval will change each month according to a wide variety of factors, different for each faction, as well as the [![Image 53: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval")**party approval** modifier. Many actions and [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), as well as completing [![Image 54: Faction objective](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Political_parties.png/28px-Political_parties.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Faction_objectives "Faction objective") faction objectives, can also immediately change faction approval.

The overall [![Image 55: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**Support in the Senate** is sum of all faction approvals weighted by their [![Image 56: Senate control](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Senate_seat.png/28px-Senate_seat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Senate control") number of controlled seats, and determines how many seats will vote in favour of the government's actions. A faction's support will slowly change from month to month depending on a wide variety of factors differing for each faction, and is also impacted by changing [![Image 57: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, many [character interactions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_interaction "Character interaction"), and certain [diplomatic actions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy "Diplomacy").

There are three different categories of actions which require senate approval, largely the same as those that impact approval:

*   Changing [![Image 58: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")
*   Many character interactions, such as banishing, imprisoning, etc.
*   All diplomatic interactions with other nations

The senate will completely block an action if fewer than [![Image 59: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**30** senate seats support it, and will pass it without issue if there is at least [![Image 60: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**51** Senate support. If between 30 and 51 senators back the action, it can be forced through at the cost of around [![Image 61: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**+0.67** tyranny for each point of support less than 51 (to a maximum of [![Image 62: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**14** tyranny), which can quickly become debilitatingly high if actions are forced through too often.

#### Faction objectives[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Faction objectives") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Faction objectives")]

Every ruler term, each faction will have its own [![Image 63: Faction objective](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/aa/Political_parties.png/28px-Political_parties.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Faction_objectives "Faction objective")**objective** or **agenda** that it wishes to have passed. These objectives vary widely from changing [![Image 64: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, appointing certain characters to [![Image 65: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") office, declaring [![Image 66: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, developing certain [provinces](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"), increasing [![Image 67: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability, and more. Completing a faction's objectives is generally the fastest way to gain [![Image 68: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") Senate approval, though they may be costly to do.

If a faction's objective is completed, it will gain either [![Image 69: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**10** or **20** approval by the faction (beyond any approval gained from making the actions required for the objective), after which the faction will have no objective until the next ruler election. The ruling faction will expect to have its objectives completed during while in government, and if their objective has not yet been met and the country has at least [![Image 70: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**50** Senate approval they will eventually pass their demands through the Senate anyways, forcing the country to choose between accepting the fulfillment of the objective or using the **consular veto** to stop it and lose [![Image 71: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**50** approval from the ruling faction. Either way, once the faction's agenda is forced it will be considered either completed or aborted and the faction will have no objective until the next election.

All possible objectives and the factions that can have them are listed in the table below.

| Objective | [![Image 72: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs | [![Image 73: Party traditionalist.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4a/Party_traditionalist.png/28px-Party_traditionalist.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traditionalists "Traditionalists") Traditionalists | [![Image 74: Party democratic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Party_democratic.png/28px-Party_democratic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Democrats "Democrats") Democrats | [![Image 75: Party optimates.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/37/Party_optimates.png/28px-Party_optimates.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Optimates "Optimates") Optimates | [![Image 76: Party boni.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0a/Party_boni.png/28px-Party_boni.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Boni "Boni") Boni | [![Image 77: Party populares.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Party_populares.png/28px-Party_populares.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Populares "Populares") Populares | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 78: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") Declare War | [![Image 79: Yes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/77/Yes.png/28px-Yes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Yes.png "Yes") | [![Image 80: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | [![Image 81: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | [![Image 82: Yes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/77/Yes.png/28px-Yes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Yes.png "Yes") | [![Image 83: No](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/No.png/28px-No.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:No.png "No") | [![Image 84: Yes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/77/Yes.png/28px-Yes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Yes.png "Yes") | Will only fire for a country within 1 [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank") of the current country that is not currently [![Image 85: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") allied, or has a [![Image 86: Truce.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Truce.png/28px-Truce.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Truce "Truce") truce. |

#### General factions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: General factions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: General factions")]

These factions are present in all countries except for [![Image 267: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome"), who has its own special set.

### Dictators[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Dictators") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Dictators")]

In times of [![Image 351: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, a republic can appoint their ruler as a temporary **dictator** in order to deal with the expediencies of war more quickly and efficiently without having to seek Senatorial approval for their actions. Using the **Appoint Dictator** character interaction requires at least [![Image 352: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval")**75** Senate support and costs [![Image 353: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**10** tyranny, giving **+5%** Morale of Armies and [![Image 354: Succession](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/24/Election_term.png/28px-Election_term.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Election term duration")**+1** Election Term Duration as well as bypassing the need for Senate approval while the dictatorship is active.

When the war or the ruler's term ends, the dictator is expected to return his or her powers and restore control to the Senate. However, depending on the dictator's faction, [![Image 355: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trait "Trait"), and the country's size, the dictator may decide to retain their powers anyways and extend their dictatorship and ruler term beyond its normal limit, with temporary penalties to [![Image 356: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty and [![Image 357: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny. As the extended dictatorship proceeds, the dictator will eventually push for the enacting the [![Image 358: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") Princeps Civitas or [![Image 359: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") Lifetime Elections law, essentially making them dictator for life if accepted. If an extended dictatorship is becoming problematic, the dictator can be forced to step down with the **Return Dictatorship** character interaction if their [![Image 360: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity falls low enough or the country's [![Image 361: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability is high enough at the cost of [![Image 362: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**25** stability. Alternatively, the **Murder Dictator** interaction can be used to remove an illegal dictator at any time, with the same [![Image 363: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny and [![Image 364: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion costs as a usual assassination plus an additional cost of [![Image 365: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability")**30** stability.

While the republic is under a dictatorship - whether legally granted or illegally extended - the [dictatorship invention tree](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions#Dictatorship_tree "Oratory inventions") becomes available, allowing the country to take [![Image 366: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions that entrench the power of the dictator by revising the constitution, extending their term, and finally instituting a hereditary line of succession to reform the republic into a [monarchical](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy")**Dictatorship**, either by pushing through reforms with sufficient [![Image 367: Party oligarchic.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8a/Party_oligarchic.png/28px-Party_oligarchic.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oligarchs "Oligarchs") Oligarchs or [![Image 368: Party populares.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Party_populares.png/28px-Party_populares.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Populares "Populares") Populares support or forcing through demands with a [![Image 369: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war.

### Offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Offices")]

_Main article: [Position#Republic\_offices](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Republic\_offices "Position")_

In addition to the usual three offices, [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") have offices for [![Image 370: Party approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/Senate_approval_modifier.png/28px-Senate_approval_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senate "Party approval") party approval, [![Image 371: Stability cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Stability_cost_modifier.png/28px-Stability_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sacrifice_to_the_Gods "Sacrifice to the Gods") divine sacrifice cost, and [![Image 372: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") monthly civilization change.

| Office | Attribute | State Modifier (per effective attribute) | Character Modifiers | Description |

### Interactions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Interactions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Interactions")]

Republics can empower any of their three factions for a certain bonus at the cost of [![Image 433: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence and either [![Image 434: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny, [![Image 435: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold, or [![Image 436: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") as well as increasing that faction's [![Image 437: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") approval at the expense of the other factions, but this will also decrease the [![Image 438: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness of a certain pop class or group. Depending on the law enacted in the Legislative Body/Assembly Endorsement category, there are may also be available interactions to get improve governor attributes or get claims on neighbouring states.

## Monarchies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Monarchies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Monarchies")]

[![Image 525](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Government_monarchy_screen.png/500px-Government_monarchy_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Government_monarchy_screen.png)

The government interface for monarchies

In a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), authority lies entirely in the hands of the ruler who controls the government and can generally do as he or she wishes, so long as the rest of the realm remains loyal. Monarchs sit for life with a consort to assist them and succession runs entirely along familial lines, with the throne inherited from parent to child or from one sibling to the next depending on the exact succession law. This concentration of power in a single person, however, means that much more attention must be paid to the legitimacy of the current ruler as the country sees it as well as fending off the threats of pretenders and claimants who would drive the realm into civil war in order to claim the throne for themselves.

Monarchies are common throughout the east, from the many realms of the Diadochi to the Indian dynasties spanning its north and south, as well as the smaller states of Sogdiana and the Tarim Basin and the ancient kingdoms of Nubia and Ethiopia. Some, such as [![Image 526: Flag of Sparta](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Sparta.png/20px-Sparta.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sparta "Sparta")[Sparta](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sparta "Sparta") or [![Image 527: Flag of Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c0/Syracusae.png/20px-Syracusae.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae")[Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae"), can also be found further to the west among the more predominant republics of the region.

### Monarchy governments[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Monarchy governments") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Government&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Monarchy governments")]

Unless specified otherwise, all monarchies have the following characteristics:

| Monarchy type | Idea slots | Base modifiers | Government bonus | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Autocratic Monarchy | [![Image 528: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 529: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") 1 Civic, [![Image 530: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religious_inventions "Religious inventions") 1 Religious | [![Image 531: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**National [civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Civilization_Value "Population"):****+15%** | [![Image 532: Slave output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Pop_slaves_output.png/28px-Pop_slaves_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Slave output") National slave output: **+12%** | _Conferring absolute power, the title of an Autocratic Monarchy is hereditary._ |
| Aristocratic Monarchy | [![Image 533: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 1 Military, [![Image 534: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 2 Oratory | (default) | * [![Image 535: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") Monthly tyranny: **−0.05** * [![Image 536: Pop citizen happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") National citizen happiness: **+8%** * [![Image 537: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") Desired freemen ratio in cities: **+10%** | _Although ruled by a hereditary monarch, power in an Aristocratic Monarchy is often conferred upon members of the nobility._ |
| Stratocratic Monarchy | [![Image 538: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") 2 Military, [![Image 539: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") 1 Oratory | [![Image 540: Freeman ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a6/Pop_freemen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_freemen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Freeman ratio") Desired freemen ratio in cities: **+10%** | * [![Image 541: Freeman output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/04/Pop_freemen_output.png/28px-Pop_freemen_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_output "Freeman output") National freemen output: **+6%** * [![Image 542: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") Character loyalty: **+5** | _The military is integral to a functioning stratocratic monarchy.
