============================================================
# Civil_war
============================================================
Title: Civil war - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") While a major revolt is not very different from having a large area revolt in other Paradox games, **civil wars** are dramatically different. A particularly weak or [![Image 2: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")[tyrannical](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tyranny "Tyranny") ruler can see a significant portion of their characters, armies, and provinces revolt against in them in the name of overthrowing the government.

## Triggers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Triggers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Triggers")]

The countdown to a civil war will begin when the [![Image 3: Disloyal power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3c/Disloyal_power_base.png/28px-Disloyal_power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Disloyal power base") power base of all disloyal characters as a share of the country's total [![Image 4: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base is greater than the state's current [![Image 5: Civil war threshold](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f9/Civil_war_threshold.png/28px-Civil_war_threshold.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war threshold")**Civil War Threshold**. The base threshold is **25%**, which is decreased by [![Image 6: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny and higher [country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank") and can also be affected by other factors. Larger nations therefore tend to be more prone to civil wars, as they have more powerful characters to handle and need a smaller proportion of them to be disloyal before a civil war can break out. This timer ticks up every month, and if the situation is not brought under control, the civil war will break out after **40** months. During this time, there will be an alert notifying the player that attention should be given to the loyalty of powerful characters immediately. Of course, there are also alerts as soon as a single province or a general becomes disloyal.

It is also possible for civil wars to be triggered outside of the usual civil war counter, most notably as one of the possible outcomes when failing to successfully convict when [bringing a powerful character to trial](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Position_holder_interactions "Characters"). There are also a number of unique mission and event chains that can trigger civil wars , such as with the invasion of Magos in the [![Image 7: Flag of Cyrenaican Opposition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Cyrenaican_Opposition.png/20px-Cyrenaican_Opposition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cyrenaican_Opposition "Cyrenaican Opposition")[Cyrenaican Opposition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cyrenaican_Opposition "Cyrenaican Opposition"), the overthrow of the [![Image 8: Flag of Syracusan Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Placeholder_Flag.png/20px-Placeholder_Flag.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusan_Tyranny "Syracusan Tyranny")[Syracusan Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusan_Tyranny "Syracusan Tyranny"), and the revolt of the [![Image 9: Flag of Satrap Coalition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3e/Satrap_Coalition.png/20px-Satrap_Coalition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Satrap_Coalition "Satrap Coalition")[Satrap Coalition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Satrap_Coalition "Satrap Coalition") in the [Diadochi missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diadochi_missions "Diadochi missions").

### Character loyalty[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Character loyalty") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Character loyalty")]

[![Image 10: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") A character's **loyalty** to the government is one of their most critical aspects and is important to keep in mind when allocating [![Image 11: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") positions and making decisions that affect the loyalty of characters, particularly of those with a large [![Image 12: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base. When the loyalty of a character drops below **33**, that character is considered **disloyal** and will begin to actively go against the government, stirring up discontent in other characters, refusing to abandon their positions, armies, or governorships, and generally ignoring any orders from above. If enough powerful characters are disloyal, they will form a bloc to launch a civil war.

There are a large number of factors that affect a character's loyalty, with [![Image 13: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base being one of the most important. Powerful characters - especially [heads of families](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") - are notable as being both the hardest to keep pleased yet the most dangerous if they become disloyal. Characters of [![Image 14: Scorned family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Scorned_family.png/28px-Scorned_family.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Position#Mechanics "Scorned family") scorned families also have a significant loyalty malus, while high [![Image 15: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny will significantly decrease loyalty across all characters. [Monarchies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") must in particular contend with [![Image 16: Leader pretender.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/11/Leader_pretender.png/28px-Leader_pretender.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pretender "Pretender") pretenders, particularly if they have a large amount of support (which further decreases their loyalty), while in [republics](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") members of [factions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Faction "Faction") with [![Image 17: Faction approval](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Senate_approval.png/28px-Senate_approval.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government#Senate_approval "Faction approval") low approval will also have a significant malus. Certain actions such as revoking [![Image 18: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") positions and [![Image 19: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings will also anger characters, if temporarily. However, there are also a large number of ways to increase a character's loyalty temporarily or permanently, such as by making [friends](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Friend "Friend"), granting a [![Image 20: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") position or [![Image 21: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holding, giving bribes and free hands, resolving their grievances (if any), and more. A large variety of event options can also temporarily affect loyalty either way. Over the course of the game cumulative loyalty bonuses from [![Image 22: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions, [![Image 23: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") laws, [national ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_idea "National idea"), and other permanent modifiers will generally tend to increase loyalty overall.

Generally, it is most important to ensure the loyalty of powerful characters by any means possible, whether by plying them with flattery and bribes or choosing event options to keep them content even at the expense of something or someone else. At the same time, [![Image 24: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base should generally be divided up such that no character becomes too powerful and becomes impossible to keep loyal, which for instance means being wary of granting too large and army or governorship to a character that already has a large [![Image 25: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base (especially heads of family) or splitting up armies that take up too much of the country's total military, so that its [![Image 26: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander is not too powerful. If a character's power base is mostly tied to the position they hold (e.g. for governors and commanders), it can sometimes be simpler just to replace them with a more loyal character. Disloyal characters that have a low power base do not pose much of a threat and can generally be ignored, though there is always a chance that they might eventually at some point inherit or come into a position of power.

### Cohort loyalty[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cohort loyalty") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cohort loyalty")]

[![Image 27: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") When led by a [![Image 28: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander, every cohort in an army has a monthly chance to become **loyal** to their commander, based on the total **Cohort Loyalty Gain Chance** modifiers of the commander, army, and country. The total **chance of cohort loyalty** is a base of **+0.50%** per month and is increased by the commander's [![Image 29: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") charisma, [![Image 30: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity") popularity, and [![Image 31: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") wealth, by many [![Image 32: Phalera helmetman.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/14/Phalera_helmetman.png/28px-Phalera_helmetman.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Distinction "Distinction") distinctions, and when [![Image 33: Drill army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Ability_drill_army.png/28px-Ability_drill_army.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Toggles "Drill army") drilling the army, but is reduced to **10%** of its normal value when not in battle. Cohort loyalty can also be gained when [sacking cities in a war](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Warfare_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Warfare events (page does not exist)").

When a cohort in a [![Image 34: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion is loyal to a character, the country pays only **66%** of its normal maintenance for it, as the cohort's sponsor will pay part of the unit's upkeep him or herself. A cohort that is loyal to a character also gets a [![Image 35: Discipline.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/59/Discipline.png/28px-Discipline.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Discipline "Discipline")**+10%** discipline modifier. Loyal cohorts, however, always provide [![Image 36: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base to the character they are loyal to - significantly more than cohorts that are not loyal to any particular character - and will join their sponsor's side in any [![Image 37: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil wars, rather than following their commander, even if the two are different - and even if the character they are loyal to is no longer a commander at all.

Cohort loyalty is generally of little consequence for [![Image 38: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies, as levied armies are usually not raised long enough for a significant portion of their cohorts to become loyal to their commander, and all cohort loyalty is lost once a levy is disbanded at the end of a war. Instead, cohort loyalty is primarily relevant in [![Image 39: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions, where cohorts exist semi-permanently and will be loyal to their sponsor as long as he or she lives. A particularly long-lasting and/or successful commander that has gained the loyalty of many of their cohorts will therefore continue to keep the [![Image 40: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base associated with those cohorts even if dismissed, which can make the character quite powerful and difficult - but important - to appease. Disbanding a loyal cohort in a [![Image 41: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion will not completely remove the character's influence but instead converts the cohort into **loyal veterans** that continue to provide a reduced amount of [![Image 42: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base (even though they are no longer part of the army) and can be raised again if their sponsor rebels as part of a [![Image 43: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war.

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

When the civil war begins, a new dynamic tag is created with all disloyal characters as well as their [friends](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Friend "Friend") and [immediate family members](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Relatives "Characters") joining the revolt, bringing along all held [![Image 44: Position](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governorships and [![Image 45: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings as well as commanded [![Image 46: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") armies. [![Image 47: Loyalty gain chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/07/Loyalty_gain_chance.png/28px-Loyalty_gain_chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cohort_loyalty "Cohort loyalty") loyal cohorts, and loyal veterans. [![Image 48: Subject loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Subject_loyalty.png/28px-Subject_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty") Disloyal subjects may also side with the civil war revolter, who will additionally start new wars with any nation that the parent country was already at war with. Civil war tags can be recognized by their name, which will always be the adjective of their parent nation followed by "**Revolt**". The flag will normally be randomly generated, but a few major states have pre-scripted flags for their civil war revolters that reflect an inversion of their normal flags:

*   [![Image 49: Flag of Roman Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/82/Roman_Revolt.png/20px-Roman_Revolt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Roman_Revolt "Roman Revolt")[Roman Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Roman_Revolt "Roman Revolt")
*   [![Image 50: Flag of Carthaginian Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Carthaginian_Revolt.png/20px-Carthaginian_Revolt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthaginian_Revolt "Carthaginian Revolt")[Carthaginian Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthaginian_Revolt "Carthaginian Revolt")
*   [![Image 51: Flag of Seleukid Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7f/Seleukid_Revolt.png/20px-Seleukid_Revolt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Seleukid_Revolt "Seleukid Revolt")[Seleukid Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Seleukid_Revolt "Seleukid Revolt")
*   [![Image 52: Flag of Antigonid Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bc/Antigonid_Revolt.png/20px-Antigonid_Revolt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Revolt "Antigonid Revolt")[Antigonid Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Revolt "Antigonid Revolt")
*   [![Image 53: Flag of Egyptian Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Egyptian_Revolt.png/20px-Egyptian_Revolt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Egyptian_Revolt "Egyptian Revolt")[Egyptian Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Egyptian_Revolt "Egyptian Revolt")
*   [![Image 54: Flag of Macedonian Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Macedonian_Revolt.png/20px-Macedonian_Revolt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Macedonian_Revolt "Macedonian Revolt")[Macedonian Revolt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Macedonian_Revolt "Macedonian Revolt")

Additionally, certain event/mission-triggered civil wars will also have a unique rebellion name.

A civil war is a war-to-the-death, where territories automatically switch owners when they are occupied, and losing a civil war is considered a game over like any other annexation. Peace deals cannot be made during civil wars; the only way to end them is to completely destroy the other side, typically by destroying or decisively defeating all of their armies and taking most to all of their territories. Generally, unless the country is very small, civil wars are a large drain on time and resources and should generally be avoided at all costs; equally, a period of civil war is a great time to strike at a now-weakened rival or even a significantly larger power that under normal circumstances would be far too strong to take on.

When a civil war is won, the victorious side will be able to choose what to do with all the characters that sided against them. They can choose to either spare the traitors - who will still permanently carry a stain of dishonour - and regain some of the [![Image 55: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower lost from the war, or have all the traitors executed and get a large [![Image 56: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") character loyalty and [![Image 57: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty boost at the expense of [![Image 58: Integrated culture happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png/28px-Happiness_for_same_culture_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture#Integrated_culture "Integrated culture happiness") integrated culture happiness (as well as losing all the characters who rebelled).

## Strategies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Strategies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Civil_war&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Strategies")]

The following thread in Paradox Forum discusses more strategies suggested by players to deal with characters' loyalty leading to civil war:

*   **[forum:1451459](https://forum.paradoxplaza.com/forum/index.php?threads/1451459 "forum:1451459")**


============================================================
# Province
============================================================
Title: Province - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

A **province** is an administrative division of land in _Imperator: Rome_, formed by groupings of **[territories](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories")** and themselves grouped into **[regions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region")**. Ingame, many important mechanics such as [![Image 1: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food, [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade"), and [![Image 2: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") territorial loyalty are handled at the provincial level, as well as certain diplomatic actions such as fabricating [claims](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim "Claim") and [peace term demands](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare#Peace_Terms "Warfare").

## Provincial loyalty[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Provincial loyalty") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Provincial loyalty")]

Every province has a [![Image 3: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**province loyalty** value, representing the local level of support for the central government and the willingness of the province to follow its orders. Disloyal provinces will refuse to follow orders from the capital and may eventually revolt in a [rebellion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion") or join a [![Image 4: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war - managing the loyalty of provinces is one of the most important aspects of running an empire.

Province loyalty changes monthly depending on the sum of all province loyalty modifiers. [![Image 5: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**Territory Provincial Loyalty** modifiers affect individual territories and are scaled to the territory's [![Image 6: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") relative population in the province before being applied to the territory's province's loyalty, [![Image 7: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**Local Provincial Loyalty** modifiers are applied to provinces and affecting its loyalty directly, and [![Image 8: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**Provincial Loyalty** modifiers affect all owned provinces in the country.

[![Image 9: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") Unrest - itself driven largely by [![Image 10: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") the happiness of the population - is the main factor that decreases [![Image 11: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty, giving [![Image 12: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**-0.08** territory provincial loyalty for every point of [![Image 13: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest. As territory provincial loyalty is scaled to the share of the province's [![Image 14: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population in the territory, large cities will therefore have a much greater effect on province loyalty than lightly populated settlements. Governor [![Image 15: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption (**-0.25** at [![Image 16: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**100** corruption) as well as lacking a governor to administer the region altogether (**-0.35**) will also give a significant malus to [![Image 17: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty. The main way to reduce province loyalty loss is by tackling the source, such as building [![Image 18: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") forts or increasing [![Image 19: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") pop happiness to reduce [![Image 20: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest or by replacing corrupt governors.

If dealing with the source of loyalty loss is not enough, monthly [![Image 21: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty can also be increased by:

In addition to its monthly loyalty change effect, the [![Image 22: Anabasis](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e0/Ability_anabasis.png/28px-Ability_anabasis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unit_ability "Anabasis") Anabasis unit ability will also immediately increase the province's loyalty by **5**.

Once a province's loyalty drops below **33**, the province is considered disloyal and will refuse follow orders from the central government. Territories in disloyal provinces cannot be interacted with, blocking building construction and pop movement. Changing [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy"), however, is still allowed (as long as the governor is still loyal).

If a [![Image 23: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province's loyalty drops all the way down to **0**, it will immediately [![Image 24: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion")[rebel](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion "Rebellion"). A new state will be formed from the province (or possibly a previously existing one that has been annexed), and it will either declare a independence war or join an ongoing one if there is a nearby province that has already rebelled. While the rebellion of a single province is unlikely to be successful, widespread discontent and unhappiness can be dangerous for even the strongest empires.

## Province capital[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Province capital") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Province capital")]

_Main article: [Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province\_capital "Province capital")_

Every province has a [![Image 25: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital")**province capital** for each country that controls part of it, representing the center of the local administration. The province capital gets significant bonuses and is generally the largest and most important territory in the province, determining who controls the province during a war and being one of the main recipients of [![Image 26: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") enslaved pops. If desired, the province capital can be moved during peacetime to a better location at the cost of [![Image 27: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty.

## Capital province[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Capital province") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Capital province")]

The province with the country's capital is considered its _capital province_.

Every pop in the capital province has a [![Image 28: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output")**+10%** output modifier (stacking with the capital city and capital region modifiers). In addition, the capital province is fixed at [![Image 29: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**100** loyalty regardless of any province loyalty modifiers and so will never rebel.

The capital province is also where the special countrywide capital [trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") surplus bonuses are gained. As the target of the [![Image 30: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") capital trade routes modifier, which usually has contributions from the default [commerce policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy#Economic_policies "Economy") and country ranks as well as usually being the most populous province with a high population of [![Image 31: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 32: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, this together means the capital province is usually the center of trade in a country with a large variety of imported [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods").

## Provincial investments[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Provincial investments") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Provincial investments")]

Provinces can be improved repeatedly with **province investments** to improve the administration and infrastructure of the province. Province investments have a base cost of [![Image 33: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**80** political influence each, modified by the specific province investment cost modifiers ([![Image 34: Price state investment military cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e4/Price_state_investment_military_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_state_investment_military_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_state_investment_military_cost_modifier.png)**military provincial investment cost**, [![Image 35: Price state investment civic cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Price_state_investment_civic_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_state_investment_civic_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_state_investment_civic_cost_modifier.png)**civic provincial investment cost**, [![Image 36: Price state investment oratory cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b0/Price_state_investment_oratory_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_state_investment_oratory_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_state_investment_oratory_cost_modifier.png)**oratory provincial investment cost**, and [![Image 37: Price state investment religious cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/44/Price_state_investment_religious_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_state_investment_religious_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Price_state_investment_religious_cost_modifier.png)**religious provincial investment cost**) that can be reduced by heritages and various [![Image 38: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")[inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"). There is also an additional cost of [![Image 39: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**20** gold for every currently existing investment in the province, including others of a different type, which is not affected by the provincial investment cost modifiers. Each investment takes **6** months to prepare and reduces the [![Image 40: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Local output") output of the province by **1%** during the process. The resulting effects are permanent as long as the province remains colonized and the owner does not change. Province investments cannot be cancelled once started, and only one investment can be made in a province at a time. Once a province investment is finished, [a notification event](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=State_improvement_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "State improvement events (page does not exist)") will appear in the event queue.

It is also possible to get [![Image 41: Province investment](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/State_free_investments.png/28px-State_free_investments.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment")**free province investments** from [forming nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations"), adopting certain [![Image 42: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and [![Image 43: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, and as a reward for certain [mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mission "Mission") tasks, which allows a country to make a provincial investment without spending any [![Image 44: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political influence or [![Image 45: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold. Note that the 6-month duration to implement the investment still applies.

All 4 types of investments can be applied to the same province an unlimited amount of times.

With the [![Image 46: Magna Graecia (DLC).png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Magna_Graecia_%28DLC%29.png/28px-Magna_Graecia_%28DLC%29.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Magna_Graecia_(DLC) "Magna Graecia (DLC)")[Magna Graecia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Magna_Graecia_(DLC) "Magna Graecia (DLC)") DLC, Sparta gets two unique [province investments](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province_investment "Province investment") for the military and religious slots, replacing the standard province investments:

## Governor policies[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Governor policies") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Province&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Governor policies")]

Every province can have a **governor policy** set which determines the priority of the province's administration. Governor policies allow a country to [![Image 47: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") extract more from a province's population, deal with [![Image 48: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest by increasing [![Image 49: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") population happiness or cracking down on the population, build up the [![Image 50: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value of the province's territories, and more.

When a [governor](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor") is assigned, they chose a random policy based on the factors listed in the Chance column (with a base chance of **0**). This includes directly reassigning a governor without having a vacancy first. As long as the region has a governor, the policy can be changed at a base cost of [![Image 51: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**10** political influence, modified by the [![Image 52: Change governor policy cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Change_governor_policy_cost.png/28px-Change_governor_policy_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Change governor policy cost")**change governor policy cost** modifier. Changing governor policy manually, except for in the [![Image 53: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital region/governorship, also gives [![Image 54: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny based on the current ruler's popularity, scaling between [![Image 55: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**1.25** at [![Image 56: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**0** ruler popularity to [![Image 57: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**0.25** at [![Image 58: Popularity.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/ff/Popularity.png/28px-Popularity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Popularity "Popularity")**100**.

All policy effects are modified by the governor's [![Image 59: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions")[Finesse](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes#Finesse "Attributes"), with the values below are given for the baseline level of [![Image 60: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions")**9** finesse. Each point of the governor's [![Image 61: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") finesse above 9 increases the modifier by **10%**, while each point below decreases it **10%** to a minimum value of **10%** of the base. This is described by the equation below:

![Image 62: {\displaystyle {\text{Effective Policy Modifier}}={\frac {{\text{Governor Finesse}}+1}{10}}\cdot {\text{Base Policy Modifier}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/01ae0533664bc0c5ecedbe5ac5b38997f98e7f74)
