monthly civilization: **+0.05%** Additional chance each month, based on governor [![Image 141: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse, to: * Increase the [![Image 142: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization level of neighbouring [![Image 143: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") barbarian strongholds by **2**, if any territory in the province has at least [![Image 144: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**30** civilization value * Downgrade or remove an adjacent [![Image 145: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians")barbarian stronghold, if it and any territory in the province have at least [![Image 146: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**35** civilization value | _This policy will increase the speed at which a province is civilized and slowly pacify any neighboring barbarian strongholds. Only infrastructure, cultural dissemination, and learning can bring enlightenment._ | * **+11** if the governor has the [![Image 147: Intelligent](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6d/Intelligent.png/28px-Intelligent.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Intelligent") intelligent trait * **+11** if the governor has the [![Image 148: Polymath](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f6/Polymath.png/28px-Polymath.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Polymath") polymath trait * **+20** if any adjacent territory is a [![Image 149: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") barbarian stronghold * **-30** if the country is tribal and has less than or equal to [![Image 150: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization")**40** centralization |
| [![Image 151: Centralize pop.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/8c/Centralize_pop.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Centralize_pop.png) | Centralize Population | * [![Image 152: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") Pop migration speed: **+2** For the [![Image 153: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital: * [![Image 154: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") Migration attraction: **+1** For all other territories: * [![Image 155: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") Migration attraction: **-5** | _This policy will encourage migration into a province's Provincial Capital and speed up the migration of local Pops, concentrating the population. A bustling metropolis is a sure sign of a prosperous province._ | * (Never) |
| [![Image 156: Decentralize pop.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/9/91/Decentralize_pop.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Decentralize_pop.png) | Decentralize Population | * [![Image 157: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed") Pop migration speed: **+2** For the [![Image 158: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital: * [![Image 159: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") Migration attraction: **-5** For all other territories: * [![Image 160: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") Migration attraction: **+1** | _This policy will encourage migration away from a province's Provincial Capital and speed up the migration of local Pops, dispersing the population. Sometimes, overpopulation cannot be solved by grain alone._ | * (Never) |


============================================================
# Territories
============================================================
Title: Territories - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

![Image 1](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/tvoIEuO0_UQ/hqdefault.jpg)

Imperator Tutorial - Pops & Cities - pt 3

Load video

Imperator: Rome tutorial #3 - pops and cities.

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/33/Blank_Map.png/500px-Blank_Map.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Blank_Map.png)

A paintable map of territories.

**Territories** are the smallest unit of land in _Imperator: Rome_, and they are the basic unit of territorial organization for most ingame mechanics, such as [territorial ownership](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country "Country"), [![Image 3: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") pops with their associated [![Image 4: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") output, [![Image 5: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") culture, and [![Image 6: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religion, [![Image 7: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") army movement, occupation, and recruitment, and more. Every territory belongs to a particular [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Provinces "Provinces"), which themselves are grouped into [regions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region").

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

Most territories can have an **[owner](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country "Country")** and be **colonized**, or not be owned by any country at all; unowned territories are considered **uncolonized**. Uncolonized territories are not necessarily uninhabited and can be **[colonized](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Colonization "Colonization")** by neighbouring countries to directly take ownership of them. Ownership is not to be confused with having **control** over a territory, which is typically the owner but can also be an enemy occupier during times of [![Image 8: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war. Certain territories are **[uncolonizeable](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Uncolonizable_territories)** and cannot be properly owned or have a population; they may be merely [**uninhabitable**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Uninhabitable_territories) but still accessible by armies or consist of completely [**impassable terrain**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Impassable_terrain). Separately, there are also [![Image 9: Ocean](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/26/Terrain_ocean.png/28px-Terrain_ocean.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Ocean")**sea** territories, including [![Image 10: River](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Terrain_river.png/28px-Terrain_river.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "River")**major rivers**, that cannot be owned and are inaccessible by armies, but can be traversed by ships.

All [![Image 11: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population")**pops** live in a certain territory, and they are the source of nearly every resource that a territory produces. A territory's population is limited by the territory's [![Image 12: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity")**population capacity**, which gives scaling penalties if exceeded, and the demographic makeup of a territory's pops determines its [![Image 13: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture")**dominant culture** and [![Image 14: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion")**dominant religion**. The owner of the territory, if there is one, determines who the [![Image 15: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") pop's output is paid towards and what modifiers affect their [![Image 16: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness, which produces [![Image 17: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion")**unrest** if too low and in turn affects [![Image 18: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") province loyalty. Pops can [![Image 19: Migration speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Global_migration_speed.png/28px-Global_migration_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration speed")**migrate** from territory to territory of their own accord, with pops moving from territories of low to high [![Image 20: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction")**migration attraction**, and certain pop types - [![Image 21: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves, as well as [![Image 22: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") countries - can be instantly **moved** in and out of owned territories by the state at the cost of [![Image 23: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold. It is possible for a territory to lose all its population to war or migration, which will automatically make it uncolonized if it is owned.

Each territory has a specific **[terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Terrain types")** that affects [![Image 24: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food production, [![Image 25: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, [![Image 26: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") movement cost, combat dice rolls, and more. **Rivers**, including [![Image 27: River](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Terrain_river.png/28px-Terrain_river.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "River") major rivers, cross between and through many territories and also give a significant modifier. All terrain features are set at the beginning of the game and cannot be changed.

[![Image 28: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") Armies are **raised** and [![Image 29: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") ships **built** from individual territories, with the buildable types depending on the trade routes that the territory's province has access to. Every army and navy is always located in a particular territory, and can perform unit actions that may have specific effects on the territory that it is present in. Armies and navies can always move into neighbouring territories, as long as they have [![Image 30: Has military access.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/94/Has_military_access.png/28px-Has_military_access.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_access "Military access") access; certain territories that are not actually adjacent but separated only by a narrow body of water may be considered connected across the **strait**.

Every territory has a certain type of **[trade good](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods")** that is produced there, with the exact amount depending on the number of [![Image 31: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves compared to the [![Image 32: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus")**slaves needed for local surplus'** as well as any [![Image 33: Base resources](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f7/Base_resources.png/28px-Base_resources.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Production "Base resources")**base resource production** modifiers. The trade good of a territory is normally constant, but can be changed through certain actions and [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events").

A territory can have one of three **[ranks](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory_rank "Territory rank")** - [![Image 34: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement, [![Image 35: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city, and [![Image 36: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis - which determines its [![Image 37: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity and pop class ratios, and indirectly the main types of output that its population will produce. A country can invest [![Image 38: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold and [![Image 39: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") political power to upgrade a territory's rank, or spend [![Image 40: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny to tear a city or metropolis down.

Depending on its territory rank, territories can have a number of **[buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings")** built in them that grant various modifiers and bonuses, particularly for increasing various types of pop [![Image 41: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") output and [![Image 42: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") happiness as well as modifying the pop ratio and increasing [![Image 43: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") local civilization value. While [![Image 44: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlements can only have one building built, [![Image 45: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and [![Image 46: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolises can have multiple buildings, capped by the territory's number of [![Image 47: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot")**city building slots**. Buildings can also provide [![Image 48: Fortification.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/89/Fortification.png/28px-Fortification.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort_level "Fort level")**fort level**, which defends the territory from enemy occupation and requires a [siege](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege") to reduce as well as lowering local [![Image 49: Unrest.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Unrest.png/28px-Unrest.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rebellion#Unrest "Rebellion") unrest, and [![Image 50: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port")**port level**, which allows the territory to build and service [![Image 51: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ship "Ship") ships. [![Image 52: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") Wonders are a special type of building that are much more expensive in terms of gold and time, but equivalently have powerful, country-wide effects.

In addition to buildings, territories can be connected by [![Image 53: Ability road building.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9b/Ability_road_building.png/28px-Ability_road_building.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Road "Road")**roads**, which significantly increases [![Image 54: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") army movement speed, and hold [![Image 55: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site")**holy sites** honouring a certain deity, improving the territory's economy and enhancing the deity's bonuses if it is worshipped in the owner's [![Image 56: Change pantheon cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b7/Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png/28px-Change_pantheon_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pantheon "Pantheon") pantheon. Every territory, except for [![Image 57: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") country capitals, has a [![Image 58: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding")**holding slot** that may be granted or acquired by [heads of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") to increase their [![Image 59: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power and [![Image 60: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") wealth, though they may also invest in their owned territories.

Every province has a single territory designated as its [![Image 61: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital")**province capital**; similarly, every country that owns at least 1 territory has a [![Image 62: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital")**country capital**. These capital territories get various bonuses and are important to control during [![Image 63: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") wars, and can be moved around within the province or country to larger cities or more defensible locations.

Territories can also have **[territory modifiers](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Modifiers "Modifiers")** that grant various bonuses and maluses to the territory and the pops that live in them. [Certain special modifiers](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Unique_City_Modifiers "Unique City Modifiers") are permanently placed in certain territories at the beginning of the game, and cannot be gained or lost.

Every territory has a [![Image 64: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**[civilization value](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Civilization_value "Territories")**, representing the overall level of development and how settled the territory is, which impacts [![Image 65: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness") pop happiness, [![Image 66: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, and [![Image 67: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit. Other territory modifiers and values include [![Image 68: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food")**[food](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food")**, which is produced in a territory according to its [terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Terrain types") and consumed by pops in the [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province"); [![Image 69: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit")**supply limit**, which determines how large of an army a territory can support before it starts to take [![Image 70: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition; and [![Image 71: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians")**barbarian power**, which determines the rate at which [![Image 72: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") barbarian hordes will rise up from barbarian strongholds in impassable terrain.

Finally, every territory always has the following base modifiers:

## Territory rank[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Territory rank") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Territory rank")]

Every territory has a certain **territory rank** that broadly determines its economic role and general level of development. Each rank has a certain associated modifier that is the main determinant of a territory's [![Image 73: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity and pop class ratio, as well as the [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") that are available to develop and specialize the territory. Every additional rank also grants an extra altar to any [![Image 74: Holy Site.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Holy_Site.png/28px-Holy_Site.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holy_site "Holy site") holy sites in the territory, allowing it to hold one more [treasure](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treasure "Treasure").

A territory's rank generally does not change unless it is specifically modified by certain actions or [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), and will retain its rank even if it is completely depopulated and then recolonized.

### Settlements[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Settlements") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Settlements")]

[![Image 75: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement")**Settlements** represent more sparsely populated rural areas, typically with a heavy focus on agriculture and other primary production and comprising the vast majority of territories in the game. In monarchies and republics, they have a base population ratio of **16%**[![Image 76: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, **16%**[![Image 77: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") freemen, and **66%**[![Image 78: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves, while tribal settlements have a base ratio of **11%**[![Image 79: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, **44%**[![Image 80: Tribesmen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Pop_tribesmen.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Tribesmen "Tribesmen") tribesmen, and **44%**[![Image 81: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves, which means that they are generally the main source of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") and [![Image 82: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food")[food](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") in a province, as well as producing a significant portion of a country's [![Image 83: Tax income.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Tax_income.png/28px-Tax_income.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tax "Tax") tax income and [![Image 84: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower. Every settlement has the following modifiers:

Settlements normally can only ever have one [![Image 85: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot") building slot, but have their own unique set of powerful buildings that can be used to specialize settlements towards manpower, trade good production, or tax income. Adopting the [![Image 86: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Rural Infrastructure [![Image 87: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic Invention will give an extra building slot to every territory, though each building will still be limited one per territory.

### City[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: City") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: City")]

[![Image 88: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City")**Cities** represent urbanized areas that have been granted **city status**, historically often a set of special civic privileges and political autonomy granted to a particular urban community that would become the commercial and political center of the area. Cities have a significantly higher [![Image 89: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity than settlements and more importantly can sustain significant populations of [![Image 90: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 91: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, which makes cities the main source of [![Image 92: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") trade routes and [![Image 93: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology") research points. Cities have a bonus to [![Image 94: Base resources](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f7/Base_resources.png/28px-Base_resources.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Production "Base resources") Base Resource Production, but the lower proportion of [![Image 95: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves and higher [![Image 96: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") surplus threshold means that cities are not especially efficient at producing large amounts of [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") (though very large cities will still end up accumulating enough [![Image 97: Slaves](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Pop_slaves.png/28px-Pop_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Slaves "Slaves") slaves to produce a significant amount of surplus trade goods). The large [![Image 98: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population and significant proportion of higher pop classes also means that cities will have significant [![Image 99: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food consumption, which in some cases will require the import of food [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") to sustain. Unlike settlements, cities always have multiple [![Image 100: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot") building slots, with the exact amount depending on the city's [![Image 101: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population and many other factors that give [![Image 102: Local building slot.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c7/Local_building_slot.png/28px-Local_building_slot.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Building_slot "Building slot") local and global city building slot modifiers. Cities can be easily distinguished on the map as a group of buildings in the territory, which shrinks and grows with the actual population of the city. At the start of the game, many cities large and small are found all across the civilized world, from the shores of the Mediterranean through the ancient cities of the Middle East and the great centres of Central Asia and India.

Every [![Image 103: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city has the following modifiers:

New cities can be **founded** from owned settlements at any time, allowing countries to slowly shape and develop the world according to its desires and reflecting the great city-building empires of the era. Founding a city takes **2** years to be complete, during which the settlement will get the **City Under Construction** modifier, giving [![Image 104: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output")**-100%** Population Output. Any settlement producing an agricultural trade good ([![Image 105: Grain](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/05/Goods_grain.png/28px-Goods_grain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Grain "Grain") grain, [![Image 106: Fish](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Goods_fish.png/28px-Goods_fish.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Fish "Fish") fish, [![Image 107: Livestock](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Goods_cattle.png/28px-Goods_cattle.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Livestock "Livestock") livestock, or [![Image 108: Vegetables](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Goods_vegetables.png/28px-Goods_vegetables.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods#Vegetables "Vegetables") vegetables) will switch to a new trade good once the city is finished; if city status is subsequently revoked, the trade good produced in the territory will revert to the old food trade good. While granting city status will not have any immediate impact on the population, the significantly higher [![Image 109: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") migration attraction of cities means that pops in the surrounding territories will slowly migrate to nearby cities, particularly if it is also the [![Image 110: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital. [![Image 111: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") Enslaving pops during wars can also be a good method of filling up cities with pops.

The base cost of founding a city is [![Image 112: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**200** gold and [![Image 113: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**50** political influence, adjusted by the [![Image 114: Found city cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b1/Price_found_city_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_found_city_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "Found city cost")**Found City Cost Modifier**. Some of the more important sources of the modifier are:

It is also possible to [![Image 115: Revoke city status](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Downgrade_to_settlement.png/28px-Downgrade_to_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "Revoke city status")**revoke city status** from a city as long as it is not the [![Image 116: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital and no construction or other upgrades are ongoing in the territory, whether to centralize the population more effectively, force the [![Image 117: Local pop demotion speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Local_pop_demotion_speed.png/28px-Local_pop_demotion_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Demotion "Demotion") demotion of unwanted [![Image 118: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 119: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens, or even simply as an act of spite. Revoking city status costs a base of [![Image 120: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**5** tyranny, modified by the [![Image 121: Revoke city status cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Price_revoke_city_status_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_revoke_city_status_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "Revoke city status cost")**Revoke City Status Cost** modifier (with **-50%** for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") governments as the only source), which will instantly downgrade the city back to a [![Image 122: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement and give the **Revoked City Status** modifier for **5** years, giving [![Image 123: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")**-10%** Local Population Happiness and [![Image 124: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction")**-2** Migration Attraction as well as preventing city status from being granted back as long as the modifier exists. The significant decrease in [![Image 125: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity will usually put the territory well over the limit if it was a city with significant population, which will quickly decrease the population by emigration and pop death.

### Metropolis[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Metropolis") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Metropolis")]

[![Image 126: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") A **metropolis** is considered the peak of a city’s urban evolution and can only be made from the very largest cities of a region. Metropolises do not have any special mechanics that set them apart from cities, but instead have generally stronger modifiers that allows it to support an even larger and more productive population, making them advantageous to construct in a country's largest cities, particularly the [![Image 127: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital by the mid to late game. The only metropolis that exists at the start of the game is the [![Image 128: Flag of Maurya](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5b/Maurya.png/20px-Maurya.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maurya "Maurya")[Mauryan](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Maurya "Maurya") capital of Pataliputra.

Every [![Image 129: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis has the following modifiers:

Note that a city that has been upgraded to a metropolis is still considered to have city status.

A metropolis can be **founded** on an existing [![Image 130: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city that has at least [![Image 131: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population")**80** population and is the [![Image 132: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital. Founding a metropolis costs a base of [![Image 133: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth")**400** gold and [![Image 134: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**100** political influence; like cities, the process takes **2** years to complete during which the settlement will get the **Metropolis Under Construction** modifier giving [![Image 135: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output")**-100%** Population Output. It is possible to have multiple metropolises in a single province by simply moving the [![Image 136: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital") province capital to another city and building a new metropolis there; this will not destroy the previously existing one.

Like with cities, it is possible to [![Image 137: Revoke city status](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Downgrade_to_settlement.png/28px-Downgrade_to_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "Revoke city status")**revoke city status** from a metropolis as long as it is not the [![Image 138: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital and no building or upgrades are ongoing, which will downgrade the territory all the way back to a [![Image 139: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement. Revoking city status from a metropolis costs a base of [![Image 140: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**10** tyranny, modified by the [![Image 141: Revoke metropolis status cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Price_revoke_metropolis_status_cost_modifier.png/28px-Price_revoke_metropolis_status_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Revoke metropolis status cost")**Revoke Metropolis Status Cost** modifier (with **-50%** for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") governments as the only source) and has the same effects as revoking city status from a [![Image 142: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city.

## Country capital[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Country capital") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Country capital")]

[![Image 143: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") Every country that owns at least one territory has a **capital territory**, the seat of the government and usually the largest, wealthiest, and most important city in the country. The capital territory gets the following modifiers:

In addition to these baseline bonuses, the capital territory is also often granted permanent bonuses by a number of [decisions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Decisions "Decisions") and [missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions"), most notably all [country formation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations") decisions and the [generic infrastructure mission](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic_infrastructure_mission "Generic infrastructure mission"). This makes it advantageous to make the capital as large as possible, as pops in the capital are usually far more productive than pops in any other territory and usually have the highest [![Image 144: Citizen ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Pop_citizen_ratio.png/28px-Pop_citizen_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Citizen ratio") citizen and especially [![Image 145: Noble ratio](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9e/Pop_noble_ratio.png/28px-Pop_noble_ratio.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Pop_ratio "Noble ratio") noble ratio given the combined effects of the base capital bonus and the number of buildings that are typically built there due to its high population. By the midgame, the capital territory will often have outgrown its [![Image 146: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") local food supply and begin to necessitate imports of food goods to feed its growing population, though the high proportion of [![Image 147: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") noble and [![Image 148: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizen pops usually means that the capital has many more [![Image 149: Global state trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/46/Global_state_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_state_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") import route slots than is needed to feed it.

The [province](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital_province "Capital province") and [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital_region "Capital region") that the country capital is in also have special modifiers and mechanics that generally make them more valuable than more peripheral areas, such as not being subject to a [![Image 150: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governor who would otherwise collect a portion of the taxes as his or her wage. Control of a country's capital is important during [![Image 151: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare")[wars](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare"), as it is worth extra [warscore](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warscore "Warscore") and provides bonus war enthusiasm for countries that still control their capitals, as well as being one of the main targets that [![Image 152: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") enslaved pops are sent to - slaves will generally go disproportionately to the capital territory, unless it has reached its [![Image 153: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity. The capital is also the point from which [![Image 154: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range") diplomatic range is calculated from (for non-neighbouring countries).

If desired, the capital can be moved at any time to a territory of the [![Image 155: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") primary culture (note that being integrated is not sufficient) and [![Image 156: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") state religion in a loyal province as long as the country is not at war. The cost is [![Image 157: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**50 + 5 times** the difference in population between the current capital and the new capital in political influence, with a minimum cost of [![Image 158: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**80** and a maximum of [![Image 159: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**250**. This means that it is much cheaper to move the capital to a more populated territory than vice versa.

## Provincial capital[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Provincial capital") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Provincial capital")]

Every province that a country owns or partially owns has a [![Image 160: Province capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cc/State_capital.png/28px-State_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories#Provincial_capital "Province capital")**province capital**, representing the center of the local administration. If multiple countries own part of the same province, each country's portion of the province will have its own provincial capital. The capital of the capital province is always the same as the [![Image 161: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") national capital.

Provincial capitals get the following bonuses:

Note that this is not applied to the national capital, which instead gets a much stronger modifier.

Province capitals are important in wartime as they determine who controls the province as a whole. Taking control of the province capital will trigger the occupation of all non-[fortified](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Fort "Fort") territories in the province without needing to occupy each territory manually, armies can only resupply in foreign territory if the capital of the province is occupied, and a province cannot be demanded in a peace treaty unless its capital (as well as all forts) are occupied (though occupied territories can still be demanded individually). Province capitals are also the primary targets for receiving [![Image 162: Enslavement efficiency.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Enslavement_efficiency.png/28px-Enslavement_efficiency.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Enslavement "Enslavement") slaves when occupying enemy territory, generally taking priority after the [![Image 163: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") national capital.

When a country acquires territory in a new province, the province capital is automatically set to the [![Image 164: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") most populous territory that the country controls in the province at that point in time, but after that will generally remain static. Provincial capitals can be relocated during peace time at the cost of [![Image 165: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") provincial loyalty, which can be useful if another territory is larger and/or has more potential for growth than the current province capital. Relocating the provincial capital to a [![Image 166: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") settlement lowers [![Image 167: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty") provincial loyalty by **30**, to a [![Image 168: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city lowers loyalty by **20**, and to a [![Image 169: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis by **10**.

Only territories that are the provincial capital can be raised to [![Image 170: Territory metropolis.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Territory_metropolis.png/28px-Territory_metropolis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Metropolis "Metropolis") metropolis status.

## Terrain[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Terrain") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Terrain")]

### Terrain types[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Terrain types") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Terrain types")]

Every territory has a fixed, predefined **terrain type** that cannot be changed. Terrain type has a significant impact on a territory's habitability through [![Image 171: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity, [![Image 172: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food production, and [![Image 173: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value modifiers, and is important to consider for army maneuvering and supply as well. Generic heritages are also assigned partly based on the terrain type of a country's [![Image 174: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital territory at the start of the game.

Sea territories also have terrain types like land territories. The primary effect of sea terrain is to affect the movement speed of ships.

[![Image 175](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3a/Simple_terrain.png/1200px-Simple_terrain.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Simple_terrain.png)

Terrain map.

#### Sea terrain[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Sea terrain") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Sea terrain")]

|  | Name | Combat width | Modifiers | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 217: Terrain coastal big.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5c/Terrain_coastal_big.png/400px-Terrain_coastal_big.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Terrain_coastal_big.png) | **Coastal Sea** | **30** | * [![Image 218: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") Movement Cost: **+15%** | _Along coastlines and in archipelagos the sea is generally more shallow and affords less room for maneuver._ |
| [![Image 219: Terrain ocean big.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/4/45/Terrain_ocean_big.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Terrain_ocean_big.png) | **Ocean** | **50** | * None | _Consisting of calm, glassy seas, as well as roiling waves, the sea carries the lifeblood of the ancient world: trade._ |
| [![Image 220: Terrain river big.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0f/Terrain_river_big.png/400px-Terrain_river_big.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Terrain_river_big.png) | **River** | **10** | * Attackers get a **-1** penalty to their dice rolls. * [![Image 221: Movement cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/48/Movement_cost.png/28px-Movement_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army#Army_movement "Movement cost") Movement Cost: **+15%** | _Most rivers are not fit to be navigated by ship but some are wide and deep enough for smaller ships to navigate them._ |

### Climate[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Climate") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Climate")]

Each territory also has one of five possible **climates** depending on its geography, which gives an additional smaller modifier to [![Image 222: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") population capacity and [![Image 223: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food production. Certain climates also have **winter** for part of the year, which has an effect on [![Image 224: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition.

**Warm Climate** is the most hospitable type and covers the largest part of the map, from the shores of the Mediterranean to the Fertile Crescent and the rich plains of India. Every territory with Warm Climate gets the following modifiers:

**Temperate Climate** is common directly to the north of warm areas, such as in Britannia, Gaul, Moesia and Illyria, northern Anatolia, and the south of central Asia. Territories with temperate climate have no default modifiers.

**Frigid Climate** represents colder areas of the map where harsher winters have a significant effect on habitability, such as in Scandinavia, Germania, and the north of central Asia. Every territory with Frigid Climate gets the following modifiers:

**Alpine Climate** describes cold mountainous areas, such as Tibet and parts of the Caucascus. Every territory with Alpine Climate gets the following modifiers:

**Arid Climate** describes desert regions and nearby drier areas, such as the areas around the Sahara, parts of the Arabian Peninsula, and much of Persia. Every territory with Arid Climate gets the following modifiers:

### Rivers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Rivers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Rivers")]

**Rivers** were central to trade and transportation in this time period and the presence of a significant river usually made that area much more attractive to settle in, as well as being more defensible given the inherent difficulties of crossing a river. Ingame, a territory with a **Nearby River** gets the following modifiers:

Parts of certain very large rivers, such as the Rhine, Danube, Nile, or Indus are considered **major rivers**, which makes them navigable by [ships](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") and crossable only at certain points. Mechanically, major rivers are considered sea tiles for most intents and purposes, with crossing points marked as straits, except that [![Image 225: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") ports cannot be built in all territories next to major rivers (only those that already have a port at the start of the game). A territory that is **Adjacent to Major River** gets the following modifiers:

The population capacity bonus means that territories next to a river, especially a major river, are generally good [![Image 226: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city sites. Note that if a territory is both adjacent to a major river and has a nearby (minor) river, it will have the effects of both modifiers.

### Coastal territories[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Coastal territories") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Coastal territories")]

**Coastal** territories also get small bonuses by virtue of their access to the sea, which like rivers often served to connect areas together even if there was no good port nearby. [![Image 227: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") Ports can be built in any coastal territory to improve access for pops and and allow [![Image 228: Naval combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/54/Naval_combat.png/28px-Naval_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Navy "Navy") ships to be built and serviced in these territories. All coastal territories without a [![Image 229: Port icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/36/Port_icon.png/28px-Port_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Port "Port") port get the following modifier:

Note that this modifier can potentially stack with both river modifiers, if the territory meets all the requirements. While ports do not get this modifier, each port level gives a similar modifier to the territory.

## Supply limit[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Supply limit") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Supply limit")]

_See also: [Attrition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition"), [Food#Army supply](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food#Army\_supply "Food")_

The [![Image 230: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit")**supply limit** of a territory determines how large of an [army](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") it can sustain before the army begins to take [![Image 231: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition, with every point of [![Image 232: Army weight modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Army_weight_modifier.png/28px-Army_weight_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army_weight_modifier "Army weight modifier") army weight over the supply limit giving **1** point of [![Image 233: Attrition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/85/Attrition.png/28px-Attrition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attrition "Attrition") attrition. As attrition increases [![Image 234: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food consumption rather than immediately causing loss of soldiers, being over the supply limit is not particularly problematic as long as the army is just quickly passing through or is adequately supplied with enough [![Image 235: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food to feed itself, but can become a significant issue with longer campaigns in low [![Image 236: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit areas.

Generally speaking, more fertile, developed, and highly populated areas have a higher [![Image 237: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit. All modifiers affecting the supply limit of a territory are listed below (note that national modifiers, e.g. [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") and [deities](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion#Pantheons "Religion"), are based on the owner of the army, not the owner of the territory):

## Civilization value[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Civilization value") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Civilization value")]

A territory's [![Image 274: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**civilization value** represents its level of infrastructure and urbanization and is the biggest contributors to [![Image 275: Population capacity](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/61/Population_capacity.png/28px-Population_capacity.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_capacity "Population capacity") pop capacity, [![Image 276: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") pop output and [![Image 277: Happiness.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a0/Pop_happy.png/28px-Pop_happy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Happiness")[pop happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Happiness_of_pops "Population"), especially for [![Image 278: Pop noble.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c8/Pop_noble.png/28px-Pop_noble.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Noble "Noble") nobles and [![Image 279: Citizens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/06/Pop_citizen.png/28px-Pop_citizen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Citizens "Citizens") citizens. While slow to grow and improve, civilization value is in general one of the most important economic modifiers that a territory can have, particularly for settled nations. Increasing the current civilization value of the capital to **40** is also one of the main requirements for a [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nation to reform into a [republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") or [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy"), and some [city](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City")[buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") also require a minimum civilization level. Most countries, except for [migratory tribes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal"), will generally benefit from a higher civilization level in their territories.

A territory's civilization value spans between **0** and **100**, with each point granting the following local effects:

| Civilization Value | [![Image 280: Global population growth.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Global_population_growth.png/28px-Global_population_growth.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population_growth "Population growth") Population Growth | [![Image 281: Population
