"Supply limit") Supply Limit | [![Image 283: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") Barbarian Growth | [![Image 284: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Local output") Population Output | [![Image 285: Migration attraction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Local_migration_attraction.png/28px-Local_migration_attraction.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Migration "Migration attraction") Migration Attraction | [![Image 286: Noble happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Pop_noble_happiness.png/28px-Pop_noble_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Noble happiness") Noble Happiness | [![Image 287: Citizen happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4c/Pop_citizen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_citizen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Citizen happiness") Citizen Happiness | [![Image 288: Freeman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f0/Pop_freemen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_freemen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Freeman happiness") Freemen Happiness | [![Image 289: Tribesman happiness](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png/28px-Pop_tribesmen_happiness.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Happiness "Tribesman happiness") Tribesmen Happiness |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0% |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| +50% | **+0.15%** | **+12.5%** | **+50%** | **-0.25** | **+50%** | **+2.5** | **+15%** | **+10%** | **+5%** | **-20%** |
| +100% | **+0.3%** | **+25%** | **+100%** | **-0.5** | **+100%** | **+5** | **+30%** | **+20%** | **+10%** | **-40%** |

### Maximum civilization level[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Maximum civilization level") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Maximum civilization level")]

Each territory has a maximum possible civilization value, which is the sum of its owner's [![Image 290: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**country civilization level** and any province or territory-specific modifiers to [![Image 291: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value")**local civilization level**. The country civilization level is generally the most consequential, as it is applied across all the territories in the country, and its value is determined largely by a country's [government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") form, its level of [![Image 292: Research points.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cb/Research_points.png/28px-Research_points.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology")[Oratory Advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology#Oratory_Advances "Technology"), and positive [![Image 293: Centralization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Centralization.png/28px-Centralization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Centralization "Centralization") centralization for [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") nations. Local modifiers can increase the civilization level of certain territories, much higher than the country-wide value and come mostly from [buildings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings"), particularly important in [![Image 294: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities, as well as from certain decision modifiers (especially [![Image 295: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital")[capital](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") modifiers given when creating [formable nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations")). Note that while a territory's maximum civilization is uncapped, its actual civilization level will never rise above 100 even if its maximum level is higher.

All static sources of country maximum civilization level are listed below.

| Type | Modifier | [![Image 296: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") Civilization Cap |

### Civilization change[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Civilization change") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Civilization change")]

Maximum civilization level does nothing by itself, but allows the territory's actual civilization level to slowly rise to that level. Each month, the civilization value of every territory that is below its local maximum civilization level will increase by the value of the [![Image 342: Global monthly civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Global_monthly_civilization.png/28px-Global_monthly_civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_monthly_civilization.png)**monthly civilization change** modifier, which can be increased with various [omens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Omen "Omen"), [inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"), [governor policies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy"), and other modifiers. If the local maximum civilization level is lower than the territory's current civilization level, it will instead decrease at a fixed rate of **-0.10%** per month until it reaches the cap again.

| Type | Modifier | [![Image 343: Global monthly civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Global_monthly_civilization.png/28px-Global_monthly_civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Global_monthly_civilization.png) Monthly Civilization Change |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Base | Base | **+0.01%** |
| At Peace | **+0.02%** |
| [![Image 344: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") Barbarian power | **-0.025%** per point |
| [![Image 345: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations")[Tribal vassal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations#Tribal_vassal "Subject nations") | **+0.01%** |

## Uncolonizable territories[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Uncolonizable territories") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Uncolonizable territories")]

Certain particularly harsh or desolate territories are considered **uncolonizable**. These territories have no true owner or [![Image 392: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") pops and for the most part cannot be interacted with, though they will be considered owned for the purposes of map colouring and naming purposes if there is a single country that owns (or otherwise has coloured in) a double majority of both the adjacent colonizable territories and all adjacent territories, including neighbouring uncolonizable territories. Similar behaviour is used to colour in uncolonizable territories in some other mapmodes, such as culture, religion, province, and region. This behaviour can be toggled on and off using the **Fill in impassable terrain** setting.

All uncolonizable territories have a [![Image 393: Policy cultural assimilation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/96/Policy_cultural_assimilation.png/28px-Policy_cultural_assimilation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Culture "Culture") culture, [![Image 394: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religion, [![Image 395: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value, [![Image 396: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") barbarian power value, and [![Image 397: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit, which depending on the type of the uncolonizable territory may have some gameplay relevance. Whether or not a territory is considered uncolonizable is set at the beginning of the game and cannot be changed.

### Uninhabitable territories[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Uninhabitable territories") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Uninhabitable territories")]

**Uninhabitable** territories cannot be owned or colonized, but still allow [armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") to pass through them. They exist in a number of passes through many arid and desert areas, such as in the Arabian pennisula and Central Asia. Despite their low habitability, there are no special penalties for armies marching through uninhabitable territories beyond their low [![Image 398: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit and inability to replenish [![Image 399: Monthly food.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Monthly_food.png/28px-Monthly_food.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Food "Food") food supply. Unlike impassable terrain, uninhabitable territories have a [terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Terrain_types "Terrain types") that helps to determine its [![Image 400: Supply limit.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/97/Supply_limit.png/28px-Supply_limit.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Supply_limit "Supply limit") supply limit.

All uninhabitable territories also have a culture and religion that is set at the beginning of the game, appears on the relevant mapmodes, and cannot be changed, though this is purely cosmetic and does not have any gameplay effect.

### Impassable terrain[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&veaction=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Impassable terrain") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Territories&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Impassable terrain")]

_See also: [Barbarians](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians")_

**Impassable terrain** represents the harshest and most inaccessible areas of the map, and in addition to being uncolonizable also cannot be passed by [armies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") and cannot be interacted with directly. Many exist as mountain ranges, inaccessible jungles, or unforgiving deserts crossing through even relatively developed areas, serving to isolate certain areas from one another and represent the difficulties that these types of terrain posed for states trying to exert their control across them.

Certain areas of impassable terrain hold [![Image 401: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians")**barbarian strongholds**, from which barbarian hordes will occasionally rise up to pillage and settle neighbouring territories. These barbarian strongholds can be particularly dangerous and require some military investment to control and hold off, but can eventually be pacified by raising the stronghold's [![Image 402: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization value high enough using the [![Image 403: Policy civilization effort.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/81/Policy_civilization_effort.png/28px-Policy_civilization_effort.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Policy_civilization_effort.png) Civilization Effort [governor policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") in a neighbouring province.

Impassable terrain has a culture and religion that is used when generating [![Image 404: Barbarian power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/19/Barbarian_power.png/28px-Barbarian_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarians "Barbarians") barbarian hordes that rise up from that territory. However, in the culture and religion mapmodes the colour of impassable terrain will instead be determined by the majority colour of the surrounding territories according to the same rules as colouring for ownership, which may be different from the actual culture or religion of the territory.


============================================================
# Region
============================================================
Title: Region - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a8/Region_mapmode.png/550px-Region_mapmode.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Region_mapmode.png)

The region mapmode

[![Image 2: Region.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c4/Region.png/32px-Region.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Region.png) A **region** is the highest level of geographical organization in the game, formed by groupings of [provinces](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Provinces "Provinces").

## Region mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Region&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Region mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Region&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Region mechanics")]

Each region can be administered by a [![Image 3: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office")[governor](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor "Governor"), who gives various bonuses or maluses to all owned provinces and territories in the region based on their traits and attributes. Additionally, regions are the fundamental unit by which a country's [![Image 4: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levies and [![Image 5: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legions are raised and organized. Each region in a country has a certain army [![Image 6: Strength.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Strength.png/28px-Strength.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy_strength "Levy strength") strength based on its non-slave integrated culture population, which be either raised as a [![Image 7: Levy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b6/Levy.png/28px-Levy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Levy "Levy") levy under the command of the region's governor or recruited to form part of a [![Image 8: Legion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legion.png/28px-Legion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Legion "Legion") legion based in that region.

### Capital region[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Region&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Capital region") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Region&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Capital region")]

The region containing a nation's capital [city](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") is the nation's **capital region**. The capital region is always governed by the nation's [![Image 9: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler")[ruler](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler"), with the effects of his/her attributes and traits applied to all provinces and territories in the capital region as if the ruler was a governor.

Every state/territory in the capital region gets the following modifiers:

*   [![Image 10: Province loyalty](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4e/Monthly_state_loyalty.png/28px-Monthly_state_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province#Provincial_loyalty "Province loyalty")**+0.05** Local Provincial Loyalty

Note that this modifier stacks with the modifiers for the capital province and territory.

As the capital region does not have a governor who takes a portion of the income, is more loyal than other regions of the state, and helps the ruler with their [![Image 11: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base, full control is recommended before expansion elsewhere.

However, during a [![Image 12: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war, the territories where a rebelling character has [![Image 13: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding")[holdings](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") (even those within the capital region) will always join the rebels.

## List of regions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Region&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: List of regions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Region&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: List of regions")]

| Region | Provinces | ID |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Africa](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italian_and_African_regions#Africa "Italian and African regions") | Byssatis, Carthago, Emporia, Maxyesia, Musulamia, Thabracania, Zeugitana | africa_region |
| [Albania](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Caucasian_regions#Albania "Caucasian regions") | Arran, Artshak, Caucasian Albania, Kahetia, Paytankert, Sarmatia Hyrcania, Scythia Transmontem | albania_region |
| [Aparanta](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Indian_regions#Aparanta "Indian regions") | Anupa, Aparanta, Asika, Kuntala, Mulaka, North Sahyadri, South Sahyadri, Vindhya | aparanta_region |
| [Aquitania](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Gallic_and_Britannic_regions#Aquitania "Gallic and Britannic regions") | Aquitania, Auscia, Biggeronia, Celtica, Lemovicia, Nitiobrogia, Pictonia, Santonia | aquitaine_region |
| [Arabia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Arabian_and_East_African_regions#Arabia "Arabian and East African regions") | Arabia Ad Sinus, Arabia Maritimae, Arabia Relicta, Dedan, Iathrippa, Taifa | arabia_region |
| [Arabia Felix](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Arabian_and_East_African_regions#Arabia_Felix "Arabian and East African regions") | Arabia Felix Centralis, Arabia Felix Occidentalis, Arabia Felix Orientalis, Hadhramut Occidentalis, Hadhramut Orientalis, Qataba, Saba | arabia_felix_region |

============================================================
# Holding
============================================================
Title: Holding - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

A [![Image 1: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding")**holding** represents a controlling stake in the industry or economy of a particular [![Image 2: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories") territory that can be owned by a particular [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character"), usually a [head of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family"), giving them considerable influence over the area. Holdings provide [![Image 3: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income and [![Image 4: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base to their owner, and can be viewed in the [![Image 5: Holdings mm.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Holdings_mm.png/28px-Holdings_mm.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Holdings_mm.png)**Holdings** mapmode.

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

### Holding slots[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Holding slots") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Holding slots")]

Except for the [![Image 6: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") country capital, each [![Image 7: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories")[territory](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territory "Territory") has one [![Image 8: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding")**holding slot** which can have a single holding owned by a particular character. There is no limit on the number of holdings a character can have.

### Power base[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Power base") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Power base")]

Every holding gives [![Image 9: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base") power base proportional to the total number of territories in the country, irrespective of its [![Image 10: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") wealth or [![Image 11: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population. A character will get [![Image 12: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**+0.5** power base for every percentage of the country's territories it controls as holdings (alternatively, all territories together - including territories without held [![Image 13: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings - are worth [![Image 14: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**50** power base in total, with power base from holdings determined how what proportion of the country's territories the character owns as [![Image 15: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings). For instance, if a character owns 10 holdings in a country with 21 territories, they will get 10/21×50=23.81 power base from their holdings.

Every [![Image 16: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holding is also additionally capped at [![Image 17: Power base](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/73/Power_base.png/28px-Power_base.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Power_base "Power base")**10** power base, which is relevant in small countries with fewer than 5 territories.

### Revenues[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Revenues") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Revenues")]

A holding is a source of [![Image 18: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") monthly income for its owner, generally proportional to the income that the territory produces for the state.

### Acquiring holdings[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Acquiring holdings") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Acquiring holdings")]

Holdings in territories with an empty holding slot can be granted to a [head of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") using the **Grant Holding** interaction at a cost of [![Image 19: Treasury.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7e/Treasury.png/28px-Treasury.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Economy "Economy")**40** gold. The beneficiary will gain [![Image 20: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**5** loyalty, decreasing at a rate of **−1** per year, stacking to a maximum of **20** loyalty. If the beneficiary is the [![Image 21: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler, an additional cost of [![Image 22: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption")**5** ruler corruption will apply.

[Heads of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") can buy holdings in territories with an empty holding slot using their [![Image 23: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") personal wealth with the **Buy Holding** scheme. With this scheme, characters will always prefer to buy holdings that are adjacent to currently held holdings. Characters that are not heads of families usually do not have holdings of their own, but can sometimes earn them via [plots](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Scheme "Scheme") (e.g. by stealing holdings from other characters) or [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events").

When a [head of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") dies, all their holdings will be inherited by their successor as head of family; if a non-family head character dies, their holdings are inherited by the head of their family. These means that holdings, with the wealth and influence that they entail, will almost always be concentrated among the heads of families.

### Developing holdings[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Developing holdings") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Developing holdings")]

Characters may develop territories that they have holdings in through various [schemes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Investment_schemes "Characters") at their own personal expense. [![Image 24: Territory settlement.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Territory_settlement.png/28px-Territory_settlement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Settlement "Settlement") Settlements with the appropriate [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") and an empty building slot may be upgraded with a [![Image 25: Farming Settlement](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Building_farm_estate.png/28px-Building_farm_estate.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Farming_Settlement "Farming Settlement") Farming Settlement or [![Image 26: Mine](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a5/Building_mine.png/28px-Building_mine.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Mine "Mine") Mine with the **Latifundia** and **Expanding Mines** schemes, respectively, if the character has enough [![Image 27: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold to build them. Note that [![Image 28: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") cities and settlements that do not have a farmable or mineable trade good will never have buildings built by character schemes.

A head of family may also choose to establish a **Family Estate** in one of their more populous holdings with the **Expand Estate** scheme, which when completed gives [![Image 29: Local output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Local output")**+3%** Local Population Output but will prevent the holding from being revoked normally. The Expand Estate scheme can be repeatedly used by characters to further increase the output of the territory.

### Revoking holdings[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Revoking holdings") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Revoking holdings")]

A holding can be **revoked** from a [head of family](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Head_of_family "Head of family") with the **Revoke Holding** interaction at a cost of [![Image 30: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty")**−5** loyalty, decreasing at a rate of **+2** per year. Holdings with the **Family Estate** modifier cannot be revoked normally. This can be used to slowly reduce the power of a family by slowly revoking their holdings at a rate that does not anger them too much. In larger countries - where each individual holding matters less - revoking will generally become less useful over time, as each individual holding becomes less important.

If the country has at least [![Image 31: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**40** tyranny and the head of a great family is imprisoned, they can be **proscribed** at a cost of a further [![Image 32: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny")**30** tyranny (reduced by **15** if the character sided with the rebels in a [![Image 33: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war, and a further **5** if the character's family has less than [![Image 34: Family prestige](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3b/Monthly_character_family_prestige.png/28px-Monthly_character_family_prestige.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Family_prestige "Family prestige")**500** prestige). Proscription will revoke all of family head's holdings, including those with the **Family Estate** modifier, execute the head, and demote the entire family down to [minor character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Minor_character "Minor character") status with a [![Image 35: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty penalty of **-40** for 20 years. This extreme option can be used to completely destroy the power of a dangerously powerful family, but is highly costly in terms of [![Image 36: Tyranny](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Tyranny.png/28px-Tyranny.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/State#Tyranny "Tyranny") tyranny and requires imprisoning the head, which can itself be risky as a trial is usually needed.

### Civil war[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Civil war") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Civil war")]

If a [![Image 37: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war breaks out, the territories where a revolting character has [![Image 38: Holding](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/88/Holdings.png/28px-Holdings.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Holding "Holding") holdings will always join the rebels. This includes territories within the capital [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region")!

## Strategy[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Strategy") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Holding&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Strategy")]

Holdings are important in the balance of power between the great families of your empire. If left unchecked, the heads of great families could try to buy more and more holdings to snowball wealth and power base. Mark the heads of great families to keep track of their activities, and find opportunities to revoke holdings; befriend them for increased [![Image 39: Loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1b/Loyalty.png/28px-Loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_loyalty "Character loyalty") loyalty.

You should be careful when you grant a holding to your ruler because of the [![Image 40: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption; if removing the corruption is critical, remember that setting wages to High (in the Economy tab) will remove the 5 [![Image 41: Corruption.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/75/Corruption.png/28px-Corruption.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Corruption "Corruption") corruption in 50 months (as well as remove the same amount from your officials and governors, provided they do not have traits which passively increase corruption gain).

Note that while every holding grants the same amount of power base, their income is highly variable according to the wealth of their territory. So if you play a monarchy, try to preempt big cities and highly productive territories, and consider giving your ruler holdings when their health status is "Near Death", as the increased corruption is not likely to last long. If you play a republic or a tribe, try to preserve equilibrium between the families since the ruling family will change at one point or another.

If a family becomes too powerful, you can revoke their holdings to try to contain them. If the situation becomes really serious, you can bring the head of family to a trial to imprison and proscribe him. But be aware that if you lose the trial, a civil war might occur.

Since the holding system gives literally no advantages in comparison with the landslide of the downsides, there is an alternative viable strategy to completely revoke all holdings from the very start of a campaign, including those from the current ruler. This greatly facilitates the flow of any Civil War if one is inevitable.

Decisions
Jump to navigation
Jump to search

This article has been verified for the current version (2.0) of the game.

The decision interface

In Imperator, each country has a set of  decisions that can be taken after a certain set of requirements have been met, visible under the  Nation Overview screen. All decisions whose potential requirements the country meets, even if the allow requirements are not met, can be found in the decision tab. The requirements and effects of each decision can be seen by hovering over the box or clicking on it to show the information on the panel to the side, even if the decision cannot currently be taken. Decisions whose requirements are fully met will be moved to the top and have a  green checkmark, and can be taken by clicking the decision and then on the Enact button at any time as long as the requirements are still met. Some decisions are unique to one nation or culture, while others can be taken by any country as long as it meets the requirements. Decisions can have a wide variety of possible effects, from adding modifiers or moving the capital to changing  government type, converting  religion, starting an event chain or even forming a new nation.

Formable nations[edit | edit source]
Main article: Formable nations


These decisions allow a country to embrace a new identity generally involving the unification of a tribal or cultural region or the restoration of a fallen kingdom, changing the name and often flag of the country as well as generally giving claims or other bonuses. Nation formation decisions have a large variety of conditions, but generally they require the country to have a particular culture or culture group and control the most important territories in the region. Formable nations exist in a series of tiers; it is generally not possible to form a country of lower tier.

Cultural decisions[edit | edit source]

Cultural decisions are targeted at a particular culture inside a country and accessible from the  Culture screen rather than the Nation Overview interface. These decisions allow a country to grant various privileges to a culture to increase the happiness of its pops, usually at the cost of pop output and/or the happiness of other cultures.

Integrated culture decisions[edit | edit source]

These cultural decisions are targeted at  integrated cultures and can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/cultural_decisions/integrated_culture_decisions.txt.

id
Promote Citizen Administrators

By earning our respect this people has been elevated to the the status of citizenship within our country. This separates them from other peoples from similar cultures, and makes them uniquely suited to act on our behalf as administrators and officers in their lands.

Expand 
id
Patronage

This culture has a rich and ancient heritage, a great treasure trove of literature and aesthetics that many in the capital find captivating. Let us patronize their scholars, writers, poets and artists, bringing our two peoples closer together.

Expand 
id
Municipal Self Rule

Trust and loyalty go hand in hand. By sanctioning the rights of one of the greater communities of this culture, giving them their own elected bodies, taxation rights, and even their own army, we can earn the loyalty of even those that remain outside of the municipal borders.

Expand 
id
Create Honor Guard

This is a people renowned for their fierce martial prowess. Let us create an honor guard for our ruler composed only of their finest warriors. This will give us the protection we need and will show the people itself how much we appreciate what they do for our country.

Expand 
Unintegrated culture decisions[edit | edit source]

These cultural decisions are targeted at unintegrated cultures and can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/cultural_decisions/non_integrated_culture_decisions.txt.

id
Right of Inheritance

By guaranteeing the rights of inheritance for a culture we are ensuring that the family fortunes of its members are not squandered or usurped by the citizens of our country.

Expand 
id
Right to Enter Legal Contracts

Extending the right to enter into legally binding contracts with our citizens and nobles will tie this culture closer to ours and allow them to participate in the economic life of our country.

Expand 
id
Protection against Torture

We will let it be decreed that torture cannot be used to exact a lawful confession from this people, except in cases of high treason.

Expand 
id
Right of Intermarriage

By granting the right to intermarry with our citizens we open up a way for the adherents of a culture to reach citizenship, if not for themselves at least for their children.

Expand 
id
Right to Lead

By opening up lower officer positions in units composed of their own people we can bring new opportunities and a sense of hope to the masses of this culture.

Expand 
id
Found Colony

The best way to ensure lasting loyalty in a region will always be to send those we trust to rule it for us.
Let us send a number of trusted [COUNTRY.GetAdjective] men and women to build a new life deep in foreign territory.

Expand 
Primary culture decisions[edit | edit source]

These cultural decisions are targeted at the primary culture and can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/cultural_decisions/primary_culture_decisions.txt.

id
Protected Land Rights

By ensuring that the property and land of our culture is protected from the state and its greedy citizens we can be sure that they will feel a deeper sense of belonging.

Expand 
id
Ease Restrictions on Citizenship

While Citizenship is restricted by birth it is possible for some to earn the right to be considered citizens. We should lighten these restrictions and rules and open more ways in which someone might aspire to the honor of citizenship.

Expand 
id
Right of Appeal

By ensuring that our citizens have the right to have their grievances tried in a court, rather than just decided by the local governor, we can give them a sense of safety and fairness.

Expand 
id
Exemption from Census Tax

It is not befitting that our treasured Citizens and Nobles, the people who the foundation of this country rests upon, pay the census taxes to which we subject our foreign subjects.

Expand 
id
Revoke Census Tax Exemption

The freedom from Census taxes is a hated and expensive reform. Revoking it will have us face the ire of our privileged classes but will surely help the treasury.

Expand 
Antigonid decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Antigonid Kingdom#Decisions


The  Antigonid Kingdom has a single unique country-specific decision that allows the Antigonids to secure their control over Asia if they manage to conquer Korinthos and maintain control over the most important cities of their Asian empire, which will prevent the The Antigonid Cause Wavers event from firing and breaking up the kingdom[1]. The decision can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/phrygia.txt.

id
Secure Antigonid Position

To ensure the strength of Antigonos' new eastern kingdom and the freedom of the Greeks; Kassandros' occupiers in Korinthos must be cast out, our southern gains kept safe from jealous Ptolemaic schemes, and the new capital of Antigoneia defended from Seleukid ambition.

Expand 
Carthaginian decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Carthage#Decisions


 Carthage has a number of country-specific flavour decisions about naval expansion, colonial development, and focusing on its mercenary traditions. All of them have since been superseded by tasks in the unique Carthaginian mission trees, and will not appear if the free Punic Wars DLC is enabled. The files can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/carthage.txt.

id
A New Carthage!

Hispania is a land of great riches. In order to continue to build a strong presence there we should endeavor to find a great new port on its southern coastline, a second capital on the other side of the sea. This new city, Qadr Hasht, will be to Hispania what our own capital was to the Punic settlers of Africa, a focal point for all colonies on the coastline and a clear leader for all Punic cities.

Expand 
id
Restructure Military Harbor of Carthage

The Carthaginian harbor has always been one of the greatest in the Punic world but as our Empire has grown, along with the city itself, a need has been born for a restructured and re-imagined harbor. We must spare no expense and expand the capabilities of our capital as a naval base and shipbuilding center.

Expand 
id
Mercenary Patron
Our history with the local mercenary companies is a long one, where we have often turned to their help to secure our goals. Particularly in the many drawn out wars in Sicily, they played an essential part in protecting our interests.

As we are already close friends with some of the mercenary warlords, we should be able to convince them to settle down in Carthage. With our patronage, the companies will flourish and we will secure their loyalty at a cheaper price in future conflicts.

Expand 
Government conversion decisions[edit | edit source]

Apart from the Tribal Reform missions and various country-specific events and mission tasks, all government changes are done through specific decisions. Swapping between government types of the same group can generally be done freely given the country meets the requirements, but switching between different groups is significantly more difficult (if possible at all). The possible government conversions are as follows:

Settled Tribes and Migratory Tribes can always convert between each other. Federated Tribes can also become Migratory Tribes, but the only way to form a Federated Tribe is through certain formable nation decisions.
Settled and Federated Tribes can reform into either an Autocratic Monarchy or a Democratic Republic.
An Autocratic Monarchy can reform into one of the more advanced monarchy types (Aristocratic, Stratocratic, Plutocratic, or Theocratic Monarchy). These four can interconvert between each other, but cannot go back to becoming an Autocratic Monarchy.
Similarly, a Democratic Republic can reform into one of the more advanced republic types (Aristocratic, Oligarchic, Plutocratic, or Theocratic Republic). These four can also interconvert between each other, but cannot go back to becoming an Democratic Republic.
Any of the four advanced republic types can become a Dictatorship to adopt a monarchical government form (through the Line of Succession  Oratory Inventions).
Any of the five base monarchy types (Autocratic, Aristocratic, Stratocratic, Plutocratic, or Theocratic Monarchy), as well as Dictatorships, can become an Empire.
All Monarchical governments except for Autocratic monarchies and Dictatorships can become an Imperial Cult.

Other conversions are possible through special country-specific events or missions. These decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/conversions.txt.

id
Abandon Sedentary Lifestyle

Times are changing and this land can no longer support our great tribe. It is time to once more embrace the traditions of our ancestors, and prepare for migrations to more peaceful and fertile lands.

Expand 
id
Form Tribal Kingdom

We have found a good home, it is time we settle down and adopt a more sedentary lifestyle. Let us proclaim ourselves a kingdom, separating ourselves from the migratory tribes to which we once belonged.

Expand 
id
Embrace Autocracy

[COUNTRY.GetRuler.GetName], inspired by the Kings and Tyrants of foreign lands, might exert greater authority by discarding the tribal trappings of our ancestors.

Expand 
id
Embrace Democracy

The Chiefs, Elders, and Magistrates of [COUNTRY.GetName] believe that adopting a more constitutional form of democracy will allow us to prosper fully.

Expand 
id
Investigate Tribal Reform

It is time for our people to consider new methods of rule. Foreign ideals flood our country - can we truly remain a nomadic people?

Expand 
id
Adopt Plutocratic Monarchy

The lure of profit and prosperity is something we must enshrine into our very constitution; abandoning the trappings of lesser kingdoms.

Expand 
id
Embrace an Aristocratic Society

Those of noble blood should not be required to mingle with commoners...

Expand 
id
Adopt Stratocratic Monarchy

The [COUNTRY.GetAdjective] people yearn to show the world their might. By stratifying our very society in a militarized hierarchy, we shall show the glory of [COUNTRY.GetName] to all!

Expand 
id
Proclaim Theocratic Monarchy

The glory of the divine shall shine upon [COUNTRY.GetRuler.GetName] - the [COUNTRY.GetAdjective] people are truly blessed.

Expand 
id
Enact Plutocratic Republic

The lure of profit and prosperity is something we must enshrine into our very constitution; abandoning the trappings of lesser nations.

Expand 
id
Promote Oligarchy

Constitutionalizing the inherent superiority of those who have, versus those who do not have; will ensure that [COUNTRY.GetName] maintains a prosperous government.

Expand 
id
Enshrine Aristocracy

The ability to rule clearly runs in the family. Whilst democracy serves us well, perhaps we might ensure that the plebeian masses are prevented from interfering in government.

Expand 
id
Proclaim Theocratic Republic

By the help of the divine, and with the zeal of her people, [COUNTRY.GetName] shall endure for all eternity!

Expand 
id
Imperial Ambition

[COUNTRY.GetRuler.GetName], resplendent in the glory of conquest, has eclipsed all other petty Kingdoms; a new title is needed for the ruler of the [COUNTRY.GetAdjective] Empire.

Expand 
id
Divine Sanction

Few are in denial that [COUNTRY.GetRuler.GetName] is blessed. It follows, therefore, that such divine heritage can only be the property of a divine being.

Expand 
id
Codify State Lands

The division of land and property in our capital remains muddled and impenetrable. Key plots must be ceded to the chief, and their collective ownership codified.

Expand 
Deluxe Edition Alexander's Mausoleum decisions[edit | edit source]

If the Hellenistic World Flavor Pack DLC is enabled, a country that has seized Alexander's body to rebury it in a different location through the Deluxe Edition Hellenistic body events can take a decision to build his mausoleum there, which gives various bonuses and allows a number of other events. The decision can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/dde_alexander_mausoleum_construction.txt.

id
Conqueror's Mausoleum

Now that we have triumphantly returned the body of the great conqueror, Alexander, to our lands, we should construct a mausoleum worthy of the memory of this great man.

Expand 
Epirus Flavor Pack Pyrrhus decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Epirus#Decisions


 Epirus has a couple of unique decisions available with the Epirus Flavor Pack DLC as part of the mission trees and event chains added in the content pack. The decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/dhe_dde_pyrrhus_decisions.txt.

id
Expand the Oracle of Dodona

The oracle of Dodona is the oldest in all of the Greek world, and was once among the most important religious centers of Hellas. The temple complex is centered around the ancient sacred oak tree of Zeus Naios, whose sanctuary covers a holy spring. Over the years the temple complex has fallen into shabbiness and decay as other more famous oracles have overshadowed it.
By building an amphitheater, renovating the temple of Zeus, and establishing regular festivals at Dodona, the site may once again take its rightful place among the hallowed homes of the gods.

Expand 
id
Install Greek Claimant

Once the illegitimate ruler has been vanquished, their rightful leader can at last take the crown.

Expand 
Dynamic Historical Event decisions[edit | edit source]

There are a several decisions relating to certain dynamic historical event chains, specifically for starting the wonder construction event chains as well as repairing the damage from volcano events. These decisions are found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/dhe_decisions.txt.

id
Pharos Lighthouse

Maritime journeys are never entirely safe, from pirates and wreckers to storms and tempests the Mediterranean is a dangerous place to be. Nonetheless it is the quickest way to travel for a grand city such as Alexandria on the Nile it is imperative that we are able to guarantee the safety of all ships and sailors.
The small island of Pharos just outside our port would be the perfect location for a great lighthouse to guide the way for all shipping. This venture would also put an end to the infamous wreckers that often roam the island.

Expand 
id
A Temple to the Muses

As the natural center of the Post-Alexandrine world the city of Alexandria already attracts many of the best and the brightest Hellenistic minds. We must embrace this role as the carrier of Greek culture and philosophy, and create a temple to the Muses themselves. In this Museion all texts ever written in Greek would be assembled, and any texts yet unknown to Greek scholarship translated.
While it has been the ambition of many a great monarch to assemble a personal library worthy of admiration and envy, few have dared aim as high as this. Should we succeed our memory will live on for centuries, if not millennia.

Expand 
id
Colossus of Rhodes

Many say we were blessed by Helios himself when we managed to stop Demetrios I the Besieger from taking our island and city. We fought valiantly to beat back the oppressing Antigonids, and with the help of the Ptolemies of Egypt we managed to secure our independence.

It is time to thank the god for his help in protecting the island, and celebrate our victory. Our architects have prepared the necessary documents for a massive statue in the honor of the god, that will forever protect the ports of Rhodes.

Expand 
id
Vesuvius Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Vesuvius was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand 
id
Aetna Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Aetna was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand 
id
Ararat Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Ararat was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand 
id
Methana Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Methana was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand 
id
Aragats Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Aragats was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand 
id
Argaeus Mons Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Argaeus Mons was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand 
id
Argaios Mons Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Argaios Mons was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand

Huge swaths of land around Ausara was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Expand

Bamni Restoration

Huge swaths of land around Bamni was destroyed as the volcano erupted. It is time we set aside the funds to start restoring the land so we can use it more effectively yet again.

Qarqar Restoration

Gender Equality decisions[edit | edit source]

If the Mixed Gender Rules option is checked when starting a new game, the player can toggle gender equality on and off in their country by decision. These decisions are found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/enshrine_equal_rights.txt.

id
Embrace Social Equity

For too long have we ignored a full half of our populace. Let us break the barriers of bygone tradition, and let all stand for office.

Expand 
id
Abolish Social Equity

The traditionalists whisper in dark corners, and the bearded ancients pour scorn on us. Our attempts at progressive governance must come to an end.

Expand 
Heirs of Alexander country decisions[edit | edit source]

With the Heirs of Alexander DLC, there are a few Diadochi decisions for installing characters in new or existing  subject states, intended mostly for  Egypt. These decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/hoa_decisions.txt.

id
Install Philokles

The Phoenician prince of Sidun came to us after the Antigonids refused to support his claim to the city's throne. Securing the seat for him is sure to be repaid by strategic naval dividends.

Expand 




id
Install King of Soloi

Our attempts to control the island of Cyprus were foiled by Antigonus the One-Eyed, but the old city-state of our Pasikratid allies may be resurrected - a kindness that will no doubt be generously rewarded.

Expand 
Heirs of Alexander treasure decisions[edit | edit source]

With the Heirs of Alexander DLC, there are two flavour decisions for compiling several treasures together into a single item that combines all of their effects, necessary for the unique Diadochi mission tree Hellenistic Empire. These decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/heirs_of_alexander.txt.

id
Panoply of Alexander the Great

In his absence, Alexander's arms and armor were present at the council of Babylon, and many continue to attribute great things to the objects that were close to this man. Should we be able to reassemble Alexander's panoply, we will be seen as credible custodians of his legacy.

Expand 
id
Anthologia Philosophike

In time people will wonder about the events of our age - let us collect the greatest written words of our era and preserve them for posterity.

Expand 
Iberian decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Iberia#Decisions


 Iberia has a single unique country-specific decision as part of its unique event chain to improve its capital of Meschistha-Harmozike. The decision can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/iberia_decisions.txt.

id
Pharnavaz' Improvements

Pharnavaz have long had several ideas on how to improve the capital, and it is time to put them into motion now that he is in charge of Iberia. No longer will foreigners look at us as some backwater region, but with envy.

Expand 
Indo-Greek decisions[edit | edit source]

 Parthia,  Bactria, and  Seleukid Empire have a decision to move their centre of power to Northern India and embrace greater cultural syncretism if they make conquests in the region, representing the historical creation of the Indo-Greek kingdoms. The decision can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/indo_greek_kingdom.txt.

id
An Indo-Greek Kingdom

The lure of friends, empires, and riches to the East leads many of our people to the realization that fighting over the scraps of Alexander's Empire, may not be a goal worth pursuing. Greater glory lies in tying our destiny to the land beyond the Indus.

Expand 
Magna Graecia decisions[edit | edit source]

These decisions are available only with the  Magna Graecia DLC, all revolving around investing in the production of certain territories. They can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/mg_decisions.txt.

id
Sulfurous Investments

A roaring trade in sulfur has begun sweeping the Mediterranean. Who are we to pass up such a lucrative opportunity? All that remains is to acquire a means of production.

Expand 
id
Expand Thasos Gold Mines

The ancient gold mines of Thasos have fallen on hard times. It behooves us to address this with haste.

Expand 
id
Restore Laureion Ironworks

The iron mines at Laureion shall serve us better than petty tin and copper. It is high time we reordered the focus of our people in Thorikos.

Expand 
Pontic decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Pontus#Decisions


 Pontus has a couple of country-specific decisions revolving around moving its capital and religious syncretism, if the country chose to embrace greater Hellenistic influences in its country-specific events. These decisions are only available, however, if Pontus was formed by  Kios by decision or through the event chain, not if the nation started as Pontus (even if the Mithridatic dynasty was embraced). They can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/pontic_decisions.txt.

id
Pontic Capital of Sinope

It is time to leave our old capital behind, and move our administration to Sinope. Settled at a defensive position of the northern Anatolian coastline, it is the perfect location from which to rule our nation.

Expand 
id
Embrace Greco-Pontic Omens

Our people look to many different deities, be it Zoroastrian, Cybelene or Hellenic. It is time for us to start celebrating and worshipping these gods and goddesses as well, rather than limiting ourselves to only those of our ancient past.

Expand 
Religious Conversion decisions[edit | edit source]

Apart from a few special events, religious conversion is handled through a unique decision for each religion, appearing if there is a character of that religion in the court. The requirements for conversion are identical for every religion, requiring among other things that the High Priest of the country and a majority of the non-slave population of the capital be of the religion that the country is converting to. These decisions are found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/religious_conversion.txt.

id
Embrace Buddhism

The teachings of Buddha are spreading rapidly in our lands. It is time to embrace his philosophy ourselves and start promoting these words among our subjects.

Expand 
id
Embrace Jainism

Jain teachings are spreading rapidly in our land and among the characters of our court. The time has come to embrace these deep philosophies and incorporate them into the very fabric of our state itself.

Expand 
id
Embrace Hinduism

Let the Vedas and the ancient teachings of old India be the official policy of our state, let us promote Hinduism among our subjects and at our court.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Kemetic Pantheon

The followers of Isis have become a prominent part of our society, and our people are looking to them for guidance in their daily lives. It is time we put our old traditions behind and accept their teachings.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Hellenic Pantheon

We have seen temples and shrines to the many deities of the Greek Pantheon being raised in [COUNTRY.GetCapital.GetName] lately, and our citizens turning to their priests and oracles for their wisdom. Maybe it is time we all started looking to them for guidance?

Expand 
id
Embrace the Canaanite Pantheon

Melqart and his followers have been commonplace in [COUNTRY.GetCapital.GetName] since we first met those who worshiped him, but now we have seen a newfound interest in Ba'al, Astarte and the other deities as well. Traditions that was once an important part of our identity are being left behind as foreign exotic rituals are becoming a matter of course. It is time to accept the Canaanite Pantheon as our own.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Zalmoxian Faith

The teachings of great Zalmoxis has become more apparent and important among our young these last years. They look to his words on how to live their lives, and even some of our elders have been affected by this shift to the new faith. It is time the rest of us follow his ideas as well.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Armazic Pantheon

Be it the stories of the mystical Tir, the truth of Mihr or the greatness of Aramazd, we have heard it all lately. It seems all our old temples and shrines have been replaced by those of the Armazic pantheon, and similarly have our priests and priestesses.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Chaldean Pantheon

The ancient myths and legends of Ishtar and Gilgamesh have filled our halls and inspired our people. No longer do we listen to the priests of the [COUNTRY.GetReligion.GetName] faith, and their worthless traditions are put aside and replaced by the ancient rituals of the Babylonians, Sumerians and Akkadians.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Khaldic Faith

Our soldiers were the first to pick up the old Khaldic traditions, proving invaluable for morale and discipline as they were sent off to war. Now the rest of our people are looking to the ancient pantheon, picking up the traditions of old once more.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Cybelene Faith

The belief in the Mother Goddess has long been an important one next to all the other deities in our faith, but more recently the rest has seen a clear decline of interest. Cybelene, the Mother Goddess, has become the most important figure in our religious traditions and practices.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Druidic Class

Though the Druids have yet to become an official part of our faith, they have long played the informal role as advisors and shown guidance to our people. Maybe it is time to rid ourselves of our old shackles of traditions and faith, and truly welcome them into our society?

Expand 
id
Embrace the Iberic Pantheon

It seems the prophets and oracles of Endouellicus has foreseen a great future for our nation if we turn to his worship. The Iberic pantheon is already revered among our people, and our court officials advise us to embrace this new faith.

Expand 
id
Embrace Judaism

We have lately seen a large swathe of our people turn to the worship of only one god, the Judean one. Defying the polytheistic teachings of other faiths, the strength of their belief have convinced many of the lower class to turn their prayers to the Judean god.

Expand 
id
Embrace Zoroastrianism

The teachings of Zoroaster has permeated Persian society since he was given the wisdom of Ahura Mazda. The truth and inherent benevolence of the faith quickly spreads throughout [COUNTRY.GetName], and it is time for us to devote ourselves entirely to the one path.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Megalithic Traditions

Though the people of the Megalithic faith were once inspired and affected by their neighbors, they now in turn have affected us. Our people have started picking up the beliefs of the desert tribes, looking to Nanna Tala and Shaheded as much as they do any local deity.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Tuistic Faith

Our people have started looking to the teachings of the priests of Tuis and the traditions of the Germanic people for guidance in how to live their lives. Though they seemed strange and warlike at first, our people have adepted their customs to our own and familiarized them.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Heptadic Pantheon

The seven deities of the Scythian horsemen have become popular in our lands lately. Particularly the ancient Targitaos have seen large shrines built in his honor, and our people have turned to him as if he was our own forefather, similar to how the Scythians treat the deity.

Expand 
id
Embrace the Arabic Pantheon

The caravans has brought the Arabic pantheon with them into [COUNTRY.GetName], and now we see our people look to the guidance of the oracle of Ta'lab or asking for Manat to turn the tides of fate in their favor. It is time for us to take up their worship.

Expand 
id
Embrace Ritualistic Traditions

Animism and ancestor worship have become an important part of the [COUNTRY.GetAdjective] religious ceremonies lately, and we have seen old rituals and traditions flourish in addition to our [COUNTRY.GetReligion.GetName] faith. Maybe it is time to put aside some of these newer ideas and revert back to our ancient heritage?

Expand 
id
Embrace the Bon Faith

The ideas of the Tibetan Bon have quickly found a home in [COUNTRY.GetName], and our people have taken to prayers quicker than anyone could expect. Maybe it is time for us to put aside our [COUNTRY.GetReligion.GetName] faith and devote ourselves entirely to the Bon rituals?

Expand 
id
Embrace the Matrist Traditions

The Aestuian Mother Goddess has found a home in [COUNTRY.GetName], and our people are putting aside all our old rituals in favor of those in her honor. It is time we do the same.

Expand 
Roman decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Rome#Decisions


 Rome has a few country-specific decisions relating to expanding and embellishing various important areas in the city, as well as reorganizing conquered areas. These decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/rome.txt.

id
Expanding the Campus Martius
The Campus Martius plays an essential role in training our troops and inspire them to greatness. By expanding the training site we will have a greater capacity for recruiting and preparing new soldiers for warfare.

In addition we will build new temples that will make the Campus a place worth remembering. As they train, our men will be able to look to these places for valor and strength.

Expand 
id
Embellish the Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus
The Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus stands at the center of our faith and city. At the Capitoline Hill it stands as a testament to our rule, and the blessing of the heavens.

Embellishing and improving the temple further is sure to be a popular move among our people, and what better way to secure the boons of the gods?.

Expand 
id
Institute Corinth as Local Capital

Our wars in Greece have exacted a heavy toll on the area. A show of magnanimity is required to build confidence in our rule, and our advisors suggest that Corinth would be the ideal place for a concerted effort to implement a program of public works.

Expand 
Seleukid decisions[edit | edit source]
Main article: Seleukid Empire#Decisions


The  Seleukid Empire has two country-specific decisions around moving the capital to the city of Antiocheia (the former Antigoneia, capital of the  Antigonid Kingdom at start) and improving its nearby port of Seleukia Pieria. Both of them have since been superseded by tasks in the unique Seleukid mission trees, and will not appear if the Heirs of Alexander DLC is enabled. The decisions can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/seleucids.txt.

id
A Mediterranean Capital!

For too long the rightful heirs of Alexander's empire have been disconnected from the Macedonian heartland which bore the greatest conquest of history and all the fortunes of our people. Fertile Babylonia has served us well, but we must restore our ties with the lost lands to the west and establish a central point of governance on the Mediterranean if we are to succeed in re-claiming the lands of Megas Alexandros.

Expand 
id
A port for Antioch!

With a new focus towards the Mediterranean and the moving of the Seleukid capital to Syria the need for a new great port in Syria has grown ever more urgent. Let us pool our resources to expand the port of Seleuika Pieria and work for the establishment of Seleukid hegemony in the west.

Expand 
Slave Negotiation decisions[edit | edit source]

If the country is being overwhelmed by a slave revolt, a decision can be taken at any time to enter into negotiations and end the revolt by making concessions to the rebels. The decision can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/decisions/slave_negotiation.txt.

id
Negotiate with revolting Slaves

We must end the current unrest among the slaves quickly and at any price. If we allow them to settle in peace, promising them that no harm will come to them and helping them start new lives, we may convince them to lay down their arms.

Expand 
Notes[edit | edit source]
↑ Despite the decision's tooltip, the  Antigonid Kingdom does not actually need to maintain control of all the required cities after enacting the decision to prevent the event from firing.

# Events
============================================================
Title: Events - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

Events occur throughout the course of play of [Imperator: Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Rome "Imperator Rome"). There are a whole range of events in the game, which can result in positive, negative and mixed outcomes for a player's country. They can either take the form of a pop-up notification on the player's screen or may optionally appear in the Event Queue, and they might present a player with a choice between various options or simply inform the player that something has happened and let them understand the context and consequences. Events can be fired randomly as the result of a regular on action pulse or be triggered by an action, decision, mission, or as a followup to another event, possibly with time delays or from another country's actions.

## Contents

*   [1 Event pages](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events#Event_pages)

### Mission events[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Events&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Mission events") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Events&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Mission events")]

All mission events are sorted by patch/DLC, and their folders can be found in /ImperatorRome/game/events/mission_events.

#### 1.3 - Livy events[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Events&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 1.3 - Livy events") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Events&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 1.3 - Livy events")]

These events are stored in /ImperatorRome/game/events/mission_events/1.3 - Livy and relate to the mission trees added in [that patch](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Patch_1.3 "Patch 1.3").

*   [Generic infrastructure mission events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic_infrastructure_mission_events "Generic infrastructure mission events")

#### 1.3 - Punic wars events[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Events&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 1.3 - Punic wars events") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Events&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 1.3 - Punic wars events")]

These events are stored in /ImperatorRome/game/events/mission_events/1.3 - Punic Wars and relate to the mission trees added in the [Punic Wars](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Punic_Wars "Punic Wars") DLC.
