============================================================
# Missions
============================================================
Title: Missions - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/51/Carthage_mission_screen.png/450px-Carthage_mission_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Carthage_mission_screen.png)

Carthaginian mission trees available at start.

[![Image 2: Menu mission.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Menu_mission.png/28px-Menu_mission.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions")**Missions** are tasks that can be completed for certain bonuses and rewards ranging from [![Image 3: Fabricate claim cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim "Claim") claims and [![Image 4: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") pops to special modifiers or even gaining or releasing new and unique [![Image 5: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subjects and access to special [formable nations](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations"), providing optional direction for expansion and development as well as flavour and a historically-based narrative for countries that have more unique missions. Some missions, especially timed ones, can trigger events and some can lead to long and complex event chains with many different possible options and outcomes.

All missions are structured as part of a **mission tree**, with prerequisites that enforce a natural flow through the tasks or mutually exclusive paths to force the country to choose between different options as well as optional branches that can be completed for additional bonuses or ignored if it does not the fit the country's current goals. Some mission trees, particularly the generic ones, are highly versatile with the tree structure and task targets dynamically generated to fit the country and region's current circumstances. The current mission tree, or the queue of available trees if no mission tree is currently selected, can be seen and interacted with in the [![Image 6: Menu mission.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/bb/Menu_mission.png/28px-Menu_mission.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions")**Missions** screen.

## National Missions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: National Missions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: National Missions")]

[![Image 7](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d2/National_mission_map.png/600px-National_mission_map.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:National_mission_map.png)

Nations with unique mission trees at 450 AUC/304 BCE. 

 Hatched countries either potentially have access to 2 sets of national missions or do not start with access to national missions, but can gain access to them over the course of the game.

Certain countries have their own dedicated sets of mission trees exploring their culture and history, current situation, and specific ambitions. These are by far the most detailed and fleshed out mission trees, and all require a certain DLC to be enabled in order to appear ingame. Note that countries will still be able to take their national missions even after [forming another nation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations").

| Countries | Mission page | Mission trees | DLC required | Notes |

| [![Image 40: Flag of Thrace](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Thrace.png/20px-Thrace.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Thrace "Thrace")[Thrace](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Thrace "Thrace") | [Thracian missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Thracian_missions "Thracian missions") | 3 | [![Image 41: Banner Heirs of Alexander.jpg](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a5/Banner_Heirs_of_Alexander.jpg/40px-Banner_Heirs_of_Alexander.jpg)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Banner_Heirs_of_Alexander.jpg)[Heirs of Alexander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heirs_of_Alexander "Heirs of Alexander") | Most of these missions are also available to any [![Image 42: Menu government.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Menu_government.png/28px-Menu_government.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government")[monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") whose ruler has the [![Image 43: Blood of Lysimachus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5b/Blood_of_Lysimachus.png/28px-Blood_of_Lysimachus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Traits "Blood of Lysimachus") Blood of Lysimachus trait |

## Group Missions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Group Missions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Group Missions")]

[![Image 44](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ee/Group_mission_map.png/600px-Group_mission_map.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Group_mission_map.png)

Group with unique mission trees at 450 AUC/304 BCE. 

 Hatched countries either only have group missions without a particular DLC, do not start with access to the missions but can gain them by becoming independent, expanding, creating a local [formable nation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Formable_nations "Formable nations"), or completing some of their national missions, or potentially have access to multiple group mission trees. Note that this map is not completely comprehensive and it is possible for other countries to gain access to group missions under some circumstances.

Certain countries have access to specific "group" missions, which are available to many different countries based on their culture, location, and/or historical context.

| Mission Group | Mission page | Mission trees | DLC required | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Diadochi | [Diadochi missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diadochi_missions "Diadochi missions") | 1 | [![Image 45: Banner Heirs of Alexander.jpg](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a5/Banner_Heirs_of_Alexander.jpg/40px-Banner_Heirs_of_Alexander.jpg)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Banner_Heirs_of_Alexander.jpg)[Heirs of Alexander](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Heirs_of_Alexander "Heirs of Alexander") | Available to all five [![Image 46: Flag of Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Antigonid_Kingdom.png/20px-Antigonid_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[![Image 47: Flag of Egypt](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/37/Egypt.png/20px-Egypt.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Egypt "Egypt")[![Image 48: Flag of Macedon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Macedon.png/20px-Macedon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Macedon "Macedon")[![Image 49: Flag of Seleukid Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c4/Seleukid_Empire.png/20px-Seleukid_Empire.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Seleukid_Empire "Seleukid Empire")[![Image 50: Flag of Thrace](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Thrace.png/20px-Thrace.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Thrace "Thrace") Diadochi kingdoms as well as [![Image 51: Flag of Epirus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/63/Epirus.png/20px-Epirus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Epirus "Epirus")[Epirus](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Epirus "Epirus") after completing a certain number of their national missions. |
| Hellenistic | [Greek missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Greek_missions "Greek missions") | 4 | None | The minor tags of the Greek 'regions' of the western Mediterranean, Magna Graecia, Greece, and the Black Sea have a single mission tree each. |

## Generic Missions[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Generic Missions") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Generic Missions")]

_Main article: [Generic missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic\_missions "Generic missions")_

There are 3 generic missions available to all countries in the game:

*   **[The Matter of "Region"](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic_missions#The_Matter_of_.5BRegion.5D "Generic missions")** - the generic conquest mission directs the country towards the conquest, integration, and assimilation of a neighbouring region.
*   **[Pearl of [Region]/Growth of [Region]/Stabilize and Grow [Region]](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic_missions#Infrastructure_Mission "Generic missions")** - the generic infrastructure mission leads to building up and improving the infrastructure, economy, and productivity of one of the country's regions.
*   **[Tribal Reform](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Generic_missions#Tribal_Reform "Generic missions")** - the tribal reform mission allows a country to reform from a [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") government form into either a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") or a [republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic").

## Mechanics[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Mechanics") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Mechanics")]

### Mission Tasks[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Mission Tasks") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Mission Tasks")]

#### Potential[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Potential") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Potential")]

Not all tasks will always appear in the mission tree, particularly for the generic conquest and infrastructure missions. Mission tasks will only appear if their **potential** conditions are fulfilled, usually corresponding to if the particular branch was selected or if the task is at all relevant to the country's current situation. Some tasks are mutually exclusive with each other, with only one out of a possible set ever appearing at a time depending on the exact circumstances (or possibly being chosen randomly). Tasks whose potential are not met do not appear on the mission tree screen and cannot be completed for their effects and rewards; if they are listed as a prerequisite for another task, this requirement is ignored.

#### Prerequisites[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Prerequisites") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Prerequisites")]

Other than a few starting missions, most tasks have **prerequisites** that need to be completed before they can be taken or completed, indicated in the interface by an arrow going from the prerequisite to the task that it unlocks. For the purposes of proceeding along the tree, prerequisites also count as completed if they have been bypassed or do not meet their potential (therefore not appearing on the tree at all).

#### Bypassing[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Bypassing") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Bypassing")]

Tasks can be **bypassed** under certain conditions, usually the task requirements either being no longer possible or relevant, or if a mutually exclusive path is taken. Bypassed tasks can be easily identified by their statue icon being replaced with a heap of rubble; when bypassed, a task is considered completed for most intents and purposes (in particular for the purposes of prerequisites) except that the country does not get the task's rewards.

#### Completion and Effects[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Completion and Effects") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Completion and Effects")]

When a mission's requirements are met and its prerequisites have been finished or bypassed, its tile in the mission tree screen will turn from grey to peach and can be taken simply by clicking on it. Most tasks will **complete** and give their effects immediately, but some missions are **timed tasks** (indicated by the progress bar at the bottom of the tile) which may take anywhere from a few months to up to two years. Timed tasks may trigger effects when they begin and/or end and frequently have special events that can trigger while they are running as well, and do not count as completed until it has run its entire course. Typically, only one timed task can be running at a time, though instantly completed tasks can still usually be taken while a timed task is running. A few timed missions can fail under certain circumstances, which stops the progress timer and may prevent the country from retaking the task, blocking them from going down that path.

### Mission Trees[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mission Trees") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mission Trees")]

#### Generation[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Generation") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Generation")]

While many of the more country-specific mission trees are largely fixed, the more generic ones are more dynamically **generated** with different branches and tasks as well as the regions, provinces, or nations they target being selected either randomly or based on the country's current circumstances. Some of the higher-level target selection, such as the region target of the generic conquest and infrastructure missions, is done **on potential** when the mission first appears in the selection screen; this will not be reset unless the mission is taken and completed or aborted, or if the country no longer meets the requirements (and it therefore loses it from its pool) and then somehow regains it. Most of the lower-level target selection, such as the conquest targets for the generic conquest mission is instead done **on start**, i.e. when the mission is selected and started. Once a mission tree is generated and started, it will usually not change the composition of its structure or the target of its tasks while running.

#### Selection[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Selection") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Selection")]

Each country can only have a single mission tree active at a time. When first starting the game, or after completing or aborting a mission tree, the country will be presented between a choice of up to **4** possible mission trees to take, drawn from the pool of mission trees for which it meets the requirements; nation or culture-specific missions are more likely to appear than generic ones, though it is rare that a country will be eligible for more than 4 mission trees at a time. Each mission can be selected to display a description and a brief overview of the goals of the mission, and then started with the **Start Mission** button. The available missions are redrawn from the pool each time a mission is completed or aborted, making it possible to take and then abort a mission if the desired mission is possible but does not currently appear. Newly available missions will also automatically appear in the pool after a few days if the country previously did not meet the requirements for any mission trees at all (which is often the case for small nations that are [![Image 52: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations") subject to another power).

#### Structure[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Structure") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Structure")]

Most mission trees are structured to have a generally linear flow from top to bottom, with at least some of the missions in each row required to start the ones following, and the final completion tasks at the bottom of the tree. Some have varying degrees of branching where the country may be able to choose from a number of different options and outcomes to focus on, with some being mutually exclusive and bypassing the other possible options. In addition to those required to progress down the tree, there are also often many optional tasks and even entire optional branches that do not need to be completed in order to finish the tree, but may provide extra bonuses if the country takes the additional effort to complete them.

#### Completion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Completion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Completion")]

All mission trees have certain **final tasks**, usually those at the end of the main branch, that need to be completed before finishing the tree; the possible final tasks can be viewed by hovering over the greyed-out **Finish Mission** button, and they are invariable mutually exclusive if there are multiple possible finishers. Finishing the mission tree can be done at any time after any single final task is completed by pressing the Finish Mission button, which may also give some additional effects or rewards. Waiting to finish the mission tree, for instance to complete various optional tasks, is allowed and there is generally no time limit on how long a country is allowed to take to finish a mission tree. Most mission trees can only be taken once and will disappear from the pool forever once completed, though some are repeatable and can be taken over and over as long as their requirements are met.

#### Abortion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&veaction=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Abortion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Missions&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Abortion")]

Mission trees can be **aborted** at any time using the **Abort Mission** button, and some missions will also be automatically aborted if the country no longer meets certain requirements, though this is generally rare. Aborting a mission tree will prevent the country from proceeding down any remaining tasks, though most currently collected rewards and modifiers will usually be kept and the country will be allowed to select a new mission tree in the mission selection screen. Some missions, particularly the generic conquest mission, also have a cost to aborting that makes extensive use of mission abortion to get the desired target less viable. Aborted mission trees can generally usually be restarted from the beginning if the country still meets the requirements, though most have a cooldown of **20** years after aborting before they can be restarted.


============================================================
# Attributes
============================================================
Title: Attributes - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

Each [character](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character "Character") has four **attributes**, each with their own value. These attributes determine how well characters perform as the [![Image 1: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") ruler and when appointed to [![Image 2: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") positions and can also affect various events triggers and outcomes, with higher attributes almost always better. Each attribute has a base value that is mostly accumulated over the course of a character's childhood, though it can still change during adulthood through various events and mission tasks as well as when adopting certain [![Image 3: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and [![Image 4: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") military traditions, and then are modified by a character's [traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Traits "Traits") and modifiers.

## Martial[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Martial") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Martial")]

[![Image 5: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial")**Martial** represents the character's ability to fight and lead troops. Characters with high martial skills make excellent generals.

For each point of [![Image 6: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial, a character gets the following bonuses:

If the character is a [![Image 7: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Ruler (or co-ruler/consort with a higher attribute), the following bonuses are also applied to the country for every point of [![Image 8: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial:

If the character is a [![Image 9: Commander.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Commander.png/28px-Commander.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commander "Commander") commander, the following bonuses are also applied to the cohorts they command for every point of [![Image 10: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial:

An army will also get [![Image 11: Chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Chance.png/28px-Chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Chance.png)**+1** for dice rolls in a [battle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Land_warfare "Land warfare") for every **2** martial more the commander has compared to the enemy commander, and [![Image 12: Chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Chance.png/28px-Chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Chance.png)**+1** for dice rolls in a [siege](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Siege "Siege") for every **5** martial of its commander.

If the character is a [![Image 13: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") admiral, the following bonuses are also applied to the ships they command for every point of [![Image 14: Martial.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial "Martial") martial:

An army will also get [![Image 15: Chance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Chance.png/28px-Chance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Chance.png)**+1** for dice rolls in a [battle](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Naval_warfare "Naval warfare") for every **2** martial more the admiral has compared to the enemy admiral.

## Finesse[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Finesse") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Finesse")]

[![Image 16: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse")**Finesse** represents the character's skill in disciplines requiring a high attention to detail. High finesse characters make excellent governors.

For each point of [![Image 17: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse, a character gets the following bonuses:

If the character is a [![Image 18: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Ruler (or co-ruler/consort with a higher attribute), the following bonuses are also applied to the country for each point of [![Image 19: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse:

If the character is a [![Image 20: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") governor, the following bonuses are also applied to their governorship [region](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Region "Region") for each point of [![Image 21: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse:

*   [![Image 22: Population output](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Local_population_output.png/28px-Local_population_output.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Output_modifier "Population output") Population Output: **+1.5%**

[Governor policies](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") are also scaled by the Governor's [![Image 23: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") Finesse, according to the following formula:

![Image 24: {\displaystyle {\text{Effective Policy Modifier}}={\frac {{\text{Governor Finesse}}+1}{10}}\cdot {\text{Base Policy Modifier}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/01ae0533664bc0c5ecedbe5ac5b38997f98e7f74)
If the character is a [![Image 25: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") construction leader for a [![Image 26: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder") great wonder, the wonder will have a [![Image 27: Total workpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ce/Total_workpower.png/28px-Total_workpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Workpower "Workpower")**+5%** bonus to overall workpower for each point of [![Image 28: Finesse.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Finesse "Finesse") finesse.

## Charisma[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Charisma") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Charisma")]

[![Image 29: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma")**Charisma** represents the character's ability to charm and persuade others. High Charisma characters often have an advantage when interacting with foreign countries.

For each point of [![Image 30: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") charisma, the character gets the following bonuses:

If the character is a [![Image 31: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Ruler (or co-ruler/consort with a higher attribute), the following bonuses are also applied to the country for every point of [![Image 32: Charisma.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Charisma "Charisma") charisma:

## Zeal[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Zeal") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Zeal")]

[![Image 33: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal")**Zeal** represents the character's ability to inspire faith in other characters, and also in calling upon the favor of the gods.

For each point of [![Image 34: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal, the character gets the following bonuses:

If the character is a [![Image 35: Leader.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/50/Leader.png/28px-Leader.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Ruler "Ruler") Ruler (or co-ruler/consort with a higher attribute), the following bonuses are also applied to the country for every point of [![Image 36: Zeal.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Zeal "Zeal") zeal:

## Other effects[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other effects") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other effects")]

### Researchers[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Researchers") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Researchers")]

The effectiveness of [![Image 37: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Office.png "Position") researchers is determined by their attribute level in the field they are assigned to. Each corresponding attribute point that the researcher has will give a [![Image 38: Technology speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ed/Technology_speed.png/28px-Technology_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Technology "Technology speed")**+10%** research speed to progress in that field of [advances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Advance "Advance"), which can have a very significant effect on how quickly a nation's technology level advances.

### Government Offices[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Government Offices") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Government Offices")]

Each [![Image 39: Office.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/78/Office.png/28px-Office.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office")[government office](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Office "Office") is associated with a particular attribute, and the effectiveness of a character in that office depends largely on their skill in that attribute. Unlike other positions, this effectiveness is multiplied first by the character's [![Image 40: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship level, which means that a character with lower attributes but high [![Image 41: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship can still outperform, at least in the short term, a character with higher attributes but lower [![Image 42: Statesmanship](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/62/Statesmanship.png/28px-Statesmanship.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Statesmanship "Statesmanship") statesmanship.

### Tribal succession[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Tribal succession") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Attributes&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Tribal succession")]

Succession in a [tribal](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribal "Tribal") country is determined by the attributes of the other [![Image 43: Clan chiefs.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fa/Clan_chiefs.png/28px-Clan_chiefs.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Clan_chief "Clan chief") clan chiefs when a ruler dies or is deposed. Each attribute has the following weight when determining a clan chief's succession score:


============================================================
# Diplomacy
============================================================
Title: Diplomacy - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy

Markdown Content:
This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

[![Image 1](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ed/Diplomacy_screen.png/500px-Diplomacy_screen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Diplomacy_screen.png)

The main diplomacy interface

In Imperator, **diplomacy** involves the actions and treaties that countries conduct and sign with each other, ranging from a simple improvement of relations to demands for tribute and fully-fledged alliances and defensive pacts. The most important facet of diplomacy is a country's [![Image 2: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**opinion** of other countries, which significantly affects what diplomatic actions are possible and whether they look for cooperation and friendly relations or pursue a more aggressive policy. Most diplomatic actions and modifiers can be seen and accessed through the [![Image 3: Menu diplomacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Menu_diplomacy.png/28px-Menu_diplomacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_diplomacy.png)**Diplomacy** screen.

## Opinion[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Opinion") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Opinion")]

Every country has a specific [![Image 4: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**opinion** value of every other country ranging from **-200** to **+200**, which represents how well that country sees the other country and is one of the most important factors in how they act towards each other. Countries with a high opinion of another country will be much more receptive to diplomatic and trade requests and will likely pursue a cooperative attitude, while countries with a low opinion will usually refuse requests or demands, break existing treaties, and regard the other country as a rival or enemy. Opinion values go only one way; a country's opinion of another country is not necessarily the same and often different from the second country's opinion of the first, and it is entirely possible that one first country is friendly to the other while the second is hostile.

A country's opinion of another country is determined by the sum of all [![Image 5: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**opinion modifiers** that the first country has towards the second. Some opinion modifiers are based on some persistent property of the two countries and their relationship, such as having the same [![Image 6: Religious power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ea/Religious_power.png/28px-Religious_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Religion "Religion") religion, being in an [![Image 7: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") alliance, or having a particular [diplomatic stance](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_stance "Diplomatic stance"); these modifiers have a fixed value and will remain as long as the source of the opinion modifier is still valid. Other opinion modifiers, usually given by [events](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Events "Events"), [missions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Missions "Missions"), or other actions - particularly accumulating [![Image 8: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion - are temporary modifiers and have a specific **yearly decay** that reduces the effect of the modifier each year, until it reaches 0 and disappears. The [Relation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Relation_Actions) diplomatic actions category allows a country to directly influence another country's opinion of it, spending [![Image 9: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") gold to improve relations or [![Image 10: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion to worsen them, though there is a limit to how much a country's opinion can be improved through diplomacy alone.

## Diplomatic relations[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Diplomatic relations") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Diplomatic relations")]

Most [treaties](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Treaties "Treaties"), such as [![Image 11: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") alliances, [![Image 12: Defensive league.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Defensive_league.png/28px-Defensive_league.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Defensive_league "Defensive league") defensive leagues, and [![Image 13: Guaranteeing.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Guaranteeing.png/28px-Guaranteeing.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Guarantee "Guarantee") guarantees, as well as many types of [![Image 14: Vassalized.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8b/Vassalized.png/28px-Vassalized.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations")[subject states](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations "Subject nations"), take up one of a country's [![Image 15: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_relations "Diplomatic relations")**diplomatic relations** slots. Every country has a cap on the amount of diplomatic relations it can have normally, with each relation over the limit giving a [![Image 16: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**−10%** political influence modifier. This diplomatic relations cap forces countries to carefully choose their allies and to some extent subjects to be as useful and reliable as possible, rather than simply accepting every treaty they can and creating a vast network of clients to easily overpower its rivals.

The most important modifiers for a country's diplomatic relations cap are listed below:

| _Achaean Heritage_ (cultural) | **-1** |
| _Religious State heritage_ (mission created) | **-1** |

## Diplomatic reputation[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Diplomatic reputation") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Diplomatic reputation")]

A country's [![Image 55: Diplomatic reputation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Diplomatic_reputation.png/28px-Diplomatic_reputation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_reputation "Diplomatic reputation")**diplomatic reputation** represents the effectiveness of its diplomats and affects how likely a country's diplomatic proposals will be accepted. Generally, every point of diplomatic reputation gives **+1** point towards another country's acceptance of any diplomatic action. It is possible for diplomatic reputation to become negative, at which point it will make other countries less likely to accept diplomatic offers.

In addition, every point of [![Image 56: Diplomatic reputation.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/21/Diplomatic_reputation.png/28px-Diplomatic_reputation.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_reputation "Diplomatic reputation") diplomatic reputation above 0 gives the following effects:

The main sources of diplomatic reputation are listed below:

## Diplomatic range[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Diplomatic range") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Diplomatic range")]

A country can only use most diplomatic actions on other states within [![Image 91: Diplomatic range.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/23/Diplomatic_range.png/28px-Diplomatic_range.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_range "Diplomatic range")**diplomatic range**, except for ending or declaring wars and asking for or canceling military access. Another country is considered to be within diplomatic range if the distance from the between the two closest border territories is within the current country's diplomatic range value. A country's diplomatic range is significantly increased by [![Image 92: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") inventions and [country rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank"); this means that it is possible for a larger power to initiate diplomacy with a smaller, somewhat distant country even if the converse is not possible. In addition, **all neighbouring** countries are considered to be within diplomatic range, regardless of the distance to their capital.

## Diplomatic stance[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Diplomatic stance") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Diplomacy&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Diplomatic stance")]

Each country chooses a _default_ diplomatic stance from five choices. Changing stance costs a base of 30 [![Image 93: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence") Political Influence, increasing by **+10%** for each previous stance change done by the current ruler.

These are as follow:

| Diplomatic stance | Modifiers | Opinion modifiers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 94: Neutral icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/5/5d/Stance_neutral.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Stance_neutral.png "Neutral icon") Neutral A Neutral diplomatic stance means there is no active focus towards any particular diplomatic end. This lack of direction limits efficiency in pursuing specific goals but it also allows greater flexibility in making new agreements and alliances. | [![Image 95: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_relations "Diplomatic relations") Diplomatic Relations: **+1** | None |
| [![Image 96: Appeasing icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/5/53/Stance_appeasement.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Stance_appeasement.png "Appeasing icon") Appeasing An Appeasing stance is focused on presenting a more palatable image abroad while strengthening ties with existing friends. Some would say it a way of making amends for past sins. | [![Image 97: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggressive Expansion Decay: **+0.20%** [![Image 98: Improve relation cost](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/03/Improve_relation_cost_modifier.png/28px-Improve_relation_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Improve_Opinion.2FCancel_Improve_Opinion "Improve relation cost") Improve Relation Cost: **−25%** | [![Image 99: Alliance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e8/Alliance.png/28px-Alliance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Alliance "Alliance") Allies: [![Image 100: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**+25** Others: [![Image 101: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**+10** |
| [![Image 102: Mercantile icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/b/b1/Stance_trading.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Stance_trading.png "Mercantile icon") Mercantile A Mercantile diplomatic stance means that all efforts are focused on making new trade deals as well as increasing the profitability of existing ones. | [![Image 103: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Commerce "Commerce") National Commerce Income: **+25%** | States with [![Image 104: Mercantile stance](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b1/Stance_trading.png/28px-Stance_trading.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_stance "Mercantile stance") same Diplomatic Stance: [![Image 105: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**+30** Others: [![Image 106: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**+20** |
| [![Image 107: Bellicose icon](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/4/4e/Stance_warmongering.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Stance_warmongering.png "Bellicose icon") Bellicose A Bellicose diplomatic stance means that all diplomatic efforts are focused on justification for expansion and intimidation of nearby countriess. | [![Image 108: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") Aggressive Expansion Impact: **-10%** [![Image 109: Claim fabrication speed](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Fabricate_claim_speed.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim#Fabricating_claims "Claim fabrication speed") Claim Fabrication Speed: **+1** [![Image 110: Warscore cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Warscore_cost.png/28px-Warscore_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_score_cost "War score cost") War score Cost: **−10%** | Neighbours: [![Image 111: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**−20** Others: [![Image 112: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**−10** |
