### Tributary[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Tributary") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Tributary")]

Perhaps the most basic subject type in the game is the **tributary**. This is a loose relationship where the subject is simply looking for protection in exchange for tribute. Unlike most other subject types, the subject can at any time cancel a tributary relationship if there is no [![Image 102: Truce.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Truce.png/28px-Truce.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Truce "Truce") truce, with the only risk being that the former overlord will get a [![Image 103: Casus belli.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d3/Casus_belli.png/28px-Casus_belli.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Casus_belli "Casus belli") casus belli for the slight on their honor. This will happen for AI-controlled countries if there any of the following conditions are met:

Tributaries with very high [![Image 104: Subject loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Subject_loyalty.png/28px-Subject_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty") loyalty may instead approach their overlord to pursue a closer relationship and become a client state. This event can only trigger once per country.

### Feudatory[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Feudatory") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Feudatory")]

A **feudatory** is a state that has obligated itself to follow the lead of a larger and more influential country in the same culture group. Historically these are countries that would often have been part of some sort of league with their overlord’s other subjects, in many ways this is a privileged subject type, if one with large obligations.

Feudatories provide [![Image 105: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower to their overlord and are expected to take part in their wars. As the only subject type that can be called to war but does not cost a [![Image 106: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_relations "Diplomatic relations") diplomatic relations slot, feudatories are one of the strongest subject types and building a large network of feudatories can substantially increase a nation's strength, but this is limited as feudatories can normally only be made of countries in the same culture group. Historical examples are the [![Image 107: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome") Socii, the Punic cities of North Africa under [![Image 108: Flag of Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0d/Carthage.png/20px-Carthage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage")[Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage"), or the city leagues in Aegean under the [![Image 109: Flag of Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Antigonid_Kingdom.png/20px-Antigonid_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[Antigonids](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonids "Antigonids").

In order to make a feudatory diplomatically or through a peace treaty, the potential overlord must own be at least [![Image 110: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories")**20** territories, while the potential subject cannot own more than [![Image 111: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories")**10** territories.

If a country has the [![Image 112: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Proportional Agreements [![Image 113: Oratory power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") Oratory invention, small countries (with less than [![Image 114: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories")**12** territories) that are [![Image 115: Guaranteeing.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Guaranteeing.png/28px-Guaranteeing.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Guarantee "Guarantee") guaranteed may occasionally request feudatory status, which can be an important method of diplomatic expansion.

Feudatories can also be created from the Autonomous Enclave mission task. This feudatory will become a Theocratic Monarchy and only have 1 territory.

### Tribal vassal[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Tribal vassal") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Tribal vassal")]

A **tribal vassal** is a tribal kingdom, chiefdom or federation that has a close relationship to a nearby stronger civilized state. For the tribal state, this means a significantly easier route towards civilizing, as it will increase both the countrywide level of [![Image 116: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") civilization and the growth of it in all their territories. The tribal state in turn pays with its [![Image 117: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower to the overlord who, will also promise to protect them. Historical subjects of this type would be the Numidian kingdoms subject to [![Image 118: Flag of Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0d/Carthage.png/20px-Carthage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage")[Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage") and various [![Image 119: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome") clients on the later imperial border. When a vassal tribe adopts a civilized form of government, it will become a tributary.

### Client state[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Client state") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Client state")]

**Client states** are in some ways more tightly integrated with their overlord, but unlike the feudatories they are not necessarily of the same culture group and they do not enjoy a privileged status in the same way, paying a large part of their [![Image 120: Wealth](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8f/Cost.png/28px-Cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Cost.png "Wealth") income instead of providing [![Image 121: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") manpower. A client king is separated from a governor mostly by his level of autonomy and having local ties to the ruled country. Client states can be created by subjugating existing countries like most other subject types, converted from loyal tributaries through an occasional event, or released from the country's currently owned [provinces](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Province "Province") in the [![Image 122: Menu overview.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f1/Menu_overview.png/28px-Menu_overview.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_overview.png) Nation Overview.

### Satrapy[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Satrapy") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Satrapy")]

**Satrapies** are a special type of subject that is only available if the junior party has access to the [![Image 123: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[Persian traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Persian_traditions "Persian traditions") military tradition tree. The fundamental unit of organization in Persia since the time of the [![Image 124: Flag of Mamertine Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Placeholder_Flag.png/20px-Placeholder_Flag.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Media "Media")[Median](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Media "Media") and [![Image 125: Flag of Achaemenid Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/dc/Achaemenid_Empire.png/20px-Achaemenid_Empire.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Achaemenid_Empire "Achaemenid Empire")[Achaemenid](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Achaemenid_Empire "Achaemenid Empire") empires, a satrap is in some ways similar to a governor but is expected to have greater authority, bigger obligations, and a more imposing realm somewhat comparable to a client state. As having powerful satraps is an expectation in the east a few of them will also help with maintaining the [![Image 126: Legitimacy.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/25/Legitimacy.png/28px-Legitimacy.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Legitimacy.png) legitimacy for their overlord kingdoms. Satrapies are notoriously independent minded and troublesome, however, and events will periodically require interactions to keep satraps happy.

Since the conquest of the Achaemenid Empire almost all the old Persian satrapies are now ruled by Macedonians, with satrapies at the start date including [![Image 127: Flag of Bactria](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/3a/Bactria.png/20px-Bactria.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Bactria "Bactria")[Bactria](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Bactria "Bactria"), [![Image 128: Flag of Parthia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/4f/Parthia.png/20px-Parthia.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Parthia "Parthia")[Parthia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Parthia "Parthia"), and [![Image 129: Flag of Arachosia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/77/Arachosia.png/20px-Arachosia.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Arachosia "Arachosia")[Arachosia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Arachosia "Arachosia") under the [![Image 130: Flag of Seleukid Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c4/Seleukid_Empire.png/20px-Seleukid_Empire.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Seleukid_Empire "Seleukid Empire")[Seleukid Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Seleukid_Empire "Seleukid Empire") and [![Image 131: Flag of Cappadocia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/38/Cappadocia.png/20px-Cappadocia.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cappadocia "Cappadocia")[Cappadocia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Cappadocia "Cappadocia") under the [![Image 132: Flag of Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Antigonid_Kingdom.png/20px-Antigonid_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom"); [![Image 133: Flag of Sardis](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/e1/Sardis.png/20px-Sardis.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Sardis "Sardis")[Sardis](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Sardis&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sardis (page does not exist)") and [![Image 134: Flag of Phrygia (Satrapy)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f3/Phrygia_%28Satrapy%29.png/20px-Phrygia_%28Satrapy%29.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Phrygia_(Satrapy) "Phrygia (Satrapy)")[Phrygia](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Phrygia_(Satrapy) "Phrygia (Satrapy)") can also be released as satrapies of [![Image 135: Flag of Thrace](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/42/Thrace.png/20px-Thrace.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Thrace "Thrace")[Thrace](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Thrace "Thrace") if the [![Image 136: Flag of Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Antigonid_Kingdom.png/20px-Antigonid_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[collapses](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diadochi_events#diadochi_flavor.8 "Diadochi events"). The [![Image 137: Flag of Hellenistic Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Argead_Empire.png/20px-Argead_Empire.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Hellenistic_Empire "Hellenistic Empire")[Hellenistic Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Hellenistic_Empire "Hellenistic Empire") can also establish a number of its own unique satrapies through the associated **[Hellenistic Empire](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diadochi_missions "Diadochi missions")** mission tree.

### Colony[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Colony") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Colony")]

**Colonies** typically represent subjects descended from the mother nation and have a loose, largely mercantile relationship with their overlord. Like tributaries, they neither provide manpower nor can be called into wars, but importantly each colony also provides a boost to the [![Image 138: Commerce value.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/4/45/Commerce_value.png/28px-Commerce_value.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") commerce income of the overlord that usually far outstrips their direct tribute, and so can be highly advantageous to establish. Colonies can mostly only be created through certain tasks in the [![Image 139: Flag of Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0d/Carthage.png/20px-Carthage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage")[Carthaginian mission trees](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Punic_missions "Punic missions"), though it is also possible for [![Image 140: Flag of Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ca/Athens.png/20px-Athens.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens")[Athens](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Athens "Athens") to become a colony of the [![Image 141: Flag of Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/55/Antigonid_Kingdom.png/20px-Antigonid_Kingdom.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom")[Antigonid Kingdom](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Antigonid_Kingdom "Antigonid Kingdom") if it finishes the loyalist path of its unique **[Athens in Chains](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Athens_in_Chains&action=edit&redlink=1 "Athens in Chains (page does not exist)")** national mission tree.

### Mercenary state[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Mercenary state") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Mercenary state")]

A **mercenary state** is a particular type of client state that has been created by granting control of a city to a mercenary company, much like the historical [![Image 142: Flag of Mamertine Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Placeholder_Flag.png/20px-Placeholder_Flag.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mamertine_Republic "Mamertine Republic")[Mamertine Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mamertine_Republic "Mamertine Republic"). As well as fighting alongside their overlord, each mercenary state will also [![Image 143: Mercenary maintenance.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/68/Mercenary_maintenance.png/28px-Mercenary_maintenance.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mercenary_maintenance "Mercenary maintenance") reduce the overlord's costs when hiring other mercenary companies, and if the overlord is a [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") with the [![Image 144: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Mercenary Contract Law](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws#Military_Reforms "Laws") enacted, the overlord can use the **Elect New Leader**[Character Interaction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Characters#Interactions "Characters") to replace their current leader with that of the mercenary state at the cost of some [![Image 145: Stability.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ae/Stability.png/28px-Stability.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Stability "Stability") stability, much like Agathokles himself once did.

Mercenary states can generally only be created by releasing an owned [![Image 146: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city through the [![Image 147: Create mercenary state.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1a/Create_mercenary_state.png/28px-Create_mercenary_state.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Create_mercenary_state.png)**Create Mercenary State** territory action at the cost of [![Image 148: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**50** political power, available from the start of the game to [![Image 149: Flag of Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c0/Syracusae.png/20px-Syracusae.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae")[Syracusae](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Syracusae "Syracusae") (representing the rich mercenary-using traditions of the Siceliotes) or to any country that has adopted the [![Image 150: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Satellite Status [![Image 151: Oratory power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") Oratory invention. The [![Image 152: Flag of Mamertine Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Placeholder_Flag.png/20px-Placeholder_Flag.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mamertine_Republic "Mamertine Republic")[Mamertine Republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Mamertine_Republic "Mamertine Republic"), when released, will also be a mercenary state.

### League city[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=10 "Edit section: League city") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: League city")]

A **league city** is a city that has been granted substantial autonomy and, like colonies, have a loose, largely mercantile relationship with their overlord. League cities are generally focused on trade and production, with powerful bonuses to [![Image 153: Global capital trade routes.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/7c/Global_capital_trade_routes.png/28px-Global_capital_trade_routes.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_route "Trade route") capital import routes and especially [![Image 154: Goods from slaves.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0b/Goods_from_slaves.png/28px-Goods_from_slaves.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Surplus "Surplus") slaves needed for surplus trade good production. This can make them useful as a trade partner to import excess goods from the mother country, as well as producing surpluses of valuable [trade goods](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade_goods "Trade goods") that can then be exported to the overlord.

League cities can only be created by releasing an owned [![Image 155: Territory city.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b5/Territory_city.png/28px-Territory_city.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City "City") city through the [![Image 156: Create mercenary state.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1a/Create_mercenary_state.png/28px-Create_mercenary_state.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Create_mercenary_state.png)**Create League City** territory action at the cost of [![Image 157: Political influence.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/76/Political_influence.png/28px-Political_influence.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Political_influence "Political influence")**50** political power, available to any country that has adopted the [![Image 158: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Coloniae [![Image 159: Civic power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/57/Civic_power.png/28px-Civic_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civic_inventions "Civic inventions") Civic invention.

## Acquiring subjects[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Acquiring subjects") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Acquiring subjects")]

There are many ways to acquire new subjects over the course of the game:

*   Waging war and defeating a country will allow for [![Image 160: Become subject.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6b/Become_subject.png/28px-Become_subject.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Become_subject.png)**forcing vassalization** of existing states in a peace deal. A country can be vassalized if its [![Image 161: Country capital.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Country_capital.png/28px-Country_capital.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Capital "Capital") capital is occupied in a war, with a smaller [![Image 162: Warscore cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d0/Warscore_cost.png/28px-Warscore_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/War_score_cost "War score cost") warscore cost and halved [![Image 163: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion")[aggressive expansion](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") compared to directly annexing the country. Defeated countries can be forced into any subject type that they and their future overlord fulfill the potential for, with all subject types being equivalent in terms of warscore cost and aggressive expansion gain. Like all other peace deal demands, forcing vassalization is not possible if the warscore cost exceeds 100, e.g. if the realm is currently too large. Both independent nations and countries that are already subjects of another country can be subjugated this way.
*   A more diplomatically inclined nation can **offer vassalization** through various [diplomatic influence actions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Influence "Diplomacy"). This requires that the potential conditions are met and that the target country have at least [![Image 164: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**100** opinion of the potential overlord, but is generally unlikely to be accepted unless the target is much weaker than, and has a very high opinion of the potential overlord. Targets are more likely to accept becoming certain subject types (such as tribal vassal or tributary status) over others (including feudatory and client status).
*   Countries can use the **Demand Subject Transfer** diplomatic action to peacefully take existing subject states of other nations if they are disloyal, and the target has at least **80** opinion of their potential new overlord. As with offering vassalization, military strength and the target's opinion of their potential new overlord are important.
*   The [![Image 165: Release subject.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/d5/Release_subject.png/28px-Release_subject.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Release_subject.png)**Release Subject** action in the Administration tab of the [![Image 166: Menu overview.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f1/Menu_overview.png/28px-Menu_overview.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Menu_overview.png) Nation overview screen allows a country to release a new client state out of any non-capital province, if there are [![Image 167: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_relations "Diplomatic relations") diplomatic relation slots available. This country will be dynamically created and take on the name, culture, and religion of its capital territory. 
*   If a pretender that the country has supported using the [![Image 168: Bribe character cost.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/af/Bribe_character_cost.png/28px-Bribe_character_cost.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character_interaction "Character interaction")**Support Pretender** interaction wins a [![Image 169: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war, they can be forced to become a client king if their country is a lower [rank](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Country_rank "Country rank") than their sponsor.
*   If the [![Image 170: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions") Proportional Agreements [![Image 171: Oratory power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Oratory_power.png/28px-Oratory_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Oratory_inventions "Oratory inventions") Oratory invention has been adopted, countries in a larger power's **Sphere of Influence** - those that are [![Image 172: Guaranteeing.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/5e/Guaranteeing.png/28px-Guaranteeing.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Guarantee "Guarantee") guaranteed and own less than [![Image 173: Local.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9c/Local.png/28px-Local.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Territories "Territories")**12** territories - will occasionally offer to become a feudatory of their protector.
*   Many **events** and especially **missions** also offer opportunities to get new subjects, both from releasing new countries out of currently owned territory (such as the many colonies and feudatories available in the [![Image 174: Flag of Carthage](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0d/Carthage.png/20px-Carthage.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage")[Carthaginian](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Carthage "Carthage") mission trees) or from vassalizing existing countries (such as in the Italic League mission/event chain of the [![Image 175: Flag of Rome](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Rome.png/20px-Rome.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome")[Roman](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Rome "Rome") mission tree). This is the only way to create new colonies.

## Subject loyalty[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Subject loyalty") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Subject loyalty")]

Each subject has a [![Image 176: Subject loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Subject_loyalty.png/28px-Subject_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty")**subject loyalty** value towards its overlord, visible in the diplomacy screen. This value modifies how much tribute is actually sent to the overlord and whether or not they will support and join rebellions and civil wars against the overlord.

Subject loyalty is a value between **0** and **100**, where 100 signifies total loyalty.

If a subject's loyalty drops below **33**, it will become **disloyal**, with the following effects:

*   If a [![Image 177: Civil war.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/17/Civil_war.png/28px-Civil_war.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civil_war "Civil war") civil war happens and a subject is disloyal, it will join the revolting side.
*   A disloyal subject will never accept an offensive call to arms, and will not act in a defensive war except to defend its own territories.

Subject loyalty has a base of **+10** and is determined by the total of the [![Image 178: Subject loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Subject_loyalty.png/28px-Subject_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty")**loyalty of subject states** modifier of the overlord and the [![Image 179: Loyalty to overlord.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/ac/Loyalty_to_overlord.png/28px-Loyalty_to_overlord.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty")**loyalty to overlord** modifier of the subject. These are affected by many factors, with the most important being the subject's [![Image 180: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion") opinion of the overlord, the [![Image 181: Land combat.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/99/Land_combat.png/28px-Land_combat.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Army "Army") relative size of its army, and the current [![Image 182: Aggressive expansion.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/fb/Aggressive_expansion.png/28px-Aggressive_expansion.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Aggressive_expansion "Aggressive expansion") aggressive expansion of the overlord. [![Image 183: Enact law cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/08/Enact_law_cost_modifier.png/28px-Enact_law_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws")[Laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws"), [diplomatic stances](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomacy#Diplomatic_Stance "Diplomacy"), [national ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas"), [![Image 184: Invention.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/13/Invention.png/28px-Invention.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions")[inventions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Inventions "Inventions"), [![Image 185: Military tradition.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/b/b3/Military_tradition.png/28px-Military_tradition.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions")[military traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions"), [![Image 186: Gw map icon.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/34/Gw_map_icon.png/28px-Gw_map_icon.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonder "Wonder")[wonders](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Wonders "Wonders"), and many event/mission modifiers can also affect subject loyalty.

## Converting subject types[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Converting subject types") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Converting subject types")]

The subject type of a particular vassal is typically fixed, but can be changed under certain circumstances.

Tribal vassals will be automatically converted into tributaries upon reforming from a [tribe](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Tribe "Tribe") to a [republic](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Republic "Republic") or [monarchy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Monarchy "Monarchy") through the [**[ROOT.GetCountry.GetName] and the [SCOPE.sCountry('overlord_country').GetAdjective] Treaty**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Country_diplomacy_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Country diplomacy events (page does not exist)") event. As tribal vassals have a bonus to [![Image 187: Civilization.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/c6/Civilization.png/28px-Civilization.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Civilization_value "Civilization value") maximum civilization value and growth, this will typically not take as long as tribes would normally reform.

Tributaries can request closer relations with their overlord and offer to become a client state through the [**[SCOPE.sCountry('tributary_more').GetAdjective] Gratitude**](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Country_diplomacy_events&action=edit&redlink=1 "Country diplomacy events (page does not exist)") event, which requires that the tributary to not have a [![Image 188: Truce.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Truce.png/28px-Truce.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Truce "Truce") truce with the overlord, not be at [![Image 189: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, and to have at least [![Image 190: Subject loyalty.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/83/Subject_loyalty.png/28px-Subject_loyalty.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_loyalty "Subject loyalty")**60** loyalty, and can only trigger once per country. As client states can be called to war and integrated this can be highly beneficial to the overlord if it is seeking to expand in the area and has enough [![Image 191: Diplomatic relations.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/5/58/Diplomatic_relations.png/28px-Diplomatic_relations.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Diplomatic_relations "Diplomatic relations") relationship slots, and means that it is possible to eventually integrate tributaries (and tribal vassals) as long as they can be made loyal enough to make the client state request.

The type of a subject can also be changed indirectly by first releasing it and then diplomatically re-vassalizing or forcibly subjugating in a [![Image 192: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") war, but this is still subject to the requirements of each subject type (i.e. cannot be used to get new colonies or mercenary states) and can take a few years given the need to wait for the [![Image 193: Truce.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/6/6e/Truce.png/28px-Truce.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Truce "Truce") to expire. It is mostly useful for converting a tributary into a client state, if it is too difficult to increase its loyalty.

As with most mechanics, there are also certain country-specific events and mission tasks that can convert subjects between different types.

## Integrating vassals[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&veaction=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Integrating vassals") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Subject_nations&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Integrating vassals")]

Only the more closely associated subject types - as a rule, those that can be called to war - can be [![Image 194: Integrating](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/7/79/Integrate.png/28px-Integrate.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Subject_nations#Integrating_vassals "Integrating")**integrated**, including feudatories, satrapies and client states. Other subject types, including tributaries and tribal vassals, cannot be integrated or annexed without first breaking or changing the existing subject relationship.

Starting integration requires that the vassal has at least [![Image 195: Opinion improvement active.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/8/8d/Opinion_improvement_active.png/28px-Opinion_improvement_active.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Opinion "Opinion")**190** opinion with its overlord. The cost of integration is equal to its **0.75** times its [![Image 196: Population.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/d/da/Population.png/28px-Population.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") population and is always at least **10**. Integration progresses by a base of **+0.25** each month and is affected by the [![Image 197: Integrate speed.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/31/Integrate_speed.png/28px-Integrate_speed.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Integration_speed "Integration speed")**integration speed** modifier, some of the more important sources of which are listed below:

Integration can take anywhere from a few months for a small city state to many decades (or more) for a large, populous client state. In some situations, it may even be faster to fabricate [![Image 198: Fabricate claim cost modifier.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/cf/Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png/28px-Fabricate_claim_cost_modifier.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Claim "Claim") claims on a subject, then release it and [![Image 199: War.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a7/War.png/28px-War.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Warfare "Warfare") declare war to annex its territory, at the cost of losing all [trade](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Trade "Trade") routes.


============================================================
# Italic_traditions
============================================================
Title: Italic traditions - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions

Markdown Content:
From Imperator Wiki

[Jump to navigation](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions#mw-head)[Jump to search](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Italic_traditions#searchInput)

This article has been verified for the current [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") (2.0) of the game.

_See also: [Military traditions](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military\_traditions "Military traditions")_

The _Italic traditions_ represent the focus on heavily armored and disciplined infantry found in the Etruscan, Roman and other Latin states. They came to favor the sword over the spear, and relied on quickly closing ground with and dispersing less well equipped infantry using strict formations, such as the Triplex Acies, with supporting light cavalry and skirmishers.

## Availability[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Italic_traditions&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Availability") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Italic_traditions&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Availability")]

[![Image 1](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f5/Latin_roman_path_1.png/250px-Latin_roman_path_1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_1.png)

_Roman_ traditions.

[![Image 2](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1d/Latin_tribal_path_4.png/250px-Latin_tribal_path_4.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_4.png)

_Italic tribe_ traditions.

The Italic military tradition groups, _Roman_ and _Italic Tribe_, are available from the start of the game for countries of the following culture groups:

*   Italic

The _Italic tribe_ traditions are additionally available for countries with:

*   Lepontic culture
*   Messapian culture
*   [![Image 3: Flag of Senones](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/3/39/Senones.png/20px-Senones.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Senones "Senones")[Senones](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Senones&action=edit&redlink=1 "Senones (page does not exist)")

This tradition group can also be unlocked by embracing the military tradition:

*   _[Embrace Punic-Italic Influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/North\_African\_traditions#Embrace\_Punic-Italic\_Influence "North African traditions")_ (from the _North African_ military tradition group)
*   _[Embrace Celto-Italic Influence](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Barbarian\_traditions#Embrace\_Celto-Italic\_Influence "Barbarian traditions")_ (from the _Barbarian_ military tradition group)

## Focus[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Italic_traditions&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Focus") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Italic_traditions&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Focus")]

The traditions focus on strengthening the below unit types and possibly unlock some new abilities:

| --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 47: Latin support path 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/2/22/Latin_support_path_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_3.png) | Scale the Walls | _Siege engines were scarcely used in the early Republic, sieges instead relying on covert attempts to scale the walls as well as sieges of attrition. During the siege of Veii, around 396 BC, the Roman engineers reputedly proved their worth by boring a great tunnel beneath the walls of the Etruscan city, breaking the siege once and for all._ |
| [![Image 48: Latin support path 2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/c/cd/Latin_support_path_2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_2.png) | The Fabri | _The military engineers of the early Roman army were responsible for the design of military structures, as well as the logistical duties associated with organizing their construction._ |
| [![Image 49: Latin roman path 4.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/e1/Latin_roman_path_4.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_4.png) | Roman Roads | _Often credited with winning entire wars, the practice of using the military to build roads, both in peace time and in conflict, was literally world-shaping._ |
| [![Image 50: Latin support path 5.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/2/21/Latin_support_path_5.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_5.png) | Logistics of State | _As the Roman military expanded, the practice of each soldier providing his own weapons became unsustainable. Thus, by instituting a chain of supply, from natural resources, to blacksmith, to the hands of her soldiers, Rome fueled her war machine with ease._ |
| [![Image 51: Latin roman path 1.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/f/f5/Latin_roman_path_1.png/200px-Latin_roman_path_1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_1.png) | Principes | _Veteran warriors all, the wealthy Principes sported heavy armor, and fought in tight-knit maniples, making excellent line troops._ |
| [![Image 52: Greek philosophy start bonus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/9d/Greek_philosophy_start_bonus.png/200px-Greek_philosophy_start_bonus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Greek_philosophy_start_bonus.png) | Embrace Italo-Greek Influence | N/A |
| [![Image 53: Latin roman path 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/1/1f/Latin_roman_path_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_3.png) | Triarii | _It is said that Rome's opponents know the end is near when the Triarii lead the charge. Often used to rout an already exhausted foe, the Triarii were equipped with huge spears known as hasta, capable of punching through light armor._ |
| [![Image 54: Latin roman path 2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/2/2e/Latin_roman_path_2.png/200px-Latin_roman_path_2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_2.png) | Triplex Acies | _The triple battle line was a manipular formation, primarily utilizing the Principes, Triarii and Hastati, with the Velites in freeform support. This highly flexible formation relied on the absolute discipline of each soldier, who had to know his place amidst the chaos of battle. In extreme circumstances, the Triplex was said to have reached over a mile long._ |
| [![Image 55: Latin roman path 5.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/ee/Latin_roman_path_5.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_5.png) | Professional Soldiers | _The evolution of a levied fighting force into a professional standing army, paid dividends in terms of quality and expectation. The salaried soldier was much less likely to baulk at orders, or flee from combat._ |
| [![Image 56: Latin roman path end bonus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/c/ce/Latin_roman_path_end_bonus.png/200px-Latin_roman_path_end_bonus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_end_bonus.png) | March of the Eagles | _AAA_ |
| [![Image 57: Latin support path 6.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/e5/Latin_support_path_6.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_6.png) | Scutum | _Adopting the iconic shield, the scutum, alongside the development of the manipular system, gave Rome a decisive advantage over her local enemies. Subsequently, the scutum became almost a national icon for the Romans; the very presence of a Roman shield wall was often enough to win a battle before it started._ |
| [![Image 58: Latin roman path 7.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/9/99/Latin_roman_path_7.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_7.png) | The Honor of Service | _As Rome grew in stature, military service became a matter of honor for young Roman men. Indeed, even after defeats as humiliating as the Battle of Cannae, in which up to 80,000 of Rome's soldiers were reportedly killed, the efforts of the senate were able to raise enough soldiers to take the places of the fallen._ |
| [![Image 59: Latin tribal path 7.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/f/f2/Latin_tribal_path_7.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_7.png) | Rousing Oratory | _The popularity of the Hellenic model of democracy spearheaded the empowerment of populist sentiment throughout a land hungry for rights. A skilled orator could sway entire cities in support of his or her cause, which could, at times, be useful._ |

|  | Tradition | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [![Image 102: Latin philosophy start bonus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/1/15/Latin_philosophy_start_bonus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_philosophy_start_bonus.png) | The Walled City | _Heavily fortified cities dotted the landscape of the Italian peninsula for generations. In Rome, the Servian wall looms high, built after the catastrophic sacking at the hands of Brennus the Gaul, and affording the inhabitants a small measure of peace from the strife to come._ |
| [![Image 103: Latin tribal path 6.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/d/d2/Latin_tribal_path_6.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_6.png) | Mare Nostrum | _The coast played a huge part in the economic life of Italy. Ships, and viable control over the locality, were vital for the continuation of prosperity in every nascent state. Skilled shipwrights, cheap labor, and abundant building material could be found in all Mediterranean harbors._ |
| [![Image 104: Latin support path 1.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/8/83/Latin_support_path_1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_1.png) | State Navy | _During the first Punic war, the Roman senate mandated the construction of a navy to rival that of their foe, the Carthaginian Empire. Modelled largely on the Carthaginian designs as well as sporting significant Greek influence, the enthusiasm for naval dominance continued throughout Rome's republican era._ |
| [![Image 105: Latin tribal path 4.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/1/1d/Latin_tribal_path_4.png/200px-Latin_tribal_path_4.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_4.png) | The Acquisition of Wealth | _Italic tribal cultures, and the nascent Roman Republic all engaged in the practices of raiding, pillaging, and piracy, at times. From the mythical rape of the Sabine women, to the siege of Veii, instances of looting are present throughout the history of Italy._ |
| [![Image 106: Latin tribal path 1.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/4/42/Latin_tribal_path_1.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_1.png) | Artisanal Bronzework | _Even after the advent of iron-working, magnificent bronze shields, spears, and helmets of all kind, pour from the forges of nascent Italic civilizations. Even tribal cultures possessed a mixture of potent bronze and iron equipment, rendering them a fearsome sight to behold._ |
| [![Image 107: Latin tribal path 5.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/5/52/Latin_tribal_path_5.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_5.png) | The Aulos | _Numerous musical instruments are depicted in Etruscan art, but none more mysterious than the Aulos. Originally a Greek invention, the signature double-reed and pipes would have been heard accompanying all manner of occasion, perhaps even lifting soldiers' spirits on the eve before battle._ |
| [![Image 108: Latin roman path 6.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/c/cc/Latin_roman_path_6.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_roman_path_6.png) | Velites | _Comprised predominantly of young, unproven soldiers, the velites were light troops which fell outside the standard army organisation, instead acting as mobile skirmishers. The Velites are said to have sought glory and recognition by their daredevil acts during battle._ |
| [![Image 109: Latin tribal path end bonus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/6/62/Latin_tribal_path_end_bonus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_end_bonus.png) | Hill Dwellers | _From the Apennines to the Alps, hills and mountains were an ever-present feature of Italian life. It might be said that our armies are more accustomed than most, to the foot-slogging associated with fighting in adverse conditions._ |
| [![Image 110: Latin tribal path 2.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/c/c2/Latin_tribal_path_2.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_2.png) | The Equestrian Order | _In Rome, the equites constituted an entire sub-class of patricians; wealthy aristocracy whose holdings indicated their prestige. As such, the superior wealth and training of the equites afforded them the liability to serve in the exclusive cavalry contingent of the republic's forces. Elsewhere in the Italic world, warhorses were regarded as the domain of the elite, the horses themselves often being laid to rest alongside their earthly masters in the event of their death._ |
| [![Image 111: Latin support path 7.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/3/33/Latin_support_path_7.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_7.png) | Foederati | _Rome's primary source of cavalry in the mid-republic, had shifted from the equites to mercenaries and allied states. This allowed a diverse array of styles and abilities to be incorporated into the ever-growing Roman military._ |
| [![Image 112: Latin support path 4.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/5/56/Latin_support_path_4.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_4.png) | Castra | _Cohorts, and later legions, were adept at constructing temporary; and in many cases permanent, fortresses, which served as resupply depots, rallying points, or as bases to extend a zone of military control over an unpacified area._ |
| [![Image 113: Latin support path end bonus.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/e/e6/Latin_support_path_end_bonus.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_support_path_end_bonus.png) | Rome, Ascendant | _After a great victory, Roman custom dictated that honor be paid to the responsible commander. Lavish celebrations, often encompassing athletic events, feasts, and processions, would occupy the city for days - reinforcing in friend and foe alike, the might of Rome._ |
| [![Image 114: Celtic germanic path 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/5/51/Celtic_germanic_path_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Celtic_germanic_path_3.png) | Embrace Italo-Celtic Influence | N/A |
| [![Image 115: Latin tribal path 3.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/f/f4/Latin_tribal_path_3.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/File:Latin_tribal_path_3.png) | Pietas | _Relying on the devoutness of their own soldiers, and that of their opponents, the Etruscan forces are reputed to have used their priestly orders to curse their foes in plain sight, causing them to rout. In any event, the peoples of ancient Italy left nothing to chance; proper obeisance was made to the gods, before each battle was made._ |

============================================================
# Modifiers
============================================================
Title: Modifier - Imperator Wiki

URL Source: https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Modifiers

Markdown Content:
(Redirected from [Modifiers](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifiers&redirect=no "Modifiers"))

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.

At least some were last verified for [version](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Imperator_Wiki:Versioning "Imperator Wiki:Versioning") 1.0.

A **modifier** is a number (an integer or a float) which, once unlocked, is inserted into a term. The unlocking of modifiers to buff (increase) gained points is one of the main means of player to play the game.

_Note:_ While modifiers are always either integers or floating points numbers, the UI regularly represents them as percentage values.

## Purpose[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Purpose") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Purpose")]

Imperator: Rome is a _paint-the-map_ type of game: there is a map, and it is divided into many sub-entities called cities (which are grouped into regions and regions are grouped into areas). Cities belonging to the same state are all printed in the same predefined color (in the political map mode).

To conquer more cities, you wage war, and you wage war by pushing your legions across the map. Besides pushing your legions (and fleets) across the map, the game faces you with decisions, most of these decisions unlock or alter modifiers into the base terms to calculate end values. _The higher the end values, the more troops you can afford._

In order to really play Imperator: Rome, as opposed to simply pushing buttons without fully understanding the implications, the player need to know the exact terms which are used to calculate all of the relevant end values, so that he can make the decisions, which maximize these values.

### Abstract example[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Abstract example") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Abstract example")]

The default term used to calculate some end value may be:

base_value1 + base_value2 = end_value
Then the game designers simply invent/define some **modifiers**:

(base_value1 × modifier1 × modifier2 + base_value2 + modifier3) × modifier4 = end_value
## Concrete examples[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Concrete examples") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Concrete examples")]

### Building: Training Camp[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Building: Training Camp") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Building: Training Camp")]

One [pop](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population "Population") belonging to the [![Image 1: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen")_Freemen_-social class generates 10 Manpower per day. In case there is a [building](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings "Buildings") of the type [![Image 2: Training Camp](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/e/ec/Building_military.png/28px-Building_military.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Buildings#Training_Camp "Training Camp") Training Camp, a **modifier** of **+10%** is added to the term which calculates the total [![Image 3: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/24px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") generates in that city. The term might look like:

[![Image 4: Manpower.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/0/0c/Manpower.png/28px-Manpower.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Manpower "Manpower") per month (of one city) = # of [![Image 5: Freemen](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/a/a3/Pop_freemen.png/28px-Pop_freemen.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Population#Freemen "Freemen") × 10 × modifier_average_happiness × (1 + (# of Training Camp × 0.1) × other_modifiers

### Military tradition[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Military tradition") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Military tradition")]

Unlocking the [Military tradition](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Military_traditions "Military traditions") "Triarii" for 800 [![Image 6: Military power.png](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/images/thumb/9/98/Military_power.png/28px-Military_power.png)](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Martial_inventions "Martial inventions") adds a modifier of +15% offense to all Heavy infantry-type cohorts.

### Slave pops required to produce one additional trade good[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Slave pops required to produce one additional trade good") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Slave pops required to produce one additional trade good")]

The base value defaults to 15 or 13 for the **fields**[terrain type](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/City#Terrain_types "City"). The [Government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") type and [Laws](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Laws "Laws") can give -1 to +2 to this value. Thus the number varies between a minimum of 12 and a maximum of 17.

## Basic terms in Imperator: Rome[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Basic terms in Imperator: Rome") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Basic terms in Imperator: Rome")]

### Monthly income[[edit](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&veaction=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Monthly income") | [edit source](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/index.php?title=Modifier&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Monthly income")]

The term to calculate the monthly income of a state _might_ look something like the following:

![Image 7: {\displaystyle {\text{Monthly income}}=\left({\text{Total Taxes}}\,+\,{\text{Total Commerce}}\right)\,-\,{\text{Expenditures}}}](https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/0ff7d38c62457d89de4561c90a1af644c53de5fd)

*   Total Taxes = total sum of taxes generated in each city × (1 + [modifier_government](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Government "Government") + [modifier_ruler_faction](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Politics "Politics") + [modifier_ruler_attributes](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Attributes "Attributes") + [modifier_ruler_traits](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Character#Traits "Character") + [modifer_national_ideas](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/National_ideas "National ideas") + …) 
    *   taxes generated in one city = ((# of slave-pops) × 0.03) × modifier_average_happiness × (1 + modifier_building + modifier_event_1 + modifier_event_2 + modifier_capital_city + modifier_capital_province + modifier_capital_area + [modifier_province_policy](https://imperator.paradoxwikis.com/Governor_policy "Governor policy") + modifier_governor + …)

Note that is it full of modifiers, most of which can be influenced by player decisions. Some can be changed at any time, some need to be unlocked first. Some of the unlockable ones become available more or less automatically, if just enough time has passed, some need to be worked towards deliberately.
